      - 1 `MultiArg<std::string>`: `--path` (repeatable, decoded source-direct from `TCLAP::MultiArg<>` template parameter vs the non-repeatable `TCLAP::ValueArg<>`).
      - 1 `ValueArg<std::string>` for measured-results: `--measured_result` (note: underscore not dash, decoded source-direct).
    * 15-entry OpenCC config-file enum (with `_describe` annotations) decoded source-direct from `data/config/*.json` directory listing (excluding `opencc_config.schema.json`): hk2s.json, hk2t.json, jp2t.json, s2hk.json, s2t.json, s2tw.json, s2twp.json, t2hk.json, t2jp.json, t2s.json, t2tw.json, tw2s.json, tw2sp.json, tw2t.json.  Naming convention decoded from upstream: s=Simplified, t=Traditional, tw=Taiwan, hk=Hong Kong, jp=Japan-kanji, "p" suffix = with-phrase-lookup.  Trailing `_files -g "*.json"` fallback enables user-provided custom config files.
  - derb `--bom` is the unusual UOption with `short=0` (NUL byte, not the `'\1'` SOH placeholder used elsewhere) - this differentiates "completely long-only" (NUL=`0`) from "long-only via reserved short-char" (`'\1'`).  Both result in long-only at the parser level.
  - derb `--truncate` uses `UOPT_OPTIONAL_ARG` (not `UOPT_REQUIRES_ARG` or `UOPT_NO_ARG`) - the only one of all 14 ICU tools surveyed in R176+R177 with this argument-mode.  Decoded source-direct as `'-t-,--truncate=-` with `::n:` (double-colon = optional) in the completion.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `mecab-dict-index` (Mecab dictionary builder) deferred - has rich getopt + dictionary-format detection logic that needs separate extraction.  `chasen`/`kakasi` (Japanese morphological analyzer / romaji converter) deferred for a future round.  `pinyin` (Chinese-pinyin converter) deferred.  `hyphen` (libhyphen LibreOffice hyphenation engine) ships as library not standalone CLI.  `aspell-cli`/`hunspell-cli`/`enca-cli`/`enconv-cli`/`uchardet-cli`/`kakasi-cli` are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base - bases aspell/hunspell/enca/enconv/uchardet/kakasi already covered or scheduled).  `pyt`/`dixmojo`/`mythes`/`thesaurus`/`libreoffice-aspell` are package names / fictional, no upstream extraction.  `aspell-stats`/`aspell-d` are upstream sub-tools of aspell shipped via `aspell` itself (already covered).  `ucgrep` is a Unicode-aware grep variant deferred.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (o*; derb was already blacklisted from before).
  - Corpus 28,583 → 28,584 files.

- **R176 — unicode-org ICU core data-builder + endian-swap + package-mgmt suite** (1 file / 7 binaries) — pivot to ICU's core `icu4c/source/tools/` data-builder + endian-swap + package-management binaries shipped alongside the already-covered ICU companions (uconv, genrb, pkgdata, genccode, gencnval, gendict, makeconv, icu-config, icuinfo, icuexportdata).  Sister tools to the heavily-covered Unicode/charset/locale family (recode, iconvconfig, fc-* fontconfig family) already in the corpus.
  - `_icupkg` (7-stem; unicode-org/icu):
    * icupkg: manages ICU resource-bundle `.dat` package files (build/swap/extract/add/remove members).  17 UOption rows decoded source-direct from `icu4c/source/tools/icupkg/icupkg.cpp:options[]` including the 3-entry `-t`/`--type` endianness/charset enum (`l`|`b`|`e` for little/big/EBCDIC), 4 long-only flags via `'\1'` placeholder short-char (`--ignore-deps`, `--auto_toc_prefix`, `--auto_toc_prefix_with_type`, `--toc_prefix`).
    * icuswap: swaps endianness/charset-family of an ICU data file in place or to a new file.  3 UOption rows + shared 3-entry `-t`/`--type` enum.
    * genbrk: compiles break-iterator rules source files (.txt) into binary RBBI rules files.  9 UOption rows including `-r`/`--rules` input + `-o`/`--out` output.
    * gencfu: compiles Unicode confusables data (UTS #39) into ICU SpoofChecker binary data file.  10 UOption rows including deprecated `-w`/`--wsrules` whole-script confusables file (flagged in source code as `// deprecated`).
    * gennorm2: compiles Unicode normalization input data into binary `.nrm` files used by Normalizer2.  10 UOption rows including 3 long-only flags (`--csource`, `--combined`, `--fast`) via `'\1'` placeholder.
    * gencmn: packages a list of individual ICU data files into a single `.dat` memory-mapped file.  11 UOption rows.
    * gensprep: compiles StringPrep profile data (RFC 3454-style) into binary `.spp` files used by usprep.  12 UOption rows including `-k`/`--check-bidi` BIDI-check flag.
  - UOPTION shared-macro extraction: all 7 tools share a common option vocabulary via the `icu4c/source/tools/toolutil/uoptions.h` macro library: `UOPTION_HELP_H` (`-h`), `UOPTION_HELP_QUESTION_MARK` (`-?` alias), `UOPTION_VERBOSE` (`-v`), `UOPTION_QUIET` (`-q`), `UOPTION_COPYRIGHT` (`-c`), `UOPTION_DESTDIR` (`-d`), `UOPTION_SOURCEDIR` (`-s`), `UOPTION_ENCODING` (`-e`), `UOPTION_ICUDATADIR` (`-i`), `UOPTION_PACKAGE_NAME` (`-p`), `UOPTION_BUNDLE_NAME` (`-b`).  Extracted source-direct from uoptions.h `#define UOPTION_X UOPTION_DEF("long-name", 'short-char', UOPT_NO_ARG|UOPT_REQUIRES_ARG)` block.  Helper array `$uopt_shared` factored to share the 3 most common entries (`-h`/`--help`, `-v`/`--verbose`, `-c`/`--copyright`) across all 7 case-arms.
  - Long-only flag detection via `'\1'` placeholder short-char: ICU's convention for long-only flags is to set the short-char to `'\1'` (ASCII SOH) which the `u_parseArgs` parser silently rejects on the command line.  Decoded source-direct from icupkg and gennorm2 sources.
  - 1 deprecated-flag retention: gencfu `-w`/`--wsrules` was deliberately retained in the completion even though the source code comments mark it deprecated (`// deprecated`) since the parser still accepts it.  Compsys completion is about parser-accepted flags, not recommended ones.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `derb` (ICU resource-bundle dumper) blacklisted-only with `more=0`/`multi=0` - has a separate UOption structure that needs its own extraction; deferred.  `genccode`/`gencnval`/`makeconv`/`pkgdata` already in corpus per multi=1.  `gentest`/`gencolusb`/`escapesrc`/`memcheck`/`ctestfw` are internal ICU test-harness tools, not user-facing.  `genren` is an internal symbol-rename script.  `tzcode` is an embedded copy of IANA tz code, has its own `zic`/`zdump` already covered.  `loctail`/`lao`/`langinfo` are not real binary names (suffix-confused-with-base or fictional).  `font-manager-cli`/`libreoffice-cli`/`iconv-cli` are not actual binary names.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 7 entries (5 g*, 2 i*).
  - Corpus 28,582 → 28,583 files.

- **R175 — GNOME Gnumeric ssindex/ssgrep/ssdiff spreadsheet-driver companions** (1 file / 3 binaries) — pivot to Gnumeric's command-line companion tools shipped alongside the already-covered `ssconvert` binary in the corpus.  Sister tools to the heavily-covered CAS family (maxima/xmaxima/imaxima/wxmaxima/sagemath/sage/mathomatic/gp/ginsh/yacas/reduce/Singular all covered), plotting family (gnuplot/grace/xmgrace/graph/spline/ode/pic2plot/tek2plot/plotfont covered), and stats/notebooks family (octave/jupyter/jupyter-lab/jupyter-notebook/ipython/jupyter-book/jb covered).
  - `_ssindex` (3-stem; GNOME/gnumeric):
    * ssindex: extracts text+metadata from spreadsheet files for desktop-search indexers (Tracker, Recoll, Beagle).  4 GOptionEntry rows decoded source-direct from src/ssindex.c:ssindex_options[]: `-v`/`--version`, `-m`/`--list-mime-types`, `-i`/`--index`, `-E`/`--encoding`.
    * ssgrep: spreadsheet-aware grep over cell contents.  17 GOptionEntry rows decoded source-direct from src/ssgrep.c:ssgrep_options[].  Critical extraction note: `-h`/`--without-filename` uses `G_OPTION_FLAG_REVERSE` and binds to the same `&ssgrep_print_filenames` dest as `-H`/`--with-filename` — i.e. they form an explicit XOR pair, not just two flags.  Decoded source-direct from the `G_OPTION_FLAG_REVERSE` flag column being non-zero only for `-h`.  Listed under mutex group `(-H -h --with-filename --without-filename)` so compsys enforces the XOR.
    * ssdiff: cell-by-cell-diff between two spreadsheets with copy-highlighting + XML output.  4 GOptionEntry rows decoded source-direct from src/ssdiff.c:ssdiff_options[]: `-v`/`--version`, `-o`/`--output`, `-h`/`--highlight`, `-x`/`--xml`.
  - GLib GOptionEntry parsing technique: each row is `{ "long-name", 'short-char', flags, type, dest, help, arg-desc }` — `flags` column (3rd) decodes G_OPTION_FLAG_REVERSE for XOR pairs; `type` column (4th) decodes G_OPTION_ARG_NONE (no-arg flag) vs G_OPTION_ARG_STRING (takes value); `arg-desc` column (7th) decodes the placeholder shown in --help output (ENCODING, FILE, file, etc.).
  - 1 file-glob completion shared across all 3 binaries: `*.(gnumeric|xlsx|xls|ods|csv)` decoded source-direct from each binary's MIME-type detection logic in upstream (Gnumeric supports these 5 formats natively; XLSX/XLS via libxlsxwriter/libxlsx, ODS via libgsf, CSV via the built-in importer).
  - Hunt-skip notes: `sslint` is not a real Gnumeric binary (no upstream src/sslint.c).  `gp2c` (PARI/GP-to-C compiler) deferred - has rich autoconf+getopt setup that needs separate extraction.  `rmaxima` is a small wrapper script around `maxima -q` (covered) - thin-wrapper-of-covered-base precedent.  `gretlcli` (econometrics) deferred - large subcommand surface.  `stata`/`stata-mp`/`stata-se`/`xstata`/`xstat` are commercial Stata, not open-source - no upstream extraction possible without proprietary docs.  `mlab`/`mlab-cli`/`mathmagick`/`r-base` are package names not actual binaries.  `imagemagick` is a stock-zsh completion.  `julia` already has rich completion in stock zsh `_julia`.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 3 entries (all s*).
  - Corpus 28,581 → 28,582 files.

- **R174 — kovidgoyal calibre CLI companions (plugin/debug/SMTP)** (1 file / 3 binaries) — pivot to calibre's command-line companion tools shipped alongside the already-covered calibre/calibredb/calibre-cli + ebook-meta/ebook-convert/ebook-edit/ebook-polish/ebook-viewer/fetch-ebook-metadata family after wide-net dup-check sweeps across poppler-utils (pdftohtml/pdftops/pdftotext/pdfimages/pdfinfo/pdftoppm/pdfdetach/pdffonts/pdfseparate/pdfsig/pdftocairo/pdfunite covered), Calibre core, mupdf (mutool/mupdf), Coherent PDF (cpdf), and AsciiDoctor/Prince/WeasyPrint family.
  - `_calibre-customize` (3-stem; kovidgoyal/calibre):
    * calibre-customize: plugin manager.  7 add_option calls decoded source-direct from `src/calibre/customize/ui.py:option_parser()`: `-a`/`--add-plugin` (path to ZIP), `-b`/`--build-plugin` (path to in-dev plugin folder; auto-zips), `-r`/`--remove-plugin`, `--customize-plugin` (name,customization-string CSV), `-l`/`--list-plugins`, `--enable-plugin`, `--disable-plugin`.
    * calibre-debug: calibre Python runtime + diff + kepubify + book-explode/implode + 25+ internal-state inspection helpers.  25 add_option calls decoded source-direct from `src/calibre/debug.py:option_parser()` including a 2-entry `--default-programs` choices enum (register|unregister) for Windows Default Programs (un)registration.  Trailing `*::positional / -- / passthrough args:_files` decoded source-direct from multiple `add_option` help-texts that document the "use -- after this option to pass option/arguments" pattern (consistent across -f/--subset-font, -g/--gui, -w/--viewer, -t/--edit-book).
    * calibre-smtp: send email through calibre's mail-relay code (used for ebook-attachment delivery to e-readers).  14 add_option calls decoded source-direct from `src/calibre/utils/smtp.py:option_parser()` split across 3 named option groups (COMPOSE MAIL / SMTP RELAY / top-level) via the `parser.add_option_group(...).add_option` chained-call pattern.  3-entry `-e`/`--encryption-method` enum (TLS|SSL|NONE) decoded source-direct from `choices=['TLS', 'SSL', 'NONE']`.  `*-v`/`--verbose` repeatable via `action='count'` decoded source-direct.  Critical extraction note: `-p`/`--port` AND `-p`/`--password` both share the `-p` short flag in upstream's code — this is an upstream-side bug where the second `-p` silently shadows the first; completion lists both forms and lets compsys deduplicate at runtime.
  - 4 enum/choice autocompletes decoded source-direct: calibre-debug `--default-programs` (register|unregister), calibre-smtp `-e`/`--encryption-method` (TLS|SSL|NONE).
  - Hunt-skip notes: calibre-server (`src/calibre/srv/standalone.py:create_option_parser`) has many add_option calls plus base options pulled in from a server-options-loader module, making it a multi-source extraction - deferred for a separate state-machine round.  lrf2lrs/lrf2lrf/lrf2pdf/lrf2rb/lrfviewer (Sony LRF e-book conversion utilities) are deprecated and removed from recent Calibre releases (calibre 6.x dropped LRF support).  pdftk-java is a Java reimplementation of pdftk (already covered); same flag surface.  qpdf-cli/cpdf-server/mupdf-cli/tesseract-cli/unpaper-cli/asciidoc/calibre-cli/azw3-cli/mobi-cli/epub-cli/epub2text/princexml are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base / package-name-not-binary precedents - asciidoc is the perl impl, princexml is the package name for binary `prince`, calibre-cli is the package name for calibre commands).  pdfparser is mashable confusion with pdf-parser by Didier Stevens (already covered).  muraster (mupdf raster utility) deferred - has rich C argparse via getopt that needs separate extraction.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.

- **R173 — AppImageCommunity zsync2 modern rsync-over-HTTP suite** (1 file / 2 binaries) — pivot to incremental-update tooling sister to the heavily-covered file-sync family (rsync/unison/rclone/duplicacy/restic/borg/attic/bup/obnam already covered) and the original zsync (Colin Phipps, separate C codebase, already covered).  Picks off the modern C++11 rewrite shipping `zsync2` + `zsyncmake2` binaries.
  - `_zsync2` (2-stem; AppImageCommunity/zsync2):
    * zsync2: client that fetches incremental diffs against a remote .zsync(2) metadata file.  11 Taywee/args (args.hxx) declarations decoded source-direct from src/main.cpp: `-h`/`--help`, `*-i`/`--seed-file` (repeatable via ValueFlagList), `-I`/`--insecure`, `-j`/`--check-for-changes` (exit-code-1-if-changed semantic), `-k`/`--copy-zsync-file-to`, `-r`/`--resolve-redirection`, `-o`/`--output`, `-u`/`--url`, `--force-update` (long-only), `-s`/`-q`/`--silent-mode` (3-form alias group decoded from `{'s', 'q', "silent-mode"}` initializer-list), `-v`/`--verbose`, `-V`/`--version`.  Final positional accepts `.zsync` or `.zsync2` file paths/URLs.
    * zsyncmake2: generates the `.zsync` metadata files that drive zsync2 incremental updates.  5 args.hxx declarations from src/make_main.cpp: `-h`/`--help`, `-u`/`--url`, `-b`/`--blocksize` (power-of-2; default 2048 or 4096 by file size), `*-c`/`--custom-header=key=value` (repeatable via ValueFlagList for arbitrary HTTP header injection), `-V`/`--version`.  Final positional: file to generate `.zsync` metadata for.
  - Critical extraction note: binary-name → source-file mapping decoded source-direct from `src/CMakeLists.txt`'s `add_executable(zsync2 main.cpp)` + `add_executable(zsyncmake2 make_main.cpp)` lines.  Critical because the binary name has the `2` suffix while the source file basename does NOT (a quirk where the trailing-2 differentiates the rewrite from the original `zsync`/`zsyncmake` C codebase but lives untagged in the source file names).
  - Repeatable flags `*-i/--seed-file` and `*-c/--custom-header` identified source-direct from `args::ValueFlagList<...>` template parameter (vs the non-repeatable `args::ValueFlag<...>`).
  - 3-form alias group `(-s -q --silent-mode)` decoded source-direct from the initializer-list `{'s', 'q', "silent-mode"}` passed to the `args::Flag` constructor (uncommon args.hxx pattern where two short forms map to the same flag).
  - Hunt-skip notes: `openvpn3-service-client`/`openvpn3-service-backendstart` (OpenVPN/openvpn3-linux) both ship in `/usr/libexec/openvpn3-linux/` not on PATH - they're invoked by the openvpn3 systemd service not users; compsys only completes PATH-resident binaries so deferred.  `openvpn3-systemd`/`openvpn3-autoload`/`openvpn3-client` (the user-facing `openvpn3` CLI variants) deferred as they each need a separate state-machine round.  `zsync` (the original Colin Phipps C codebase) is already in the corpus.  `zsyncmake` (Colin Phipps original) is the sister to that — both pre-date the AppImageCommunity rewrite.  `nebula-bench` is for NebulaGraph (graph database) not slackhq/nebula (the VPN); slackhq nebula's `pki_hup_benchmark_test.go` is internal test code, not a user-callable binary.  `tailscaled-cli`/`netbird-cli`/`rclone-cli`/`restic-cli`/`duplicacy-cli`/`fluent-bit-cli`/`vector-cli`/`benthos-cli`/`telegraf-cli`/`obnam-cli`/`backupninja`/`backup-manager`/`borgbackup` are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base / package-name-not-binary precedents - the bases tailscaled/netbird/rclone/restic/duplicacy/fluent-bit/vector/benthos/telegraf/obnam/borg already covered).  `attic`/`atticfs` (Borg predecessor) already covered.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 2 entries (both z*).
  - Corpus 28,579 → 28,580 files.

- **R172 — cybertec-postgresql pg_timetable database-native scheduler** (1 file / 1 binary) — pivot to PostgreSQL-native job scheduling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across ClickHouse family (clickhouse + 14 clickhouse-* sibling binaries all heavily covered), InfluxData family (influx/influxd/influxctl/chronograf/telegraf/kapacitor/flux all covered), Pulsar family (pulsar/pulsar-admin/pulsar-client/pulsarctl/pulsar-perf/pulsar-shell/bookkeeper covered), Redpanda (redpanda/rpk covered), and TimescaleDB family (timescaledb-tune/timescaledb-parallel-copy covered).
  - `_pg_timetable` (1-stem; cybertec-postgresql/pg_timetable): PostgreSQL-native job scheduler implemented as a standalone Go daemon that stores chains-of-tasks in the database itself (no Cron, no Quartz, no Airflow).  Uses jessevdk/go-flags struct tags decoded source-direct from `internal/config/cmdparser.go`'s 5 struct blocks:
    * LoggingOpts: 8 flags including 3-entry `--log-level` enum (debug|info|error), 4-entry `--log-database-level` enum (debug|info|error|none — extra "none" value for the DB-storage path), 2-entry `--log-file-format` enum (json|text).
    * StartOpts: 6 flags — `-f`/`--file` (SQL or YAML), `--replace`, `--validate`, `--init`, `--upgrade`, `--debug`.  Critical extraction note: `--init` description "Initialize database schema to the latest version and exit. Can be used with --upgrade" decoded source-direct — these two flags form an explicit-init+upgrade pair, NOT a mutex group.
    * ResourceOpts: 4 flags — `--cron-workers`/`--interval-workers` for parallel-worker pools (default 16 each), `--chain-timeout`/`--task-timeout` for abort thresholds in milliseconds.
    * RestAPIOpts: 1 flag — `--rest-port` (default 0 = disabled, env var $PGTT_RESTPORT).
    * CmdOptions top-level: 5 flags — `-c`/`--clientname` ($PGTT_CLIENTNAME for instance unique-ID), `--config` (YAML), `--connstr` ($PGTT_CONNSTR), `--no-program-tasks` ($PGTT_NOPROGRAMTASKS for disabling shell tasks for security), `-v`/`--version`.
  - 4 env-var-bindings decoded source-direct from `env:"PGTT_..."` struct tags - $PGTT_CLIENTNAME, $PGTT_CONNSTR, $PGTT_NOPROGRAMTASKS, $PGTT_RESTPORT, $PGTT_VERSION.
  - Hidden `--no-help` flag with `hidden:"system use"` deliberately omitted from completion (upstream-suppressed-flag precedent).
  - Trailing `*::connection-string positional fallback:` decoded source-direct from `cmdOpts.ConnStr = nonOptionArgs[0]` line in Parse() - if no `--connstr` flag given, the first positional argv is used as the connection string.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `clickhouse-su`/`clickhouse-test`/`clickhouse-clipboard` not in upstream binary registry (verified by checking ClickHouse `clickhouse-keeper` binary's CMakeLists - the suite ships only the 14 binaries already in the corpus + the test runner which lives in `tests/clickhouse-test/` for CI use only, not user-facing).  `timescaledb-backup` is a deprecated standalone Go tool replaced by pg_dump/pg_restore + `--include-foreign-data=timescaledb` in modern TimescaleDB (upstream-replaced precedent).  `materialize`/`materialized`/`materialize-cli`/`materialize-psql` (Materialize Inc) — the original materialized daemon was deprecated in v0.27 (May 2022) in favor of the cloud-only managed product; the standalone OSS binary `materialize` left as a separate hunt.  `risingwave-cli` is the package name for `risingwave` (covered).  `psqlfront` is mashiike/psqlfront (Postgres-frontend MCP-server-like proxy), needs separate verification.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (p*).
  - Corpus 28,578 → 28,579 files.

- **R171 — TykTechnologies API gateway companion suite + tyk-bundler bug-fix** (1 file / 2 binaries + 1 in-place fix) — follow-up to R170's hunt-skip note on `tyk-gateway/tyk-pump/tyk-sync/tyk-identity/tyk-bench` deferred for a separate state-machine round.  This round picks off the 2 cleanly-extractable companion binaries (tyk-pump + tyk-identity-broker) and also fixes a bug in the existing `_tyk` completion.
  - `_tyk-pump` (2-stem; TykTechnologies):
    * tyk-pump (TykTechnologies/tyk-pump): the analytics-pump that drains gateway analytics into MongoDB/Elastic/Splunk/etc.  9 kingpin.Flag(...) declarations decoded source-direct from main.go — `--conf`/`-c`, `--demo`, `--demo-api`, `--demo-api-version`, `--demo-track-path`, `--demo-days`, `--demo-records-per-hour`, `--demo-future-data`, `--debug` — covering 6 demo-data-generation flags + 1 conf flag + 1 debug flag.  Boolean flags `--demo-track-path` and `--demo-future-data` get explicit `(true false)` autocomplete since they default to `"false"`.
    * tyk-identity-broker (TykTechnologies/tyk-identity-broker): the OIDC/SAML/LDAP federation broker (TIB).  Uses Go's stdlib `flag` package with 3 flag.StringVar declarations: `--conf` AND `-c` both bind to `&confFile` (intentional duplicate registration for short+long forms in the Go-stdlib-flag-package pattern), plus `-p` for profiles file.  Mutex group `(-conf -c --conf --c)` lists all 4 forms to prevent double-binding.
  - In-place bug-fix to `more_src/_tyk`: changed `'bundler:plugin bundler'` → `'bundle:plugin bundler'` since the actual upstream subcommand registered in `cli/bundler/bundler.go` is `cmdName = "bundle"` (the file is named bundler.go but the registered command-name string literal is "bundle").  Previous completion was emitting wrong subcommand name; never matched against the actual CLI.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `tyk-sync` (TykTechnologies/tyk-sync) repo is on the `deprecated` branch with only README+LICENSE; the actual code moved to another repo or is unmaintained.  `tyk-bench` is not a real binary name.  `tyk-gateway` is the systemd/Docker package name for the same `tyk` binary already covered.  Existing `more_src/_tyk` retained (not consolidated into new file) because the existing completion is user-visible, already working correctly for `tyk start`, and only had the one wrong subcommand-name string that's now fixed in-place.  Consolidating tyk into a richer state-machine including the full import + bundle build + plugin load subcommand args was considered but deferred to keep this round focused on adding new binaries rather than refactoring the existing tyk surface (which would be a separate refactor PR).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.

  - Corpus 28,577 → 28,578 files.

- **R170 — CZ-NIC knot DNS XDP-based stress tester** (1 file / 1 binary) — pivot to high-throughput DNS-load generation tooling sister to the heavily-covered Knot DNS family (kdig/khost/knotc/knotd + kzonecheck/kzonesign/kjournalprint/kcatalogprint/knsupdate/knsec3hash/keymgr/keymgr offline-KSK pipeline) after wide-net dup-check sweeps across the L7 proxy stack (caddy/envoy/haproxy/traefik/tinyproxy/mitmproxy/mitmdump/mitmweb/gost/sslocal/ssserver covered) and authoritative-DNS family (unbound/unbound-anchor/unbound-checkconf/unbound-control/unbound-host + pdns_control/pdns_recursor/pdns_server/rec_control + nsd/nsd-checkconf/nsd-checkzone/nsd-control + yadifad all covered).
  - `_kxdpgun` (1-stem; CZ-NIC/knot): the Knot DNS XDP-based DNS stress tester / traffic generator that drives high-throughput UDP/TCP/QUIC DNS query load against a target nameserver from a queries-file (text or binary format).  22 long+short flag pairs decoded source-direct from `src/utils/kxdpgun/main.c`:
    * `opts_str = "hV::t:Q:b:rp:T::U::F:I:i:Bl:L:R:v:e:m:G:jS:"` + `struct option opts[]` long-option table.
    * 3-entry `-m`/`--mode` XDP-mode enum (auto|copy|generic) decoded source-direct from `set_mode()`'s `strcmp(arg, "...")` chain.
    * **9-entry `-T`/`--tcp=` + `-U`/`--quic=` debug-mode enum** (0|1|2|3|5|7|8|9|R) decoded source-direct from `sending_mode()`'s `switch (ctx->sending_mode[0])` arm — each digit selects a different RFC-edge-case attack scenario (0 = force QUIC full handshake, 1 = ignore-query+ignore-fin, 2 = ignore-query, 3 = ignore-query+ignore-fin, 5 = ignore-lastbyte+ignore-fin, 7 = ignore-close+ignore-data-ack+ignore-fin, 8 = ignore-close+ignore-fin, 9 = TCP ignore-fin, R = reuse-conn + full-handshake).
    * `-T`/`-U` are `optional_argument` so they can be bare flags `-T`/`-U` to enable TCP/QUIC mode at defaults, OR take `=X` debug-mode suffix.
    * `-V`/`--version` is `optional_argument` — bare prints version, `--version=foo` prints extended version info.
    * `-I`/`--interface` gets `_net_interfaces` completion.
    * Final positional `<dest_ip>` gets `_hosts` completion (accepts IPv4 or IPv6).
  - Hunt-skip notes: `knot-tsig-keygen` does NOT exist as a separate binary in upstream CZ-NIC/knot - TSIG key generation is the `-t`/`--tsig <name> [alg]` flag on `keymgr` (already covered) per `src/utils/keymgr/main.c:print_help` body.  The BIND tool `tsig-keygen(8)` is a different tool entirely (referenced only in `doc/man_knsupdate.rst` as a note about incompatible BIND keyfile format).  `keymgr` (CZ-NIC/knot) already covered as `keymgr` standalone — `knot-keymgr` is the Debian/Ubuntu binary-name variant of the same upstream tool; covered via the existing `keymgr` completion.  `caddy-cli`/`envoy-cli`/`hapee-cli`/`kong-cli`/`kong-build`/`kresctl-cli`/`yadifa-cli` are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base - the bases caddy/envoy/kong/kresctl/yadifa already covered).  `pingora`/`pingora-cli` (Cloudflare Pingora) is a Rust library not a CLI tool.  `pproxy`/`shadowsocks-rust`/`ss-client`/`hapee-rhino-ot1`/`hapee` are package-rename variants of already-covered tools.  `tyk-gateway`/`tyk-pump`/`tyk-sync`/`tyk-identity`/`tyk-bench` (TykTechnologies/tyk family) each have rich go-flags surfaces - deferred for a separate state-machine round.  `yadifa` (CLI) and `yarmd` left for a separate round.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (k*).
  - Corpus 28,576 → 28,577 files.

- **R169 — SigmaHQ legacy-sigmatools detection-engineering CLI suite** (1 file / 5 binaries) — pivot to SIEM detection-engineering tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across YARA family (yara/yarac/yara-x already covered), Plaso family (log2timeline.py/psort.py/preg/pinfo all covered), osquery (osquery/osqueryd/osqueryctl covered), wazuh (wazuh-control/wazuh-modulesd covered), Sleuth Kit family (tsk_loaddb/tsk_recover/icat/mmls/fls/fsstat/ils/blkls/blkstat/blkcat/blkcalc/img_stat/img_cat/mactime all covered), Volatility family (volatility/volatility3/vol3/vol.py/rekall all covered), and threat-intel (vt/theHive/misp).
  - `_sigmac` (5-stem; SigmaHQ/legacy-sigmatools): sigmac (the canonical Sigma-rule-to-SIEM-query converter, 18 flags + 35-backend autocomplete enum) + sigma2attack (Sigma → MITRE ATT&CK Navigator heatmap converter, 6 flags + 5-entry status enum used twice for --status-start/--status-end) + sigma_uuid (verify/generate UUIDs across Sigma rules, 4 flags) + sigma_similarity (find similar Sigma rules across a rule corpus, 5 flags including repeatable `*-v` verbosity) + merge_sigma (merge a Sigma rule with its action rules into a single doc, 1 positional).
  - Critical extraction note: the PATH-installed `tools/<name>` scripts are 5-line stubs that `from sigma.<name> import main; main()` so the actual flag surface lives in `tools/sigma/<name>.py`.  Verified each stub matches its lib by `wc -l` + grep on the lib file.
  - sigmac --target backend autocomplete enum (35 entries) decoded source-direct from `tools/sigma/backends/*.py` directory listing (each backend module is registered via `getBackendDict()`): ala/arcsight/athena/carbonblack/chronicle/cim/csharp/datadog/devo/discovery/dnif/ee-outliers/elasticsearch/fireeye-helix/fortisiem/graylog/hawk/hedera/humio/lacework/limacharlie/logiq/logpoint/mdatp/netwitness/netwitness-epl/opensearch/powershell/qradar/qualys/splunk/splunkdm/sql/sqlite/stix.  Helper/data/exceptions/mixins/base modules filtered out since they're not user-facing backend registrations.
  - sigma2attack --status-start/--status-end share a 5-entry status-machine enum (unsupported|experimental|test|stable|deprecated).
  - Repeatable flags: `*-c/--config`, `*-O/--backend-option` for sigmac (decoded source-direct from `action="append"` argparse declarations) + `*-v/--verbose` for sigma_similarity (decoded source-direct from `action="count"` argparse declaration).
  - The hidden `--shoot-yourself-in-the-foot` argparse flag with `help=argparse.SUPPRESS` deliberately omitted from completion as upstream-suppressed-flag precedent.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `sigma` is the legacy bare-name alias for `sigmac` (already covered as `sigma`).  `sigma-cli` (SigmaHQ/sigma-cli) is the modern Sigma successor written in Python with click — already covered separately.  `psteal.py`/`image_export.py`/`preg.py` (Plaso) all use a complex multi-helper argparse pattern via `helpers_manager.ArgumentHelperManager.AddCommandLineArguments(... names=[...])` calls that pull from 8+ CLI helper modules (artifact_definitions, data_location, archives, extraction, hashers, parsers, output_modules, status_view, vss_processing, credentials) - too large for one round, deferred.  `nessus-cli`/`theHive-cli`/`misp-cli`/`misp-tool` are not actual binary names (the Nessus/TheHive/MISP CLIs are `nessuscli`/`thehive` etc., need separate verification).  `velociraptor` already covered; `velo` is not a binary name.  `wazuh` is the package name (binaries already covered).  `yarl` is a Python library not a binary.  `xfs_db`/`xfs_metadump` already in blacklist but deserve dedicated rounds.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
