- **R168 — cea-hpc Environment Modules low-level engine + env-runner** (1 file / 2 binaries) — pivot to HPC environment-modules tooling sister to the heavily-covered Slurm + PBS + flux scheduler family already in the corpus (R162 slurmctld/slurmd/slurmdbd/slurmstepd, sbatch/srun/scancel/squeue/sinfo/sacct/sshare/scontrol/sbcast/strigger/salloc/sattach, PBS qsub/qstat/qdel/qrerun/qhold/qrls/qmove/qmsg/qalter/qsig/qselect/qrun/qmgr, flux + flux-jobs).
  - `_modulecmd` (2-stem; cea-hpc/modules):
    * modulecmd: low-level execution engine the `module` shell function invokes to emit shell-specific environment-modification code.  Direct invocation is primarily for debugging + advanced usage (end users normally use the `module` shell function not this binary).  3-level state machine via `_arguments -C` + nested case $state:  `modulecmd <shell> [switches] [sub-command [sub-command-args]]`.
      - 16-entry shell-target `_describe` decoded source-direct from `doc/source/modulecmd.rst` body "The following shells are supported: sh, csh, tcsh, bash, ksh, zsh, fish, cmd, pwsh, python, perl, ruby, tcl, cmake, r and lisp."
      - 32 command-line switches decoded source-direct from `doc/source/module.rst` `.. _Command line switches:` section (`.. option::` entries: --all/-a, --auto, --color, --contains/-C, --debug/-D/-DD, --default/-d, --dumpname, --force/-f, --help/-h, --icase/-i, --ignore-cache, --ignore-user-rc, --indepth, --json/-j, --latest/-L, --long/-l, --no-auto, --no-indepth, --no-pager/-P, --no-redirect, --output/-o, --paginate/-p, --redirect, --silent/-s, --starts-with/-S, --tag, --terse/-t, --timer, --trace/-T, --verbose/-v/-vv, --version/-V, --width/-w).
      - 68 module sub-commands decoded source-direct from `.. _Module Sub-Commands:` section (`.. subcmd::` entries: add/add-any/aliases/append-path/apropos/avail/cachebuild/cacheclear/clear/config/describe/disable/display/edit/help/info-loaded + 6 init-* + 4 is-* + keyword/lint/list/load/load-any/mod-to-sh/path/paths/prepend-path/purge/refresh/reload/remove-path/reset/restore/rm + 4 save-* + search/sh-to-mod/show/source/spider + 5 stash-* + state/swap/switch/test/try-add/try-load/unload/unuse/update/use/whatis).
    * envml: one-shot env-prepared command runner.  Parses leading args as MODULE_ACTIONs until `--` or until a non-action arg is found, then exec's the command with the modified env.  5 documented action forms decoded source-direct from `doc/source/envml.rst` "MODULE_ACTION FORMAT" section: `purge`, `restore[=coll]`, `unload=mod1[&mod2...]`, `switch=mod1&mod2`, `[load=]mod1[&mod2...]`.  Multiple actions can be colon-joined in one arg.  Completion uses `_command_names -e` since after the actions/`--` the rest is an arbitrary command line.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `module` is a shell function defined by `init/zsh.in` not a binary (cannot be completed via compsys directly - it's compdef'd via init).  `ml` is a shorter alias shell function with the same property.  `lmod` (TACC/Lmod) standalone binary is mostly internally invoked by the Lmod `module` shell function; user-facing flags are documented but the subcommand dispatch comes through MainControl's s_nameTbl (load/mgrload/unload/refresh/show/access/spider/computeHash/checkSyntax/quiet) which is intentionally smaller than the full `module` sub-commands set - deferred for a future round.  `modulecmd.tcl` is a private internal helper - `modulecmd` is its public-facing wrapper.  `nx-cli`/`lerna-cli`/`turbo-cli` are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base - the bases nx/lerna/turbo already covered).  `cross-rs` is the package name for `cross` (covered).  `berry`/`pkx`/`rattler`/`fpm-package-manager`/`fpm-fortran` etc. all out-of-scope or need separate rounds.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 2 entries (1 e*, 1 m*).
  - Corpus 28,574 → 28,575 files.

- **R167 — grigio obs-cmd OBS WebSocket v5 CLI** (1 file / 1 binary) — pivot to streaming/recording remote-control tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across OBS family (obs/obs-studio/obs-cli already covered), AV1 family (ab-av1/av1an/svt-av1-enc/rav1e/aomenc/aomdec/dav1d/vpxenc/vpxdec covered), opus tools (opusenc/opusdec/opusinfo covered), and libsndfile family (sndfile-cmp/-convert/-deinterleave/-info/-interleave/-jackplay/-metadata-get/-metadata-set/-play/-resample/-salvage all covered).
  - `_obs-cmd` (1-stem; grigio/obs-cmd): OBS WebSocket v5 CLI client.  The canonical headless OBS Studio remote-control tool on Wayland Linux (where OBS's native UI scripting is constrained) and the de-facto bridge for REAPER/Keyboard-Maestro/HyperKey integrations.  22 top-level subcommands + 16 nested `#[derive(Subcommand)]` enums decoded source-direct from `src/cli.rs`.
  - 4-level state-machine completion via `_arguments -C` + nested `case $words[1]` + per-subcommand `_arguments`/`_values`/`_describe` dispatch:
    * Top level: `--websocket obsws://host:port/password` global flag + 22-entry `_describe` dispatch on first positional.
    * scene: 18-action `_arguments :scene action:(...)` covering current/switch/list/create/remove/rename + 5 transition-* actions + 4 studio-mode-* actions + 2 preview-* actions.
    * scene-collection: 4-action _values (current/list/create/switch).
    * profile: 5-action _values (current/list/create/remove/switch).
    * video-settings: get/set + 6 `--base-{width,height}`/`--output-{width,height}`/`--fps-{num,den}` flags.
    * stream-service: get/set + 3 flags (--service-type/--server/--key).
    * record-directory: 2-action _values (get/set).
    * replay: 6-action _values (start/stop/toggle/save/status/last-replay).
    * virtual-camera: 3-action _values (start/stop/toggle).
    * streaming: 4-action _values (start/stop/status/toggle).
    * recording: 9-action _values (start/stop/toggle/status/status-active/pause/resume/toggle-pause/create-chapter).
    * save-screenshot: 3 positional (source, format, file_path) + 3 optional flags (--width, --height, --compression-quality).
    * audio + filter: bare positional 2- and 3-arg forms.
    * scene-item: 16-action _values (list/create/remove/duplicate/enable/disable/toggle/lock/unlock + 4 transform get/set pairs).
    * trigger-hotkey: positional hotkey name.
    * fullscreen-projector + source-projector: --monitor-index flag.
    * media-input: 5-action _values (set-cursor/play/pause/stop/restart).
    * input: 16-action _values (list/list-kinds/create/remove/rename/settings/volume/mute + 5 audio-* + default-settings/specials).
    * completion: 5-shell autocomplete enum (bash|elvish|fish|powershell|zsh).
  - Critical extraction note: clap kebab-cases enum variant names by default (CamelCase → kebab-case), so `SceneCollection` → `scene-collection`, `VideoSettings` → `video-settings`, `RecordDirectory` → `record-directory`, `VirtualCamera` → `virtual-camera`, `SceneItem` → `scene-item`, `ListHotkeys` → `list-hotkeys`, `TriggerHotkey` → `trigger-hotkey`, `FullscreenProjector` → `fullscreen-projector`, `SourceProjector` → `source-projector`, `MediaInput` → `media-input`, `SaveScreenshot` → `save-screenshot`, `StreamService` → `stream-service`, `LastReplay` → `last-replay`, `StatusActive` → `status-active`, `TogglePause` → `toggle-pause`, `CreateChapter` → `create-chapter`, `ListKinds` → `list-kinds`, `AudioBalance` → `audio-balance`, `AudioSyncOffset` → `audio-sync-offset`, `AudioMonitorType` → `audio-monitor-type`, `AudioTracks` → `audio-tracks`, `DefaultSettings` → `default-settings`, `GetTransform`/`SetTransform`/`GetIndex`/`SetIndex`/`GetBlendMode`/`SetBlendMode` → `get-transform`/etc. All variant-rename mappings verified source-direct by walking the `pub enum X { ... }` blocks.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `jamulus` is a Qt-based audio jamming GUI - its CLI surface is documented but tied tightly to Qt UI; deferred.  `ardour-cli`/`renoise-cli`/`reaper-cli`/`sox-cli`/`lame-cli`/`ecasound-cli` are not actual binary names (suffix-confused-with-base pattern - the bases ardour/renoise/reaper/sox/lame/ecasound already covered).  `streamlink-cli`/`yt-dlp` already covered.  `sndfile-mix-create`/`sndfile-regtest`/`vorbis-tools`/`obs-studio` left as alias-confusions: sndfile-mix-create is a libsndfile rare utility (separate hunt), sndfile-regtest is internal test runner not user-facing, vorbis-tools is the package name (binaries oggenc/oggdec/ogginfo already covered), obs-studio is the package name (binary `obs` already covered).  `obs-cli` is a different older Bash-based CLI (already covered) - the new Rust obs-cmd is a distinct project.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (o*).
  - Corpus 28,573 → 28,574 files.

- **R166 — rordenlab dcm2niix DICOM→NIfTI converter** (1 file / 1 binary) — pivot to medical-imaging tooling after wide-net dup-check sweeps across DCMTK (dcmdump/dcmodify/dcmconv/dcmmkdir/dcmsend/dcmrecv/dcmqridx/dcmqrscp/dcmqrti/dcmpsmk/dcmpschk/dcmprscp/dcmpssnd/movescu/storescu/storescp/findscu/echoscu/getscu/wlmscpfs all heavily covered), BLAST+ family (blastp/blastn/tblastn/psiblast covered), HMMER (hmmsearch/hmmscan/hmmalign/hmmbuild/jackhmmer/phmmer covered), and multiple sequence alignment (mafft/muscle3/clustalw/clustalo covered).
  - `_dcm2niix` (1-stem; rordenlab/dcm2niix): the workhorse DICOM-to-NIfTI converter used throughout neuroimaging pipelines (BIDS conversion, fMRI/MRI preprocessing, AFNI/FSL/FreeSurfer/SPM ingestion).  Adds compressed `.nii.gz` output, BIDS sidecars, NRRD/MGH/JSON export, Philips precise float scaling.  41 flags total decoded source-direct from `console/main_console.cpp:showHelp(...)` body + the surrounding hand-rolled `strcmp(argv[i], "-X")` argv-walking loop in `main()`.
  - Sub-flag dispatching technique: dcm2niix uses argv[i] inner-character inspection (NOT separate short-flag arms) to distinguish related options:
    * `-b`, `-ba`, `-bi`, `-bv` decoded source-direct from inner `else if (argv[i][2] == 'a')` / `(argv[i][2] == 'i')` / `(argv[i][2] == 'v')` chain (so `-b y` is BIDS-sidecar y/n/o, `-ba y` is anonymize-BIDS, `-bi M2022` is BIDS subject ID, `-bv 1222` is BIDS subject visit).
    * `-1..-9` decoded from a numeric-range arm `(argv[i][1] >= '1') && (argv[i][1] <= '9')` that sets gz compression level.  Mapped to 9 separate completion entries `-1`/`-2`/.../`-9` instead of a parametric `-N` since each is a distinct token in compsys.
  - Enum autocompletes decoded source-direct from per-flag default-letter chain `(argv[i][0] == 'y') || (argv[i][0] == 'Y') ... (argv[i][0] == '0')` patterns:
    * `-b` BIDS sidecar (y|n|o).
    * `-e` export format (y|n|o|j|b) - NRRD, NIfTI, MGH, JSON/JNIfTI, BJNIfTI.
    * `-g` defaults file (y|n|o|i).
    * `-i` ignore derived/localizer/2D (y|n|o).
    * `-l` lossless 16-bit scaling (y|n|o).
    * `-m` merge 2D slices (n|y|0|1|2).
    * `-p` Philips precise float scaling (y|n|o).
    * `-q` directory-only search (y|n|l).
    * `-v` verbose level (n|y|0|1|2).
    * `-w` write-conflict behavior (0|1|2).
    * `-x` crop 3D acquisitions (y|n|i).
    * `-z` compress NIfTI output (y|o|s|i|n|3) - pigz, optimal pigz, zstd, internal zlib/miniz, no, no/3D.
    * `--big-endian` (y|n|o) and `--progress` (y|n).
  - `-n` flagged repeatable via `*-n` prefix decoded from the help-text note "can be used up to MAX_NUM_SERIES times".
  - Hunt-skip notes: `nifti1_tool`/`nifti_tool` (NIFTI-Imaging/nifti_clib) has ~80 flags and would deserve its own dedicated round - left for a future hunt.  `diamond` (bbuchfink/diamond) ships 40+ subcommands - too large for one round, deferred.  AFNI 3d* family (3dcalc/3dvolreg/3dANOVA/3dttest++/3dDeconvolve/3dDespike) and FSL fsl* family (fslmaths/fslstats/fslmerge/fslsplit/fslroi/fslorient/fslreorient2std) each have rich flag surfaces and live in distinct upstream projects - deferred.  FreeSurfer recon-all/mri_convert/mri_synthstrip/mri_synthseg also deferred (mostly Perl wrappers around C binaries with overlapping flag surfaces). `freeview`/`itksnap-cli`/`osirix-cli`/`mango-cli` are GUI apps without significant CLI surfaces.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 1 entry (d*).
  - Corpus 28,572 → 28,573 files.

- **R165 — llama.cpp companion examples + pocs** (1 file / 6 binaries) — pivot to the remaining ggml-org/llama.cpp companion binaries not yet covered, completing the llama-* family sister to the heavily-covered llama-cli / llama-server / llama-bench / llama-quantize / llama-perplexity / llama-batched / llama-batched-bench / llama-tokenize / llama-gguf / llama-gguf-split / llama-cvector-generator / llama-imatrix / llama-export-lora / llama-finetune / llama-eval-callback / llama-embedding / llama-lookahead / llama-lookup / llama-parallel / llama-passkey / llama-retrieval / llama-save-load-state / llama-speculative / llama-tts already in the corpus.  Vector DB sweep (chromadb/qdrant/weaviate/milvus/pinecone/vespa) and mlx sweep (mlx/mlx_lm/mlx-llm/mlx-server) all came back already-covered or not-real-binary.
  - `_llama-simple` (6-stem; ggml-org/llama.cpp):
    * llama-simple: minimal generation example with hand-rolled `strcmp(argv[i], "-X")` loop - `-m model.gguf [-n n_predict] [-ngl n_gpu_layers] [prompt]`.
    * llama-simple-chat: minimal chat example - `-m model.gguf [-c context_size] [-ngl n_gpu_layers]`.
    * llama-lookup-merge: n-gram lookup decoder helper that merges multiple `.bin` lookup parts into a single merged file.  Variadic positional: last argv is output, all prior argv are inputs.
    * llama-convert-llama2c-to-ggml: converts Karpathy llama2.c models to GGUF.  Hand-rolled long-only argparse with `_`/`-` normalization (the parser does `std::replace(arg.begin(), arg.end(), '_', '-')` so `--llama2c_model` and `--llama2c-model` both work).  `--llama2c-model` is REQUIRED.
    * llama-vdot: bare 3-positional-int dot-product micro-benchmark from `pocs/vdot/vdot.cpp` (nloop scalar useQ4_1).
    * llama-q8dot: bare 2-positional-int Q8 dot-product micro-benchmark from `pocs/vdot/q8dot.cpp` (nloop type).
  - Binary-target mapping decoded source-direct from each directory's `CMakeLists.txt` `set(TARGET llama-<name>)` line - critical because the source file basename and binary name don't always match (e.g. `vdot.cpp` → `llama-vdot`, `simple-chat.cpp` → `llama-simple-chat`).
  - Hunt-skip notes: `llama-infill` was removed upstream — replaced by the unified `--in-prefix`/`--in-suffix` flags on `llama-cli` (covered).  `llama-lookup-create` + `llama-lookup-stats` both use the full `common_params_parse(..., LLAMA_EXAMPLE_LOOKUP)` shared CLI surface, which is essentially the entire llama-cli flag set — would be near-duplicates of the llama-cli completion already in the corpus.  `llama-train-text-from-scratch` was removed upstream (replaced by `llama-finetune`).  `lm-studio`/`lmstudio` are GUI apps with no stable CLI surface.  `chroma-cli`/`pinecone-cli`/`vespa-cli` already covered.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 6 entries (all l*).
  - Corpus 28,571 → 28,572 files.

- **R164 — Verilator companion-tool suite** (1 file / 6 binaries) — pivot to the verilator/verilator `bin/` companions shipped alongside the already-covered `verilator` Perl driver, completing the verilator family in tandem with R163's verible siblings.  Six binaries follow-up the upstream open question noted in R163's hunt-skip list (verilator_coverage/verilator_gantt/verilator_includer/verilator_profcfunc each have distinct flag surfaces).
  - `_verilator_coverage` (6-stem; verilator/verilator):
    * verilator_coverage: merges per-test coverage databases, ranks them by goodness, and annotates source with hit-counts.  14 `DECL_OPTION(...)` declarations decoded source-direct from `src/VlcMain.cpp` since the `bin/verilator_coverage` Perl wrapper just passes flags through (`pass_through` + `"<>"=>sub{}`) to the underlying `verilator_coverage_bin_dbg` C++ binary.  Critical extraction note: verilator parses BOTH `-flag` AND `--flag` for every switch (the parser is `argv++` based not getopt-based), so each flag entry is registered twice in the completion under both prefixes via paired mutex groups `(--flag)-flag[...]` / `(-flag)--flag[...]` so the user gets per-prefix exclusion + completion of both.
    * verilator_gantt: 3 argparse flags + positional `profile_exec.dat` input.
    * verilator_includer: NO flags - each argv is either `-D<name>=<value>` macro or a header path.  Pattern decoded source-direct from `re_arg_d = re.compile(r"^-D([^=]+)=(.*)")` argv-walking loop; if matched, emits `#define name value`, else `#include "arg"`.
    * verilator_profcfunc: 1 argparse flag (--debug) + positional gprof-output input.
    * verilator_ccache_report: 1 required `-o OUTFILE` flag + positional log-directory.
    * verilator_difftree: 2 argparse flags (--debug, --no-lineno) + 2 positional file/dir args; exit status 0/1/2.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `bin/redirect` is an internal helper script that re-execs verilator (not user-callable). `bin/verilator` itself already covered.  Pure positional binaries with no flag surface (like verilator_includer) still benefit from `*:...:_files -g "*.(h|hpp|hh|hxx)"` completion since they accept many filenames.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 6 entries (all v*).
  - Corpus 28,570 → 28,571 files.

- **R163 — Verible Verilog/SystemVerilog sibling-binary suite** (1 file / 5 binaries) — pivot to additional Verible tools sister to the already-covered verible-verilog-format + verible-verilog-syntax binaries, after wide-net dup-check sweeps across FPGA/EDA family (yosys + 4 yosys-* siblings, nextpnr-ice40/-ecp5/-nexus/-gowin/-machxo2/-himbaechel, icestorm utility set icepack/icebram/icetime/iceprog, openocd, sigrok-cli, verilator).
  - `_verible-verilog-lint` (5-stem; chipsalliance/verible): verible-verilog-lint (the Verilog/SystemVerilog linter) + verible-verilog-diff (format-equivalence vs obfuscate-equivalence diff) + verible-verilog-project (project-wide symbol-table + file-deps tool) + verible-verilog-obfuscate (identifier obfuscation/de-obfuscation with translation map) + verible-verilog-ls (LSP server).  All ABSL_FLAG(type, name, default, doc) declarations parsed source-direct via a custom balanced-paren scanner that handles the multi-line ABSL_FLAG syntax and nested template types like `std::vector<std::string>`.
  - verible-verilog-lint: 9 ABSL_FLAGs decoded + 6-entry `--autofix` autocomplete enum (no|patch-interactive|patch|inplace-interactive|inplace|generate-waiver) decoded source-direct from the `EnumNameMap<AutofixMode> kAutofixModeEnumStringMap({...})` table.
  - verible-verilog-diff: 1 ABSL_FLAG (`--mode`) with 2-entry `DiffMode` enum (format|obfuscate) decoded source-direct from `kDiffModeStringMap`.
  - verible-verilog-project: 3 ABSL_FLAGs + 3 subcommands (`symbol-table-defs`, `symbol-table-refs`, `file-deps`) decoded source-direct from `static const std::pair<std::string_view, SubcommandEntry> kCommands[]` table; subcommand state-machine via `_describe` with nested SV-file completion in the args state.
  - verible-verilog-obfuscate: 5 ABSL_FLAGs decoded — `--load_map`/`--save_map` form a save→reload roundtrip pair; `--decode` reverse-applies the translation map and requires `--load_map`; `--preserve_interface` + `--preserve_builtin_functions` form an identity-mapping pair.
  - verible-verilog-ls: 1 ABSL_FLAG (`--push_diagnostic_notifications`) — LSP server is mostly stdin/stdout-driven, all behavior controlled via LSP messages not CLI flags.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `verilator_bin` is the underlying C++ executable wrapped by the `verilator` Perl driver (already covered) — the driver delegates all flags to verilator_bin so they share a flag surface (wrapper-binary-of-covered-base precedent).  `verilator_coverage`/`verilator_gantt`/`verilator_includer`/`verilator_profcfunc` left for a separate round since each has distinct flag surfaces.  `nextpnr-mistral` Cyclone V target was deprecated upstream — `--mistral` is now a per-flag mode on the unified nextpnr binary, not a sibling executable.  `cocotb-extension` is a build helper, not a user-callable binary.  `icemulti` + `icebox_asc2hlc` + `icebox-stat` left for a future round — icestorm sibling extraction would need separate research per upstream's hand-rolled argparse.  `pulseview` is GTK GUI without significant CLI surface beyond `--input-file`/`--input-format`/`--driver` (3-flag thin wrapper, deferred).  `svls` (dalance/svls) is an alternative SV LSP server (independent project from verible-verilog-ls).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 5 entries (all v*).
  - Corpus 28,569 → 28,570 files.

- **R162 — Slurm DB + step daemons + Flux jobs CLI** (3 files / 3 binaries) — pivot to HPC scheduler daemons + flux-framework user-facing tooling sister to the already-covered Slurm srun/sbatch/scancel/squeue/sinfo/sacct/sshare/scontrol/sbcast/strigger/salloc/sattach + PBS qsub/qstat/qdel family + flux umbrella driver, after wide-net dup-check sweeps across slurm-utils (sview/saconf), salt-stack (salt + 8 salt-* sibling binaries all covered), Tinker / LAMMPS / GROMACS / Quantum ESPRESSO (covered), and flux subcommand stack.
  - `_slurmdbd` (1-stem; SchedMD/slurm): Slurm Database Daemon brokering accounting RPCs between slurmctld and a SQL backend (MySQL/MariaDB).  Sister daemon to slurmctld + slurmd (both covered).  Getopt argstring `"Dhn:R::suvV"` + case-arm switch decoded source-direct from `src/slurmdbd/slurmdbd.c:_usage` body — 8 flags including `-D` foreground, `-n` nice, `-R` deprecated 23.11+ lft/rgt re-roll, `-s` chdir-to-logdir, `-u` DB-version-only mode, `*-v` repeatable verbosity, `-V` version.
  - `_slurmstepd` (1-stem; SchedMD/slurm): per-job-step daemon spawned by slurmd to manage a single step's namespaces, rlimits, SPANK plugin loading, and OOM management on a node.  Unusual extraction: slurmstepd is invoked internally by slurmd over a socket with a special-mode positional argv pattern (NOT a conventional getopt parse).  4 user-callable modes decoded source-direct from `src/slurmd/slurmstepd/slurmstepd.c:_process_cmdline` body via `xstrcmp(argv[1], "<mode>")` arms: `getenv` (print rlimits + environ), `infinity` (poll loop with STEPD_OOM_ADJ -1000), `ns_infinity <name>` (namespace-mode infinity), `spank <mode>` (SPANK plugin handler).  Wrapped in `_describe` state-machine so each mode gets nested arg completion.
  - `_flux-jobs` (1-stem; flux-framework/flux-core): per-user job listing/filtering CLI for the flux scheduler — sister to the already-covered `flux` umbrella driver.  `flux-*` family ships as standalone executables in PATH so `flux jobs` and `flux-jobs` are both valid.  25 argparse `parser.add_argument(...)` flags decoded source-direct from `src/cmd/flux-jobs.py` including 7-entry `-f`/`--filter` autocomplete enum (pending|running|inactive|completed|failed|canceled|timeout), `-u`/`--user` with `_users` completion, `-R`/`--recursive` + `-L`/`--level` cooperating recursion-depth pair, `--color` enum (always|never|auto), `-w`/`--width` optional-argument form (omitted defaults to terminal width).
  - Hunt-skip notes: `flux-resource` has 9 subcommands (acquire-mute/drain/undrain/status/list/info/reload/R/eventlog) — left for a separate state-machine round.  `salt-*` family (salt/salt-call/salt-cp/salt-key/salt-master/salt-minion/salt-run/salt-ssh/salt-syndic/salt-cloud) all blacklist-only — not in more_src or src; pending separate completion round.  `cromwell-cli`/`snakemake-cli`/`nextflow-cli`/`toil-cli` are not actual binary names — Cromwell/Snakemake/Nextflow/Toil expose their CLIs as bare `cromwell`/`snakemake`/`nextflow`/`toil` (suffix-confused-with-base precedent).  `gmx_mpi`/`mdrun-mpi` are MPI build variants of `gmx`/`mdrun` (architecture-variant-of-covered-base).
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 3 entries (1 f*, 2 s*).
  - Corpus 28,566 → 28,569 files.

- **R161 — Mapbox tippecanoe sibling-binary suite** (1 file / 4 binaries) — pivot to vector-tile post-processing tools shipped alongside the already-covered `tippecanoe` main binary, after wide-net dup-check sweeps across GDAL family (gdaltransform/gdalwarp/gdaldem/ogrinfo/gdal_rasterize/gdal_translate/gdal_polygonize/gdal_grid/gdal_contour/gdaladdo/gdalbuildvrt all heavily covered), OSM family (osmconvert/osmupdate/osmosis/osm2pgsql/osmium-tool all covered), and DB/SQL clients/modern shells.
  - `_tippecanoe-decode` (4-stem; felt/tippecanoe): tippecanoe-decode (mbtiles/pmtiles → GeoJSON dumper with -s/--projection EPSG:3857/EPSG:4326 enum) + tile-join (merge + reproject + CSV-join multiple tilesets with 38 flags including the -o/--output xor -e/--output-to-directory mutex) + tippecanoe-overzoom (single-tile overzoom + filter with 16 flags including 6 control-char-encoded long-only flags --exclude-prefix/--preserve-input-order/--unidecode-data/--tiny-polygon-size/--no-tile-compression/--deduplicate-by-id) + tippecanoe-json-tool (GeoJSON pre-processor: --wrap into FeatureCollection + --extract attribute splitter + --csv join).  All 4 surfaces decoded source-direct from per-binary `struct option long_options[]` tables + the dynamic getopt-string builder loop (each binary walks long_options[] and pushes val (+":" if required_argument) into the getopt short-string at runtime).  Per-binary source mapping decoded from Makefile target blocks (e.g. `tippecanoe-decode: decode.o ...`).  Repeatable flags `*-l/--layer`, `*-x/--exclude-metadata-row`, `*-y/--include`, `*-c/--csv`, `*-x/--exclude`, `*-E/--accumulate-attribute`, `*-p/--prevent` identified from per-binary case-arm `.insert(optarg)` / `.push_back(optarg)` calls on `std::set` / `std::vector` containers.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `gdal2tiles` is a Python wrapper sitting on top of `gdal_rasterize` (already covered) — wrapper-not-primary precedent; skipped.  `osm2pgrouting` build is gated behind a libosmium toggle and the upstream main repo branch is unstable — left for a future round once the upstream stabilizes.  `osmconvert64` is an alias for `osmconvert` (covered).  `mapbox-cli`/`h3cli`/`valhalla_*` candidates either deprecated, mostly-library, or unrelated to a single CLI surface — pending separate hunt.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 4 entries (1 t* triplet + 1 standalone).
  - Corpus 28,565 → 28,566 files.

- **R160 — Go fuzzer pair + retargetable-decompiler suite** (2 files / 6 binaries) — pivot to RE/fuzzing tooling adjacent to the heavily-covered family (radare2/r2pm/rasm2/rabin2/rax2/rahash2/ragg2/radiff2/ghidra/binwalk/yara/yarac/angr/honggfuzz) after wide-net dup-check sweeps across DB clients (mycli/litecli/pgcli/mssql-cli/iredis/usql/harlequin/gobang/sqls), modern shells (xonsh/elvish/hilbish), and afl-* tools (all heavily covered).
  - `_go-fuzz` (2-stem; dvyukov/go-fuzz): `go-fuzz-build` instruments + builds the fuzz-target binary, then `go-fuzz` orchestrates the fuzzing loop (coordinator/worker) against that binary.  16 Go `flag.<Type>(name, default, usage)` declarations decoded source-direct from `go-fuzz/main.go` (`-workdir`/`-procs`/`-timeout`/`-minimize`/`-coordinator`/`-worker`/`-connectiontimeout`/`-bin`/`-func`/`-dumpcover`/`-dup`/`-testoutput`/`-covercounters`/`-sonar`/`-v`/`-http`).  10 flags from `go-fuzz-build/main.go` (`-tags`/`-o`/`-func`/`-work`/`-race`/`-cpuprofile`/`-libfuzzer`/`-x`/`-preserve`/`-go`).  `-covercounters` + `-sonar` get explicit `(true false)` autocomplete enums since they default to true and toggling them off needs `=false`.
  - `_retdec-decompiler` (4-stem; avast/retdec): retdec-decompiler (C++ main binary) + retdec-fileinfo + retdec-unpacker + retdec-archive-decompiler (3 Python wrappers).  retdec-decompiler is hand-rolled argv-walking via `isParam(i, "-x", "--long")` pairs decoded source-direct from `src/retdec-decompiler/retdec-decompiler.cpp` — 35 flag pairs total including 13 backend-tuning long-only flags.  4 enum-autocomplete decoded from if/throw chains in the parser: `-m`/`--mode` (bin|raw), `-b`/`--bit-size` (16|32|64), `-a`/`--arch` (mips|pic32|arm|thumb|arm64|powerpc|x86|x86-64), `-e`/`--endian` (little|big), `-f`/`--output-format` (plain|json|json-human).  retdec-fileinfo/-unpacker/-archive-decompiler args extracted from each `scripts/retdec-*.py` argparse `add_argument(...)` blocks.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `ngs` (ngs-lang/ngs) had no flag surface — `int main(int argc, char **argv)` passes argc/argv directly to `vm_init(&vm, argc, argv)` with no getopt; bootstrap file via `NGS_BOOTSTRAP` env var.  `closh`/`scsh`/`bonsh` either covered already or not standalone CLI tools.  `retdec-utils.py` is a shared helper module (library-not-binary precedent).  `retdec-macho-extractor` skipped — internal helper invoked from retdec-archive-decompiler.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 6 entries (2 g*, 4 r*).
  - Corpus 28,563 → 28,565 files.

- **R159 — interactive syscall viewer + transparent proxy orchestrator** (2 files / 2 binaries) — pivot to live-system tooling adjacent to the heavily-covered tracing family (bpftrace/bpftool/sysdig/falco/falcoctl/tetragon/hubble/tracee) after wide-net dup-check sweeps across code-analysis (semgrep/osv-scanner/kics/trufflehog/gitleaks/detect-secrets/ggshield/noseyparker/pmd/checkstyle/spotbugs/cppcheck/slither/solhint), secrets (whispers/credential-digger/leaktk all blacklisted or out-of-scope), and eBPF/network (bpftool/bpftrace/cilium/tetragon/falco/sysdig/bettercap/p0f/arpwatch heavily covered).
  - `_csysdig` (1-stem; draios/sysdig): ncurses-based interactive system inspection viewer — the curses sister to the `sysdig` CLI already covered.  14 short+long flag pairs decoded source-direct from `userspace/sysdig/csysdig.cpp` `long_options[]` table + getopt_long argstring `"AB::d:Ehk:K:jlm:n:p:Rr:s:v:X"` by reading every case-arm in the switch(op) block.  Optional-argument flags `-B--bpf` + `-l--list` (zero or one optional probe-path / source-filter argument) decoded source-direct from `optional_argument` markers in `long_options[]`.  10 long-only flags decoded from entries marked `0, 0` in the val column (`--cpus-for-each-buffer`, `--from`, `--interactive`, `--large-environment`, `--list-views`, `--modern-bpf`, `--page-faults`, `--raw`, `--logfile`, `--force-term-compat`, `--sortingcol`, `--to`, `--version`).
  - `_ss-tproxy` (1-stem; zfl9/ss-tproxy): bash transparent-proxy orchestrator that wires shadowsocks/v2ray/xray through iptables TPROXY + REDIRECT rules with chinadns/gfwlist/chnroute mode dispatch.  Sister to v2ray/xray/shadowsocks-libev already in the corpus.  16-entry subcommand state-machine via `_describe` decoded source-direct from the `case "${arg_list[0]}"` dispatch in `main()`.  Shell-glob action prefixes (`r*` for `restart`, `stat*` for `status`, `show*` for `show-iptables`, `v*` for `version`, `h*` for `help`) decoded back to their long forms by reading the function bodies invoked from each arm.  3 short flags (-x debug, -d basedir, -c config) extracted from the leading argv-walking `for ((i=0; ...))` loop's case arms.  `set-proxy-group` / `set-dns-group` get nested `_command_names -e` completion for the command-to-cgroup-assign argument.
  - Hunt-skip notes: `csysdig` had 30 long-options vs only `"AB::d:Ehk:K:jlm:n:p:Rr:s:v:X"` in the short-string — long-only entries identified by their `0, 0` val column.  `ss-tproxy` is bash, not getopt — argument parsing is hand-rolled in a `for ((i=0; i<${#argv[@]}; ++i))` loop; this matches the same hand-rolled-strcmp pattern noted in earlier rounds for argv-walking C tools.
  - Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`.
  - Blacklist additions: 2 entries (1 c*, 1 s*).
  - Corpus 28,561 → 28,563 files.


- **More exotic stragglers** — `nph` (Nim source formatter), `vab` (V Android Builder), `leo` (Aleo/Provable zero-knowledge language CLI), `cellranger` (10x Genomics single-cell sequencing pipeline) — flags from upstream README/usage docs / clap derive sources / 10x Cloud command-line argument reference
- **WebAssembly compiler toolchain** (Binaryen + WABT) — `wasm-as`, `wasm-dis`, `wasm-merge`, `wasm-shell`, `wasm-split`, `wasm-reduce`, `wasm-metadce`, `wasm-ctor-eval`, `wasm-fuzz-types`, `wasm-emscripten-finalize` (Binaryen, from `src/tools/*.cpp` + shared `ToolOptions` + 21-feature `feature.def`) and `wat-desugar` (WABT, from `src/tools/wat-desugar.cc`)
- **Bioinformatics** — `mash` (genome distance, marbl/Mash), `kraken2` + `kraken2-build` (taxonomic classification, DerrickWood), `centrifuge` + `centrifuge-build` + `centrifuge-inspect` (DaehwanKimLab), `trim_galore` (Rust rewrite — FelixKrueger), `macs2` (ChIP-seq peak caller — macs3-project/MACS, 138 flags across 12 subcommands), `deeptools` (NGS viz suite launcher) — verified against `GetOptions`, clap `cli.rs`, and argparse `add_argument` blocks
- **ML/AI tooling** — `llamafile` (Mozilla-Ocho, forwards llama.cpp common args), `vllm` (vllm-project, `entrypoints/cli/`), `wandb` (Click-based CLI), `mlflow` (Click-based CLI), `clearml` (multi-binary dispatch covering `clearml`, `clearml-session`, `clearml-serving`)
- **Astronomy, robotics, and misc** — `topcat` (Starlink/Bristol tabular data analyzer, SUN/253), `stilts` (TOPCAT's command-line companion, 50 tasks from SUN/256), `webots` (Cyberbotics robot simulator), `gazebo` + `gzserver` + `gzclient` (Gazebo Classic — new `gz` Ignition CLI deliberately not included; different architecture), `wllvm` + `wllvm++` + `extract-bc` (whole-program LLVM, travitch), `sox-ng` (modern SoX fork using `_ng` suffixed binaries), `pwn` (pwntools CTF/exploit-dev CLI, 20 subcommands from `pwnlib/commandline/main.py`)
- **Web3 / Blockchain** — `forc` (Sway/Fuel language, with plugin passthroughs to forc-fmt/forc-doc/forc-debug/forc-lsp/forc-publish/forc-tx), `btcwallet` (btcsuite Bitcoin wallet from `config.go` go-flags struct), `bcoin` + `bcoin-cli` + `bcoin-wallet` + `bwallet` + `bwallet-cli` (bcoin-org Bitcoin node + CLI), `movefmt` (Move language formatter, getopts-based), `move-prover` + `mvp` (Move formal prover, clap-derive from aptos-core)
- **TeX/LaTeX niche** — `latex2html` (113 Getopt::Long options), `mk4ht` (TeX4ht macro driver — 86 `<variant><dialect>` subcommands), `ltx2x` (LaTeX-to-XML/RTF/text), `latex-make` (CTAN make targets). `bibtexextract` deliberately skipped — no canonical tool exists by that name in CTAN or GitHub.
- **APL / Lisp / Smalltalk REPLs** — `gnu-apl` + `apl` (45 verified options from `UserPreferences.cc`), `klong` (kg.c usage), `kona` + `k` (kc.c args getopt), `ecl` (Embeddable Common-Lisp 30 options from cmdline.lsp), `pharo` (Pharo VM parameters from `parameters.c` + Smalltalk option handler)
- **Audio / EDA / Modern JS / Cargo** — `sonic-pi` + `sonic-pi-cli` (Widdershin/sonic-pi-cli `stop` subcommand only), `librepcb` + `librepcb-cli` (24 export options across open-project/library/symbol/package/step), `tjs` (txiki.js JS runtime: run/eval/serve/test/bundle/compile/app), `llrt` (AWS Low-Latency Runtime: compile/test), `cargo-dylint` (trailofbits Dylint clap structs), `cargo-features` (ToBinio cargo-features-manager `prune` subcommand)
- **Software-Defined Radio** — `rtl_433` (merbanan/rtl_433 — 38 options from src/rtl_433.c usage()), `hackrf-info` + `hackrf_info` (greatscottgadgets/hackrf — takes no flags upstream), `hackrf_sweep` (16 options + FFTW wisdom), `hackrf_clock` (HackRF Pro CLKIN/CLKOUT signal selection), `urh` + `urh_cli` (jopohl/urh Universal Radio Hacker — 37 cli flags for RX/TX/modulation/encoding), `sdrangel` + `sdrangelsrv` + `sdrangelbench` (f4exb/sdrangel MainParser flags), `sdrpp` (AlexandreRouma/SDRPlusPlus core/src/command_args.cpp defineAll())
- **Esoteric languages + live coding + niche cloud** — `lci` (justinmeza LOLCODE interpreter; main.c longopt table), `awib` (matslina Brainfuck compiler — no CLI flags, target via `@TARGET` directive in source), `foxdot` + `FoxDot` (Qirky/FoxDot Python live coding env for SuperCollider), `chicken` (Chicken Scheme compiler — 70+ flags from upstream wiki "Using the compiler" manual), `lakefs` (treeverse/lakeFS Git-for-data server — cobra subcommands run/setup/migrate/superuser/lua/gc/kv/flare, with nested migrate {version,up,goto,task} and kv {get,scan,dump,load})
- **Model Context Protocol server family** — All 7 active reference servers from `modelcontextprotocol/servers` (`mcp-server-everything` with stdio/sse/streamableHttp transports, `mcp-server-fetch` with `--user-agent`/`--ignore-robots-txt`/`--proxy-url`, `mcp-server-filesystem` with variadic allowed-directory paths, `mcp-server-git` with click `-r/--repository`/`-v/--verbose`, `mcp-server-memory`, `mcp-server-sequential-thinking`, `mcp-server-time` with `--local-timezone`) plus 11 archived servers from `modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived` still installable via `npx` (`mcp-server-{aws-kb-retrieval,brave-search,everart,gdrive,github,gitlab,google-maps,postgres,puppeteer,redis,slack}` — all are env-driven; only postgres/redis take a positional connection URL, only gdrive takes an optional `auth` token)
- **OCR + Modern AI/ML platforms** — `surya` family (`surya_ocr`, `surya_layout`, `surya_detect`, `surya_table`, `surya_latex_ocr`, `surya_gui`, `texify_gui` from datalab-to/surya), `marker` family (`marker`, `marker_single`, `marker_chunk_convert`, `marker_server`, `marker_gui`, `marker_extract` from datalab-to/marker), `nougat` + `nougat_api` (Meta Nougat scientific PDF OCR), `modal-client` (Modal serverless platform — 21 top-level + 53 nested subcommands), `marimo-cli` (Marimo reactive notebooks — 13 top-level subcommands), `cog-cli` (Replicate Cog ML packaging — 12 top-level subcommands), `e2b-cli` (E2B sandbox developer CLI — `auth`/`template`/`sandbox` groups with nested commands)
- **Linux / Bluetooth / AppImage / OIDC** — `appimage` + `*.AppImage` (meta-completion for AppImage runtime args from type2-runtime `runtime.c`), `appimaged` (probonopd/go-appimage daemon — `wrap`/`update`/`run`/`start` subcommands), `gattool` + `gatttool` (BlueZ legacy BLE attribute-protocol tool from bluez/attrib/gatttool.c), `iwspy` (wireless-tools spy list — positional-only grammar), `oidc-login` + `kubectl-oidc_login` (int128/kubelogin OIDC kubectl plugin — 23 flags across get-token/setup/clean)
- **ARM Cortex embedded toolchain** (`architecture_src/`) — all 20 `arm-none-eabi-*` cross-compiler + binutils binaries (`c++`, `g++`, `gcc`, `gcc-ar`, `gcc-nm`, `gcc-ranlib`, `addr2line`, `ar`, `as`, `c++filt`, `cpp`, `elfedit`, `ld`, `nm`, `objcopy`, `objdump`, `ranlib`, `readelf`, `size`, `strip`), mirrored from the existing `aarch64-linux-gnu-*` template family. `arm-none-eabi-gdb` was already covered by `more_src/_gdb-multiarch`'s multi-stem compdef.
- **VR/AR + IoT** — `monado-service` + `monado-cli` (Monado OpenXR runtime, test/probe subcommands; gitlab.freedesktop.org/monado), `openxr-explorer` + `openxrexplorer` (maluoi/openxr-explorer), `wivrn-server` + `wivrn-dashboard` (WiVRn streaming server — CLI11-based 9 flags), `mqtt-cli` + `mqtt` (HiveMQ MQTT CLI — 8 top-level subcommands + ~90 per-subcommand options from MqttCLICommand.java), `hass-cli` upgraded (Home Assistant CLI — full state-machine across 16 plugins)
- **Modern dev tools** — `mpy-cross` (MicroPython cross-compiler from mpy-cross/main.c), `cling` + `cling-jupyter` (C++ interactive interpreter — ClingOptions.td + clang passthroughs), `protoc-gen-ts` (TypeScript protoc plugin — stdin/stdout, no CLI; documented real OPTIONS keys in comment), `granted-cli` (common-fate Granted — urfave/cli 13 top-level subcommands), `aws-sso-cli` (synfinatic/aws-sso-cli — Kong-based 14 top-level subcommands)
- **3D printing + GIS** — `prusaslicer` + `prusa-slicer` (PrusaSlicer 42 CLI actions/transforms from PrintConfig.cpp; upgrade — old file was config-options-level), `cura-engine` + `CuraEngine` (Ultimaker CuraEngine slice/connect/help subcommands), `octoprint` (Click-based 11 top-level subcommands incl. serve/daemon/safemode/systeminfo/analysis/client/config/dev/plugins/timelapse/user), `qgis_process` (QGIS headless processing from qgsprocess.cpp), `grass8` + `grass` (GRASS GIS 8.x — `--tmp-project`/`--tmp-location`/`--tmp-mapset`/`--timeout`/`--config`), `grass7` (GRASS 7.x — `-wxpython`/`-wx`/`--wxpython`/`--wx`)
- **F# / .NET + Robotics** — `fake-cli` (FAKE F# Make build automation — Cli.fs docopt block), `paket-bootstrapper` + `paket.bootstrapper` (Paket bootstrapper from BootstrapperHelper.cs HelpText), `fsdocs` (F# docs generator — 4 verbs build/watch/init/convert, 37 flags across Option attributes), `fsx` + `fsxc` (F# script runner from nblockchain/fsx Fsxc.fs ParseArgsInternal), `rqt` (ROS Qt GUI framework — qt_gui_core/qt_gui/main.py argparse, 22 flags)
- **Cloud niche + Audio + Nix** — `upcloud-cli` + `upctl` (UpCloudLtd 21 top-level cobra subcommands + 104 nested), `exoscale-cli` (exoscale/cli 14 top-level subcommands, `_exo` already covers the actual binary), `fluidsynth` (FluidSynth real-time SoundFont synthesizer — 29 flags from src/fluidsynth.c long_options), `nix-direnv` + `nix-direnv-reload` (direnv stdlib extension — minimal CLI: only the generated `nix-direnv-reload` shim with no arguments). `hcloud-cli` deliberately skipped (Debian package name; binary is `hcloud`, already covered).
- **AI image/video upscaling (NCNN+Vulkan)** — `realsr-ncnn-vulkan` (Real-ESRGAN/Real-SR), `waifu2x-ncnn-vulkan`, `video2x` (k4yt3x — 42 flat flags via boost::program_options, not subcommands as spec assumed), `anime4kcpp` + `ac_cli` (Anime4KCPP — CLI rewrote to CLI11, has video subcommand)
- **Streaming + speech + media indexer** — `jacktrip` (Stanford CCRMA network audio — 59 flags from Settings.cpp longopts), `livekit-cli` + `lk` (LiveKit Cloud — 15 top-level cobra subcommands, ~50 nested), `livekit-egress` (LiveKit egress service), `rhvoice-test` + `rhvoice-service` + `RHVoice-test` + `RHVoice-service` (RHVoice speech synthesis), `moonlight-qt` + `Moonlight` (Moonlight Qt game-streaming client — 4 subcommands stream/list/pair/quit), `moonlight-embedded` + `moonlight` (Moonlight embedded — single-dash long options, 7 positional actions), `prowlarr` + `Prowlarr` (indexer aggregator — both `-flag` and `/flag` forms), `pavucontrol-qt` (Qt pavucontrol — QCommandLineOption block)
- **Hardware control + boot managers + Wi-Fi forensics + Wayland terminal IPC** — `ratbagctl` (libratbag gaming-mouse config — 8 top-level + nested actions), `poop` (Andrew Kelley's Performance Of Our Programs benchmark — 4 flags from src/main.zig), `zfsbootmenu` + `generate-zbm` (ZBM Boot Menu — 16 flags from bin/generate-zbm Perl Getopt::Long), `refind-install` + 4 sibling refind-* binaries (rEFInd EFI boot manager — refind-install/mkrlconf/mkdefault/mvrefind/mkfont per-binary dispatch), `hcxdumptool` + 8 sibling `hcx*tools` (Wi-Fi pcap/PMKID/handshake tools from ZerBea/hcxtools — `hcxpcapngtool` 31 flags / `hcxhashtool` 50 flags / etc.), `footclient` (foot Wayland terminal IPC client — 19 flags; agent fixed spec which incorrectly listed `foot-msg`)
- **Crypto wallet + PGP + QUIC stacks + game engine** — `trezorctl` (Trezor click-based CLI — 22 top-level subcommands + 10 globals), `sequoia-keystore` (Sequoia-PGP keystore daemon — clap derive, 7 flags), `ngtcp2-client` + `ngtcp2-server` (ngtcp2 reference QUIC implementation — 56/44 flags), `quicinterop` + `quicattack` (Microsoft msquic test tools — 12/8 flags), `quiche-client` + `quiche-server` (Cloudflare quiche QUIC — 39/30 flags from apps/src/args.rs ClientArgs/ServerArgs), `defold` (Defold game engine launcher — 67 flags + 5 subcommands build/bundle/clean/distclean/resolve from Bob.java; agent corrected spec which listed non-existent compile/run/update subcommands)
- **Bioinformatics + Astronomy (deep)** — `freebayes` (Bayesian variant caller — 79 long flags from src/Parameters.cpp long_options[]), `igv` (Integrative Genomics Viewer Java launcher), `sextractor` + `sex` + `source-extractor` (74 PARAM overrides from src/preflist.h), `scamp` (98 PARAM overrides), `swarp` + `SWarp` (64 PARAM overrides), `solve-field` (Astrometry.net solver wrapper — 92 long flags from solver/solve-field.c + augment-xylist.c), `astrometry-engine` (the actual Astrometry.net engine — 13 flags from solver/engine-main.c; agent corrected spec flags `-i/--input`/`-d/--inputs-dir`/`-s/--single`/`-r/--ranges` which don't exist upstream — real names are `--inputs-from`/`--base-dir`/`--solved`)
- **Hare language + OpenFOAM + DB + DNS** — `harec` (Hare compiler from sr.ht ~sircmpwn/harec — getopt string `"a:D:hM:m:N:o:Tt:v"`), `haredoc` (Hare doc extractor from ~sircmpwn/hare), `openfoam` + `openfoam-docker` + `openfoam-docker-arm` (OpenFOAM container wrapper — 26 flags), `pimpleFoam` (foamRun PIMPLE solver — argList common opts + `-solver`), `foamCleanCase` (OpenFOAM case-cleanup — 2 flags from bin/foamCleanCase usage()), `foamListTimes` (14 flags from foamListTimes.C addBoolOption + timeSelector::addOptions), `mssql-conf` (Microsoft SQL Server config — 8 subcommands setup/set/unset/traceflag/set-collation/set-edition/setup-ad-keytab/validate-ad-config + 36 settings), `aerospike-cli` (reugn/aerospike-cli SQL JDBC CLI — closest real binary by that exact name), `knot-dig` (Knot DNS dig alias — 139 entries: 16 `-` opts + ~123 `+[no]…` plus-options from kdig_params.c)
- **NCBI BLAST+ database utilities** — `blastdbcmd` (32 flags from c++/src/app/blastdb/blastdbcmd.cpp), `update_blastdb` + `update_blastdb.pl` (14 flags from update_blastdb.pl Getopt::Long table), `blastdb_aliastool` (24 flags incl. real `-oid_masks`/`-seqid_*` block), `blastdbcheck` (17 flags; spec's `-isam`/`-no_legacy`/`-binary_size` don't exist). Agent downloaded the NCBI BLAST+ 2.17.0 source tarball for verification.
- **HMMER 3.x utilities** — `hmmstat` (only `-h` upstream — spec's `--cpu`/`--mpi` aren't in ESL_OPTIONS), `hmmfetch` (5 flags incl. real `-O`), `hmmsim` (39 flags — full ESL_OPTIONS including `--mpi`/`--Eft`-family), `hmmemit` (15 flags incl. real `-C`/`-L`/`--local`/`--unilocal`/`--glocal`/`--uniglocal`/`--minl`/`--minu`), `hmmpgmd` (11 flags — spec's `--cs`/`--ws` don't exist, real is `--ccncts`/`--wcncts`), `hmmer` (no actual upstream binary — file written as documented placeholder for user-defined wrappers).
- **Voice / speech recognition + Arch build tools** — `whisper-cli` (whisper.cpp 66 flags from examples/cli/cli.cpp; legacy `main` upstream-deprecated, prints redirect warning to whisper-cli), `wyoming-piper` (rhasspy/wyoming-piper 20 flags incl. `--noise-w-scale`/`--noise-w` alias), `wyoming-faster-whisper` (21 flags). `wyoming-cli` deliberately skipped (rhasspy/wyoming is a protocol library with no console_scripts). `mkarchroot` (9 flags from src/mkarchroot.in getopts `'hUC:M:c:f:s'`; spec's `-N` doesn't exist), `makechrootpkg` (15 flags from src/makechrootpkg.in getopts; spec misclassified `-C` and bind flags — real semantics from upstream).
- **DCMTK DICOM medical-imaging toolkit** (OFFIS) — 8 DIMSE network apps (`storescp` 165, `storescu` 138, `findscu` 92, `movescu` 165, `getscu` 125, `echoscu` 69, `termscu` 16, `dcmqrscp` 208 — counts include all TLS/network/transfer-syntax flags) plus 8 conversion/modification apps (`dcmdump` 76, `dcmconv` 76, `dcmodify` 59, `dcmsign` 84, `dcm2json` 37, `dcm2xml` 37, `dcm2pdf` 29 from the dcmdecap proxy, `dcmsr` 43 from the `dsrdump` analog — compdef extended to cover `dsrdump dcmsr`) plus 8 compression/encoding apps (`dcmdjpeg` 42, `dcmcjpeg` 84, `dcmcrle` 34, `dcmdrle` 30, `xml2dcm` 36, `pdf2dcm` 46 from the dcmencap proxy, `dump2dcm` 37, `dcm2pnm` 133 from the dcm2img proxy — compdef extended to cover `dcm2pnm dcm2img dcm2pgm`) plus 7 specialized apps (`dcmftest` 0 flags — multi-mandatory file param only, `img2dcm` 42, `dcmscale` 50, `dcmprscp` 12, `dcmprscu` 23, `dcmpsmk` 41, `dcmqrti` 17) plus 8 data-conversion + DICOMDIR apps (`cda2dcm` 38 / `dcm2cda` 27 / `stl2dcm` 37 — all dcmencap/dcmdecap proxies, `dcmcjpls` 50 / `dcmdjpls` 34 JPEG-LS, `json2dcm` 35, `dcmgpdir` + `dcmmkdir` both 95-flag DICOMDIR profile-aware) plus 7 Presentation State + modern net apps (`dcmpschk` 12, `dcmpsprt` 62 print-management, `dcmpsrcv` 11 / `dcmpssnd` 13 — config-file-driven, `dcmp2pgm` 16 PS-to-PGM render, `dcmrecv` 63 modern storage receiver with full TLS, `dcmsend` 41 modern sender — no TLS in upstream) plus 8 encap/image-processing/Q-R apps (`dcmencap` 48 canonical encapsulator, `dcmdecap` 29 canonical extractor — `_pdf2dcm`/`_cda2dcm`/`_stl2dcm` proxy to dcmencap and `_dcm2pdf`/`_dcm2cda` proxy to dcmdecap, `dcmdspfn` 22 display function generation, `dcmicmp` 40 pixel-level image comparison, `dcmj2pnm` + `dcml2pnm` both 135 flags via `dcm2img` proxy, `dcmquant` 57 color quantization, `dcmqridx` 12 Q/R database index utility) plus 8 SR/RT/LUT helpers (`dsr2html` 49, `dsr2xml` 37, `xml2dsr` 31 SR converters; `drtdump` 17 Radiotherapy dump; `dcmmkcrv` 16, `dcmmklut` 35 PS curve/LUT makers; `dcod2lum` 0, `dconvlum` 0 — no OFCommandLine upstream, positional only) plus 3 final apps (`dcmtk` umbrella dispatcher with 56 real tools + 13 aliases forwarding to `_<tool>`; `wlcefs` + `wlmscpfs` 46 flags each — share WlmConsoleEngineFileSystem class). Result: 56 DCMTK completion files cover every primary `apps/` source across all DCMTK modules.
- **Modern data + VPN** — `polars-cli` + `polars` (pola-rs/polars-cli SQL CLI — 4 real flags from src/main.rs clap derive; spec's `--output-mode`/`--format` don't exist), `amneziawg-go` (amnezia-vpn/amneziawg-go WireGuard fork — 2 flags `--version` and `-f/--foreground`; rest of behavior is env-var driven: `WG_TUN_FD`/`WG_UAPI_FD`/`WG_PROCESS_FOREGROUND`/`LOG_LEVEL`).
- **CERN ROOT data-analysis framework** — `root` + `root.exe` (16 flags from core/base/src/root-argparse.py + TApplication::GetOptions()), `rootcling` (33 llvm::cl::opt flags from rootcling_impl.cxx) + `rootcint` + `genreflex` (built from the same source — 26/25 flags), `hadd` (24 flags from hadd-argparse.py incl. -k/-O/-dbg/-L/-Ltype/-f0..-f9), `rootbrowse` (3 real flags — spec's -l/-q don't exist), `rootls` (7 flags from opts.AddFlag in rootls.cxx), `root-config` (32 build-config branches from config/root-config.in), `rootcp` 7 / `rootmv` 4 / `rootrm` 6 / `rootmkdir` 3 (ROOT C++ file ops; spec wrongly claimed Python from cmdLineUtils.py — they're main/src/*.cxx).
- **iCE40 FPGA + Draco 3D mesh compression** — `icetime` (YosysHQ/icestorm timing analyzer — 16 flags from icetime/icetime.cc), `icepll` (PLL frequency calculator — 10 flags; spec's `-p` is "use SB_PLL40_PAD primitive" not "print params"), `draco_encoder` (google/draco mesh encoder — 13 flags; spec's `-help`/`-version`/`-qtg`/`-qw`/`-precise_distance_measurement_during_encoding` don't exist upstream), `draco_decoder` (4 flags only — `-h`/`-?`/`-i`/`-o` per src/draco/tools/draco_decoder.cc main()).
- **whisper.cpp tool family** (ggml-org/whisper.cpp) — 8 executables beyond `whisper-cli`: `whisper-bench` (7 flags), `whisper-server` (55 flags — full HTTP server: whisper inference + HTTP server + VAD), `whisper-quantize` (3 positionals: input/output/ftype with `q4_0`/`q4_1`/`q5_0`/`q5_1`/`q8_0` enum), `whisper-stream` (23 flags — real-time streaming), `whisper-command` (24 flags — command-detection), `whisper-talk-llama` (34 flags — voice chat with llama.cpp), `whisper-vad-speech-segments` (12 flags — agent followed documented usage-text contract over a buggy duplicate `-vsd` binding in source), `whisper-lsp` (17 flags — LSP bridge). All flag sets verified by walking each tool's `if (arg == "...")` branches.
- **llama.cpp tool family** (ggml-org/llama.cpp) — 16 tools/examples beyond the existing `llama-cli`/`llama-server`/`llama-quantize`/`llama-bench`/`llama-perplexity`/`llama-tokenize`/`llama-embedding`/`llama-gguf-split`/`llama-imatrix` baseline: `llama-batched` (69 flags, `LLAMA_EXAMPLE_BATCHED`), `llama-batched-bench` (60 + bench-specific `-npp`/`-ntg`/`-npl`/`-pps`/`-tgs`/`--output-format`), `llama-export-lora` (12 — filtered to `LLAMA_EXAMPLE_EXPORT_LORA` only: `-m`/`-o`/`--lora`/`--lora-scaled`/`-t`), `llama-gguf` (3 — custom argv parser, no common_params), `llama-passkey` (60 + `--junk`/`--pos`/`--keep`/`-gan`), `llama-parallel` (70 + `-np`/`-ns`/`--junk`/`-pps`/`-tgs`), `llama-mtmd-cli` (87 — multimodal: `-mm`/`--mmproj`, `--image`, `--audio`, `--mmproj-auto`, `--image-min/max-tokens`, `--jinja`, `-sys`), `llama-cvector-generator` (59 + `--positive-file`/`--negative-file`/`--pca-batch`/`--pca-iter`/`--method`), `llama-rpc-server` (6 — own parser; spec's `--mem`/`--ssl*` don't exist upstream), `llama-tts` (63 — TTS-specific `-mv`/`--tts-use-guide-tokens`/`--tts-speaker-file`/`--tts-oute-default`), `llama-lookahead` (63 common; spec's `-W`/`-N`/`-G` are hardcoded constants, not parser flags), `llama-lookup` (68 + `-lcs`/`-lcd`/`--spec-draft-n-max`/`--spec-draft-n-min`), `llama-retrieval` (60 + `--context-file`/`--chunk-size`/`--chunk-separator`/`--pooling`), `llama-speculative` (75 — 13 SPECULATIVE-tagged draft flags `-md`/`-hfd`/`-td`/`-ngld`/`-ctkd`/`-ctvd` + `--spec-draft-*`; spec's `--draft`/`--n-draft` marked `arg_removed` upstream), `llama-eval-callback` + `llama-save-load-state` (55 common each; the latter's source moved to `tests/test-save-load-state.cpp`).
- **BEDOPS genomic BED file ops + VCF/audio/TimescaleDB** — BEDOPS family: `bedops` (30 flags incl. all set ops u/i/d/s/m/p/c/e/n/w mutually exclusive), `bedextract` (4 — minimal, modes 2/3 are positional), `bedmap` (57 — full aggregation suite: --echo*, score aggregations, --count, --indicator, --overlap-*, --bp-ovr, --range, --fraction-*, --exact, formatting), `closestfeats` + `closest-features` (12 — real upstream binary is `closest-features`), `sort-bed` (7 — --check-sort/--max-mem/--tmpdir/-u/-d), `starch` (8 from starch_client_long_options) + `unstarch` (26 incl. archive-version alias group and per-chromosome query flags). Plus `vcfanno` (brentp/vcfanno — 5 flag.Parse() flags), `vcfstats` (pwwang/vcfstats 19 args), `vcf2tsv` (vcflib — 3 flags), `aubiocut` (aubio onset segmenter — 15 doc/aubiocut.txt options), `timescaledb-parallel-copy` (33 flags incl. real --quote/--escape/--column-mapping/--auto-column-mapping/--skip-lines/--skip-batch-errors/--import-id/--buffer-byte-size/--batch-byte-size/--on-conflict-do-nothing/--disable-direct-compress/--enable-client-side-sorting/--disable-windows-1252-handling/--no-retry/--retry-timeout missing from brief).

plus 13 root persistent flags from commands/commandeer.go `initRootCommand` incl. `-s/--source`, `-d/--destination`, `-e/--environment`, `--themesDir`, `--clock` RFC3339, `--logLevel` debug/info/warn/error, `-M/--renderToMemory`, plus a 25-flag build/server layer with `-w/--watch`, `--minify`, `-D/--buildDrafts`, `-E/--buildExpired`, `-F/--buildFuture`, `-t/--theme`, `--gc`, full server flags `-p/--port`, `--bind`, `--liveReloadPort`, `--tlsAuto`/`--tlsCertFile`/`--tlsKeyFile`), `yggdrasil` (yggdrasil-network/yggdrasil-go end-to-end encrypted IPv6 mesh — 14 flags from cmd/yggdrasil/main.go incl. `-genconf`, `-useconf`, `-useconffile`, `-normaliseconf`, `-exportkey`, `-autoconf`, `-logto` syslog/stdout, `-loglevel` error/warn/info/debug/trace, `-address`/`-subnet`/`-publickey` config introspection), `yggdrasilctl` (Yggdrasil admin socket client — 4 flags `-ver`/`-json`/`-no-borders`/`-endpoint` plus 11 admin commands `list`/`getSelf`/`getPeers`/`getTree`/`getPaths`/`getSessions`/`getNodeInfo`/`getMulticastInterfaces`/`getTun`/`addPeer`/`removePeer` from cmd/yggdrasilctl/main.go), `datasets-cli` (huggingface/datasets CLI — 3 subcommands env/test/delete_from_hub from src/datasets/commands/datasets_cli.py with 10 test-cmd flags incl. `--name`/`--cache_dir`/`--data_dir`/`--all_configs`/`--save_info`/`--ignore_verifications`/`--force_redownload`/`--clear_cache`/`--num_proc`), `isolate` (ioi/isolate IOI competitive-programming sandbox — 23 short+long flags from isolate.c getopt_long table incl. `-b/--box-id`, `-c/--chdir`, `--cg-mem`, `-d/--dir` (repeatable), `-E/--env` (repeatable), `-f/--fsize`, `-i/--stdin`, `-k/--stack`, `-m/--mem`, `-M/--meta`, `-t/--time`, `-w/--wall-time`, `-x/--extra-time`, `--as-uid`/`--as-gid`, plus boolean `-D/--no-default-dirs`, `-e/--full-env`, `--cg`, `--share-net`, `--tty-hack`, `--special-files`, `--wait`, and the 5 lifecycle commands `--init`/`--run`/`--cleanup`/`--check-config`/`--print-cg-root`).
- **Radio astronomy pipeline + SuperCollider + cargo-binutils + Gulp** — `wsclean` (Andre Offringa's wide-field radio-interferometric imager — 150+ flags from main/commandline.cpp covering threading (`-j`, `-parallel-gridding`), memory (`-mem`, `-abs-mem`), primary beam (`-apply-primary-beam`, `-primary-beam-model` Hamaker/Lobes/OskarDipole/OskarSphericalWave), weighting (`-weight` natural/uniform/briggs, `-taper-gaussian`/`-taper-tukey`/`-taper-edge`), inversion (`-name`, `-size`, `-scale`, `-channels-out`, `-grid-mode` kb/nn/rect, `-use-idg` with `-idg-mode` cpu/gpu/hybrid, `-use-wgridder`), a-term gridding (`-aterm-config`, `-apply-facet-beam`), data selection (`-pol`, `-interval`, `-channel-range`, `-data-column`, `-maxuvw-m`/`-minuvw-m`), deconvolution (`-niter`, `-nmiter`, `-threshold`, `-auto-threshold`, `-auto-mask`, `-multiscale` family, `-iuwt`, `-moresane-*`, `-fits-mask`, `-fit-spectral-pol`, `-parallel-deconvolution`), restoration (`-restore`, `-beam-size`, `-beam-shape`, `-circular-beam`/`-elliptical-beam`), PSF reuse (`-reuse-psf`, `-reuse-dirty`)), `aoflagger` (Andre Offringa's RFI flagger from aoflagger.readthedocs.io — 4 mutually-exclusive read modes `-direct-read`/`-reorder`/`-memory-read`/`-auto-read-mode` plus `-v`/`-strategy`/`-column`/`-bands`/`-fields`/`-threshold-*`/`-statistics`/`-combine-spws`/`-uvw`/`-skip-flagged`), `DP3` + `DPPP` + `NDPPP` (LOFAR streaming pre-processing pipeline from lofar-astron/DP3 base/DP3.cc — minimal CLI by design: `--help`/`-help`/`-h`, `--usage`/`-usage`, `-v`/`--version`; takes a `.parset` file + key=value parset overrides on the command line; default-loads DP3.parset/DPPP.parset/NDPPP.parset), `supernova` (supercollider/supercollider multi-threaded server from server/supernova/server/main.cpp — 15 long-option flags `--port`, `--rt-pool-size`, `--memory-locking`, `--non-rt`, `--ugen-paths`, `--hw-name`, `--input-channels`/`--output-channels`, `--blocksize`, `--samplerate`, `--hardware-buffer-size`, `--safety-clip-threshold`, `--load-synthdefs`, `--verbosity`; sibling to existing `_scsynth`), `cargo-objdump` + `cargo-ar` + `cargo-as` + `cargo-cov` + `cargo-lld` + `cargo-nm` + `cargo-objcopy` + `cargo-profdata` + `cargo-readobj` + `cargo-size` + `cargo-strip` (rust-embedded/cargo-binutils — 11-binary multi-stem compdef from src/tool.rs `Tool` enum, with full cargo flag set from src/lib.rs incl. `--lib`/`--bin`/`--example`/`--test`/`--bench`, `--release`/`--profile`, `--target`/`--target-dir`/`--manifest-path`, `--features`/`--all-features`/`--no-default-features`, `-p/--package`, `-j/--jobs`, `--frozen`/`--locked`/`--offline`, `-Z` nightly flags), `gulp` (gulpjs/gulp-cli streaming JS build/task runner — 17 options from lib/shared/options/cli-options.js incl. `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`, `--preload`, `-f/--gulpfile`, `--cwd`, `-T/--tasks`, `--tasks-simple`/`--tasks-json`, `--tasks-depth`, `--compact-tasks`, `--sort-tasks`, `--color`/`--no-color`, `-S/--silent`, `--continue`, `--series`, `-L/--log-level` (stackable); task completion auto-discovers via `gulp --tasks-simple` when a gulpfile exists).

plus root flags `--api`/`--cid-base` multibase enum, `-c/--config`, `--enc`/`--encoding` json/xml/text, `-L/--upgrade-cidv0-in-output`, `--offline`, `--timeout`; daemon-cmd flags `--init`, `--init-config`, `--init-profile`, `--routing` dht/dhtclient/none/auto/autoclient, `--mount`, `--writable`, `--enable-gc`, `--manage-fdlimit`, `--migrate`; note: binary is usually `ipfs` but legacy man_src/_ipfs covers IPFilter's `ipfs` — modern Kubo users should symlink as `kubo`).
