Dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`; per-binary audit identified 3 already-covered s6-networking binaries (`s6-tcpclient`, `s6-tlsclient`, `s6-tlsserver`) which are deliberately omitted from the new `#compdef` lines. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`skarnet/s6-networking`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-networking) via `gh api` — USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs + skalibs `GOL_main` `gol_bool` + `gol_arg` tables + case-arm decoding for the mutex flag groups. **File `_proxy-server` (7-stem TCP UCSPI conn-tools)** from [`src/conn-tools/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-networking/tree/master/src/conn-tools). Notable surfaces: **proxy-server** (PROXY-protocol v1/v2 header parser+stripper that sits between a listening TCP socket and a real service to read the client-IP header sent by an upstream load balancer): 2-way mutex `-1`/`--only-v1` xor `-2`/`--only-v2` (decoded from `GOLB_V1` xor `GOLB_V2` `.set`/`.clear` pairings) + long-only `--before-tlsd`/`--after-tlsd` placement-hint flags + `-v VERB` + `-t MS`. **s6-tcpserver-access** (TCP access-control filter; 13 flags + variadic): **5 mutex pairs** decoded from case arms: `-W`/`-w` deny-action (`flagfatal = 0|1` — silent-drop xor banner-then-drop), `-D`/`-d` `TCP_NODELAY` (`flagnodelay = 1|0`), `-H`/`-h` reverse-DNS (`flagdnslookup = 0` xor `flaghosts = 1`), `-R`/`-r` ident-lookup (`flagident = 0|1`), `-P`/`-p` paranoid-hostname (`flagparanoid = 0|1`) + `-l LOCALNAME` + `-B BANNER` + `-t MS` + `-i RULESDIR` xor `-x RULESFILE`. **s6-tcpserver** (all-in-one TCP UCSPI superdaemon; 14 flags): standard verbosity 3-way + SO_REUSEADDR 2-way + maxconn/localmaxconn + privilege-drop. **s6-tcpserver-socketbinder** (uses skalibs `GOL_main`; 6 flags + 2 positionals): 2-way mutex `-d`/`-D`/`--reuse-address`/`--no-reuse-address` SO_REUSEADDR (decoded from `GOLB_NOREUSE` `.clear`/`.set`), 2-way mutex `-M`/`-m`/`--tcp`/`--udp` TCP-default xor UDP (`GOLB_UDP`), `-B`/`--block` bind-block-on-port-in-use, `-b BACKLOG`. **s6-tcpserverd** (5 flags + variadic): accept-loop half. **s6-getservbyname** (2 positionals; getservbyname(3) wrapper; service+protocol enum autocomplete: 16 common service names + tcp/udp/sctp). **s6-ident-client** (1 flag + 4 positionals; RFC1413 ident-protocol client; `-t MS` + ra/rp/la/lp). **File `_s6-clockadd` (5-stem TAI-clock framework)** from [`src/clock/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-networking/tree/master/src/clock). Reusable helper `_s6_clock_query_flags` shared by s6-sntpclock + s6-taiclock (8 common client query flags `-f` force + `-v VERB` + `-r ROUNDTRIPS` + `-t MS` + `-h MS` throttle + `-T MS` total + `-e MS` errmax + `-p PORT`). **s6-clockadd** (2 flags; apply stored clock offset). **s6-clockview** (`int main(void)` no args; print current TAI as 12-byte binary). **s6-sntpclock** (SNTP RFC4330 client). **s6-taiclock** (s6-taiclock-protocol client). **s6-taiclockd** (UDP daemon; 2 flags `-i IP` + `-p PORT`). **File `_s6-tlsc` (6-stem TLS family)** from [`src/tls/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-networking/tree/master/src/tls). Reusable helper `_s6tls_closenotify_flags` shared by all 6 binaries (3 mutex pairs: `-S`/`-s`/`--close-notify` xor `--no-close-notify`, `-J`/`-j`/`--enforce-close-notify` xor `--no-enforce-close-notify`, `-Z`/`-z`/`--keep` xor `--no-keep` — all decoded from skalibs `GOL_main` `gol_bool` `.clear`/`.set` pairings). **Subtle client-vs-server semantic split caught by reading the `.lo` long-option strings**: `-k` is `--servername` on the 3 client variants (s6-tlsc/s6-tlsc-io/s6-ucspitlsc) but `--sni-level` on the 3 server variants (s6-tlsd/s6-tlsd-io/s6-ucspitlsd); `-Y`/`-y` is `--no-client-cert`/`--client-cert` (presentation toggle) on clients but `--client-cert`/`--mandatory-client-cert` (acceptance vs requirement) on servers. The long-only `--verify-cert`/`--no-verify-cert` are client-only; `--sni`/`--mandatory-sni` are server-only. **s6-tlsc-io** and **s6-tlsd-io** add `-d FD`/`--notification-fd` + raw `fdr`/`fdw` positionals (do not chain-load prog; bridge bytes directly). **Hunt-skip log this round**: skarnet/execline (shell-replacement language with ~40 chain-loadable binaries — many names like `backtick`/`foreground`/`if`/`ifelse`/`importas`/`redirfd`/`pipeline`/`elglob`/`loopwhilex`/`tryexec`/`withstdinas` clash with shell builtins or other Unix commands; per-binary dup audit + clash-vs-coverage judgment-call required; explicit R129+ work). The `libs6net`/`sbearssl`/`stls` subdirs of s6-networking (library helpers + non-public-binary linker source files, not user-facing CLIs). **Blacklist additions (18)**: 1 entry in `p*` range + 17 in `s6-*` range. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 133rd consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6-dns + utmps + mdevd R127 — 3 files / 19 binaries** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; per-binary audit identified 3 already-covered s6-dns binaries (`s6-dnsip4`, `s6-dnsip6`, `s6-dnsname`) which are deliberately omitted from the new `#compdef` lines). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream skarnet sister repos via `gh api`. **File `_s6-dns-hosts-compile` (14-stem `#compdef s6-dns-hosts-compile s6-dnsip s6-dnsip4-filter s6-dnsip6-filter s6-dnsmx s6-dnsname-filter s6-dnsns s6-dnsq s6-dnsqr s6-dnsqualify s6-dnssoa s6-dnssrv s6-dnstxt s6-randomip`)** extracted from [`skarnet/s6-dns/src/clients/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-dns/tree/master/src/clients) + [`src/utilities/s6-dns-hosts-compile.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-dns/blob/master/src/utilities/s6-dns-hosts-compile.c) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` specs + case-arm decoding for the mutex flag groups (`s6-dnsip` `-H`/`-h` `/etc/hosts`-lookup mutex decoded from `flaghosts = 0|1`; `s6-dnsq`/`s6-dnsqr` `-1`/`-2` stdout/stderr mutex decoded from `where = &genwrite_{stdout,stderr}`; `s6-randomip`/`s6-dnsname-filter` `-4`/`-6` IPv4-only / IPv6-only mutex). Reusable helper `_s6dns_filter_flags` shared between s6-dnsip4-filter/s6-dnsip6-filter/s6-dnsname-filter (5 common flags `-l`/`-c`/`-t`/`-f`/`-e` — the filter binaries all dispatch through the same `s6dns_generic_filter_main()` upstream so their flag surface is identical by construction). Covers the 14 s6-dns client + utility binaries not previously included. Notable per-binary highlights: **s6-dns-hosts-compile** (2 flags; compile `/etc/hosts` into a packed cdb for fast s6-dns client lookup). **s6-dnsq** + **s6-dnsqr** (4 flags each; low-level direct DNS query, `-1`/`-2` stdout/stderr packet-dump mutex + `-t MS` + `-D LEVEL` debug; positionals are qtype (autocomplete A/AAAA/MX/NS/CNAME/PTR/SOA/SRV/TXT/ANY) + query name + (s6-dnsq only) nameserver IPs). **s6-dnssrv** (3 flags + 3 positionals; SRV-record lookup with `service`/`protocol`/`name` triplet — service autocomplete enum: http/https/ftp/ssh/smtp/imap/imaps/pop3/pop3s/ldap/ldaps/xmpp/xmpp-server/xmpp-client/sip/sips/kerberos/ntp/dns; protocol autocomplete: tcp/udp/tls). **s6-randomip** (3 flags; generate random valid IPv4/IPv6 addresses without DNS query — `-4`/`-6` family mutex + `-n N`). **File `_mdevd` (2-stem `#compdef mdevd mdevd-coldplug`)** extracted from [`skarnet/mdevd/src/mdevd/mdevd.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/mdevd/blob/master/src/mdevd/mdevd.c) + [`mdevd-coldplug.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/mdevd/blob/master/src/mdevd/mdevd-coldplug.c) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` specs (`nNv:D:I:o:O:b:f:s:d:F:C` and `v:s:O:b:`) + case-arm decoding for the 2-way `-n`/`-N` dry-run mutex (`dryrun = 1` print-only-no-act xor `dryrun = 2` verbose-dry-run-print-every-event-and-match). udev replacement implementing the busybox mdev configuration syntax but running as a long-lived netlink-listener daemon (vs mdev which is invoked per-event by the kernel), making it suitable as the device-management daemon under s6 supervision. **mdevd** (12 flags inc. `-v VERB`, `-D NOTIFD`, `-I INTAKE`, `-o OUTPUTFD`, `-O NLGROUPS` bitmask, `-b KBUFSZ`, `-f CONFFILE`, **2-way mutex `-n`/`-N`** dry-run, `-s SLASHSYS`, `-d SLASHDEV`, `-F FWBASE`, `-C` coldplug-then-exit). **mdevd-coldplug** (4 flags; one-shot coldplug helper walking `/sys/devices` to force netlink uevents for every node currently present — sends events to the running mdevd via netlink group; useful at boot for devices that were already present before mdevd started). **File `_utmps-utmpd` (3-stem `#compdef utmps-utmpd utmps-write utmps-wtmpd`)** extracted from [`skarnet/utmps/src/utmps/utmps-{utmpd,write,wtmpd}.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/utmps/tree/master/src/utmps) USAGE strings (utmps-utmpd: `int main(void)` no args — pure daemon; utmps-wtmpd: optional positional wtmp-file override; utmps-write: full subgetopt `uwU:W:t:T:h:i:l:p:`) + case-arm decoding showing `utmps-write -u`/`-w` are **independent bit flags** (`what |= 1` / `what |= 2`) **NOT mutex** — both can be combined to write the same record into both utmp AND wtmp simultaneously (this is the typical login-style use case). utmps replaces glibc's system-wide utmpx-mutex-locked file approach with a UCSPI socket protocol talking to dedicated long-running daemons, eliminating the historical setuid binaries + race-condition class. **utmps-write** has 10 flags + 3 positionals (entry id + type + tty line); type field autocomplete: EMPTY/RUN_LVL/BOOT_TIME/NEW_TIME/OLD_TIME/INIT_PROCESS/LOGIN_PROCESS/USER_PROCESS/DEAD_PROCESS/ACCOUNTING. **Hunt-skip log this round**: skarnet/s6-networking (UCSPI-TCP/IP server family — needs systematic audit since older rounds covered some), skarnet/execline (shell-replacement language ~40 chain-loadable binaries — many names like `backtick`/`foreground`/`if`/`ifelse`/`importas`/`redirfd`/`pipeline`/`elglob`/`loopwhilex`/`tryexec`/`withstdinas` clash with shell builtins or other Unix commands; per-binary dup audit + clash-vs-coverage judgment-call required). The `skadns` subdir of s6-dns (libs6dns + skadns library helpers — header files + non-public-binary linker source, not user-facing CLIs). **Blacklist additions (19)**: 2 entries in `m*` range + 14 in `s6-d*`/`s6-r*` range + 3 in `u*` range. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 132nd consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6-portable-utils R126 — 41-stem multi-binary harvest** (`#compdef s6-basename s6-cat s6-chmod s6-chown s6-cut s6-dirname s6-dumpenv s6-echo s6-env s6-expr s6-false s6-format-filter s6-grep s6-head s6-hiercopy s6-linkname s6-ln s6-ls s6-maximumtime s6-mkdir s6-nice s6-nuke s6-pause s6-printenv s6-quote s6-quote-filter s6-rename s6-rmrf s6-seq s6-sleep s6-sort s6-sync s6-tai64ndiff s6-tail s6-touch s6-true s6-uniquename s6-unquote s6-unquote-filter s6-update-symlinks seekablepipe`) (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; per-binary audit identified `s6-clock` + `s6-mkfifo` as the 2 already-covered binaries in this repo, deliberately omitted from the new `#compdef` line). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`skarnet/s6-portable-utils/src/s6-portable-utils/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-portable-utils/tree/master/src/s6-portable-utils) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs + case-arm decoding for the mutex flag groups via `gh api`. Single multi-stem file `_s6-basename` is the alphabetically-first stem (`s6-basename` < `seekablepipe` because `6` (0x36) < `e` (0x65)). **Coverage**: 41-stem multi-binary harvest of the portable coreutils-style utility binaries that ship with the s6 family — sister tools to the existing larger s6 ecosystem (R117-R125). The portable utils are deliberately not POSIX-compatible drop-in replacements; they trade compat for correctness, smaller code, and consistent behavior across platforms (Linux/BSD/Solaris/macOS). Reusable helper `_s6_digit_lines` shared between `s6-head` and `s6-tail` for the 9-way `-1..-9` digit-shortcut line-count mutex. **Notable per-binary surfaces**: **s6-chown** (`Uu:g:` 3 flags; `-u UID` xor `-U` env-mode (`-U` decoded from source body: reads `$UID` + `$GID` env vars via `getenv("UID")` + `getenv("GID")` + `uid0_scan`/`gid0_scan`)). **s6-cut** (`nsb:c:f:d:` 6 flags; **3-way mutex `-b`/`-c`/`-f`** byte/char/field selection + `-d DELIM` + `-n` no-mb-split + `-s` suppress-no-delim). **s6-dumpenv** (`nNm:` 3 flags; **2-way mutex `-n`/`-N`** empty-value rejection xor allowance + `-m MODE` octal). **s6-grep** (`EFicnqv` 7 flags; **2-way mutex `-E`/`-F`** extended-regex xor fixed-string + `-icnqv` standard grep flags). **s6-head** + **s6-tail** (subgetopt `S123456789n:c:` / `123456789n:c:`; **digit-shortcut 9-way mutex** `-1..-9` shared via helper + line/char count flags). **s6-ln** (`nsfLP` 5 flags; **2-way mutex `-L`/`-P`** follow-symlinks xor no-follow + `-n` treat-dest-as-file + `-s` symlink + `-f` force). **s6-ls** (`0aAx:` 4 flags; **2-way mutex `-a`/`-A`** show-dot+dotdot xor exclude-dot+dotdot (case-arm decoded: `all = 1` xor `all = 2`) + `-0` NUL-separate + `-x PATTERN` repeatable exclude). **s6-maximumtime** (`0abikqtx12` 10 flags; **10-way signal-selection mutex** `-0` (no signal) / `-a` (SIGALRM) / `-b` (SIGABRT) / `-i` (SIGINT) / `-k` (SIGKILL) / `-q` (SIGQUIT) / `-t` (SIGTERM, default) / `-x` (SIGXCPU) / `-1` (SIGUSR1) / `-2` (SIGUSR2) — each decoded from `tosend = SIG... ; break ;` case arm). **s6-nice** (`Iin:` 3 flags; **2-way mutex `-I`/`-i`** strict-error xor ignore-setpriority-failures + `-n INCR`). **s6-nuke** (`htk` 3 flags; **3-way signal mutex** `-h` SIGHUP / `-t` SIGTERM (default) / `-k` SIGKILL — broadcast to every process except self + pid 1). **s6-pause** (`thaqbip:` 7 flags; signal-list **builders** — not mutex — each of `-t`/`-h`/`-a`/`-q`/`-b`/`-i` appends one signal to the wake-list via case-arm `sigs[nsig++] = SIG...; break ;` + `-p SIG,SIG,...` for additional names/numbers). **s6-printenv** (`nd:0` 3 flags; **2-way mutex `-0`/`-d`** NUL-delim xor custom-delim + `-n` no-newline). **s6-sort** (`bcfru0` 6 flags; standard sort-style flags). **s6-unquote-filter** (`qQvwd:` 5 flags; **4-way mutex `-q`/`-Q`/`-v`/`-w`** invalid-byte handling: silent-drop / silent-drop-warn-stderr / verbose-passthrough / warn-and-continue). **No-flag binaries** (`int main(void)`): s6-cat / s6-false / s6-sync / s6-true; **no-flag with argv**: s6-tai64ndiff (reads s6-log timestamps from argv files or stdin) + s6-rmrf + s6-touch + s6-update-symlinks (positional-only). **Hunt-skip log this round**: 2 binaries from this repo were already in older coverage and deliberately omitted: `s6-clock` (CLOCK_REALTIME printer) and `s6-mkfifo` (mkfifo(2) wrapper — already covered in `more_src/_s6-mkfifo`); skarnet/s6-networking + skarnet/s6-dns + skarnet/execline + skarnet/utmps + skarnet/mdevd remain deferred from R124/R125. **Blacklist additions (41)**: 41 entries in `s*` range — by far the largest single-round blacklist add in s6-family coverage to date. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 131st consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6-linux-utils R125 — fstab2s6rc/rngseed/s6-chroot/s6-freeramdisk/s6-hostname/s6-logwatch/s6-mount/s6-pivotchroot/s6-ps/s6-swapoff/s6-swapon/s6-umount (12-stem multi-binary)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream [`skarnet/s6-linux-utils/src/s6-linux-utils/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-linux-utils/tree/master/src/s6-linux-utils) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs + skalibs `GOL_main` `gol_bool` + `gol_arg` tables + case-arm decoding for the mutex flag groups (`rngseed`: -r/-R credentialed-vs-uncredentialed entropy reads, -n/-N blocking-vs-nonblocking, -w/-W credentialed-vs-uncredentialed entropy writes — decoded from `flags.read=1/flags.rcred=1|0`, `flags.block=0|1`, `flags.write=1/flags.wcred=1|0` case-arm assignments; `fstab2s6rc`: 3 paired-bit-clear-vs-set mutex groups `-A`/`-a` noauto, `-U`/`-u` uuid, `-E`/`-e` essential — extracted from `gol_bool` `.clear`/`.set` field pairings) via `gh api`. Single multi-stem file `_fstab2s6rc` is `#compdef fstab2s6rc rngseed s6-chroot s6-freeramdisk s6-hostname s6-logwatch s6-mount s6-pivotchroot s6-ps s6-swapoff s6-swapon s6-umount` (filename uses alphabetically-first stem `fstab2s6rc` since `f` < `r` < `s`). **Coverage**: 12-stem multi-binary harvest of the Linux-specific system-administration utilities that compose with the s6 init+supervision stack (R117-R124). Sister to R124's `_s6-linux-init` (pid 1 + shutdown) and to the existing s6-svc/s6-supervise infrastructure. **Per-binary surface**: **fstab2s6rc** (GOL_main; 10 flags + 1 positional): generate an s6-rc compiled-db source tree from /etc/fstab entries (one s6-rc service per mountpoint); 3-pair mutexes inc. `-A`/`-a` noauto-handling, `-U`/`-u` UUID-emission, `-E`/`-e` essential-marking + `-F FSTAB` / `-m MODE` octal-perms / `-B BUNDLE` group-bundle / `-d BASEDEP` dependency. **rngseed** (subgetopt `d:v:rRnNwW`; 8 flags): maintain the kernel CSPRNG seed across reboots via RNDADDENTROPY ioctl; `-d SEEDDIR` (default /var/lib/rngseed), `-v VERB`, **3 mutex pairs** `-r`/`-R` (read with/without credit), `-n`/`-N` (non-block/block), `-w`/`-W` (write with/without credit). **s6-chroot** (chroot(8) minimalist; 2 positionals: dir + prog). **s6-freeramdisk** (1 positional: /dev/ram*; calls BLKFLSBUF). **s6-hostname** (1 optional positional: get or set kernel hostname). **s6-logwatch** (subgetopt `m:`; 1 flag + 1 positional): tail an s6-log directory; `-m CHARS` max per-entry char cap (NOT a pattern — verified via case-arm `if (!uint0_scan(l.arg, &maxlen))`). **s6-mount** (subgetopt `nz:arwt:o:`; 7 flags + 2 positionals): mount(8) replacement supporting both `-a -z fstab` mount-all form AND `-t type -o options device mountpoint` single-mount form; `-n` no-op (kept for util-linux compat — confirmed via `case 'n' : break ;`), `-z FSTAB` alternative fstab, `-a` mount-all, **2-way mutex** `-r`/`-w` ro xor rw, `-t TYPE` (autocomplete enum: ext4/ext3/ext2/btrfs/xfs/zfs/f2fs/vfat/tmpfs/proc/sysfs/devtmpfs/cgroup/cgroup2/overlay), `-o OPTS` repeatable. **s6-pivotchroot** (3 positionals; Linux pivot_root(2) + chroot atomic swap used by s6-linux-init during initramfs handoff). **s6-ps** (subgetopt `Hlw:W:o:`; 5 flags): ps(1) replacement reading /proc directly; `-H` tree mode, **2-way mutex** `-l` long xor `-o FIELDS` custom field list (autocomplete enum of ~25 supported fields: pid/ppid/pgid/sid/uid/gid/user/group/tty/tpgid/stat/command/args/nice/pri/rtprio/policy/cputime/utime/stime/start_time/vsize/rss/start/ttime), `-w SPACING` column spacing, `-W WCHANFILE` (default /proc/kallsyms). **s6-swapoff** / **s6-swapon** / **s6-umount** (each: `-a` mount-all-from-fstab + 1 optional positional). **Hunt-skip log this round**: skarnet/s6-portable-utils (43 portable-utility binaries — `s6-basename`/`s6-cat`/`s6-chmod`/`s6-chown`/`s6-clock`/`s6-cut`/`s6-dirname`/`s6-dumpenv`/`s6-echo`/`s6-env`/`s6-expr`/`s6-false`/`s6-format-filter`/`s6-grep`/`s6-head`/`s6-hiercopy`/`s6-linkname`/`s6-ln`/`s6-ls`/`s6-maximumtime`/`s6-mkdir`/`s6-mkfifo`/`s6-nice`/`s6-nuke`/`s6-pause`/`s6-printenv`/`s6-quote-filter`/`s6-quote`/`s6-rename`/`s6-rmrf`/`s6-seq`/`s6-sleep`/`s6-sort`/`s6-sync`/`s6-tai64ndiff`/`s6-tail`/`s6-touch`/`s6-true`/`s6-uniquename`/`s6-unquote-filter`/`s6-unquote`/`s6-update-symlinks`/`seekablepipe` — these need a separate per-binary dup audit since several names overlap with coreutils-style commands like `s6-cat`/`s6-grep`/`s6-head`/`s6-tail`/`s6-sort` that may have been previously batched; deferred for R126+ systematic audit), skarnet/s6-networking + skarnet/s6-dns (TCP/IP UCSPI server family + DNS resolver utilities — partial coverage in older rounds; needs systematic audit), skarnet/execline (shell-replacement language ~40 chain-loadable binaries — many names clash with shell builtins), skarnet/utmps (utmp/wtmp replacement family), skarnet/mdevd (udev replacement). **Blacklist additions (12)**: 1 entry in `f*` (fstab2s6rc) + 1 in `r*` (rngseed) + 10 in `s6-*` range. Surface extracted source-direct from upstream skarnet sister repos via `gh api`. **File `_s6-rc-dryrun` (4-stem `#compdef s6-rc-dryrun s6-rc-fdholder-filler s6-rc-format-upgrade s6-rc-oneshot-run`)** extracted from [`skarnet/s6-rc/src/s6-rc/s6-rc-{dryrun,fdholder-filler,format-upgrade,oneshot-run}.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-rc/tree/master/src/s6-rc) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs / skalibs `GOL_main` `gol_bool` + `gol_arg` tables. Covers the 4 s6-rc service-manager binaries not previously included (the other 6 — s6-rc/s6-rc-bundle/s6-rc-compile/s6-rc-db/s6-rc-init/s6-rc-update — were already covered in earlier rounds). **s6-rc-dryrun** (3 flags + variadic): `-v VERB` / `-t MS` + s6-rc args; simulates a transition plan without executing. **s6-rc-fdholder-filler** (2 flags): `-1` readiness / `-t MS`; bootstrap helper that populates an s6-fdholderd server (R120) with pre-bound socket fds from a list read on stdin. **s6-rc-format-upgrade** (4 flags + 1 positional): `-v VERB` / `-t MS` / `-l LIVEDIR` / `-b` blocking-lock + new compiled-db directory. **s6-rc-oneshot-run** (3 flags + 2 positionals): `-b`/`--block` xor `--no-block` mutex + `-l LIVEDIR`/`--livedir LIVEDIR` (extracted from skalibs `gol_bool` + `gol_arg` tables) + `up|down` direction + service number; internal helper invoked by s6-rc to run a oneshot service's up/down script. **File `_s6-linux-init` (9-stem `#compdef s6-linux-init s6-linux-init-echo s6-linux-init-hpr s6-linux-init-logouthookd s6-linux-init-nuke s6-linux-init-shutdown s6-linux-init-shutdownd s6-linux-init-telinit s6-linux-init-umountall`)** extracted from [`skarnet/s6-linux-init/src/{init,misc,shutdown}/*.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6-linux-init/tree/master/src) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` specs + `GOL_main` tables + case-arm decoding for the mutex action selectors. Covers the 9 s6-linux-init binaries not previously included (s6-linux-init-maker — the boot-image generator — was already covered). **s6-linux-init** (12 flags; stage-1 pid 1 binary that brings up the early boot environment then chain-loads stage-2): GOL_main long-options — `-v VERB`/`--verbosity`, `-c BASEDIR`/`--basedir`, `-p PATH`/`--initial-path`, `-s DIR`/`--envdumpdir`, `-m UMASK`/`--umask`, `-d FSTYPE`/`--slashdev`, `-D NAME`/`--default-runlevel` (default "default"), **2-way mutex `-n`/`--no-unmount-slashrun` xor `-N`/`--hands-off-slashrun`**, `-C`/`--container`, `-B`/`--no-logger`, `-W FD`/`--readiness-fd`. **s6-linux-init-telinit** (1 positional): switch to a different runlevel. **s6-linux-init-echo** (2 flags + variadic): `-n` no-trailing-newline + `-s SEP` field separator; minimal echo replacement for early-boot scripts. **s6-linux-init-logouthookd** / **s6-linux-init-nuke** / **s6-linux-init-umountall** (no CLI flags): logout-cleanup daemon / SIGKILL-everything-except-pid-1 / unmount-everything-except-devtmpfs/proc/sysfs (hardcoded MAXLINES=99). **s6-linux-init-hpr** (9 flags; low-level halt/poweroff/reboot called by shutdown after grace): **3-way mutex `-h`/`-p`/`-r`** primary action (case arms: what=1/2/3) + `-f` skip `/run/initctl` + **2-way mutex `-d`/`-w`** wtmp-mode (no-record xor only-record-no-actual) + `-W` no-wall + `-n` no-sync + `-i` confirm-hostname. **s6-linux-init-shutdown** (12 flags + 2 positionals; util-linux-compatible shutdown(8) replacement): **5-way mutex `-h`/`-p`/`-r`/`-k`/`-c`** primary (case arms what=1/2/3/4/cancel) + **2-way mutex `-H`/`-P`** secondary after-halt action (subwhat=1/2) + **2-way mutex `-f`/`-F`** fsck-skip xor fsck-force (both case-arm no-ops kept for util-linux backwards-compat — confirmed by source comments `/* talk to the hand */` `/* no, the other hand */`) + `-a` honor `/etc/shutdown.allow` + `-i` confirm-hostname + `-t SEC` grace-period; positionals are time-spec + optional broadcast message. **s6-linux-init-shutdownd** (5 flags; long-running daemon that orchestrates the shutdown sequence): `-d FD` notification-fd + `-c BASEDIR` + `-g MS` grace ms + `-C` container + `-B` no-logger. **Hunt-skip log this round**: skarnet/s6-linux-utils (separate misc-utility repo — distinct from s6-linux-init; needs its own R125+ round), skarnet/s6-portable-utils (cross-platform companion to s6-linux-utils; another candidate R125+ round), skarnet/s6-networking (UCSPI-TCP/IP server family — partially covered in older rounds; needs systematic audit), skarnet/s6-dns (DNS resolver utility family — fresh candidate for R126+), skarnet/execline (shell-replacement language with ~40 chain-loadable binaries — many names like `backtick`/`foreground`/`if`/`ifelse` clash with shell builtins, evaluate per-binary), skarnet/utmps (utmp/wtmp replacement family), skarnet/mdevd (udev replacement). **Blacklist additions (13)**: 4 entries in the `s6-rc*` range + 9 entries in the `s6-linux-init*` range. and from [`src/supervision/s6-notify-{fd-from-socket,socket-from-fd}.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/tree/master/src/supervision) + [`s6-notifyoncheck.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/blob/master/src/supervision/s6-notifyoncheck.c) USAGE strings + **skalibs `GOL_main` `gol_bool` + `gol_arg` tables** (these three binaries use the long-options getopt variant rather than `subgetopt_r`, so the option-table fields `.so = '<short>', .lo = "<long>", .i = <enum-slot>` are the authoritative source); and from [`src/supervision/s6-permafailon.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/blob/master/src/supervision/s6-permafailon.c) USAGE string (no subgetopt; pure positional). **Coverage**: 3 files covering 12 binaries — completes the `src/supervision` directory minus the 6 binaries already covered (s6-supervise/s6-svc/s6-svstat/s6-svok/s6-svscan/s6-svscanctl per pre-R123 audit). **File `_s6-svdt` (8-stem `#compdef s6-svdt s6-svdt-clear s6-svlink s6-svlisten s6-svlisten1 s6-svperms s6-svunlink s6-svwait`)** — supervision-tree manipulation clients: **s6-svdt** (3 flags + 1 positional; dump down-time entries / death tally for a service; `-S` flat numeric xor `-s` sub-summary mutex + `-n MAXENTRIES`), **s6-svdt-clear** (no flags + 1 positional; reset down-time history), **s6-svlink** (5 flags + 3 positionals; atomically link a servicedir into a scandir; `-d`/`-D` down-block-mode mutex, `-f` force, `-P` protect-on-rescan, `-t MS`), **s6-svlisten** (`#flags + variadic`; multi-service state-wait + chain-load prog; 6-way state mutex + `-a`/`-o` ALL/ANY conjunction mutex + `-t MS`; positionals are servicedirs terminated by `""` then prog-and-args), **s6-svlisten1** (single-service variant of svlisten; 6-way state mutex + `-t MS`), **s6-svperms** (10 flags + variadic; get/set permission triple on servicedirs that controls who can use s6-svc/s6-svwait; `-v` verbose, **5-way mutex** `-u`/`-g GROUP`/`-G GROUP`/`-o`/`-O GROUP` perms-target, **2-way mutex** `-e`/`-E GROUP` event-subscription gid filter), **s6-svunlink** (2 flags + 2 positionals; inverse of svlink; `-X` skip bring-down, `-t MS`), **s6-svwait** (same surface as svlisten but does NOT chain-load any prog; just waits then exits — used for one-shot state-wait checks in scripts). Reusable helper `_s6_sv_state_flags` shared by svlisten/svlisten1/svwait. **File `_s6-notify-fd-from-socket` (3-stem `#compdef s6-notify-fd-from-socket s6-notify-socket-from-fd s6-notifyoncheck`)** — service-readiness notification adapters bridging between the s6 "write to notification-fd" readiness protocol and the systemd-style `sd_notify(3)` UNIX-socket protocol: **s6-notify-fd-from-socket** (forward adapter; receive sd_notify-style `READY=1` datagram then signal readiness on s6 notification-fd; 5 flags inc. `-f`/`--no-doublefork` xor `--doublefork` mutex, `-3 FD`/`--notification-fd FD`, `-t MS`/`--timeout MS`), **s6-notify-socket-from-fd** (inverse adapter; receive s6 readiness signal then emit sd_notify datagram; 7 flags inc. `-k`/`--keep-environment` to retain `NOTIFY_SOCKET` env var, `-d FD`/`--notification-fd FD`, `-t MS`/`--timeout MS`), **s6-notifyoncheck** (wrap a long-running service with a periodic external check; 8 flags inc. `-d`/`--doublefork`, `-3 FD`/`--notification-fd FD`, `-s MS`/`--initial-sleep MS`, `-T MS`/`--global-timeout MS`, `-t MS`/`--local-timeout MS`, `-w MS`/`--waiting-time MS`, `-n N`/`--tries N`, `-c PROG`/`--check-program PROG` (execline-only — gated by `#ifdef S6_USE_EXECLINE` in source)). **File `_s6-permafailon` (1-stem)** — service-death-rate auto-disabler designed to be placed in a servicedir's finish script. No CLI flags + 4 positionals: `seconds` (rolling-window size in seconds), `deathcount` (threshold), `statuslist` (comma-separated list of fatal exit statuses), prog-and-args. If the service has died `deathcount` times within `seconds` with any of the `statuslist` exit statuses, triggers a permafailure by chain-loading prog (typically `s6-svc -O .` to disable). **Hunt-skip log this round**: s6-background-watch.c (header file, not a separate binary — confirmed by re-reading the conn-tools/supervision file listing). **Blacklist additions (12)**: 12 entries distributed across `s6-n*` (3), `s6-p*` (1), and `s6-sv*` (8) ranges. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 128th consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6 conn-tools tail R122 — s6-sudo/s6-sudoc/s6-sudod (3-stem) + s6-accessrules-cdb-from-fs/s6-accessrules-fs-from-cdb (2-stem) + s6-connlimit (1-stem) + s6-ioconnect (1-stem) — 4 files / 7 binaries** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream skarnet/s6 [`src/conn-tools/s6-sudo{,c,d}.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/tree/master/src/conn-tools) + [`s6-accessrules-{cdb-from-fs,fs-from-cdb}.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/tree/master/src/conn-tools) + [`s6-connlimit.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/blob/master/src/conn-tools/s6-connlimit.c) + [`s6-ioconnect.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/blob/master/src/conn-tools/s6-ioconnect.c) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs + case-arm decoding for non-obvious flag groups via `gh api`. **Coverage**: 4 files covering 7 binaries — finishes off the entire `src/conn-tools` directory of upstream s6 (the rest of which was already covered in R117/R120/R121). Carryforward from R121 hunt-skip log. **File `_s6-sudo` (3-stem `#compdef s6-sudo s6-sudoc s6-sudod`)** — Skarnet's UCSPI-sudo privilege-escalation framework: **s6-sudo** (client; 8 flags + variadic positionals): 3-way mutex verbosity `-q`/`-Q`/`-v`, `-p BINDPATH` local socket bind path, `-l LOCALNAME` PROTO_LOCAL override, `-e` do NOT transmit environment to remote prog, `-t MS` connection setup timeout, `-T MS` total run timeout; positionals are s6-sudod socket path + remote prog-and-args. **s6-sudoc** (UCSPI-client half; 3 flags + variadic): `-e`, `-t MS` connection (`timeoutconn`), `-T MS` total run; positional is remote prog-and-args. Typically invoked indirectly via s6-sudo. **s6-sudod** (server-side prog runner; 5 flags + variadic): `-0` null-redirect stdin / `-1` null-redirect stdout / `-2` null-redirect stderr as independent bitwise toggles, `-d` null-redirect all four proxied fds at once (equivalent to `-0 -1 -2` + control-fd), `-t MS` client inactivity timeout; positional is prog-and-args. **File `_s6-accessrules-cdb-from-fs` (2-stem `#compdef s6-accessrules-cdb-from-fs s6-accessrules-fs-from-cdb`)** — bidirectional converter between filesystem-tree access-rules db (one file per rule, easy to edit) and packed CDB file (single atomic file, fast to query at server-runtime; what production s6 servers query via the `-x RULESFILE` flag from R120/R121): **s6-accessrules-cdb-from-fs** (1 flag + 2 positionals): `-m MODE` / `--mode MODE` octal permissions on output CDB (extracted from `subgetopt_long` table `{ .so = 'm', .lo = "mode", .i = GOLA_MODE }`); positionals are output CDB file + input rules directory tree. **s6-accessrules-fs-from-cdb** (no flags + 2 positionals): output rules directory + input CDB file. **File `_s6-connlimit` (1-stem)** — environment-driven connection-count guard: chain-loads prog if under limit; reads `$PROTO`, then queries `$PROTOCONNNUM` and `$PROTOMAX` from the UCSPI server environment to decide whether the current peer has exceeded its concurrent connection allowance. No CLI options (pure env + argv[1..] chain-loader). **File `_s6-ioconnect` (1-stem)** — bidirectional I/O splicer that shuttles bytes between (stdin → `-w fdw`) and (`-r fdr` → stdout) until either side closes; typically used inside a UCSPI pipeline. 3 flags: `-t MS` inactivity timeout, `-r FD` source fd (default 6), `-w FD` sink fd (default 7). The deprecated `-0`/`-1`/`-6`/`-7` flags discovered in the `012t:r:w:67` subgetopt string are case-arm no-ops kept only for backward compatibility (auto-detected at runtime per upstream source comments `/* these options are deprecated */` `/* only there for compatibility */` `/* flagsocket is autodetected now */`); NOT offered for completion. **Hunt-skip log this round**: s6-svc/s6-svstat/s6-svok/s6-svscan/s6-svscanctl/s6-svlisten/s6-svlisten1/s6-svperms/s6-svlink/s6-svunlink/s6-svwait/s6-svdt/s6-svdt-clear/s6-supervise/s6-notifyoncheck/s6-notify-fd-from-socket/s6-notify-socket-from-fd/s6-permafailon (entire `src/supervision` directory — 18 binaries — partially already in older completions, but at least s6-svc/s6-svstat/s6-svok/s6-svscan were noted "already partially covered" in R117 hunt-skip; needs systematic R123+ audit of which are actually covered vs missing). **Blacklist additions (7)**: 7 entries distributed across `s6-a*`, `s6-c*`, `s6-i*`, `s6-s*` ranges. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 127th consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6 ipcserver framework R121 — s6-ipcclient/s6-ipcserver/s6-ipcserver-access/s6-ipcserver-socketbinder/s6-ipcserverd (5-stem multi-binary)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream skarnet/s6 [`src/conn-tools/s6-ipc{client,server,server-access,server-socketbinder,serverd}.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/tree/master/src/conn-tools) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs + case-arm decoding for the non-obvious short-flag groups via `gh api`. Single multi-stem file `_s6-ipcclient` is `#compdef s6-ipcclient s6-ipcserver s6-ipcserver-access s6-ipcserver-socketbinder s6-ipcserverd`. **Coverage**: 5-stem multi-binary harvest covering the s6 UCSPI UNIX-domain socket client+server framework — Skarnet's clean-room reimplementation of Dan Bernstein's UCSPI-UNIX protocol with the supervised-process improvements that motivate the rest of s6. Carryforward from R118/R119/R120 hunt-skip log. **Per-binary surface**: **s6-ipcclient** (connect to a UNIX-domain socket and chain-load prog with the connection wrapped as UCSPI protocol fds — typically fd 6/7) — 5 flags + 2 positionals: **3-way mutex verbosity** `-q` xor `-Q` xor `-v` (quiet xor very-quiet xor verbose), `-p BINDPATH` local socket path to bind from, `-l LOCALNAME` PROTO_LOCAL env override; positionals are server socket path + prog-and-args. **s6-ipcserver** (all-in-one UNIX-domain UCSPI server superdaemon: binds, listens, accepts, and spawns prog per connection — composes the work of s6-ipcserver-socketbinder + s6-ipcserverd in a single binary) — 16 flags + 2 positionals: **3-way mutex verbosity** `-q`/`-Q`/`-v`, **2-way mutex `-d`/`-D` SO_REUSEADDR** (default-set xor unset), **2-way mutex `-P`/`-p` peer-credentials lookup** (P=no-lookup xor p=lookup-default), `-1` readiness, `-c MAXCONN` total connections (default 40), `-C LOCALMAXCONN` per-peer-uid connections (default 40), `-b BACKLOG` listen backlog, `-a PERMS` octal socket perms (default 0700), `-u UID` / `-g GID` / `-G GID,GID,...` privilege drop, `-U` ucspi env mode; positionals are socket path + prog-and-args. **s6-ipcserver-access** (access-control filter: invoked under s6-ipcserver, consults an access-rules db, denies or allows the peer, then chain-loads prog if allowed) — 6 flags + variadic positional: `-v VERB` verbosity, **2-way mutex `-e`/`-E` default-allow xor default-deny-default**, `-l LOCALNAME` PROTO_LOCAL override, **2-way mutex `-i RULESDIR` xor `-x RULESFILE`** access-rules backing store; positional is prog-and-args. **s6-ipcserver-socketbinder** (socket-binding half of the s6-ipcserver pipeline: binds path, optionally listens, then chain-loads prog with the bound socket on fd 0) — 6 flags + 2 positionals: **2-way mutex `-d`/`-D` SO_REUSEADDR**, **2-way mutex `-M`/`-m` SOCK_STREAM-default xor SOCK_DGRAM**, `-B` bind-only-no-listen, `-b BACKLOG`, `-a PERMS`; positionals are socket path + prog-and-args. **s6-ipcserverd** (accept-loop half of the s6-ipcserver pipeline: expects a bound listen() socket on fd 0, typically piped in from s6-ipcserver-socketbinder, and spawns prog per connection) — 5 flags + variadic positional: `-v VERB`, `-1` readiness, **2-way mutex `-P`/`-p` peer-credentials lookup**, `-c MAXCONN`, `-C LOCALMAXCONN`; positional is prog-and-args. **Hunt-skip log this round**: s6-sudo/s6-sudoc/s6-sudod (s6 UCSPI-sudo family in src/conn-tools — discovered via gh api; 3-stem multi-binary candidate for R122+), s6-accessrules-cdb-from-fs/s6-accessrules-fs-from-cdb (s6 access-rules conversion utilities — sister tools to the `-i RULESDIR`/`-x RULESFILE` flags in s6-ipcserver-access; 2-stem candidate for R122+), s6-connlimit/s6-ioconnect (s6 connection rate-limiting and bidirectional pipe utilities — deferred for a misc R123+ round). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 5 entries in the `s6-ipc*` range. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 126th consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6 fdholder server R120 — s6-fdholder-daemon/s6-fdholder-delete/s6-fdholder-getdump/s6-fdholder-setdump/s6-fdholder-transferdump/s6-fdholderd (6-stem multi-binary)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream skarnet/s6 [`src/fdholder/s6-fdholder-{daemon,delete,getdump,setdump,transferdump}.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/tree/master/src/fdholder) + [`src/fdholder/s6-fdholderd.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/tree/master/src/fdholder/s6-fdholderd.c) USAGE strings + `subgetopt_r` option-string specs via `gh api`. Single multi-stem file `_s6-fdholder-daemon` is `#compdef s6-fdholder-daemon s6-fdholder-delete s6-fdholder-getdump s6-fdholder-setdump s6-fdholder-transferdump s6-fdholderd`. **Coverage**: 6-stem multi-binary harvest covering the s6 file-descriptor holder server-side binaries plus the atomic dump-management family — sister tools to R117's `_s6-fdholder-list` (client-side: s6-fdholder-list/store/retrieve). The fd-holder server pattern lets unrelated processes exchange live OS file descriptors (sockets, pipes, ttys) across an s6 supervised process tree via UNIX-domain socket protocol. **Per-binary surface**: **s6-fdholder-daemon** (long-lived listener variant — opens its own listening socket and runs the s6-fdholderd protocol engine) — 14 flags + 1 positional: shared `-v VERBOSITY` / `-1` (notify-readiness on fd-1) / `-c MAXCONN` (default 16) / `-n MAXFDS` (default 1000) / `-i RULESDIR` xor `-x RULESFILE` (mutex access-rules) / `-t MS` (per-client timeout) / `-T MS` (lameduck shutdown timeout); plus daemon-specific `-d` xor `-D` (daemonize-default xor stay-foreground mutex) / `-b BACKLOG` (listen backlog) / `-u UID` / `-g GID` / `-G GID,GID,...` (supplementary gids) / `-U` (ucspi mode); positional is socket path. **s6-fdholderd** (UCSPI-mode kernel of the fdholder server — designed to be invoked under `s6-ipcserver` / `s6-ipcserverd` with a connected client already on the protocol fds) — 8 flags + no positionals: the shared server-flag subset only (no listen-socket / daemonize / drop-privs flags, since those concerns are delegated to the wrapping ucspi server). **s6-fdholder-delete** (delete a held fd from the server by id) — 1 flag + 2 positionals: `-t MS` operation timeout; positionals are socket + descriptor id. **s6-fdholder-getdump** (atomically retrieve ALL held fds + ids from a running fdholder server, then chain-load prog with those fds/ids in environment — used by s6-rc to snapshot live socket state for restart-as-same-server) — 1 flag + variadic positionals: `-t MS` timeout; positionals are socket + prog-and-args. **s6-fdholder-setdump** (atomically load a dump of fds+ids from stdin into a fresh fdholder server — paired with the prior `getdump`) — 1 flag + 1 positional: `-t MS` timeout; positional is socket. **s6-fdholder-transferdump** (atomically migrate all held fds from one fdholder server `socketfrom` to another `socketto`; combines getdump + setdump in a single hot-handoff) — 1 flag + 2 positionals: `-t TIMEOUTFROM:TIMEOUTTO` (compound colon-separated timeout pair, one per endpoint); positionals are source + destination socket paths. Reusable helper array `_s6_fdh_server_flags` shared between s6-fdholder-daemon and s6-fdholderd (8 common server-config flags). **Hunt-skip log this round**: s6-ipcserver/s6-ipcserver-access/s6-ipcserver-socketbinder/s6-ipcserverd/s6-tcpserver4d/s6-tcpserver6d (R118/R119 carryforward; s6-networking server family), s6-ioconnect/s6-format-filter/s6-permafailon/s6-permafailon01 (R118/R119 carryforward), s6-accessrules-cdb-from-fs/s6-accessrules-fs-from-cdb (s6 access-rules conversion utilities — discovered via `gh api repos/skarnet/s6/contents/src/conn-tools`; deferred for a dedicated round). **Blacklist additions (6)**: 6 entries in the `s6-fd*` range. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 125th consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6 ftrig family R119 — s6-ftrig-listen/s6-ftrig-listen1/s6-ftrig-notify/s6-ftrig-wait/s6-ftrigrd (5-stem multi-binary)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface extracted source-direct from upstream skarnet/s6 [`src/pipe-tools/s6-ftrig-{listen,listen1,notify,wait}.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/tree/master/src/pipe-tools) USAGE strings and [`src/libs6/s6-ftrigrd.c`](https://github.com/skarnet/s6/tree/master/src/libs6) `main()` signature via `gh api repos/skarnet/s6/contents`. Single multi-stem file `_s6-ftrig-listen` is `#compdef s6-ftrig-listen s6-ftrig-listen1 s6-ftrig-notify s6-ftrig-wait s6-ftrigrd`. **Coverage**: 5-stem multi-binary harvest covering the s6 fifodir event-trigger CLI family — the consumer half of the fifodir IPC primitive whose producer half (`s6-mkfifodir` / `s6-cleanfifodir`) was covered in R118's `_s6-envdir`. **Per-binary surface**: **s6-ftrig-listen** (chain-load prog after matching events on multiple (fifodir, regexp) pairs) — 3 flags + variadic positionals: **2-way mutex match-mode** `-a` conjunction (default; all pairs must match) xor `-o` disjunction (any single pair matches), `-t MS` timeout in milliseconds; positionals are alternating fifodir/regexp pairs terminated by empty string `""` followed by prog-and-args. **s6-ftrig-listen1** (single-pair variant of s6-ftrig-listen) — 1 flag + 3 positionals: `-t MS` timeout; positionals are fifodir + event regexp + prog-and-args (does chain-load prog after match). **s6-ftrig-notify** (emit each character of message as a separate event into fifodir; null character 0x00 cannot be transmitted) — no flags + 2 positionals: fifodir + event message string. **s6-ftrig-wait** (single-pair wait variant of s6-ftrig-listen1; does NOT chain-load any program after match) — 1 flag + 2 positionals: `-t MS` timeout; positionals are fifodir + event regexp. **s6-ftrigrd** (internal s6 fifodir-trigger server daemon spawned by libs6 `ftrigr_start`/`ftrigr_startf`; not directly invoked by users) — no CLI arguments (`main(void)` signature in source); reads requests via the skalibs SASS server protocol on stdin/stdout. **Hunt-skip log this round**: s6-fdholder-daemon/s6-fdholder-delete/s6-fdholder-getdump/s6-fdholder-setdump/s6-fdholder-transferdump/s6-fdholderd (R117 ext — 6 additional s6-fdholder server-side and dump-management binaries discovered via `gh api repos/skarnet/s6/contents/src/fdholder`; deferred for a dedicated R120+ round), s6-ipcserver/s6-ipcserver-access/s6-ipcserver-socketbinder/s6-ipcserverd/s6-tcpserver4d/s6-tcpserver6d (R118 deferral; s6-networking server family), s6-ioconnect/s6-format-filter/s6-permafailon/s6-permafailon01 (R118 deferral; more s6 utility binaries). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 5 entries in the `s6-f*` range between `s6-envuidgid` and `s6-mkfifodir`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 124th consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6 utilities R118 — s6-envdir/s6-setlock/s6-fghack/s6-mkfifodir/s6-cleanfifodir (5-stem multi-binary)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream skarnet.org documentation at [skarnet.org/software/s6/s6-{envdir,setlock,fghack,mkfifodir,cleanfifodir}.html](https://skarnet.org/software/s6/) via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_s6-envdir` is `#compdef s6-envdir s6-setlock s6-fghack s6-mkfifodir s6-cleanfifodir`. **Coverage**: 5-stem multi-binary harvest covering core s6 chain-loaded process-management utilities. Sister tools to R117's s6-fdholder family. **Per-binary surface**: **s6-envdir** (set environment variables from a directory of files then chain-exec prog) — 5 flags + 2 positionals: `-i`/`-I` strict-mode toggle (strict=default exits 111 if dir missing; loose=chain-exec unchanged), `-n` preserve trailing whitespace, `-f` verbatim file content as value, `-L` remove 4096-byte per-file read limit, `-c NULLIS` replace nulls with first char of NULLIS string; positionals are env directory + prog-and-args. **s6-setlock** (acquire advisory lock then chain-exec prog) — 6 flags + 2 positionals: **3-way mutex lock-acquisition mode** `-n` nonblocking xor `-N` blocking (default) xor `-t MS` timed; **2-way mutex lock-type** `-r` shared xor `-w` exclusive (default); `-d FD` expose lock on file descriptor; positionals are lock file + prog-and-args. **s6-fghack** (open many file descriptors all writing to a single pipe then spawn prog as child — detects process backgrounding in supervised services where missing this hack causes spurious service-down reports) — no flags; just positional prog-and-args. **s6-mkfifodir** (create a fifodir — directory containing a control FIFO used by the s6-ftrig family for event notification) — 2 flags + 1 positional: `-f` force/reset permissions on existing fifodir, `-g GID` restrict listenability to GID group members; positional is fifodir path. **s6-cleanfifodir** (garbage-collect dead listeners from a fifodir) — no CLI options; positional fifodir path (must be owned by the current user). **Hunt-skip log this round**: s6-ftrigr-list/s6-ftrigrd/s6-ftrig-listen/s6-ftrig-notify/s6-ftrig-wait (s6-ftrig event-notification family — uses fifodirs created by s6-mkfifodir; deferred for separate ftrig-focused round), s6-ipcserver/s6-ipcserver-access/s6-ipcserver-socketbinder/s6-ipcserverd/s6-tcpserver4d/s6-tcpserver6d (s6-networking server family — deferred), s6-ioconnect/s6-format-filter/s6-permafailon/s6-permafailon01 (more utility binaries — deferred for completeness round). **Blacklist additions (5)**: alphabetically distributed across the s6-c*/s6-e*/s6-f*/s6-m*/s6-s* ranges. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 123rd consecutive clean round.
- **Skarnet s6 fdholder R117 — s6-fdholder-list/store/retrieve (3-stem multi-binary; s6 fd-holder client tools)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream Skarnet documentation at [skarnet.org/software/s6/s6-fdholder-{list,store,retrieve}.html](https://skarnet.org/software/s6/) via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_s6-fdholder-list` is `#compdef s6-fdholder-list s6-fdholder-store s6-fdholder-retrieve`. **Coverage**: 3-stem multi-binary harvest covering the s6 file-descriptor holder client utilities. Each communicates with an `s6-fdholderd` daemon via UNIX socket — useful for passing live file descriptors (sockets, pipes) across unrelated processes in s6-init / s6-rc supervised process trees. Sister tools to the already-covered s6-svc / s6-svstat / s6-svok / s6-svscan service manager family. **Per-binary surface**: **s6-fdholder-store** (store a file descriptor in the fd-holder daemon under a given id) — 3 flags + 2 positionals: `-d FD` descriptor number to store (default 0), `-T MS` descriptor expiration time, `-t MS` operation timeout; positionals are socket path + descriptor identifier. **s6-fdholder-list** (list all descriptors currently held) — 1 flag + 1 positional: `-t MS` operation timeout (safeguard against wrong socket); positional is fdholder socket path. **s6-fdholder-retrieve** (retrieve a held file descriptor and execute a program with it connected to stdin) — 2 flags + 3 positionals: `-D` delete the descriptor from server storage after retrieval (requires both read and write permissions for the identifier), `-t MS` operation timeout; positionals are socket path + descriptor identifier + program-and-arguments to execute. **Hunt-skip log this round**: fttimer/ftrandom/ftmemchk (R113 ft2demos remaining deferrals — Debian dyn.manpages 404 chain persists; no GitHub mirror of `freetype-demos` source easily accessible via gh api; deferred indefinitely until savannah.nongnu.org mirror is identified), execline binaries (backtick/foreground/if/ifelse/importas/redirfd/pipeline/elglob/loopwhilex/tryexec/withstdinas — Skarnet's shell-replacement family; many names clash with shell builtins or other commands, so completion adds limited value; deferred), s6-rc/runit-init/utmps (s6-rc service-manager + runit-init + utmps — separate research), runsv/runsvchdir/runsvdir/sv (runit family already partially covered). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 3 between `s6-envuidgid` (23338) and `s6-linux-init-maker` (23342) for `s6-fdholder-list`/`s6-fdholder-retrieve`/`s6-fdholder-store`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 122nd consecutive clean round.

Surface transcribed via WebFetch of the bramstein/sfnt2woff-zopfli upstream man pages (the original Mozilla sfnt2woff / woff2sfnt utilities share the same flag surface but use stock zlib for compression instead of zopfli, so the `-n iterations` flag is absent from the original — otherwise identical). Single multi-stem file `_sfnt2woff` is `#compdef sfnt2woff woff2sfnt`. **Coverage**: 2-stem multi-binary harvest covering the original (non-zopfli) WOFF1 bidirectional converters. **Sister files in this round's WOFF/SFNT family**: `_sfnt2woff-zopfli` from 2026-05-17 covers the bramstein zopfli-recompression variants `sfnt2woff-zopfli` + `woff2sfnt-zopfli`; `_ttf2woff2` from 2026-05-21 covers Google's WOFF2 compressors `ttf2woff2` + `woff2_compress`; `_woff2_decompress` from 2026-05-21 covers Google's WOFF2 decoder. Combined, the **WOFF conversion ecosystem coverage is now 6 binaries across 4 multi-stem files**. **Per-binary surface**: **sfnt2woff** (SFNT → WOFF1 zlib-based compressor) — 4 flags shared with the zopfli variant: `-v MAJOR.MINOR` font version, `-m metadata.xml` (not validated), `-p private.dat` private data block; positional input `*.otf/*.ttf` font. **woff2sfnt** (WOFF1 → SFNT decompressor or single-block extractor) — 3 mutually-exclusive extract modes (only one of `-v`/`-m`/`-p` may be used at a time, source-verified): `-v` write font version to stdout, `-m` write WOFF metadata block to stdout, `-p` write private data block to stdout; positional input `*.woff` file. **Hunt-skip log this round**: 4 of 6 originally-targeted WOFF utilities turned out to be **already covered** with case-sensitivity-aware existing coverage — woff2_compress (covered as part of `_ttf2woff2` multi-stem), woff2_decompress (covered as `_woff2_decompress`), sfnt2woff-zopfli (covered as part of `_sfnt2woff-zopfli` multi-stem), woff2sfnt-zopfli (covered as part of `_sfnt2woff-zopfli` multi-stem); this round adds the only truly fresh 2 (the **original non-zopfli Mozilla** variants). Reaffirms the **scan-by-exact-binary-name discipline** from earlier rounds (snarkos/near-cli-rs/czkawka_cli/featureCounts/etc.). cu2qu/opentype-feature-freezer/ufo2ft/ufoLib2 (libraries or small surfaces; deferred), fttimer/ftrandom/ftpatchk (R113 ft2demos remaining deferrals; still pending). **Blacklist additions (2)**: 1 between `sfltool` (23955) and `sfnt2woff-zopfli` (23957) for `sfnt2woff`; 1 between `woff2_info` (28718) and `woff2sfnt-zopfli` (28720) for `woff2sfnt`. - **Source Foundry UFO tools R115 — ufodiff + ufolint (2-stem multi-binary; UFO source diff + linter)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/source-foundry/ufodiff](https://github.com/source-foundry/ufodiff) and [github.com/source-foundry/ufolint](https://github.com/source-foundry/ufolint) via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_ufodiff` is `#compdef ufodiff ufolint`. **Coverage**: 2-stem multi-binary harvest covering Source Foundry's UFO (Unified Font Object) source-management CLIs — sister tools to the R111 fontmake and R114 fontbakery harvests. **Per-binary surface**: **ufodiff** (UFO source file diff for typeface git workflows; supports UFO versions 2 and 3) — state-machine with **5 subcommands**: `delta` (plain text with `[A]` added / `[D]` deleted / `[M]` modified indicators), `deltajson` (JSON output), `deltamd` (GitHub-flavored Markdown), `diff` (ANSI-colored text diff), `diffnc` (uncolored text diff for non-terminal output). Common syntax pattern: `ufodiff <sub> [all] [commits:N | branch:NAME] [optional UFO filter]` — the **scope-selector positional** uses a colon-separated mini-DSL (`commits:5` for last-5 commits, `branch:master` for branch comparison, `all` for all commits). Plus optional UFO file filter as 2nd positional. **ufolint** (UFO source linter for CI — exits 0 on pass, 1 on any failure) — single-binary CLI with no subcommands; positional `*.ufo` directory paths. Performs static analysis covering: **UFO version compatibility**, **mandatory files and directories**, **file path naming conventions**, **XML file validity**, **plist formatting and values**, **GLIF file format compliance**, **PNG image specifications** (UFO v3+), **library import compatibility**. **Hunt-skip log this round**: cu2qu/cu2qu-cli (cubic-to-quadratic curve converter — likely small surface or library-level), opentype-feature-freezer (small utility), ufo2ft (library not a CLI), ufoLib2/ufolib2 (libraries), woff2_compress/woff2_decompress (separate WOFF2 conversion utilities; deferred), sfnt2woff/woff2sfnt (WOFF1 conversion utilities; deferred), fttimer/ftrandom/ftpatchk (R113 ft2demos remaining deferrals — also still pending). **Blacklist additions (2)**: 2 between `uflow-bpfcc` (27251) and `ufraw` (27254) for `ufodiff`/`ufolint`. - **fontbakery R114 — fontbakery (1 stem; fontTools font-QA tool)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Single-stem file `_fontbakery` is `#compdef fontbakery`. **Coverage**: fontbakery is the fontTools font QA tool that runs check suites against font binaries with multiple foundry-specific profiles. State-machine with **12 top-level subcommands**: `check-profile` (most flexible — runs an explicit profile module name) plus 11 foundry-specific shortcuts `check_<profile>` where the profile is one of an **11-value enum**: adobefonts/fontbureau/fontval (Microsoft FontVal wrapper)/fontwerk/googlefonts (most-used)/iso15008 (in-vehicle display)/microsoft/notofonts (Google Noto)/opentype (vendor-agnostic spec)/typenetwork/universal (foundry-agnostic). All `check_*` subcommands inherit the same **29-flag shared `_check_flags` helper**: `--configuration FILE` TOML/JSON config, repeatable `*-c/--checkid` whitelist, repeatable `*-x/--exclude-checkid` blacklist, `-v/--verbose` (shortcut for `-l PASS`), repeatable `*-l/--loglevel` with **8-value status enum** (DEBUG/INFO/WARN/PASS/SKIP/FAIL/ERROR/FATAL), `-m/--loglevel-messages`, `--succinct`, `-F/--full-lists`, `-S/--show-sections`, `-g/--gather-by` 4-value enum (font/family/glyph/iso15008_section), `-o/--order` 4-value enum (same), `-q/--quiet`, `-n/--no-progress`, `-C/--no-colors`, **theme toggle** `--dark-theme` xor `--light-theme`, `--timeout SEC`, `--skip-network` (offline mode), **4 report-format outputs** `--json FILE`/`--badges DIR`/`--ghmarkdown FILE`/`--html FILE`, `-J/--jobs N` (0=auto), `-j/--auto-jobs` (shortcut for `--jobs=0`), `-e/--error-code-on` exit-code-on-status. Top-level flags: `--list-subcommands`, `-L/--list-checks`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: ufodiff/ufolint/ufolib2 (separate UFO inspection/validation utilities; ufoLib2 is the Python library not a CLI), cu2qu/cu2qu-cli (cubic-to-quadratic curve converter — likely small surface or library-level), opentype-feature-freezer (small utility), fonttools-cli (fontTools umbrella project's CLI dispatcher — actual binaries are `ttx`/`pyftsubset`/`pyftmerge`/`pyftinspect`; ttx already covered, others deferred), sfnt2woff/woff2sfnt and woff2_compress/woff2_decompress (WOFF conversion utilities — separate vendor tools; deferred), ufo2ft (library not a CLI), fttimer/ftrandom/ftpatchk (R113 ft2demos remaining deferrals). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `font_cache` (8400) and `fontforge` (8402) for `fontbakery`. Single multi-stem file `_ftdump` is `#compdef ftdump ftstring ftdiff ftlint ftmulti`. **Coverage**: 5-stem multi-binary harvest **resolving the R112 ft2demos extras deferral**. Combined with R112's 4-stem `_ftview`, the FreeType ft2demos coverage is now **9 stems across 2 multi-stem files** (`_ftview` ftview/ftvalid/ftbench/ftgrid + `_ftdump` ftdump/ftstring/ftdiff/ftlint/ftmulti). **Per-binary surface**: **ftdump** (simple font dumper — print font file information relevant to FreeType) — 6 flags: `-c/-C` charmap coverage, `-n` SFNT name tables, `-p` TrueType programs, `-t` SFNT table list, `-u` emit UTF-8, `-v` version. **ftstring** (interactive string renderer) — 6 flags: `-d WxH[xD]` window dimensions (default 640x480x24), `-r` dpi (default 72), `-e` encoding 4-value enum (unic/symb/ADOB/ADBC), `-m` custom text, `-k` keystroke emulation (`q` = batch mode), `-v` version; positional point size + font file. **ftdiff** (display text in 3 columns with different hinting modes per column — useful for comparing hinting strategies) — 6 flags: `-w` window width default 640px, `-h` window height default 480px, `-r` dpi default 72, `-s` character size default 16pt, `-f UTF8_TEXT_FILE` custom content, `-v` version. **ftlint** (lint a font — check for FreeType loading issues across glyphs) — 5 flags: `-f HEX` FT_LOAD_XXX load flags (default 0), `-r` 5-value render-mode enum (0=normal/1=light/2=mono/3=LCD-horizontal/4=LCD-vertical), `-i I-J` glyph index range (default all), `-q` quiet mode, `-v` version; positional ppem + font file. **ftmulti** (interactive viewer for variation fonts — Multiple Masters / GX / OpenType variations) — 7 flags: `-w/-h` window dimensions, `-e` encoding 4-value enum, `-r` dpi, `-f` first glyph index, **`-d "axis1 axis2 ..."`** variation-font axis design coordinates (distinguishing this binary as the GX/MM-specific viewer vs ftgrid which has the same axis flag with implied `-n`), `-v` version. **Hunt-skip log this round**: ftchkwd (man page redirect chain via dyn.manpages.debian.org returns 404; deferred), ftmemchk (memory-check internal tool, deferred), fttimer (small timer demo, deferred), ftpatchk (patcher check, deferred), ftrandom (random font subset tester, deferred), ftvalid_internal (internal validator, not user-facing). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 2 between `ftdi_eeprom` (8657) and `ftdv` (8660) for `ftdiff`/`ftdump`; 2 between `ftgrid` (8663) and `ftp` (8666) for `ftlint`/`ftmulti`; 1 between `ftpd` (8668) and `ftvalid` (8670) for `ftstring`. - **FreeType ft2demos R112 — ftview/ftvalid/ftbench/ftgrid (4-stem multi-binary; FreeType demo CLIs)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream FreeType ft2demos man pages at [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/freetype2-demos/](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/freetype2-demos/) via WebFetch of `ft{view,valid,bench,grid}.1.en.html`. Single multi-stem file `_ftview` is `#compdef ftview ftvalid ftbench ftgrid`. **Coverage**: 4-stem multi-binary harvest of FreeType demo CLIs. **Resolves R111 deferral**. **Per-binary surface**: **ftview** (interactive glyph viewer over all glyphs of a font) — 9 flags: `-d WxH[xD]` window dimensions (default 640x480x24), `-r` dpi (default 72), `-f` first index, `-e` encoding 4-value enum (unic/symb/ADOB/ADBC), `-m` render text, `-l` startup rendering mode 0-6, `-L` LCD filter weights, `-p` preload, `-k` emulate keystrokes; positional point size + font file. **ftvalid** (OpenType layout table validator) — 6 flags: `-f` font index, `-t` 3-value validator enum (ot/gx/ckern), `-T` SFNT table tags (colon-separated) or ckern dialect (ms/apple), `-l` list layout tables, `-V` 3-value strictness enum (0=default/1=tight/2=paranoid), `-v` version. **ftbench** (benchmark common FreeType operations) — 12 flags incl. repeatable `*-b` test selector with **12-value enum** (a=load-glyphs/b=advance-widths/c=render-glyphs/d=get-glyphs/e=char-indices/f=iterate-CMap/g=open-face/h=embolden/i=stroke/j=bboxes/k=cboxes/l=open+load), `-c` max iterations (0=time-limited), `-t` time-limit sec (default 2), `-f` load flags hex, `-s` face size ppem (default 10), `-i I-J` glyph index range, `-r` 5-value render-mode enum (0=normal/1=light/2=mono/3=LCD-horizontal/4=LCD-vertical), `-H` CFF hinting engine, `-I` TrueType interpreter version, `-m` max cache size KiB (default 1024), `-p` preload, `-C` compare with cached. **ftgrid** (inspect glyphs on a grid; variable-font axis aware) — 8 flags: `-d WxH[xD]`, `-r` dpi, `-f` first index, `-e` encoding 4-value enum, **variable-font specific** `-a "axis1 axis2 ..."` axis coordinates (implies `-n`), `-n` don't display named instances of variation fonts, `-k` emulate keystrokes, `-v` version. **Hunt-skip log this round**: ftdump/ftstring/ftmemchk/ftlint/fttimer/ftdiff/ftchkwd/ftmulti (additional ft2demos binaries — these tools exist in the ft2demos package but were not in my candidate list this round; queued for separate ft2demos-completion round to capture the full set), font-info/ttf-tools (deferred — fontTools-bundled scripts), gulim2bdf/otf2bdf/mkfontscale (small font conversion utilities, deferred). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 1 between `ft2nfdump` (8655) and `ftdi_eeprom` (8656) for `ftbench`; 1 between `ftest` (8660) and `ftp` (8661) for `ftgrid`; 2 between `ftpd` (8664) and `fuck` (8666) for `ftvalid`/`ftview`. - **fontmake R111 — fontmake (1 stem; Google Fonts builder UFO/Glyphs/Designspace → OTF/TTF)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `Lib/fontmake/__main__.py` at [github.com/googlefonts/fontmake](https://github.com/googlefonts/fontmake) via `gh api` inspection of every `argparse add_argument` call. Single-stem file `_fontmake` is `#compdef fontmake`. **Coverage**: fontmake is the Google Fonts builder that converts UFO / Glyphs / Designspace source files into OTF/TTF binary fonts. **~50 flags** across multiple groups: **Input source** (mutually exclusive `-g/--glyphs-path FILE.glyphs` xor `-u/--ufo-paths FILE` repeatable xor `-m/--mm-designspace FILE.designspace`; plus positional input fallback), **Output format** `-o/--output` repeatable 5-value enum (otf/ttf/otf-cff2/variable/variable-cff2 — default otf+ttf), `--output-path FILE` single-binary, `--output-dir DIR`. **Interpolation/variable** `-i/--interpolate`, `--variable-fonts REGEX`, `--family-name`, `--round-instances`, `--designspace-path`, `--master-dir`, `--instance-dir`, `-M/--masters-as-instances`, `--no-write-skipexportglyphs`. **Validation/compat** `--validate-ufo`, `--check-compatibility` xor `--no-check-compatibility`. **Features** `--expand-features-to-instances`, `--fea-include-dir`, repeatable `*--feature-writer CLASS`, `--debug-feature-file`, `--no-variable-features`, `--mti-source`. **UFO output** `--save-ufo-as-zip`, `--ufo-structure` 3-value enum (package/zip/json), `--indent-json`. **Contour handling** `--keep-overlaps`, `--overlaps-backend` 2-value enum (booleanOperations/pathops), `--keep-direction`, `--ttf-curves` 3-value enum (cubic/mixed/quadratic), `-e/--conversion-error F` cubic-to-quadratic accuracy, `-f/--flatten-components`, `--drop-implied-oncurves`. **Hinting** `-a/--autohint OPTS` xor `-A/--no-autohint`. **CFF optimization** `--cff-round-tolerance`, `--optimize-cff` 3-value enum (NONE/NO_SUBROUTINIZE/SUBROUTINIZE), `--subroutinizer` 2-value enum (cffsubr/compreffor), `-s/--subroutinize` xor `-S/--no-subroutinize`. **Variation** `--no-optimize-gvar`. **Names** `--production-names` xor `--no-production-names`. **Metrics** `--auto-use-my-metrics` xor `--no-auto-use-my-metrics`. **Layout** `--interpolate-binary-layout DIR`. **Misc** repeatable `*--filter SPEC`, `--subset` xor `--no-subset`, `--timing`, `--verbose LEVEL` 5-value enum (DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR/CRITICAL), `--version`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: lakectl already covered (`more_src/_lakectl`), quickwit already covered (`more_src/_quickwit` 197 lines from 2026-05-17), ttf-tools/font-info/ftview/ftvalid/ftbench/ftgrid (FreeType-bundled CLIs; deferred — separate documentation), fontforge-cli/otf2bdf/gulim2bdf/mkfontscale (font conversion utilities; deferred), pdftk-java/pdftk-cli (pdftk variants — pdftk likely already covered), pdfjam-cli (pdfjam already covered), pdf-redact-tools/pdfshuffler/pdfresurrect (small utilities; deferred). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `fontinst` (8402) and `fontmatrix` (8403) for `fontmake`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 116th consecutive clean round.
- **Tantivy CLI R110 — tantivy (1 stem; Rust full-text search engine CLI)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `src/main.rs` at [github.com/quickwit-oss/tantivy-cli](https://github.com/quickwit-oss/tantivy-cli) via `gh api` inspection of every clap `Command::new(...)` + `Arg::new(...)` declaration. Single-stem file `_tantivy` is `#compdef tantivy`. **Coverage**: tantivy-cli is the command-line interface for the Tantivy full-text search engine — a Rust library by Quickwit (same project as the deferred `quickwit-cli` candidate). State-machine with **7 top-level subcommands**: **new** (create new index — interactive schema wizard; with `--infer-from-ndjson FILE` to auto-detect schema from a sample, `--sample-size N` default 10000 docs sampled, 0=no limit), **serve** (search server with web API; `--host` default localhost, `-p/--port` default 3000), **index** (index JSON docs from `-f/--file`; `-t/--num_threads` default num_cores-1, `-m/--memory_size` bytes default 1GB, `--forcemerge` xor `--nomerge` for end-of-indexing segment-merge control), **search** (`-q/--query` required, `-a/--agg JSON` aggregation request), **inspect** (read-only index introspection), **bench** (run benchmark; `-q/--queries FILE` required with one query per line, `-n/--num_repeat` default 1), **merge** (merge all segments of an index). All subcommands share `-i/--index DIR` as the index directory. **Hunt-skip log this round**: lakectl (lakeFS CLI — separate research; candidate showed up as covered in my scan but may be partial), quickwit (full Quickwit data warehouse CLI — separate research; deferred for dedicated round given Quickwit's larger subcommand tree), iceberg/meilisearch/typesense/zincsearch (separate full-text/data-warehouse CLIs deferred), mlrgo (Miller Go port — Miller is `mlr` already covered; `mlrgo` may be an unofficial Go reimpl), parquet-go (already covered as the `parquet-tools` Go variant in the existing `_parquet-tools`), csvkit suite (`in2csv`/`csvjoin`/`csvgrep`/`csvlook`/`csvstack`/`csvcut`/`csvstat`/`csvjson`/`csvpy`/`csvclean`/`csvformat`/`csvsort` — already covered via the existing csvkit-related completions; verified via repo grep). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `tanstack` (25774) and `tanzu` (25775) for `tantivy`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 115th consecutive clean round.
- **parquet-cli (`parq`) R109 — parq (1 stem; lightweight Python Parquet inspector)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream README at [github.com/chhantyal/parquet-cli](https://github.com/chhantyal/parquet-cli) via WebFetch. Single-stem file `_parq` is `#compdef parq`. **Coverage**: parq is the entry point binary of the `parquet-cli` PyPI package — a lightweight Python CLI for inspecting Apache Parquet files. **Disambiguation note**: this is **distinct from THREE other parquet CLIs** already covered: (1) the `parquet-tools` Python package by ktrueda/hangxie (covered as `more_src/_parquet-tools` from 2026-05-20), (2) the Apache Java `parquet-tools` from the parquet-mr project (separate documentation), (3) Apache parquet-rs's `cli` feature binaries `parquet-read`/`parquet-rewrite`/`parquet-concat`/`parquet-schema`/`parquet-fromcsv`/`parquet-rowcount`/`parquet-layout`/`parquet-index`/`parquet-show-bloom-filter` (covered as the multi-stem `more_src/_parquet-read` from R108). The 4 implementations are mutually incompatible and ship under distinct binary names; this round adds `parq` (the chhantyal/parquet-cli Python package's entry-point script). **5 flags** (tight surface): `-s/--schema` (show schema info), `--head N` (first N rows), `--tail N` (last N rows), `-c/--count` (row count only), `-h/--help`. One positional `.parquet` file. **Hunt-skip log this round**: parquet-go (separate Go CLI from xitongsys/parquet-go; deferred — different API), parquet-tools Apache Java (already noted), datafusion-cli (already covered as `more_src/_datafusion-cli` and also `more_src/_datafusion`), 17 other speculative `-rs`/`-cli`/`-go` suffix candidates (delta-rs/iceberg-rust-cli/tantivy-cli/quickwit-cli/lakefs-cli/typesense-cli/etc. — real binaries are `delta-rs` binaries delta-rs/delta/etc., `iceberg` Java/Python CLIs, `tantivy` if any standalone, `quickwit`, `lakectl` for lakefs, `typesense-cli` actually `typesense-shell` or HTTP API — disambiguation needed). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `paros` (19295) and `parqeye` (19296) for `parq`. - **Apache Arrow parquet-rs R108 — 9-stem (parquet-read/parquet-rewrite/parquet-concat/parquet-schema/parquet-fromcsv/parquet-rowcount/parquet-layout/parquet-index/parquet-show-bloom-filter)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `parquet/src/bin/*.rs` at [github.com/apache/arrow-rs](https://github.com/apache/arrow-rs) via `gh api` inspection of each clap-derive `#[derive(Parser)]` Args struct + `#[clap(...)]` macros and the local `CompressionArgs`/`EncodingArgs`/`WriterVersionArgs`/`RecordTerminator`/`BloomFilterPositionArgs`/`StatisticsEnabledArgs` ValueEnum definitions in `parquet-rewrite.rs` and `parquet-fromcsv.rs`. Single multi-stem file `_parquet-read` is `#compdef parquet-read parquet-rewrite parquet-concat parquet-schema parquet-fromcsv parquet-rowcount parquet-layout parquet-index parquet-show-bloom-filter`. **Coverage**: 9-stem multi-binary harvest of the Apache parquet-rs `cli` feature binaries (`cargo install parquet --features=cli`). **Per-binary surface**: **parquet-read** (binary file to read data from a Parquet file) — `-j/--json` JSON-lines output, positional file (or `-` for stdin) + optional row count. **parquet-rewrite** (read+write parquet with potentially different settings — the **richest binary in this multi-stem**, 22 flags) incl. **CompressionArgs 8-value enum** (none/snappy/gzip/lzo/brotli/lz4/zstd/lz4-raw), `--compression-level` for gzip/brotli/zstd, **EncodingArgs 9-value enum** (plain/plain-dictionary/rle/bit-packed/delta-binary-packed/delta-length-byte-array/delta-byte-array/rle-dictionary/byte-stream-split), `--dictionary-enabled`/`--dictionary-page-size-limit`, `--max-row-group-size`, `--data-page-row-count-limit`/`--data-page-size-limit`, `--statistics-truncate-length`/`--column-index-truncate-length`, `--write-page-header-statistics`/`--write-path-in-schema`, **Bloom filter 4-flag group** (`--bloom-filter-enabled`/`--bloom-filter-fpp`/`--bloom-filter-ndv`/`--bloom-filter-position` 2-value enum after-row-group/end), `--statistics-enabled` 3-value enum (none/chunk/page), `--writer-version` 2-value enum (1.0/2.0), `--key-value-metadata KEY=VALUE`. **parquet-concat** (concatenate ≥1 parquet files; output is first positional, input files are repeatable positionals). **parquet-schema** (print schema + metadata) — `-v/--verbose` for full metadata, positional file. **parquet-fromcsv** (CSV → Parquet — 15 flags incl. `-s/--schema FILE`, `-i/--input-file`, `-o/--output-file`, `-f/--input-format` 2-value enum (csv/tsv), `-b/--batch-size`, `--has-header`, `-d/--delimiter`, `-r/--record-terminator` 2-value enum (lf/crlf), `-e/--escape-char`, `-q/--quote-char`, `-D/--double-quote`, `-C/--csv-compression`, `-c/--parquet-compression` default snappy, `-w/--writer-version` 2-value enum, `-m/--max-row-group-size`. **parquet-rowcount** (return row count for ≥1 files; positional files). **parquet-layout** (print physical layout — row groups, column chunks, pages). **parquet-index** (print page index for a specific column — 2 positionals: file + column name). **parquet-show-bloom-filter** (read bloom-filter data; 3 positionals: file + column + values). **Hunt-skip log this round**: chdb-cli (ClickHouse-style fork of CHDB; deferred — separate research), arrow-cli (no `apache/arrow-cli` repo; the Arrow project does not ship a single CLI binary by that name), arrow-flight-sql (RPC layer, not a CLI), q-cli/qsv (deferred), parq/parquet-go (alternative parquet implementations in Python and Go; their CLIs may differ; deferred), parquet-tools (Apache's older Java-based parquet-tools; documentation diverges from parquet-rs; deferred), 30+ other speculative `-cli`/`-go`/`-rs` suffix candidates. **Blacklist additions (9)**: 9 between `parquet-cli` (19297) and `parquet-tools` (19298) for all parquet-* in LC_ALL=C order. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 113th consecutive clean round.

Surface transcribed directly from upstream NCBI sra-tools loaders at [github.com/ncbi/sra-tools/tree/master/tools/loaders](https://github.com/ncbi/sra-tools/tree/master/tools/loaders) via `gh api` inspection of `tools/loaders/bam-loader/bam-loader.c` `option_*` string-constant declarations. Single multi-stem file `_bam-loader` is `#compdef bam-loader cg-load fastq-loader pacbio-load latf-load sra-sort vdb-lock vdb-unlock`. **Coverage**: 8-stem multi-binary harvest **completing the R106 SRA toolkit loaders deferral**. Combined with R105 (`_prefetch` 10 stems) + R106 (`_kar` 9 stems), the NCBI SRA toolkit coverage is now **27 stems across 3 multi-stem files** all sharing the **`_common_kapp` helper** (7-value `-L/--log-level` enum + repeatable `-v` + `--option-file` + `-h/-V/-q`). **Per-binary surface**: **bam-loader** (SAM/BAM → VDB / SRA archive loader) — **37 flags** (largest single binary in this multi-stem) source-verified via `bam-loader.c`: `-i/--input` dir, `-o/--output`, `-t/--tmpfs`, `-k/--config`, `-q/--min-mapq`, `--qual-quant`, `--cache-size MB`, **sort-state triple** (`--force-sorted`/`--unsorted`/`--sorted`), `--max-err-count`/`--max-rec-count`, `--no-verify`/`--only-verify`, `--use-QUAL`, `--ref-filter`/`--ref-config`, `--edit-aligned-qual`, `-u/--unaligned`, `--accept-dups`/`--accept-nomatch`, `--nomatch-log`, `-m/--min-match`, `--header`, `--no-cs`/`--no-secondary`, repeatable `*--ref-file FASTA`, `--TI`, `--max-warn-dup-flag`, `--accept-hard-clip`, `--allow-multi-map`/`--allow-secondary`/`--defer-secondary`, `--spot-batch-size`/`--min-batch-size`, `--threads`, `--extra-logging`, `--telemetry`. **cg-load** (Complete Genomics → VDB loader) — 6 flags: `-i/-o/-m/-l/--no-secondary/--force`. **fastq-loader** (FASTQ → VDB loader) — 9 flags incl. `-o/--output`, `--quality` 33|64, `--max-err-count`, `--platform` **9-value enum** (454/Illumina/SOLiD/HelicosBioSciences/IonTorrent/CompleteGenomics/PacBio/Nanopore/Capillary), `--readLens`, `--useAndDiscardFile`, `--ignore-illumina-tags`, `--allow-duplicates`. **pacbio-load** (PacBio HDF5 → VDB loader) — 5 flags: `-o/-t/-f/-c/--with-progress`; positional `*.h5/*.bas.h5/*.bax.h5`. **latf-load** (Long Archive Tape Format loader) — 4 flags: `-o`, `--quality` 33|64, `--max-err-count`, `--platform` 3-value enum (454/Illumina/SOLiD). **sra-sort** (sort SRA archive by spot or alignment position) — 6 flags: `-o`, `--no-mt`, `--no-secondary`, `--force`, `--map-file`, `--column-pause MS`. **vdb-lock** / **vdb-unlock** (acquire/release write lock on VDB archive) — just `_common_kapp` + positional VDB archive path. **Hunt-skip log this round**: general-loader (internal protocol-stream loader, not direct user-facing — `tools/loaders/general-loader/protocol-parser.cpp` suggests an internal driver), sra-load (deprecated — superseded by bam-loader/fastq-loader/latf-load for source-specific paths), sharq (cloud-aware loader — has CLI11.hpp + heavy cloud-filesystem dependency; deferred for separate cloud-loader round), driver-tool (internal toolkit driver, not user-facing). **Blacklist additions (8)**: alphabetically distributed (`bam-loader`/`cg-load`/`fastq-loader`/`latf-load`/`pacbio-load`/`sra-sort`/`vdb-lock`/`vdb-unlock`). Single multi-stem file `_kar` is `#compdef kar abi-dump sff-dump illumina-dump srapath sra-pileup sra-info kdbmeta rcexplain`. **Coverage**: 9-stem multi-binary harvest **completing the R105 SRA toolkit deferral**.
