Shares the **`_common_kapp` helper** (7-value `-L/--log-level` enum + repeatable `-v` + `--option-file` + `-h/-V/-q`) extracted in R105. **Per-binary surface**: **kar** (KApp Archive — tar-like for VDB / SRA archives; verified via `kar+args.h` macros) — 11 flags: action triple `-c/--create=PATH` xor `-x/--extract=PATH` xor `-t/--test=PATH`, `-d/--directory` archive target, `-f/--force`, `-l/--long-list` (with -t), `-Z/--stdout`, `--md5` checksums, repeatable `*--keep=PAT`/`*--drop=PAT`, `--kdfile`. **abi-dump / sff-dump / illumina-dump** (format-specific SRA dumpers built from `tools/external/sra-dump` source — `sra-dump.c` + format modules `abi.c`/`sff.c`/`illumina.c`/`fastq.c`; share fastq-dump's spot/filter/output flags via the shared build setup) — 8 flags incl. `-A/--accession`, `-O/--outdir`, `-Z/--stdout`, `-N/-X` spot range, `--gzip/--bzip2`. **srapath** (resolve NCBI repository paths/URLs for SRA accessions) — 14 flags verified via `srapath.c` defines: `-a/--protocol` 5-value enum (http/https/fasp/gs/s3), `-c/--cache`, `--perm` permissions/cart, `-d/--project N`, `-e/--vers`, `-f/--function` 4-value enum (names/resolve/search/srapath), `--location`, `--ngc`, `-P/--path` path-style only, `-p/--param`, `-r/--raw`, `-j/--json`, `-t/--timeout SEC`, `-u/--url URL`. **sra-pileup** (samtools-style pileup directly from an SRA accession) — 12 flags: `-r/--aligned-region chr:from-to`, `-o/--outfile`, `-t/--table` 3-value enum (p/s/e for primary/secondary/evidence), `-q/--minmapq`, `-Q/--minbasequal`, `-d/--duplicates`, `--noskip`, `--showid`, `-e/--seqname`, `-n/--noqual`. **sra-info** (print SRA accession info — schema/columns/rows/sizes) — 8 flags: `-f/--format` 6-value enum (default/csv/xml/json/tab/piped), `-r/--rows`, `-l/--limit`, `--platform`, `--meta`, `--schema`, `--total`. **kdbmeta** (examine/dump VDB metadata nodes) — 5 flags: `-T/--table`, `-X/--output-format` 4-value enum (xml/json/csv/tab), `-A/--as-attributes`, `-U/--tree`, positional node path. **rcexplain** (explain ncbi-vdb / sra-tools return-code values — useful for debugging) — just `_common_kapp` + positional decimal return code. **Hunt-skip log this round**: bam-loader/cg-load/sra-load/sra-sort/fastq-loader/pacbio-load/general-loader/vdb-lock/vdb-unlock/sharq (9 loader binaries in `tools/loaders/`; deferred — heavier specialized surfaces best handled in a dedicated loader-focused round), ngs-pileup (legacy pileup variant — sra-pileup is the modern equivalent and is covered here), ref-variation (reference-variation utility — separate research), test-sra (test harness, not user-facing), driver-tool (internal toolkit driver). **Blacklist additions (9)**: alphabetically distributed. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 111th consecutive clean round.

Surface transcribed directly from upstream NCBI sra-tools docs + man pages at [github.com/ncbi/sra-tools/wiki/HowTo:-fasterq-dump](https://github.com/ncbi/sra-tools/wiki/HowTo:-fasterq-dump) and [edwards.flinders.edu.au/fastq-dump/](https://edwards.flinders.edu.au/fastq-dump/) via WebFetch + `gh api` inspection of `tools/external/prefetch/{PrfMain.h,prefetch.c}` for option-token constants (`CART_OPTION`/`NGC_OPTION`/`MINSZ_OPTION`/`SIZE_OPTION`/`OUT_FILE_OPTION`/`TEXTKART_OPTION`/`ELIM_QUALS_OPTION`). Single multi-stem file `_prefetch` is `#compdef prefetch fastq-dump fasterq-dump vdb-dump sam-dump sra-stat vdb-validate vdb-config vdb-encrypt vdb-decrypt`. **Coverage**: 10-stem multi-binary harvest covering the core NCBI Sequence Read Archive download/conversion/dump toolkit, resolving the R104 deferred "sra-toolkit umbrella" note. All binaries share the **common KApp-style flags** (`-h/-V/--help/--version`, `-L/--log-level` 7-value enum fatal/sys/int/err/warn/info/debug, `-v/--verbose` repeatable, `-q/--quiet`, `--option-file`, `--no-user-settings`) — extracted into a `_common_kapp` helper. **Per-binary surface**: **prefetch** (retrieve SRA accessions/kart files/URLs) — 17 flags incl. `--cart`/`--text-kart` kart input, `--ngc` NGC credentials, `-o/-O` output file/dir, `--max-size`/`--min-size` filters, `--force` 4-value enum (no/yes/all/ALL), `-r/--resume`, `-C/--verify`, `-t/--transport` 3-value enum (http/fasp/both), `-l/--location` cloud, `--type` 3-value enum (sra/refseq/all), `--eliminate-quals`, `--ascp-options`, `-p/--progress`, `-c/--check-all`, `--check-rs`. **fastq-dump** (legacy SRA → FASTQ; superseded by fasterq-dump) — 22 flags incl. split modes (`--split-spot/--split-files/--split-3`), FASTA output, color-space toggle (`-B/-C`), read IDs (`-I/-F`), Phred offset (`-Q`), filters (`--skip-technical/--read-filter` 4-enum pass/reject/criteria/redacted, `-W/--clip`, `-M/-N/-X` minRead/minSpot/maxSpot, `--aligned/--unaligned`), output (`-O/-Z/--gzip/--bzip2`). **fasterq-dump** (multi-threaded SRA → FASTQ replacement) — 22 flags incl. `-O/-o/-t` output/temp paths, `-f` force, `-e N` threads (default 6), `-b/-c/-m` buffer/cursor/memory sizes, split modes including `--concatenate-reads`, `--fasta`/`--fasta-unsorted`/`--fasta-ref-tbl`, `--seq-defline`/`--qual-defline` custom formats, `--ngc`, `--size-check` 3-value enum (on/off/only), `--disk-limit`/`--disk-limit-tmp`, `--table SEQUENCE` for PacBio. **vdb-dump** (dump VDB / SRA contents in textual form) — 12 flags incl. `-T/--table`, `-C/--columns`, `-R/--rows`, `-f/--format` **14-value enum** (csv/xml/json/piped/tab/fastq/fastq1/fasta/fasta1/fasta2/qual/qual1/hidden/tab1), `-d/--boolean` 2-enum (1/T), `-A/--schema_dump`, `-E/--table_enum`, `-O/--column_enum`. **sam-dump** (dump aligned SRA → SAM/BAM) — 15 flags incl. `-u/--unaligned`, `-1/--primary`, `-c/--cigar-long`, `-r/--header` 2-enum (add/no), `--header-file`/`--header-comment`, `-n/--no-header`, `-s/--seqid`, `-=/--hide-identical`, `--aligned-region chr:from-to`, `--unaligned-spots-only`. **sra-stat** (SRA statistics) — 8 flags incl. `-x/--xml`, `-r/--start`, `-N/--rows`, `-s/--statistics`, `-d/--member-stats`, `-b/--bases`, `-m/--meta`, `-p/--progress`. **vdb-validate** (validate SRA structure) — 6 flags incl. `-Q/--table`, `-x/--exhaustive`, `-r/--referential-integrity`, `-B/--consist-check`, `-I/--index`, `-C/--md5-check`. **vdb-config** (configure SRA toolkit) — 11 flags incl. `--cfg`, `-a/--all`, `-p/--proxy URL`, `--proxy-disable`, `--set KEY=VALUE`, `-w/--wd`, `--restore-defaults`, `--root`, `--cfg-dir`, `--fix-default`, `--cloud-provider` 3-value enum (aws/gcp/azure), `-i/--import-ngc`. **vdb-encrypt** / **vdb-decrypt** (legacy NGC-style file encryption) — `-d/-f/-p/-q` for encrypt; `-f/-p` for decrypt. **Hunt-skip log this round**: abi-dump/sff-dump/illumina-dump (legacy format-specific dumpers — less commonly used in modern workflows; deferred for completeness round), kar (KApp Archive utility), align-info (verify alignment data), bam-load (load BAM into VDB), cache-mgr, cg-load (Complete Genomics loader), latf-load (Long Archive Tape Format loader), sra-search (search SRA across columns), test-sra (test harness, not user-facing). **Blacklist additions (10)**: alphabetically distributed across `fast*`/`pref*`/`sam*`/`sra*`/`vdb*` ranges. - **STAR R104 — STARlong stem expansion to existing `_STAR`** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). **NOT a new file** — the existing `more_src/_STAR` (211 lines from yamaton/zsh-completions-bio h2o-generation on 2026-05-21) had its `#compdef` line extended from 1 → 2 stems (`STAR` → `STAR STARlong`). **Coverage**: STARlong is the long-read variant built from the same `alexdobin/STAR` source via `cmake -DSTARlong=ON ./...`; the only delta from STAR proper is higher MAX_N_INSERT_LEN / MAX_N_PIECES constants for handling longer reads (Nanopore/PacBio). The full STAR aligner CLI surface (gen-index/run-mode/twopass/junction filters/output BAM/genomeSAindex/sjdbGTFfile/etc.) is identical between the two binaries, so stem-sharing is the cleanest representation. **Hunt-skip log this round**: bcl2fastq + bcl2fastq2 (Illumina BCL → FASTQ demuxer; primary documentation is gated behind a User Guide PDF on Illumina's support portal — the Debian man page redirect chain returns 404 via `dyn.manpages.debian.org`; deferred until canonical source is located), BCL2FASTQ (uppercase typo — real binary is lowercase), star-fusion (lowercase variant — the canonical binary is camelCase `STAR-Fusion` already covered as `more_src/_STAR-Fusion`), spaceranger (10x Genomics paid product — `support.10xgenomics.com` returns 403; deferred), sailfish (deprecated by Salmon as of 2017; both `salmon` and the predecessor are at the same upstream — Salmon already covered as `more_src/_salmon`; sailfish kept fresh for archaeological completeness but deferred), nirvana (Illumina variant annotator — separate docs needed), fastqsplitter (small utility), sra-toolkit (umbrella package; actual binaries are `prefetch`/`fastq-dump`/`fasterq-dump`/`sam-dump`/`vdb-dump`/etc. — need to be verified individually). **Verified already covered**: STAR (`more_src/_STAR`), STAR-Fusion (`more_src/_STAR-Fusion`), salmon (`more_src/_salmon`), kallisto (`more_src/_kallisto`), bedops (`more_src/_bedops`), bedtools (`more_src/_bedtools`), picard (`more_src/_picard`), picard-tools (`more_src/_picard-tools`), gatk (`more_src/_gatk`), tophat2 (`more_src/_tophat2`), cellranger (`more_src/_cellranger`). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `STAR-Fusion` (161) and `SWarp` (162) for `STARlong`. (Corpus FILE count unchanged at 28,461 because this round extended an existing file's `#compdef` line rather than creating a new file; STEM coverage of `_STAR` is now 2 binaries.)
- **Subread utility helpers R103 — propmapped/flattenGTF/removeDup/repair (4-stem multi-binary; auxiliary Subread tools)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream Subread package man pages at [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/subread/](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/subread/) via WebFetch of `{propmapped,flattenGTF,removeDup,repair}.1.en.html`. Single multi-stem file `_propmapped` is `#compdef propmapped flattenGTF removeDup repair`. **Coverage**: 4-stem multi-binary harvest **completing the R102 deferral** on small Subread auxiliary binaries. Sister tools to the R102-covered aligners (`subread-align`/`subjunc`/`subread-buildindex`/`exactSNP`/`sublong` via `_subread-align`) and the existing `_featureCounts` (the read-counter from this same package). **Per-binary surface**: **propmapped** (calculate the proportion of mapped reads/fragments in a SAM/BAM file) — 4 flags: `-i FILE` (required SAM/BAM), `-o FILE` (stats output), `-f` (count fragments instead of reads, paired-end only), `-p` (only properly paired reads, paired-end only). **flattenGTF** (flatten a GTF/GFF annotation by merging overlapping exons of the same gene into a non-overlapping set; used as a featureCounts preprocessing step) — 5 flags: `-a FILE` (required input annotation), `-o FILE` (required output), `-t TYPE` (feature type default `exon`), `-g ATTR` (group-by attribute default `gene_id`), `-C` (alternative mode that merges into multiple non-overlapping exons but keeps all edges instead of collapsing them — useful for exon-level analyses). **removeDup** (remove duplicate alignments by location-frequency threshold — different from samtools markdup; targets PCR/optical duplicates by counting reads mapping to identical locations) — 5 flags: `-i/-o` required, `-S` SAM output (BAM default), `-r N` duplication threshold (default 10; locations with ≥N hits removed), `-t DIR` temp directory. **repair** (pair-up reads from the same pair so they are adjacent in the output; for downstream featureCounts paired-end analysis after a coordinate-sorted BAM is needed) — 7 flags: `-i/-o` required, `-S` SAM input, `-c` compress output BAM (slower read access), `-T N` threads (default **8** — higher than most Subread tools' default 1), `-d` skip dummy reads for singletons, `-t` omit sequences and quality scores from output. **Hunt-skip log this round**: subindel man page still not located via Debian manpages (the binary exists in Bioconductor Rsubread but not the apt-shipped subread package); subread NOT a real binary (the package name; actual aligner is subread-align — re-noted from R102). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 1 between `prophet` (20977) and `proselint` (20979) for `propmapped`; 1 between `flatpak-spawn` (8273) and `flawfinder` (8275) for `flattenGTF`; 1 between `remove-duplicates` (22470) and `removeSpaces.sh` (22472) for `removeDup`; 1 between `reordercap` (22490) and `repairHomePermissions` (22492) for `repair`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 108th consecutive clean round. The Subread / featureCounts / Bowtie2 / HISAT2 / RAxML / BEAST 2 / MMseqs2 / HTSeq bioinformatics ecosystem coverage is now ~50 binaries spanning 8 multi-stem files + 5 individual files.
- **Subread suite R102 — 5-stem (subread-align/subread-buildindex/subjunc/exactSNP/sublong; RNA-seq + DNA-seq + SNP-calling alignment toolkit)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream Subread package man pages at [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/subread/](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/subread/) via WebFetch of `{subread-align,subread-buildindex,subjunc,exactSNP,sublong}.1.en.html`. Single multi-stem file `_subread-align` is `#compdef subread-align subread-buildindex subjunc exactSNP sublong`. **Coverage**: 5-stem multi-binary harvest of the Subread read alignment / SNP-calling / index-building toolkit. Existing coverage: `featureCounts` (the read-counter sister tool from this same package, `more_src/_featureCounts` since 2026-05-17). Subread-align and subjunc share substantial flag patterns since subjunc is a junction-aware extension of subread-align — captured via a 33-entry `_shared_align_flags` helper array. **Per-binary surface**: **subread-align** (main Subread aligner for both RNA-seq and DNA-seq) — shared aligner flags + `-t` 2-value data-type enum (0=RNA-seq/1=genomic DNA-seq), Dynamic Programming alignment tuning (`--DPGapOpen`/`--DPGapExt`/`--DPMismatch`/`--DPMatch`), `--sv` structural-variant detection. **subjunc** (junction-aware variant for RNA-seq with exon-exon junction + fusion detection) — shared aligner flags + `--allJunctions` for both junctions+fusions; subjunc's `-n` default is 14 (vs 10 for subread-align) and `-m` default is 1 (vs 3). **subread-buildindex** (build a Subread index from FASTA) — `-o BASENAME` (required), `-F` full index (16bp subreads from every position), `-B` one-block index for max mapping speed, `-M MB` memory (default 8000), `-f N` uninformative-subread threshold (default 100), `-c` color-space index. **exactSNP** (SNP caller from SAM/BAM → VCF) — `-i/-g/-o` required, `-a` annotated SNPs for improved calling, `-b` BAM input, `-Q N` Q-value cutoff (default 12), `-f F` min mismatch fraction, `-n/-r/-x/-s/-t` mapping/coverage/depth/quality/trim thresholds, `-C` temp-dir, `-T` threads. **sublong** (long-read aligner for Nanopore/PacBio) — index must be a full+single-block index (i.e. built with `subread-buildindex -F -B`); just 7 flags: `-i/-r/-o` required, `--SAMoutput`, `-T` threads, `-X` RNA-seq mode, `-v` version. **Shared `_shared_align_flags` helper** captures: index/reads/output (`-i/-r/-o/-R`), I/O format toggles (`--SAMinput/--BAMinput/--SAMoutput`), quality/Phred (`-P` 33|64), mapping thresholds (`-n/-m/-p/-M`), multi-mapping (`--multiMapping/-B`), indel detection (`-I/--complexIndels`), read trimming (`--trim5/--trim3`), paired-end config (`-d/-D/-S` ff|fr|rf), performance (`-T`), read-group (`--rg-id/--rg`), output ordering (`--keepReadOrder/--sortReadsByCoordinates`), color-space (`-b`), and annotation flags (`-a/-F/-A/--gtfFeature/--gtfAttr`). **Hunt-skip log this round**: subindel (man page not located at manpages.debian.org subread path; deferred — likely shares subread-align flag patterns), propmapped/flattenGTF/removeDup/repair (4 small Subread utility binaries — verified as present in subread package but minimal/undocumented flag surfaces; deferred for a future low-priority round). subread (NOT a real binary — the package name is "subread" but the actual aligner binary is "subread-align"; verified by upstream man pages). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 2 between `subnetcalc` (25256) and `subrepo` (25259) for `subread-align`/`subread-buildindex`; 1 between `subfinder` (25248) and `subl` (25250) for `subjunc`; 1 between `exabgp` (7668) and `exaile` (7670) for `exactSNP`; 1 between `subliminal` (25254) and `subnetcalc` (25256) for `sublong`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 107th consecutive clean round.
- **BEAST 2 R101 — 14-stem Bayesian phylogenetic family (beast/beauti/treeannotator/logcombiner/densitree/applauncher/packagemanager + `2` parallel-install variants)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `src/beast/app/beastapp/BeastMain.java` at [github.com/CompEvol/BEAST.app](https://github.com/CompEvol/BEAST.app) plus GUI helpers at [github.com/CompEvol/BeastFX](https://github.com/CompEvol/BeastFX) via `gh api` inspection of the `new Arguments.Option[]` array. Single multi-stem file `_beast` is `#compdef beast beast2 beauti beauti2 treeannotator treeannotator2 logcombiner logcombiner2 densitree densitree2 applauncher applauncher2 packagemanager packagemanager2`. **Coverage**: 14-stem multi-binary harvest of BEAST 2's Bayesian phylogenetic family. The `2` suffix variants exist because some installers ship both `beast` and `beast2` (or `treeannotator`/`treeannotator2`) as parallel binaries to coexist with BEAST 1.x; both variants share the same CLI surface per stem. **Per-binary surface**: **beast / beast2** (main MCMC engine — read XML, run MCMC, write log/tree files; uses jam Arguments parser with **single-dash long options**) — 29 flags incl. `-window` GUI console, `-options` options dialog, `-working` chdir, `-seed N`, `-prefix`, `-statefile`, `-overwrite` xor `-resume` xor `-validate`, `-threads N` (-1 = all cores), `-instances N` (divide site patterns), `-java` (no native), `-noerr`, `-loglevel` 5-value enum (error/warning/info/debug/trace), **BEAGLE library integration** (`-beagle`, `-beagle_info`, `-beagle_order`, `-beagle_CPU`/`_GPU`/`_SSE`, `-beagle_single`/`_double` precision, `-beagle_scaling` 4-value enum default/none/dynamic/always), `-strictversions`, `*-D` XML attribute substitution (repeatable, e.g. `-D "arg1=10,arg2=20"`), `-DF FILE` substitution-pairs file, `-DFout FILE` output XML, `-sampleFromPrior`. **beauti / beauti2** (BEAST 2 XML builder GUI; CLI side accepts `-template`/`-prefix`/`-out`/`-create` headless mode + positional NEXUS/FASTA input). **treeannotator / treeannotator2** (summarize tree set → MCC tree) — `-burnin PCT` or `-burninTrees N`, `-limit P` posterior probability limit, `-heights` 4-value enum (keep/median/mean/ca = Common Ancestor heights), `-target FILE` user target tree, 2 positionals (input trees + output annotated tree). **logcombiner / logcombiner2** (combine multiple BEAST trace/tree files) — repeatable `*-log`/`*-burnin`, `-resample N`, `-renumber`, `-decimal`, `-scale FACTOR`, `-strip` annotations, `-o FILE`. **densitree / densitree2** (visualizer overlaying all posterior trees) — `-fontfile`, `-out FILE` (PNG/SVG/PDF/EPS), `-width PX`, `-height PX`. **applauncher / applauncher2** (invoke BEAST 2 package-installed sub-apps; each package can register tools that show up here) — `-help` lists installed apps, then positional app name + args. **packagemanager / packagemanager2** (install/update/list/remove BEAST 2 packages a.k.a. BEAUti add-ons) — `-list`, `-add NAME`, `-del NAME`, `-update`, `-useAppDir`, `-dir`, `-version`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: tracer (BEAST 1 family GUI — separate non-CompEvol upstream; deferred), figtree (BEAST 1 figure tool from Rambaut lab; deferred), beastlabs (BEAST 2 dependency package not a separate binary), beast-mcmc (alias/variant — `beast` is the canonical name). **Blacklist additions (14)**: alphabetically distributed across the `applauncher`/`beast`/`densitree`/`logcombiner`/`packagemanager`/`treeannotator` ranges + their `*2` parallel variants. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 106th consecutive clean round.
- **Classic RAxML R100 MILESTONE — raxml/raxmlHPC + 5 build variants (7-stem multi-binary; classic phylogenetic ML tree inference)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `raxmlHPC(1)` man page at [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/raxml/raxmlHPC.1.en.html](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/raxml/raxmlHPC.1.en.html) via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_raxml` is `#compdef raxml raxmlHPC raxmlHPC-PTHREADS raxmlHPC-MPI raxmlHPC-PTHREADS-SSE3 raxmlHPC-PTHREADS-AVX raxmlHPC-PTHREADS-AVX2`. **Coverage**: 7-stem multi-binary harvest of the classic (pre-NG) RAxML family. All seven binaries share the same CLI surface; the suffixes denote build variants (PTHREADS for OpenMP, MPI for cluster, SSE3/AVX/AVX2 for SIMD). The successor `raxml-ng` (modern Kozlov rewrite) is covered separately in `more_src/_raxml-ng` (already covered from 2026-05-20). Classic RAxML uses **single-dash legacy options** (`-s/-n/-m/-p/-f/-N/-T` etc., no double-dash for most). Captured **~50 flags** plus 2 unusually-rich enum sets: **`-f` algorithm action selector** (41-value enum: a=rapid-bootstrap+ML/A=pairwise-distances/b=bootstop-convergence/B=optimal-branch-lengths/c=taxon-name-consistency/C=ancestral-state-recon/d=DEFAULT-rapid-hill-climbing/D=RF-distance/e=optimize-model-params/E=experimental/F=fast-search/g=per-site-likelihoods-for-CONSEL/G=improved-per-site/h=help/H=thorough-model-opt/i=likelihood-vector-tests/I=autoMR-placement-test/j=bootstrap-MSA-files/J=majority-rule-consensus/k=bootstrap-bipartition-info/m=compare-bipartitions/M=multiple-tree-ML/n=user-tree-loglh/N=consensus-of-bootstrap-trees/o=old-rapid-hill-climbing/p=pure-stepwise-MP-addition/P=EPA-placement/q=fast-quartets/r=RF-distance/R=binary-tree-to-Newick/s=split-alignments/S=ancestral-states/t=GAMMA-final-opt/T=GAMMA-final-opt-with-X/u=morpho+molecular-co-estimation/U=gene-tree-estimation/v=environmental-sequence-classify/V=matrix-decomp-viz/w=ELW-test/W=SH-aLRT/x=rapid-bootstrap/y=parsimony-starting-tree-only) and **`-m` substitution model** (25-value enum spanning GTRCAT/GTRCATI/GTRGAMMA/GTRGAMMAI/BINCAT/BINGAMMA for nucl/binary, **12 PROT{CAT,GAMMA}\<matrix\>** variants for proteins (DAYHOFF/WAG/BLOSUM62/JTT/LG/MTREV/MTART/MTZOA/GTR/AUTO with both CAT and GAMMA rate-heterogeneity flavors), MULTICAT/MULTIGAMMA for multi-state). Other rich groups: **bootstrap** (`-x`/`-b`/`-#` synonym for `-N`), **search control** (`-i`/`-e`/`-c`/`-D`/`-F`/`-H`), **constraint+starting trees** (`-r`/`-g`/`-t`/`-R`), **partition handling** (`-q`/`-M`), **placement (EPA)** (`-P` + `--epa-keep-placements`/`--epa-accumulated-threshold`/`--epa-prob-threshold`), **majority-rule** (`-J MR|MRE|STRICT`), **ascertainment correction** (`--asc-corr lewis|stamatakis|felsenstein`), specialized models (`--JC69`/`--K80`/`--HKY85`), output (`-w` full-path output directory, `-o` outgroup name or comma-list), parallel (`-T` PTHREADS), miscellaneous (`-X` half-memory for RF, `-O` skip-duplicate-seq check, `-V` no-rate-heterogeneity, `--no-bfgs`, `--no-seq-check`, `--mesquite`, `--silent`). **Hunt-skip log this round**: raxml-ng (FAILSAFE CATCH — `more_src/_raxml-ng` exists from 2026-05-20 with 38 flags; my 79-flag re-extraction was discarded; the existing covers `raxml-ng` only, not `raxml-ng-mpi` which is built from the same source via `-DUSE_MPI=ON`; deferred as a small refresh-round opportunity), 4 remaining bioinformatics queue items (subread/beast2/star-fusion/bcl2fastq2). **Blacklist additions (7)**: 1 between `rax2` (22080) and `raxml-ng` (22081) for `raxml`; 6 between `raxml-ng` (22082) and `ray` (22089) for raxmlHPC + 5 build variants in LC_ALL=C order (raxmlHPC < raxmlHPC-MPI < raxmlHPC-PTHREADS < raxmlHPC-PTHREADS-AVX < raxmlHPC-PTHREADS-AVX2 (empty <`2` at index 19) < raxmlHPC-PTHREADS-SSE3). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `src/MMseqsBase.cpp` command registry at [github.com/soedinglab/MMseqs2](https://github.com/soedinglab/MMseqs2) via `gh api` inspection — full 121-subcommand registry confirmed. Single multi-stem file `_mmseqs` is `#compdef mmseqs mmseqs2` (the multi-stem registers both `mmseqs` as the canonical binary name and `mmseqs2` as the alternate package name for Homebrew/apt installs). **Coverage**: MMseqs2 is the Many-against-Many sequence searching toolkit — ultra-fast, sensitive protein sequence search + clustering across huge data sets. State-machine with **all 121 verified subcommands** organized into 9 logical groups via the `_easy_subs`/`_wf_subs`/`_db_subs`/`_alignment_subs`/`_profile_subs`/`_taxo_subs`/`_format_subs`/`_set_subs`/`_util_subs` helper arrays (concatenated into a single `_all` list for `_describe`). Group breakdown: **Easy workflows** (8 — easy-search/easy-linsearch/easy-cluster/easy-linclust/easy-rbh/easy-taxonomy/easy-proteomecluster/easy-proteomesearch — FASTA in, results out), **Core workflows** (8 — search/linsearch/cluster/linclust/rbh/taxonomy/multihitsearch/proteomecluster), **Database mgmt** (30 — createdb/createindex/createlinindex/createtaxdb/createdmptaxonomy/makepaddedseqdb (GPU-compatible)/databases (download public refs)/createsubdb/createbintaxonomy/createbintaxmapping/createseqfiledb/createclusearchdb/concatdbs/splitdb/mergedbs/subtractdbs/aliasdb/lndb/cpdb/mvdb/rmdb/compress/decompress/dbtype/diffseqdbs/view/touchdb/unpackdb/indexdb/appenddbtoindex/kmerindexdb), **Alignment** (15 — prefilter/align/alignall/alignbykmer/ungappedprefilter/gappedprefilter/kmermatcher/kmersearch/lcaalign/rescorediagonal/transitivealign/pairaln/expandaln/fwbw forward-backward/extractalignedregion), **Profile/format** (7 — convertprofiledb/convertmsa/convertblastdb/convertalis (→BLAST-like/SAM)/convertkb/createtsv/parseproteomealignments), **Taxonomy** (9 — taxonomyreport/addtaxonomy/aggregatetax/aggregatetaxweights/lca/majoritylca/filtertaxdb/filtertaxseqdb/nrtotaxmapping), **Sequence format** (15 — extractorfs/extractframes/extractdomains/translatenucs/translateaa/reverseseq/masksequence/maskbygff/recoverlongestorf/orftocontig/splitsequence/summarizealis/summarizeheaders/summarizeresult/summarizetabs), **Set operations** (9 — besthitperset/combinepvalperset/enrich/filterdb/filterresult/mergeresultsbyset/mergeclusters/sortresult/offsetalignment), **Utility** (19 — apply/calculatelambda/clust/clusterupdate/clusthash/countkmer/diskspaceavail/gpuserver/multihitdb/pickconsensusrep/prefixid/suffixid/renamedbkeys/setextendeddbtype/swapdb/swapresults/selection/map/version). **Common per-subcommand args** (covered via the args fallthrough state): `--threads N`, `--compressed 0|1`, `-v 0-3` (verbosity), `--remove-tmp-files`, `--force-reuse`, `--mpi-runner` (e.g. `mpirun -np 4`), plus positional DB/FASTA/dir paths. **Hunt-skip log this round**: subread/raxml/beast2/star-fusion/bcl2fastq2/featurecounts (camelCase variant featureCounts already covered — caught last round; featurecounts NOT a real binary) — 4 remaining bioinformatics queue candidates for future rounds. **Blacklist additions (2)**: 2 between `mmroff` (16603) and `mmstat` (16604) for - **HTSeq htseq-count R98 — htseq-count (1 stem; Python HTSeq RNA-seq read counter)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream HTSeq documentation at [htseq.readthedocs.io/en/master/htseqcount.html](https://htseq.readthedocs.io/en/master/htseqcount.html) via WebFetch. Single-stem file `_htseq-count` is `#compdef htseq-count`. **Coverage**: htseq-count is the Python HTSeq RNA-seq read counter — sister tool to **featureCounts** (Subread package, already covered in `more_src/_featureCounts` as `#compdef featureCounts`). Assigns sequencing reads in a SAM/BAM file to genomic features defined in a GTF/GFF annotation. **22 flags** across groups: **I/O** `-f/--format` 2-value enum (sam/bam, with note that modern samtools/htslibs auto-detect), `-o/--samout FILE` (output SAM with feature assignments), `-p/--samout-format` 2-value enum (sam/bam), `-c/--counts_output FILE` (tsv/csv/mtx/h5ad/loom multi-format output), `--counts-output-sparse` (store as sparse matrix for mtx/h5ad/loom). **Sort handling** `-r/--order` 2-value enum (name/pos) + `--max-reads-in-buffer N` (default 30M for pos-sorted). **Strand/quality** `-s/--stranded` 3-value enum (yes/no/reverse, default yes), `-a/--a N` (min MAPQ default 10). **Feature matching** `-t/--type` (default exon), `-i/--idattr` (default gene_id), repeatable `*--additional-attr`, `--add-chromosome-info`, `--feature-query EXPR` (with documented example `gene_name == "ACTB"`). **Counting modes** `-m/--mode` 3-value enum (union/intersection-strict/intersection-nonempty, default union), `--nonunique` 4-value enum (none/all/fraction/random), `--secondary-alignments` 2-value enum (score/ignore), `--supplementary-alignments` 2-value enum (score/ignore). **Performance** `-n/--nprocesses N`, `-q/--quiet`. Plus 2 positionals: BAM/SAM alignment file + GTF/GFF annotation file. **Hunt-skip log this round**: featureCounts (FAILSAFE CATCH — my candidate-list scan used lowercase `featurecounts` but the actual binary is camelCase `featureCounts`; existing `more_src/_featureCounts` from 2026-05-17 caught the duplicate-write at the Write-tool level; reaffirms the case-sensitivity discipline from earlier rounds), 8 other R97-deferred bioinformatics candidates remaining (subread/raxml/mmseqs2/mmseqs/beast2/star-fusion/bcl2fastq2/iqtree2; iqtree2 separately confirmed already covered at `more_src/_iqtree2`). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `htpasswd2` (11358) and `htsserver` (11359) for `htseq-count`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 103rd consecutive clean round.
- **Bowtie2/HISAT2 index tools R97 — bowtie2-build/bowtie2-inspect/hisat2-build/hisat2-inspect (4-stem multi-binary; sequence-alignment index sister tools)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `{bowtie2,hisat2}-{build,inspect}(1)` man pages at [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/) via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_bowtie2-build` is `#compdef bowtie2-build bowtie2-inspect hisat2-build hisat2-inspect`. **Coverage**: 4-stem multi-binary harvest of the index-build / index-inspect sister binaries paired with the already-covered `bowtie2` (blacklist.txt:2455) and `hisat2` (blacklist.txt:11149) aligners. Both families share substantial flag patterns since HISAT2 is forked from Bowtie2. **Per-binary surface**: **bowtie2-build** (build a Bowtie2 index from FASTA reference sequences) — 19 flags incl. `-f` FASTA default / `-c` sequences on cmdline, `--large-index` (force large index for <4Gbp ref), `-r/--noref` / `-3/--justref` index-file-subset toggles, `-a/--noauto`, `-p/--packed`, `--bmax`/`--bmaxdivn`/`--dcv`/`--nodc` blockwise SA tuning, `-o/--offrate` (default 5), `-t/--ftabchars` (default 10), `--threads`, `--seed`, `--debug`, `--verbose`, `-q/--quiet`. **bowtie2-inspect** (inspect a Bowtie2 index — recover FASTA or show summary) — 9 flags incl. `--large-index`, `--debug`, `-v/--verbose`, `-a/--across N` (default 60 chars), `-n/--names`, `-s/--summary`, `-o/--output`. **hisat2-build** (build a HISAT2 index from FASTA + annotation files) — 25 flags inherits the Bowtie2 set plus HISAT2-specific: `--localoffrate` (default 3), `--localftabchars` (default 6), **annotation files**: `--snp`, `--haplotype`, `--ss` (splice sites), `--exon`, plus repeat-aware index family `--repeat-ref`, `--repeat-info`, `--repeat-snp`, `--repeat-haplotype`. **hisat2-inspect** (inspect a HISAT2 index — recover FASTA, list names, or show annotations) — 12 flags: shares `--large-index`/`-a/--across`/`-s/--summary`/`-n/--names`/`-v/--verbose`/`-h/--help`/`--usage` with bowtie2-inspect plus HISAT2-specific `-e/--ht2-ref` (reconstruct reference from .ht2; slow, preserves colors), `--snp` (print SNPs), `--ss`/`--ss-all` (print splice sites, with optional include-non-global toggle), `--exon` (print exons). Common positional: `<index_base>` (the index filename without trailing `.1.bt2`/`.2.bt2` or `.1.ht2`/`.2.ht2` suffix). **Hunt-skip log this round**: 40+ speculative bioinformatics names mostly with `-cli` suffixes (seqkit-cli/seqkit2/samtools-cli/bcftools-cli/pysam-cli/minimap2-cli/bwa-cli/salmon-cli/kallisto-cli/rsem-cli/featurecounts-cli/stringtie-cli/fastqc-cli/multiqc-cli/star-cli/subread-cli/cutadapt-cli/flash-cli/fastp-cli/quast-cli/megahit-cli/spades-cli/mash-cli/sourmash-cli/mmseqs2-cli/prokka-cli/busco-cli/orthofinder-cli/beast2-cli — real binaries are typically without `-cli`, and base names like `bowtie2`/`hisat2`/`mash`/`samtools` are widely already covered); real fresh candidates queued for future bioinformatics-focused rounds: `htseq-count`, `subread`, `raxml`, `mmseqs2`, `beast2`, `star-fusion`, `bcl2fastq2`, `featurecounts`, `iqtree2` (last two already covered as `iqtree2` — need check). **Blacklist additions (4)**: 2 between `bowtie2` (2455) and `box` (2458) for `bowtie2-build`/`bowtie2-inspect`; 2 between `hisat2` (11151) and `hishrink` (11154) for `hisat2-build`/`hisat2-inspect`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 102nd consecutive clean round.
- **ccwl R96 — ccwl (1 stem; Concise Common Workflow Language compiler)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `scripts/ccwl` Guile entry script at [github.com/arunisaac/ccwl](https://github.com/arunisaac/ccwl) via `gh api` inspection of every Guile `(option ...)` declaration in the `%options` list + `match` dispatch on argv in the script's `main` procedure. Single-stem file `_ccwl` is `#compdef ccwl`. **Coverage**: ccwl is a Guile-based compiler that emits CWL (Common Workflow Language) workflows or Graphviz dot graphs from concise `.scm` descriptions. State-machine with a single top-level subcommand `compile SOURCE_FILE` and 3 shared flags: `-t/--to TARGET` 2-value enum (`cwl` for Common Workflow Language output, `dot` for Graphviz; with source-verified explicit `supported = ("cwl" "dot")` validation that raises `formatted-message` on unknown value), `-o/--output FILE` (default stdout), `-h/--help`. **Implementation note**: ccwl is built with Guile Scheme using `srfi-37 args-fold`, so the option syntax follows GNU getopt conventions (`-tcwl` short or `--to=cwl` long). The top-level dispatch is implemented with a `match` on `argv`: `(program "compile" args ...)` invokes the compile path, and any other invocation falls back to the global-usage line `Usage: ~a COMMAND [OPTIONS] [ARGS]`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: rinetd (already covered, blacklist.txt:22649), proxychains (already covered, blacklist.txt:21000), koka (already covered, blacklist.txt:13286), proxychains4-ng (man page documents only `-f configfile` and `--help` — already absorbed by existing `_proxychains` coverage with -f flag), 30+ other speculative `-cli`/`-bin`/`-stats` suffix typo candidates (traefik-cli/haproxy-cli/envoy-cli/envoy-ctl/envoy-bin/contour-cli/ingress-nginx-cli/nginx-amplify/nginx-supervisord/caddy-cli/boringproxy-cli/twistd-py/uwsgi-cli/unit-cli/relayd-cli/yarrrn/ory-cli/authelia/kong-cli/kong-deck/koka-lang/fastly-cli/tailscale-cli/microsocks/rinetd-cli/wireshark-cli/tshark-cli/sandstorm-cli/filtre/stream-cli/redis-cli7/ngrep-test/mdadm-cli — most resolve to already-covered base names like `traefik`/`haproxy`/`envoy`/`caddy`/`tshark`/`tailscale`/`redis-cli`/etc.). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `ccusage` (3283) and `ccxxmake` (3284) for `ccwl`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — 101st consecutive clean round.
- **AWS Lambda RIE R95 — aws-lambda-rie (1 stem; Lambda Runtime Interface Emulator)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `internal/lambda/rie/run.go` options struct at [github.com/aws/aws-lambda-runtime-interface-emulator](https://github.com/aws/aws-lambda-runtime-interface-emulator) via `gh api` inspection of every `long:` tag in the `jessevdk/go-flags` struct. Single-stem file `_aws-lambda-rie` is `#compdef aws-lambda-rie`. **Coverage**: aws-lambda-rie is the AWS Lambda Runtime Interface Emulator used in Lambda container images to make Lambda functions invokable locally via the HTTP endpoint `/2015-03-31/functions/function/invocations`. **Discovery note**: at startup `cmd/aws-lambda-rie/main.go` checks for the `AWS_LAMBDA_MAX_CONCURRENCY` env var to dispatch between the "run" mode (`run.Run()`) and the standard "rie" mode (`rie.Run()`); the flags below apply to the rie.Run() code path which is the dominant container-image usage. **4 flags** (tight surface): `--log-level LEVEL` 7-value enum (panic/fatal/error/warn/info/debug/trace; default info; also `LOG_LEVEL` env), `--enable-init-caching` (enable Init Caching support), `--runtime-api-address HOST:PORT` (address of the AWS Lambda Runtime API to communicate with the execution environment), `--runtime-interface-emulator-address HOST:PORT` (default `0.0.0.0:8080`; address RIE listens on). Plus `-h/--help` and positional bootstrap path or handler. **Hunt-skip log this round**: dvb-scan (no real binary — v4l-utils's actual DVB scanners are `dvbv5-scan`/`dvbv5-zap` which are already covered via `src/_dvbv5-scan`/`src/_dvbv5-zap`), dvbv5-scan-tool (typo of dvbv5-scan), v4l-info/v4l2-ctl-test/cec-message (not real v4l-utils binaries — actual binaries are `v4l2-ctl`/`cec-ctl`/`cec-compliance` etc. which are typically covered), `xcaddy` (already covered as `more_src/_xcaddy`), notmuch-deliver (Debian man page returns 404 via dyn.manpages redirect chain; archived alip/notmuch-deliver repo moved to notmuch contrib/notmuch-deliver path that is gated behind 403 on git.notmuchmail.org; deferred until accessible source for the actual `main.c` is located), maildirproc (same 404 dyn.manpages issue), 30+ other speculative `-cli`/`-bin`/`-init` suffix names. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `aws-keychain` (1851) and `aws-mfa` (1852) for `aws-lambda-rie`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — one-hundredth consecutive clean round. **MILESTONE**: this round caps **100 consecutive clean LC_ALL=C sort rounds since the position-by-position alphabetic-slot bookkeeping discipline was tightened**.
- **DVB streaming/analysis R94 — dvblast + dvbtee (2-stem multi-binary; VideoLAN DVB→IP gateway + libdvbtee TS streamer/parser)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `dvblast(1)` man page at [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/dvblast/dvblast.1.en.html](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/dvblast/dvblast.1.en.html) via WebFetch and `dvbtee/dvbtee.cpp` source at [github.com/mkrufky/libdvbtee](https://github.com/mkrufky/libdvbtee) via `gh api` inspection of `getopt()` string + case handlers. Single multi-stem file `_dvblast` is `#compdef dvblast dvbtee`. **Coverage**: 2-stem multi-binary harvest covering the two main DVB streaming/parsing CLIs (paired with R93's `_dvbsnoop` snooper). **dvblast** (VideoLAN's simple+powerful streaming application based on the linux-dvb API — reads from DVB tuner/ASI/RTP/UDP input, outputs RTP/UDP multicast streams) — **57 flags** across multiple groups: I/O (`-a`/`-A`/`-D`/`*-d` duplicate), tuning (`-f`/`-s`/`-v` 3-value `0/13/18`, `-S` satellite 0-4/A/B, `-k`, `-p`), DVB delivery (`-5` 5-value delsys enum DVBS/DVBS2/DVBC_ANNEX_A/DVBT/ATSC, `-b`/`-F`/`-I`/`-m`/`-R`/`-P`/`-K`/`-G`/`-H`/`-X`), stream output (`-o`/`-t`/`-U`/`-L` default 100ms/`-E` default 40ms), multistream (`-1`/`--multistream-id-pls-mode` 3-value ROOT/GOLD/COMBO, `--multistream-id-pls-code` 0-262143, `--multistream-id-is-id` 0-255, `-2`), CAM (`-y`/`-W`/`-Y`), config (`-c`/`-w`/`-z`/`-e`/`-x` text|xml/`-Z`), metadata (`-M`/`-N`/`-B`/`-T`/`-J`), process control (`-i`/`-r`/`-O`/`-Q`/`-u`), and utility (`-n`/`-C`/`*-0` pidmap repeatable/`-j` charset/`-l` syslog/`-g`/`-q`/`-V`/`-h`). **dvbtee** (libdvbtee digital television streamer/parser/SI aggregator with telnet CLI + HTTP control) — source-verified getopt string `"a:A:bc:C:f:F:t:n:T:i:I:js::S::eE::o::O:d::H::qh?"` produces 22 flags: `-a` adapter, `-A` ATSC/QAM scan flags, `-b` bitrate stats, `-c`/`-C` channel list/scan-min/scan-max, `-f` frontend, `-F` filename, `-t` timeout, `-T` num tuners (0=all), `-s` scan with **optional 4-value mode enum** (1=speed/2=redundancy/3=both/4=optimized partial), `-n` network interface, `-S` server mode with **optional 3-value sub-mode enum** (1=cmd/2=HTTP/3=both), `-i` TCP/UDP feed URL, `-I` service IDs, `-e` ETT extended text tables (ATSC only), `-E` EPG scan with optional max-EITs, `-o` output filename (optional), `-O` output options, `-d` debug level (optional), `-H` HLS output (optional path), `-j` JSON, `-q` quiet, `-h` help. **Hunt-skip log this round**: cmpegextract/atsc-snoop/atsc/atscutil/dvb-scan/astc-snoop/tsplay/tsindex/tsdecoder/tsanalyser/tsanalyzer/tsmasher (small or speculative names; needs separate research), dvdid/dvd-slideshow/dvd-format (DVD-specific tools — different focus), mp4-validator/x264-cli/x265-cli/libvpx-cli/aac-encoder/gpac-cli/mp42ts/ffprobe-cli/libav-cli/vlc-cli/vlc-iss (`-cli` suffix typos - **dvbsnoop R93 — dvbsnoop (1 stem; DVB MPEG-2 transport stream snooper)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `dvbsnoop(1)` man page at [manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/dvbsnoop.1.html](https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/dvbsnoop.1.html) via WebFetch. Single-stem file `_dvbsnoop` is `#compdef dvbsnoop`. **Coverage**: dvbsnoop decodes DVB SI (Service Information), TS (Transport Stream), PS/PES (Program Stream / Packetized Elementary Stream), bandwidth, signal, and frontend-info modes from a DVB capture device or a binary file. Single-binary CLI with **~30 flags** (legacy `-flag` style, no double-dash; one positional PID argument in decimal or `0xHH` hex). Flag groups: **Mode** `-s` 8-value enum (ts/ps/pes/sec/pidscan/bandwidth/signal/feinfo). **Output verbosity** `-pd N` (0-9, default 6 decoding verbose), `-npd` shorthand for `-pd 0`, `-ph N` (0-4, default 4 hex verbose), `-hexdumpbuffer`/`-nohexdumpbuffer` toggle pair. **Stream control** `-n N` stop after N packets, `-N N` stop after decoding N packets (section mode), `-if FILE` read binary stream from file (`-` for stdin), `-b` binary output. **Timestamps** `-tf`/`-td`/`-tn` (full/delta/none). **DVB device selection** `-adapter N`, `-devnr N`, `-frontend /dev/dvb/...`, `-demux`, `-dvr`, `-buffersize KB`. **Section mode** `-timeout MS`, `-f VALUE` (section filter), `-m MASK`, **CRC checking 4-way toggle** `-crc`/`-nocrc`/`-softcrc`/`-nosoftcrc`, `-spiderpid` recursively follow referenced PIDs, `-privateprovider ID` for private table decoding. **TS mode** `-tssubdecode` (sub-decode SI or PS/PES inside TS) and `-tsraw`. **PID scan mode** `-maxdmx N`. **General** `-help`, `-hideproginfo` (suppress header). **Hunt-skip log this round**: bpfman-rpc/bpfman-agent/bpftop-rs/cmd-bench/tex5/mpv-mpris/pulseview/sigrok-cli-rs/analog-cli/hellaswag/boxes2/bonsai/bonsai-cli/puzzle-cli/aleph-cli/aleph-zero/aleph-bin/libgosphi-cli/zarrita-cli/zarr-cli/rerun-cli/rerun-py/vega-cli/vega-lite-cli/ndarray-cli/arrow-cli/polars-bin/tablito/termpunk/gum-cli/sg2/sg-rust — most are speculative names with `-rs`/`-cli`/`-bin`/`-py` suffixes; real binaries are typically `bpfman`/`bpftop`/`pulseview`/`sigrok-cli`/`bonsai`/`rerun`/`vega`/`gum`/`sg`, which are largely covered. **Verified already covered**: `numbat` (blacklist.txt:18115), `rerun` (blacklist.txt:22508). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `dvb-format-convert` (6962) and `dvbv5-daemon` (6963) for `dvbsnoop`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninety-eighth consecutive clean round.
- **CERT NetSA SiLK R92 — flowcap/num2dot/rwguess (3-stem multi-binary; capture daemon + IP converter + deprecated SNMP summarizer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/{flowcap,num2dot,rwguess}.html` via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_flowcap` is `#compdef flowcap num2dot rwguess`. **Coverage**: 3-stem multi-binary harvest covering the last 3 SiLK binaries identified after R91 by re-scanning all `bin_PROGRAMS`/`sbin_PROGRAMS` in `bbayles/netsa-pkg/silk-src/src/*/Makefile.am`. **Per-binary surface**: **flowcap** (NetFlow/IPFIX capture daemon — sbin_PROGRAMS sister of rwflowappend/rwflowpack/rwpackchecker covered by `_rwflowpack`; collects flow records into files for downstream rwsender(8)) — 22 flags incl. `--destination-directory DIR`, `--sensor-configuration FILE`, `--max-file-size`, `--probes NAMES`, `--timeout SEC` (default 60), `--clock-time[=OFFSET]`, `--fc-version` 4-value enum (2/3/4/5, default 5), `--freespace-minimum SIZE` (default 1GB), `--space-maximum-percent PCT` (default 98), `--compression-method` 5-enum, `--verify-sensor-config`, daemon flags `--pidfile`/`--no-chdir`/`--no-daemon`, plus full logging configuration: `--log-destination` 5-keyword-enum (none/stdout/stderr/syslog/both), `--log-directory`/`--log-pathname`, `--log-level` 8-enum (emerg/alert/crit/err/warning/notice/info/debug), `--log-sysfacility N`, `--log-basename`, `--log-post-rotate CMD` (default gzip). **num2dot** (integer-IP → dotted-decimal in textual SiLK output; IPv6 NOT supported, upstream suggests PySiLK scripts as alternative) — 2 flags: `--ip-fields FIELDS` (column numbers, default 1, up to 3) and `--delimiter C` (default `|`). **rwguess** (**DEPRECATED since SiLK 3.8.3** — will be removed in v4.0; use `rwpdu2silk(1) + rwstats(1)/rwuniq(1)` instead) — 2 flags: `--top N` (default 10 SNMP interfaces) and `--print-all` (show all SNMP interfaces organized by interface number rather than frequency). **Hunt-skip log this round**: rwflowtune (404 — does not exist), rwflowpack-cron (404 — does not exist), sendrcv-* binaries (silk-src/src/sendrcv builds rwflowappend/rwsender/rwreceiver via $(EXTRA_PROGRAMS) gated by a comment-toggle between bin_PROGRAMS and noinst_PROGRAMS; all are covered by the existing `_rwflowpack` multi-stem already). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 between `flowblade` (8302) and `flowctl` (8303) for `flowcap`; 1 between `nullmailer-inject` (18109) and `numactl` (18110) for `num2dot`; 1 between `rwgroup` (23180) and `rwho` (23181) for `rwguess`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninety-seventh consecutive clean round. The full SiLK ecosystem coverage in this repo is now **33 stems** across 6 multi-stem files (`_rwfilter` 8, `_rwcut` 8, `_rwallformats` 11, `_rwflowpack` 5, `_flowcap` 3, plus `_rwaggbag`/`_rwappend`/`_rwipfix2silk`/`_rwsetbuild` individual files).
- **CERT NetSA SiLK R91 — 11 SiLK auxiliary tools (multi-stem; rwallformats/rwcompare/rwip2cc/rwipaexport/rwipaimport/rwpdedupe/rwpdu2silk/rwpmatch/rwptoflow/rwtotal/mapsid)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/` man pages via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_rwallformats` is `#compdef rwallformats rwcompare rwip2cc rwipaexport rwipaimport rwpdedupe rwpdu2silk rwpmatch rwptoflow rwtotal mapsid`. **Coverage**: 11-stem multi-binary harvest **completing the R90 SiLK deferred queue**. Combined with R90, this covers all 19 SiLK binaries identified as fresh in R90's scan. **Per-binary surface**: **rwallformats** (write a test SiLK file in every supported record format/version) — `--basename` + shared I/O. **rwcompare** (compare two SiLK flow files for equality) — `--quiet`, `--site-config-file`; 2 positional files (or `-`/`stdin`). **rwip2cc** (map IPs to country codes — **DEPRECATED since SiLK 3.0** in favor of `rwpmaplookup(1)`) — `--address IP` xor `--input-file FILE`, `--map-file PMAP`, `--print-ips 0|1`, `--integer-ips`, `--zero-pad-ips`, column formatting. **rwipaexport** (export IPA catalog → SiLK IPset or prefix-map) — `--catalog NAME`, `--time TIMESTAMP`, `--prefix-map-name NAME`, repeatable `--note-add`/`--note-file-add`, `--compression-method`. **rwipaimport** (import SiLK IP collections → IPA catalog) — required `--catalog NAME`, `--description TEXT`, `--start-time`/`--end-time`; positional collection files. **rwpdedupe** (drop duplicate packets from tcpdump captures) — mutex `--first-duplicate` xor `--random-duplicate[=SEED]`, `--threshold MS` (0-1,000,000 ms duplicate detection window); positional `.pcap` files. **rwpdu2silk** (NetFlow v5 PDU → SiLK conversion) — `--silk-output FILE`, `--print-statistics`, `--log-destination`, repeatable `--log-flags` (all/bad/default/missing/record-timestamps 5-enum), `--note-add`/`--note-file-add`, `--compression-method`. **rwpmatch** (match packets from tcpdump against a SiLK flow file) — required `--flow-file FILE`, `--msec-compare`, `--ports-compare`; positional tcpdump file. **rwptoflow** (generate SiLK flow records from tcpdump captures) — `*--plugin .so`, `--active-time RANGE`, `--flow-output FILE`, `--packet-pass-output FILE`, `--packet-reject-output FILE`, `--reject-all-fragments`, `--reject-incomplete`, `--set-sensorid ID`, `--compression-method`, `--print-statistics`. **rwtotal** (per-bin record/byte/packet totals) — **17 mutually-exclusive keying-scheme flags**: `--sip-first-{8,16,24}`/`--sip-last-{8,16}` 5 source-IP-slice flags, `--dip-first-{8,16,24}`/`--dip-last-{8,16}` 5 dest-IP-slice flags, `--sport`/`--dport`/`--proto`/`--packets`/`--bytes`/`--duration`/`--icmp-code` 7 other keys; plus `--summation`, `--min-{bytes,packets,records}`/`--max-{bytes,packets,records}` 6 threshold flags, `--skip-zeroes`. **mapsid** (sensor name ↔ numeric ID mapping from silk.conf) — `--print-classes`, `--print-descriptions`, `--site-config-file`; positional sensor names/IDs. **Hunt-skip log this round**: none — clean targeted source-direct extraction completing the R90 deferral. **Blacklist additions (11)**: alphabetically distributed across the `mapsid` and `rw*` ranges. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninety-sixth consecutive clean round. The SiLK ecosystem coverage in this repo is now ~30 stems (8 from `_rwfilter`, 8 from R90 `_rwcut`, 11 from R91 `_rwallformats`, plus rwaggbag/rwappend/rwflowpack/rwipfix2silk/rwsetbuild as individual files).
- **CERT NetSA SiLK R90 — 8 core SiLK rw* analysis tools (multi-stem)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/` man pages via WebFetch + `gh api` inspection of `silk-src/src/*/Makefile.am bin_PROGRAMS` lines and `silk-src/src/*/*setup.c OPT_*` tables at [github.com/bbayles/netsa-pkg](https://github.com/bbayles/netsa-pkg). Single multi-stem file `_rwcut` is `#compdef rwcut rwbag rwset rwscan rwresolve rwfglob rwaddrcount rwrecgenerator`. **Coverage**: 8-stem multi-binary harvest covering core SiLK analysis tools not previously covered (existing `_rwfilter` multi-stem covers rwfilter+rwstats+rwsort+rwuniq+rwcat+rwcount+rwfileinfo+rwgroup; plus `_rwaggbag`/`_rwappend`/`_rwflowpack`/`_rwipfix2silk`/`_rwsetbuild` from earlier rounds). **Shared common flag helper** `_silk_common` (26 flags) extracted from `rwcutsetup.c` and inherited across most rw* tools: `--no-titles`, `--no-columns`, `--column-separator`, `--no-final-delimiter`, `--delimited`, `--integer-tcp-flags`, `--integer-sensors`, `--integer-ips`, `--icmp-type-and-code`, `--epoch-time`, `--output-path`, `--pager`, `--site-config-file`, `--plugin`, `--ipv6-policy` (ignore/asv4/mix/force/only), `--compression-method` (none/zlib/lzo1x/snappy/best), `--note-strip`, `*--note-add` (repeatable), `*--note-file-add` (repeatable), `--invocation-strip`, `--print-filenames`, `--copy-input`, `--xargs`, `--dry-run`, `--help`, `--legacy-help`, `--version`. **Per-binary distinctive flags**: **rwcut** (display columns from SiLK records) — `--fields`, `--all-fields`, `--help-fields`, `--num-recs`, `--start-rec-num`, `--end-rec-num`, `--tail-recs`, `--python-file`. **rwbag** (build Bag key/counter data structure) — `*--bag-file=KEY,COUNTER,OUTFILE` required+repeatable, `*--pmap-file` repeatable. **rwset** (build IPset from SIP/DIP/NHIP) — `--sip-file`, `--dip-file`, `--nhip-file`, `--any-file`, `--record-version` 5-value enum (0/2/3/4/5). **rwscan** (statistical TRW+BLR scan detection) — `--scan-model` 3-value enum (0=TRW+BLR/1=TRW/2=BLR), `--trw-internal-set`, `--trw-sip-set` (deprecated alias), `--trw-theta0` (benign-conn success prob, default 0.8), `--trw-theta1` (malicious-conn success prob, default 0.2), `--model-fields`, `--scandb`, `--threads`, `--queue-depth`, `--verbose-progress=CIDR`, `--verbose-flows`, `--verbose-results`. **rwresolve** (DNS-resolve IP columns in textual SiLK output) — `--ip-fields`, `--delimiter`, `--column-width`, `--resolver` 2-value enum (getnameinfo/gethostbyaddr), `--max-requests`. **rwfglob** (rwfilter file-globber — preview file selection) — `--class`, `--type` (with `all`), `--flowtypes`, `--sensors`, `--start-date`/`--end-date` (YYYY/MM/DD[:HH] or epoch), `--data-rootdir`, `--print-missing-files`, `--no-block-check`, `--no-file-names`, `--no-summary`. **rwaddrcount** (per-address record/byte/packet counts) — `--print-recs`, `--print-stat`, `--print-ips`, `--use-dest`, `--min-bytes`/`--byte-min` alias, `--min-packets`, `--min-records`, `--max-bytes`/`--max-packets`/`--max-records`, `--set-file` (write IPset of matching addrs), `--sort-ips`. **rwrecgenerator** (synthetic SiLK record generator for testing) — `--silk-output-path`, `--text-output-path`, `--seed`, `--num-recs`, `--start-time`/`--end-time`, `--sensor-prefix-map`, `--time-step`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: 11 more SiLK binaries deferred for a future round (rwallformats/rwpdu2silk/rwipaexport/rwipaimport/rwip2cc/rwpdedupe/rwpmatch/rwptoflow/rwcompare/mapsid/rwtotal) — fresh but less central than the 8 picked this round. Plus the SiLK rwBAGTOOL/rwSETTOOL/etc subcommands and the rwflowpack/rwflowappend/rwsender/rwreceiver flow-pipeline tools (some covered). **Blacklist additions (8)**: alphabetically distributed across the rw* range (rwaddrcount, rwbag, rwcut, rwfglob, rwrecgenerator, rwresolve, rwscan, rwset). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninety-fifth consecutive clean round.
- **CERT NetSA YAF R89 — yafcollect/ipfixDump/yafzcbalance (3-stem multi-binary; YAF inventory completion)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `src/yafcollect.c`, `scripts/ipfixDump.pod`, `scripts/yafzcbalance.pod` at [github.com/britram/cert-netsa-yaf](https://github.com/britram/cert-netsa-yaf) and [github.com/yaf-project/yaf](https://github.com/yaf-project/yaf) via `gh api` inspection of `Makefile.am bin_PROGRAMS` declarations + POD `OPTIONS` sections. Single multi-stem file `_yafcollect` is `#compdef yafcollect ipfixDump yafzcbalance`. **Coverage**: 3-stem multi-binary harvest **resolving the R88 YAF binary inventory note**. Method: inspected every `_PROGRAMS` line in YAF's autotools setup — `src/Makefile.am` declares `bin_PROGRAMS = yaf yafscii yafcollect`; `scripts/Makefile.am` declares `bin_PROGRAMS = getFlowKeyHash yafMeta2Pcap ipfixDump` plus conditional `bin_PROGRAMS += yafzcbalance` (built when PF_RING ZC is available); `airframe/src/Makefile.am` declares `bin_PROGRAMS = airdaemon filedaemon` (already covered in R88). Existing `more_src/_yaf` multi-stem covers `yaf yafscii getFlowKeyHash yafMeta2Pcap super_mediator`, so this round adds the remaining 3 YAF-side binaries. **Per-binary surface**: **yafcollect** (IPFIX collector — receives flows from yaf(1) and rotates output; AF_OPTION/airframe parser) — 10 flags: `-i/--in HOST`, `-I/--rotate-delay SEC`, `--transport tcp|udp|sctp`, `--port`, `--tls`, `--tls-ca FILE`, `--tls-cert FILE`, `--tls-key FILE`, `--tls-pass PASS`, `--print-all`. **ipfixDump** (IPFIX file dumper — pretty-print templates and records) — 6 mode flags: `--in FILE` (`-` for stdin), `--out FILE` (`-` for stdout), `--yaf` (include yaf CERT private enterprise IEs + DPI plugins), `--template` (templates only), `--data` (data records only), `--stats` (summary statistics only), `--version`. **yafzcbalance** (PF_RING ZC load balancer for YAF — distributes packets from zero-copy interfaces to multiple yaf(1) consumer processes via PF_RING cluster ID/queue) — 10 flags: `--in DEVICES` (comma-separated, e.g. `zc:eth0,zc:eth1`), `--cluster CLUSTER_ID`, `--num NUM_YAFS` (default 1), `--core CORE_ID` (bind balancer to CPU core), `--time TIME_CORE_ID` (bind hardware-timestamp thread — yaf requires per-packet timestamps which PF_RING ZC disables by default), `--pidfile PATH`, `--daemon`, `--log FILE`, `--user USER` (drop privileges after opening capture), `--stats SECONDS`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: none — clean targeted source-direct extraction. Other yaf*.c files (`yafapplabel`/`yafcap`/`yafcore`/`yafcollect`/`yafdag`/`yafhooks`/`yaflush`/`yafnfe`/etc.) confirmed as internal `_LIB`/`_SOURCES` references, not separate `_PROGRAMS` binaries. **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 between `yafc` (29560) and `yafscii` (29561) for `yafcollect`; 1 between `ipfetch` (12192) and `ipfs` (12193) for `ipfixDump`; 1 between `yafscii` (29562) and `yakuake` (29563) for `yafzcbalance`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninety-fourth consecutive clean round.
- **CERT NetSA airframe + QoF R88 — airdaemon/filedaemon/qof (3-stem multi-binary; netflow probe + airframe daemon supervisors)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream `airframe/src/{airdaemon,filedaemon}.c` and `src/qof.c` at [github.com/britram/qof](https://github.com/britram/qof) (QoF — Quality of Flow probe by Brian Trammell, derived from CERT NetSA's YAF; airframe is the shared option-parsing/lock/IO library) via `gh api` inspection of every `AF_OPTION(...)` macro. Single multi-stem file `_airdaemon` is `#compdef airdaemon filedaemon qof`. **Coverage**: 3-stem multi-binary harvest resolving the R87 airdaemon hunt-skip mystery (turned out to be CERT NetSA's airframe library, NOT aircrack-ng as my candidate list assumed). **Per-binary surface**: **airdaemon** (generic daemon supervisor — runs a child process and restarts with backoff) — 6 flags: `-r/--retry SEC` minimum retry delay, `-R/--retry-max SEC` maximum retry delay, `-P/--pidfile FILE` supervised-process pidfile, `-A/--airdaemon-pidfile FILE` supervisor pidfile, `--no-daemon` foreground mode, `-h/--help`; args after `--` are passed to the child. **filedaemon** (directory-poll file daemon — watches an input glob, runs a child on each match, then moves files to `nextdir` or `faildir` based on the child's exit status) — 10 flags: `-i/--in GLOB` input file glob, `-o/--out DIR` output directory, `-n/--nextdir DIR` move-processed-here, `-x/--faildir DIR` move-failed-here, `-e/--extension EXT` output extension, `-p/--poll SEC` input poll delay, `-k/--lock` enable airlock for concurrent input, `-P/--pidfile FILE`, `--no-daemon`, `-h/--help`. **qof** (Quality-of-Flow probe — IPFIX-exporting netflow probe) — **21 flags across 4 option groups**. Core: `-i/--in INSPEC` pcap/interface input, `-o/--out OUTSPEC` collector host:port or file, `-F/--filter BPF`, `-R/--rotate SEC`, `-k/--lock`, `--stats SEC` stats interval (default 300), `--ipfix PROTO` (tcp/udp/sctp), `--yaml FILE`. Experimental: `--template-refresh SEC` (default 600), `--observation-domain N`. IPFIX: `--ipfix-port PORT` (default 4739), `--tls`, `--tls-ca FILE`, `--tls-cert FILE`, `--tls-key FILE`. **Detune** (QoF's traffic-shaping torture knobs for testing): `--detune-bucket-max BYTES` (0=disable), `--detune-bucket-rate BPS` drain rate, `--detune-bucket-delay MS` full-bucket delay, `--detune-random-drop P` post-bucket drop probability, `--detune-random-delay MS` max random delay, `--detune-random-alpha N` random-delay alpha shape parameter. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `yafdpi`/`yafzcbalance`/`yafapplabel`/`yafzipdpi`/`yafhost-stats`/`yafmeta`/`yafmark`/`yafmid`/`yafhostchg`/`yafhost2yaf` (YAF tools — checking YAF source `src/` directory at `britram/cert-netsa-yaf` shows most `yaf*.c` files are internal modules not separate binaries; the existing `_yaf` multi-stem already covers `yaf yafscii yafzcbalance`; deferred until proper YAF binary inventory via Makefile.am inspection). **Blacklist additions (3)**: 1 between `aircrack-ng` (812) and `airdecap-ng` (813) for `airdaemon`; 1 between `filecap` (8107) and `filefrag` (8108) for `filedaemon`; 1 between `qodo` (21757) and `qoitopam` (21758) for `qof`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninety-third consecutive clean round.

Surface transcribed directly from upstream `aircrack-ng/aircrack-ng` man pages at [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/aircrack-ng/](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/aircrack-ng/) via WebFetch and `gh api` inspection of `scripts/dcrack.py` and `scripts/versuck-ng/versuck-ng` argparse calls. Single multi-stem file `_besside-ng-crawler` is `#compdef besside-ng-crawler kstats airodump-ng-oui-update dcrack versuck-ng`. **Coverage**: 5-stem multi-binary harvest covering the small aircrack-ng utilities not previously covered (existing coverage spans `_aircrack-ng`/`_airbase-ng`/`_airdecap-ng`/`_airdecloak-ng`/`_aireplay-ng`/`_airodump-ng`/`_airolib-ng`/`_airserv-ng`/`_airtun-ng`/`_airventriloquist-ng`/`_besside-ng`/`_buddy-ng`/`_easside-ng`/`_makeivs-ng`/`_packetforge-ng`/`_tkiptun-ng`/`_wesside-ng`/`_airmon-ng`/`_ivstools`/`_airdrop-ng`/`_airgraph-ng`/`_wpaclean`, so the suite is now near-complete). **Per-binary surface**: **besside-ng-crawler** (recursively scan pcap dumpfiles for WPA beacons and EAPOL frames) — just 2 positional args: input directory + output file; no documented flags. **kstats** (display FMS algorithm vote distribution for a captured WEP IVS file against a known key) — just 2 positional args: IVS file + 104-bit hex key; no documented flags. **airodump-ng-oui-update** (download and parse the IEEE OUI list for airodump-ng) — no documented CLI options per upstream man page. **dcrack** (distributed WEP/WPA cracking coordinator; `dcrack.py` from `aircrack-ng/scripts/`) — 3 top-level modes: `server` (run coordination server, default port 1337), `client SERVER_ADDR` (connect as cracking worker), `cmd SERVER_ADDR SUBCMD` (admin command with 5 sub-actions: `dict FILE` upload wordlist, `cap FILE` submit capture, `crack BSSID` initiate, `remove BSSID` delete from queue, `status` show progress and connected clients). **versuck-ng** (calculate default WEP key for Verizon-issued Actiontec wireless routers) — 2 flags: `-m/--mac MAC` and `-e/--essid ESSID` (Python argparse). **Hunt-skip log this round**: airdaemon (NOT aircrack-ng — `airdaemon` is from CERT-NSA-YAF/QOF netflow tools' airframe library; needs separate research), airdriver-ng/wzcook (origin unclear — `gh search code` found no canonical source; deferred), airuncloak-ng (not in upstream aircrack-ng source tree; defunct or fabricated name), airdecap-ng-graphs (not real — name was speculative). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 1 between `airodump-ng` (822) and `airolib-ng` (823) for `airodump-ng-oui-update`; 1 between `besside-ng` (2138) and `bessolve` (2139) for `besside-ng-crawler`; 1 between `dcparse` (5883) and `dcraw` (5884) for `dcrack`; 1 between `kstash` (13404) and `ksu` (13405) for `kstats`; 1 between `vership` (27769) and `vertx` (27770) for `versuck-ng`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninety-second consecutive clean round.
- **Knot DNS R86 — kcatalogprint + kzonesign (2-stem multi-binary; catalog printer + DNSSEC signer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream man pages at [knot-dns.cz/docs/latest/html/man_{kcatalogprint,kzonesign}.html](https://www.knot-dns.cz/docs/latest/html) via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_kcatalogprint` is `#compdef kcatalogprint kzonesign`. **Coverage**: 2-stem multi-binary harvest covering the two Knot DNS utilities not previously covered. Existing Knot family coverage in `more_src/`: `_kdig` (Knot's dig replacement), `_khost` (host replacement), `_kjournalprint` (zone journal viewer), `_keymgr` (key management — matches Knot's actual binary name), `_knot` (the resolver tool), `_kjournal`/`_kcat` neighbors. Both new tools share the `-c/--config FILE` / `-C/--confdb DIR` Knot configuration-source flag pair (extracted into `_common_conf` helper). **kcatalogprint** (prints zone catalog stored in catalog database) — 7 flags: shared conf pair, `-D/--dir PATH` (catalog database path with default config), `-a/--catalog ZONE` (filter by catalog zone name), `-m/--member ZONE` (filter by member zone name), `-h/--help`, `-V/--version` (with `-VV` for compile-time config summary). **kzonesign** (DNSSEC signing utility — signs zone files or verifies existing signatures against the keymgr KASP database/Knot config) — 8 flags: shared conf pair, `-o/--outdir DIR` (output directory for signed zone files), `-r/--rollover` (allow key roll-overs and NSEC3 re-salt), `-v/--verify` (validate signing instead of signing), `-t/--time TIMESTAMP` (sign/verify as if at this Unix timestamp), `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`. Both accept positional zone names. **Hunt-skip log this round**: knot-keymgr (typo/alternate name for Knot's `keymgr` — actual binary is `keymgr` per existing `more_src/_keymgr` coverage), nsupdate-keys/autotrust/airdaemon/airdriver-ng/besside-ng-crawler/dcrack-ng/wzcook (aircrack-ng family extras — fresh but needs separate aircrack-ng-focused round to handle the suite's shared `-i interface` / `-w wordlist` / monitor-mode patterns properly), xenstat (Xen runtime stats — single-binary but needs separate Xen-tools research), prism-cli/spectral-cli/swagger* etc (typos with -cli suffix — real binaries are `prism`/`spectral`/`swagger`/`swaggerhub`), pnpm-cli (real binary is `pnpm`, already covered). **Blacklist additions (2)**: 1 between `kcat` (12934) and `kcc` (12935) for `kcatalogprint`; 1 between `kzonecheck` (13694) and `l2control` (13695) for `kzonesign`. - **Scientific scripting R85 — gri + yorick (2-stem multi-binary; plotting language + numerical interpreter)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream man pages: `gri(1)` at [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/gri/gri.1.en.html](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/gri/gri.1.en.html) and `yorick(1)` at [manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/yorick.1.html](https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/yorick.1.html). Single multi-stem file `_gri` is `#compdef gri yorick`. **Coverage**: 2-stem multi-binary harvest covering two niche numerical scripting tools without subcommand trees. **gri** (scientific plotting language compiler — compiles `.gri` script files into PostScript) — 17 flags incl. special-form `-creator FILE.ps` (extract Gri commands from PostScript), `-h`/`-help`/`-v`/`-version`, normal-usage `-b`/`-batch`, `-chatty[N]`/`-c`, `-d`/`-debug`, `-warn_offpage` (default) + `-nowarn_offpage`, `-directory DIR`, `-directory_default`, `-no_bounding_box` (full-page bbox), `-no_expecting`, `-no_startup_message`, `-p`/`-publication`, `-s`/`-superuser` (with optional level), `-t`/`-trace`, `-y`/`-true` (auto-yes). Positional `*.gri` script files. **yorick** (numerical interpreter) — just 2 documented flags: `*-i FILE.i` (include source file at startup; repeatable, equivalent to `#include` after startup) and `-batch FILE.i` (batch mode — no `custom.i` loaded, all errors fatal); positional `*.i` script files. **Hunt-skip log this round**: hersheydemo (man page is sparse per upstream "plotutils does not have a full set of useful man pages" notice; interactive Hershey font demo with no documented CLI surface beyond `--help`; deferred — not worth a low-information completion), gle (GLE Graphics Layout Engine — has its own large language; deferred for separate research), labplot (KDE Qt GUI tool, no CLI), julia-cli/matplotlib-cli/scilab-cli/reduce-cli/idl-cli/dx-server/dx-runtime/gnudatalanguage (project names or library wrappers, real binaries are `julia`/`scilab`/`reduce`/`idl` etc. — needs disambiguation), unison-cli/yadm-init/mosh-cli (typo or wrapper variants of `unison`/`yadm`/`mosh`), sup-mail/himalaya-cli/notmuch-cli/alot-mail/neomutt-cli/abook-utils (typo or wrapper variants of `sup`/`himalaya`/`notmuch`/`alot`/`neomutt`/`abook`). **Blacklist additions (2)**: 1 between `greytiff` (10368) and `gridsome` (10369) for `gri`; 1 between `yor` (29656) and `yosay` (29657) for `yorick`. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninetieth consecutive clean round.
- **GNU plotutils R84 — 6 plotutils sister binaries (multi-stem; pic2plot/plot/plotfont/spline/tek2plot/ode)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from the upstream `{plot,ode,spline,plotfont,tek2plot}(1)` man pages at [manpages.ubuntu.com](https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/) via WebFetch. Single multi-stem file `_pic2plot` is `#compdef pic2plot plot plotfont spline tek2plot ode`. **Coverage**: 6-stem multi-binary harvest covering the GNU plotutils sister-binary suite (master `graph` driver already covered upstream). NOTE: per upstream "plotutils does not have a full set of useful man pages — most documentation is in the info system" notice, several man pages are sparse; `pic2plot` shares the plot/tek2plot flag conventions (input converter to plot output format) — its options were transcribed from sister-binary man pages where its own is empty. **Per-binary surface**: **plot** (master metafile-format plot driver) — 18 flags incl. `-T/--output-format` 14-value enum (X/png/pnm/gif/svg/ai/ps/cgm/fig/pcl/hpgl/regis/tek/meta), `-p/--page-number`, `-s/--merge-pages`, `--bitmap-size WxH`, `--page-size` 8-value enum (letter/legal/a0-a4/b5), `--rotation` 4-value enum (0/90/180/270), `-f/--font-size`, `-F/--font-name`, `--bg-color`, `--pen-color`, `-W/--line-width`, `--emulate-color`, `--max-line-length`, `-O/--portable-output`, `-h/--high-byte-first-input`, `-l/--low-byte-first-input`, `-A/--ascii-input`, `--help-fonts`/`--list-fonts`. **ode** (numerical ODE integrator) — 11 flags incl. `-f/--input-file`, `-p/--precision`, `-t/--title`, **3 integration schemes** `-R/--runge-kutta` (default, fifth-order RKF), `-A/--adams-moulton` (fourth-order predictor-corrector), `-E/--euler`, `-H/--step-size-bound`, `-r/--relative-error-bound`, `-e/--absolute-error-bound`, `-s/--suppress-error-bound`. **spline** (cubic/Bessel/tension spline interpolator) — 13 flags incl. `-f/--filter` (real-time local cubic Bessel, requires -t), `-k/--boundary-condition`, `-n/--no-of-intervals` default 100, `-p/--periodic`, `-T/--tension`, `-t/--t-spacing tmin tmax`, `-d/--y-dimension`, **4-value I/O format enum** `-I`/`-O` (a/f/d/i = ASCII/single-prec/double-prec/integer), `-P/--precision`, `-a/--auto-abscissa`, `-A/--auto-dist-abscissa` (polygonal arclength), `-s/--suppress-abscissa`. **plotfont** (display fonts in plot output) — 10 flags incl. `-T/--output-format` 14-enum, `-1/--lower-half` (default), `-2/--upper-half`, `-o/--octal`, `-x/--hexadecimal`, `--box`, `--bg-color`, `--numbering-font`. **tek2plot** (Tektronix 4010/4014 vector data → plot) — 16 flags incl. `-T/--output-format`, `-p/--page-number`, `-F/--font-name`, `--position-chars`, `-W/--line-width`, `--bg-color`, `--pen-color`, `--bitmap-size`, `--page-size`, `--rotation`, `--emulate-color`, `--max-line-length`, `--use-tek-fonts` (use original Tektronix bitmap fonts), `-O/--portable-output`. **pic2plot** (troff/groff pic → plot) — 15 flags (shared plot/tek2plot conventions). Common to all: `--help`, `--version`, `--help-fonts`/`--list-fonts` for the plot-driver-style binaries. **Hunt-skip log this round**: hersheydemo (deferred — Hershey font demo program; smaller demo surface, can be bundled in a future hersheydemo+hershey-specific round), grpcurl-mock/evans-lib/buf-cli/buf-image/buf-protoc-gen/gprc-cli (typo/library names not real binaries — actual binaries are `grpcurl`/`evans`/`buf`/`protoc-gen-go`/`grpc_cli`; existing coverage), trivy-cluster/trivy-image (trivy subcommands, not separate binaries), snyk-cli/snyk-iac/snyk-container (snyk subcommands invoked as `snyk iac`/`snyk container`), polaris-cli (polaris is the binary, already covered), popeye2 (typo of popeye), fairwinds-app (Fairwinds API tool, deferred), gvisor-containerd-shim/crio-status (deferred — needs more research). **Blacklist additions (6)**: 1 between `pic2graph` and `pic2tikz` for `pic2plot`; 1 between `plop` and `plotcon` for `plot`; 1 between `plotcon` and `plotframes` for `plotfont`; 1 between `splay` and `splint` for `spline`; 1 between `teip` and `tekhton` for `tek2plot`; 1 between `oddjobd` and `odf2xhtml` for `ode`. State-machine with **15 top-level commands** (factory keys collapsed into command groups): `server` (run the Bor node — exposed ~85 of the most-used flags from the 207 available; see flag detail), `version`, `dumpconfig`, `debug` (pprof/block sub-actions; `--server`/`--output`/`--seconds`), `chain` (watch/sethead), `account` (new/import/list), `peers` (add/remove/list/status; `--address` for Bor RPC endpoint), `status`, `fingerprint` (chain config + genesis hash), `attach` (JavaScript console; `--exec`/`--preload`/`--jspath`), `bootnode` (discovery bootnode; `--bind`/`--genkey`/`--writeaddress`/`--nodekey*`/`--nat`/`--netrestrict`/`--metrics*`), `removedb`, `snapshot` (prune-state/prune-block/inspect-ancient-db; `--datadir*`/`--bloomfilter.size`), `purge-whitelisted-entries` (checkpoint and milestone purge), `backfill-statesync-txs` (`--datadir`/`--start`/`--end`), `help`. `server` flag highlights: `--chain` (mainnet/mumbai/amoy/dev), `--syncmode` (full/snap/fast), `--gcmode` (full/archive), `--state.scheme` (hash/path), `--db.engine` (pebble/leveldb), HTTP-RPC/WS-RPC/AuthRPC/GraphQL/IPC stacks, P2P (`--bind`/`--port`/`--bootnodes`/`--maxpeers`/`--nat`/`--netrestrict`/`--nodekey`/`--v4disc`/`--v5disc`/`--nodiscover`), metrics (InfluxDB v1+v2, Prometheus), txpool (`--txpool.*` 8 flags), miner (`--mine`/`--miner.etherbase`/`--miner.gaslimit`/`--miner.gasprice`), accounts (`--unlock`/`--password`/`--allow-insecure-unlock`/`--lightkdf`/`--disable-bor-wallet`), gRPC, parallel EVM (`--parallelevm.*` 3 flags), and Polygon-specific `--bor.heimdall`/`--bor.withoutheimdall`/`--bor.runheimdall`/`--bor.runheimdallargs`/`--bor.useheimdallapp`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: cmd/abidump/abigen/blsync/bootnode/clef/devp2p/era/ethkey/evm/geth/rlpdump/keeper/workload — these are all the **stock go-ethereum CLI binaries** bundled in the bor repo (the project's Makefile builds `cmd/abigen`, `cmd/geth`, etc. as separate binaries); Global flags: `--home`, `--chain-id`, `--node`, `--keyring-backend`, `--output`, `--trust-node` (for heimdallcli). Polygon-specific modules: `clerk` (state sync), `topup` (validator topup), `checkpoint` (Bor checkpoint submission), `bor` (sidechain config), `chainmanager` (chain registry), `borvalidator` (Bor validator set). **Hunt-skip log this round**: none — clean targeted source-direct extraction completing R78 hunt-skip note. **Blacklist additions (2)**: 2 between `heimdall-frontend` (11042) and `heksa` (11043) for `heimdallcli` and `heimdalld` (at index 8 `-` (45) < `c` (99, heimdallcli) < `d` (100, heimdalld) < `s` (115, heksa via h-e-k-s-a)).
