- **Starknet Foundry snforge R75 — snforge (1 stem; Cairo testing framework for Starknet)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream Rust clap-derive source at [github.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry](https://github.com/foundry-rs/starknet-foundry) — every `#[derive(Subcommand)]` enum variant and `#[arg(...)]` macro in `crates/forge/src/lib.rs` inspected via `gh api`, plus the `Completions` struct from `crates/shared/src/auto_completions.rs`. Single-stem file `_snforge` is `#compdef snforge`. **Coverage**: snforge is Starknet Foundry's Cairo testing framework (the Foundry-equivalent for Starknet smart contracts). State-machine with **6 top-level subcommands**. Subcommands: `test [TEST_FILTER]` (25+ flags — see below), `new PATH` (`-n/--name`, `--no-vcs`, `--overwrite`, `-t/--template` cairo-program|balance-contract|erc20-contract), `clean COMPONENT...` (positional CleanComponent enum: coverage/profile/cache/trace), `check-requirements` (no flags), `completions [SHELL]` (clap_complete Shell — bash/zsh/fish/elvish/powershell, optional; falls back to `Shell::from_env`), `optimize-inlining` (shares TestArgs + extra optimize-inlining args). `test` flag detail: `-e/--exact` (requires test_filter, conflicts partition), `*--skip STRING` (repeatable), `-x/--exit-first`, `-r/--fuzzer-runs N`, `-s/--fuzzer-seed N`, `--ignored`, `--include-ignored` (conflicts only_ignored), `--detailed-resources`, `--color WHEN` (auto/always/never), `--rerun-failed`, `--save-trace-data`, `--build-profile` (conflicts coverage), `--coverage` (conflicts build-profile), `--max-n-steps N`, `--no-optimization`, `--tracked-resource` (cairo-steps/sierra-gas), `--gas-report`, `--partition INDEX/TOTAL`, `--max-threads N`, `--launch-debugger` (requires --exact), `--run-native` (cairo-native instead of cairo-vm). Inherits `scarb_ui::args::{PackagesFilter, FeaturesSpec, ProfileSpec}` so adds: `*--features F`, `--all-features`, `--no-default-features`, `*-p/--package GLOB`, `--workspace`, `--profile PROFILE`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: sshx-server (1-binary daemon paired with already-covered sshx), starknet (umbrella name — defer to a separate starknet-CLI-specific round), noir-lsp/scarb-lsp/cairo-language-server (LSP-only — accept stdio JSON-RPC, no useful CLI flag surface beyond `--help`), cargo-prove/cargo-risczero/cargo-stylus (cargo subcommands — invoked as `cargo <sub>`, completion goes through `cargo` framework), risc0-zkvm/risc0-build (Rust crate names, not binaries), aleo/snarkos (deferred — large surfaces, separate round). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `snes9x` (24379) and `snfsdefrag` (24380) for `snforge` (at index 2 `f` (102) > `e` (101, snes9x); at index 3 `o` (111) < `s` (115, snfsdefrag)). Subcommands: `compile` (24 flags incl. `--target solana|polkadot|evm|soroban`, `--emit ast-dot|cfg|llvm-ir|llvm-bc|object|asm`, `--standard-json`, `-O none|less|default|aggressive` LLVM optimizer level, 5 `--no-{dead-storage,constant-folding,strength-reduce,vector-to-slice,cse}` optimization-disable flags, `--no-log-runtime-errors`, `--no-prints`, `-g/--generate-debug-info`, `--release`, `--strict-soroban-types`, `--address-length`, `--value-length` Polkadot Parachain dimensions, `--config-file solang.toml`, `*-I/--importpath` repeatable, `*-m/--importmap` repeatable, `-o/--output`, `--output-meta`, `-v/--verbose`), `doc` (DocPackage + TargetArg + `-v/--verbose` + `-o/--output`), `shell-complete SHELL` (bash/zsh/fish/elvish/powershell), `language-server` (TargetArg + `-I/--importpath` + `-m/--importmap` repeatables for LSP), `idl INPUT...` (Anchor IDL → Solidity interface; `-o/--output`), `new --target T NAME` (scaffold new project). **Hunt-skip log this round**: 52 Cosmos SDK chain *d binaries (stride-noded/juno/sifd/kichaind/crescentd/umeed/lavad/chain-maind/cyber/bandd/icad/noted/regend/bostromd/quasarda/fetchd/cudosd/archwayd/mantra-chain-d/empowerd/lumera-d/unification-chain-d/xpla-d/agoricd/ki-tools/fxchaind/realiod/stargazed/dymensiond/cosmwasm-checks/rollkit-cli/rollup-boost/taiko-mono/evm-da-cli/scroll-cli) — all fresh but share the Cosmos SDK `gaiad`-style structure; deferred for a unified Cosmos SDK multi-stem round, foundry tooling (`foundry-cli` doesn't exist; the real binaries are `forge`/`cast`/`anvil`/`chisel`), chainlink-cli/tenderly/subwallet-js/polkadot.js-api/dapp-tools-cli/sdk-rs deferred (separate research needed). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `solana-keygen` (24471) and `solargraph` (24472) for `solang` (at index 5 `g` (103) > `a` (97 for both solana, solana-keygen) where solana-keygen wins by trailing `-` (45) > `(end)` then `g` (103) < `r` (114, solargraph)). - **Parabola pmidi R73 — pmidi (1 stem; command-line ALSA MIDI sequencer player)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from the upstream `pmidi(1)` man page at [manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/pmidi.1.html](https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/pmidi.1.html). Single-stem file `_pmidi` is `#compdef pmidi`. **Coverage**: pmidi is a tiny C single-binary CLI that plays Standard MIDI Files (SMF) via the ALSA sequencer. 3 documented flags + conventional `-h/--help` and `-V/--version`. Flags: `-p CLIENT:PORT` (ALSA sequencer destination port; accepts comma/space-separated list for files using >16 channels, e.g. `-p "64:0, 64:1"`), `-l` (list available output ports), `-d SECONDS` (post-song delay; default 2s — prevents the final note from being chopped off). Environment: `ALSA_OUTPUT_PORTS` supplies default ports when `-p` is omitted. Positional: one or more `*.mid`/`*.midi` files. **Hunt-skip log this round**: shorten (lossless audio codec — upstream repo `etree-org/shorten` returned 404; `github.com/etree-org/shorten` does not currently exist; the binary is widely shipped in Debian/Ubuntu but the canonical source is essentially abandoned and the original etree.org site is no longer indexable via WebFetch; deferred until an authoritative copy of the man page can be located), and tta/aacenc/aacdec/mp3check/sndfile-spectrogram/sndfile-regtest/jamulus/drumstick-jackmidi-daemon (deferred for separate audio-focused rounds). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `pmfunc` (20268) and `pmie` (20269) for `pmidi` (at index 2 `i` (105) > `f` (102, pmfunc); at index 3 `d` (100) < `e` (101, pmie)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — seventy-eighth consecutive clean round.

Single-binary argparse CLI (no subcommands) with **19 flags**: `-d/--database NAME`, `-I/--input FILE`, `-m/--minimal`, `*-i/--include-keys KEYS` (repeatable), `*-e/--exclude-keys KEYS` (repeatable), `-l/--pipeline raw`, `-F/--formatter` (json/json_pretty/line), `*-t/--transformer` (statement/mask/dummy/identity/prereqs/obfuscate, repeatable), `-o/--output OUT`, `-u/--username USER`, `-H/--hostname HOST`, `-P/--password PASS`, `-p/--port N`, `-V/--verbose`, `-L/--log-level` (debug/info/warning/error/critical), `-C/--no-console`, `-O/--log-file FILE`, `-U/--flush`, `-v/--version`, `-h/--help`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: mserver5 (deferred — server binary man page not located in MonetDB monorepo via gh api content scan; needs different path strategy), tomograph (removed from MonetDB main repo at same time as stethoscope; separate package not located), ucbnntp (NNTP client — limited modern relevance, deferred), shellpath (deferred — research needed on which binary), tmux-3270 (3270 terminal wrapper — niche legacy, deferred). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `stest` (24993) and `stevedore` (24994) for `stethoscope` (at index 3 `t` (116) > `s` (115, stest) < `v` (118, stevedore)). Single multi-stem file `_mclient` is `#compdef mclient msqldump monetdb monetdbd`. **Coverage**: 4-stem multi-binary harvest. **mclient** (interactive SQL/MAL client) — 27 options incl. `-h/--host`, `-p/--port`, `-d/--database`, `-u/--user`, `-l/--language` (sql/mal), `-f/--format` (sql/tab/csv/csv-noquote/raw/xml/json/trash/test/rowcount/expanded), `-t/--timer` (none/clock/performance), `-s/--statement`, `-i/--interactive`, `-D/--dump`, `-N/--inserts`, `-w/--width`, `-r/--rows`, `-L/--log`, `-n/--null`, `-a/--autocommit`, `-R/--allow-remote`, `-z/--timezone`, etc. **msqldump** (SQL dump utility) — 15 options incl. `-D/--describe`, `-N/--inserts`, `-e/--noescape`, `-f/--functions`, `-t/--table` (repeatable), `-o/--output`, `-O/--outputdir`, `-x/--compression` (gz/bz2/xz/zst). **monetdb** (control utility) — **17 top-level subcommands** + nested snapshot 5-action sub-state: `create`, `destroy`, `lock`, `release`, `status`, `start`, `stop`, `kill`, `set`/`get`/`inherit` (with property enum: name/shared/nthreads/optpipe/readonly/embedr/embedpy/embedc/mserver5/forward/dbname/mfunnel/etc.), `discover`, `master`, `replica`, `snapshot` (list/create/restore/destroy/write), `help`, `version`. **monetdbd** (daemon) — 5 subcommands: `create`, `start`, `stop`, `set`/`get` (with daemon property enum: logfile/pidfile/sockdir/listenaddr/port/exittimeout/forward/discoveryttl/discoveryport/control/passphrase/mapisock/controlsock). **Deferred for future rounds**: `mserver5` (the server binary — different option style, large surface), `stethoscope`/`tomograph` (monitoring/profiling utilities, separate docs needed), language bindings (monetdb-r/php/perl/python are NOT CLIs). **Hunt-skip log this round**: msqlodbc/msqljdbc (drivers, not binaries), monetdb-r/monetdb-php/monetdb-perl/monetdb-python (language bindings, not CLIs), mnstr (stream library). **Blacklist additions (4)**: `mclient` between `mckey` (15877) and `mcom` (15878) (at index 2 `l` (108) > `k` (107) < `o` (111)); `monetdb` and `monetdbd` between `monerod` (16646) and `money2john` (16647) (at index 4 `t` (116) > `r` (114) < `y` (121); monetdb < monetdbd by empty < `d` rule at index 7); `msqldump` between `msort` (16997) and `mss` (16998) (at index 2 `q` (113) > `o` (111) < `s` (115)). Single multi-stem file `_zoekt-dynamic-indexserver` is `#compdef zoekt-dynamic-indexserver zoekt-git-clone zoekt-indexserver zoekt-mirror-bitbucket-server zoekt-mirror-gerrit zoekt-mirror-gitea zoekt-mirror-gitiles zoekt-repo-index zoekt-sourcegraph-indexserver zoekt-test`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`zoekt-dynamic-indexserver`** (4 flags): dynamic indexserver with HTTP control — `-repo_dir`, `-index_dir`, `-index_timeout` (default 1h), `-listen` (default `:6060`). **`zoekt-git-clone`** (3 flags + URL positional): `-dest`, `-name`, `-repoid`. **`zoekt-indexserver`** (9 flags): long-running periodic mirror+index — `-index_timeout`/`-max_log_age`/`-fetch_interval`/`-mirror_config`/`-mirror_duration`/`-cpu_fraction` (default 0.25)/`-git_index_flags`/`-data_dir` (default `$HOME/zoekt-serving`)/`-index_dir`. **`zoekt-mirror-bitbucket-server`** (9 flags + 2-value type enum): Bitbucket Server — `-dest`, `-url`, `-disable-tls`, `-credentials` (default `.bitbucket-credentials`), `-project`, `-delete`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, `-type` (`NORMAL`/`PERSONAL`). **`zoekt-mirror-gerrit`** (8 flags + URL positional): Gerrit — `-dest`, `-name REGEX`, `-repo-name-format`, `-exclude REGEX`, `-delete`, `-fetch-meta-config`, `-http-credentials FILE`, `-active`. **`zoekt-mirror-gitea`** (12 flags incl. 2 repeatable): Gitea — `-dest`, `-url` (default `https://gitea.com`), `-org`, `-user`, `-token` (default `$HOME/.gitea-token`), `-forks`, `-delete`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, repeatable `*-topic`, repeatable `*-exclude_topic`, `-no_archived`. **`zoekt-mirror-gitiles`** (4 flags + URL positional + 2-value type enum): gitiles/cgit web frontend — `-dest`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, `-type` (`gitiles`/`cgit`). **`zoekt-repo-index`** (13 flags + manifest positional): Android-style multi-project repo-tool indexer — `-file_limit`, `-shard_limit`, `-parallelism`, `-rev_prefix` (default `refs/remotes/origin/`), `-base_url`, `-repo_cache`, `-index`, `-manifest_repo_url`, `-manifest_rev_prefix`, `-name`, `-url`, `-max_sub_projects` (debug), `-incremental` (default true). **`zoekt-sourcegraph-indexserver`** (14 flags): Sourcegraph-managed indexer — `-sourcegraph_url`, `-index`, `-listen`, `-hostname`, `-interval`, `-backoff_duration`, `-max_backoff_duration`, `-vacuum_interval`, `-merge_interval`, `-index_concurrency`, `-merge_target_size`, `-merge_min_size`, `-cpu_fraction`, `-shard_merging`, `-merge_min_age`. **`zoekt-test`** (5 flags + query positional): `-mem_profile FILE`, `-cpu_profile FILE`, `-repo`, `-indexDir`, `-case`. **Blacklist additions (10) across 1 insertion block**: all 10 entries inserted into the contiguous `zoekt-*` zone between `zoekt-archive-index` (29908) and `zoekt-webserver` (was 29914, now shifted) in canonical alphabetical positions. Surfaces transcribed directly from per-binary `cmd/zoekt-*/main.go` `flag.X` declarations at [github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt](https://github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt). Single multi-stem file `_zoekt-archive-index` is `#compdef zoekt-archive-index zoekt-index zoekt-merge-index zoekt-mirror-github zoekt-mirror-gitlab zoekt-webserver`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`zoekt-archive-index`** (7 flags + archive positional): index archive downloads — `-incremental` (default true), `-name`, `-url`, `-branch`, `-commit`, `-strip_components N`, `-download-limit-mbps N`. **`zoekt-index`** (3 flags + directory positional): index local directory — `-cpu_profile FILE`, `-ignore_dirs DIRS` (default `.git,.hg,.svn`), `-meta FILE` (.meta JSON). **`zoekt-merge-index`** (state-machine with 2 sub-actions): `merge` (combine shards into compound) and `explode` (split compound into parts). **`zoekt-mirror-github`** (13 flags incl. 3-value visibility enum): mirror GitHub repos — `-dest DIR`, `-url URL` (GitHub Enterprise; default github.com), `-org`, `-user`, `-token FILE` (default `$HOME/.github-token`), `-forks`, `-delete`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, repeatable `*-topic`, repeatable `*-exclude_topic`, `-no_archived`, repeatable `*-visibility` (`public`/`private`/`internal`). **`zoekt-mirror-gitlab`** (11 flags): mirror GitLab repos — `-dest`, `-url` (default GitLab.com API v4), `-token FILE`, `-membership`, `-public`, `-delete`, `-exclude_user`, `-name REGEX`, `-exclude REGEX`, `-last_activity_after DATE`, `-no_archived`. **`zoekt-webserver`** (16 flags): HTTP + RPC frontend — `-log_dir`, `-log_refresh` (default 24h), `-listen` (default `:6070`), `-index`, `-html` (default true), `-rpc`, `-indexserver_proxy`, `-print`, `-pprof`, `-ssl_cert`, `-ssl_key`, `-host_customization HOST1=QUERY,HOST2=QUERY`, `-template_dir`, `-dump_templates`, `-cors_origin`, `-version`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: 9 additional zoekt binaries deferred for later rounds — `zoekt-dynamic-indexserver`, `zoekt-git-clone`, `zoekt-indexserver`, `zoekt-mirror-bitbucket-server`, `zoekt-mirror-gerrit`, `zoekt-mirror-gitea`, `zoekt-mirror-gitiles`, `zoekt-repo-index`, `zoekt-sourcegraph-indexserver`, `zoekt-test` (similar surface patterns; future round). Plus `odo` already covered (deprecated 2025-10-23). **Blacklist additions (6) across 1 insertion block**: 6 consecutive insertions between `zoekt` (29907) and `zola` (29915) for `zoekt-archive-index` + `zoekt-index` + `zoekt-merge-index` + `zoekt-mirror-github` + `zoekt-mirror-gitlab` + `zoekt-webserver` (within zoekt-* at index 6 `a` (97, archive) < `g` (103, git) < `i` (105, index) < `m` (109, merge/mirror) < `w` (119, webserver); within zoekt-m* at index 7 `e` (101, merge) < `i` (105, mirror); within zoekt-mirror-* at index 14 `h` (104, hub) < `l` (108, lab)). Single-mode invocation `oats [flags] [test YAML files or directories]`. 13 flags: `-timeout DUR` (test-case timeout, default 30s), `-absent-timeout DUR` (absence-assertion timeout, default 10s), `-lgtm-version VER` (docker-otel-lgtm version, default `latest`), `-manual-debug` (keep containers running for debugging), `-lgtm-log-all` (logging for all LGTM containers), `-lgtm-log-grafana`/`-lgtm-log-loki`/`-lgtm-log-tempo`/`-lgtm-log-prometheus`/`-lgtm-log-pyroscope`/`-lgtm-log-collector` (per-container logging toggles), `-host HOST` (request-host override, default `localhost`), `-log-limit N` (max log output length per entry). Subcommands: `login` (with `--provider issuer,client_id[,client_secret,scopes]`, `-i KEY` custom key name, `--create-config` for `~/.opk/config.yml`), `logout` (with `-i KEY` to remove specific SSH key pair), `inspect PATH` (display SSH key/certificate details), `audit` (validate `/etc/opk/auth_id` + `/etc/opk/providers` consistency), `add <user> <identity> <issuer>` (with shorthand issuers `google`/`azure`/etc), `permissions` (3-action enum `check`/`fix`/`install`). **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `evemu-record` (7586) and `event_rpcgen.py` (7587) for `event-generator` (within ev* at index 3 `m` (109, mu-record) < `n` (110, nt*); within event* at index 5 `-` (45, -generator) < `_` (95, _rpcgen.py)); 1 between `opkg` (18648) and `opl2ofm` (18649) for `opkssh` (within op* at index 2 `j` (106, j_dump) < `k` (107, k*) < `l` (108, l2ofm); within opk* at index 3 `g` (103, g) < `s` (115, ssh)). Distributes validator private keys across multiple signer nodes via threshold Ed25519 signatures with Raft leader election and high watermark consensus. State-machine with **10 top-level subcommands**: `config` (with `init`/`migrate` actions; init carries 12 flags incl. `-m/--mode` 2-value enum `threshold`/`single`, repeatable `*-n/--node`, repeatable `*-c/--cosigner`, `-t/--threshold N`, `-d/--debug-addr`, `-k/--key-dir`, `--raft-timeout` default 500ms, `--grpc-timeout` default 500ms, `-o/--overwrite`, `--bare`, `-g/--gprc-address`, `--max-read-size` default 1048576), `start` (with `--accept-risk` for single-signer mode), `address`, `create-cosigner-ed25519-shards`, `create-cosigner-ecies-shards`, `create-cosigner-rsa-shards` (DEPRECATED), `elect` (raft leader election), `leader` (show current leader), `state` (with `show`/`s`/`set`/`import`/`i` 5-action enum; `show <chain-id>`, `set <chain-id> <height>`, `import <chain-id>` for legacy priv_validator_state.json migration), `version`. Persistent flag `--home DIR` (default `$HOME/.horcrux`). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `aws-lambda-rie` (env-var driven, no CLI flag surface), `amazon-cloudwatch-agent` (config-file driven), `copilot` already covered, `cozystack` no CLI binary, `letsencrypt boulder` no end-user CLI, `qiskit`/`pennylane`/`qsc` quantum libraries not CLIs. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `hopsworks-mcp` (11194) and `hornet` (11195) for `horcrux` (within ho* at index 2 `p` (112, p*) < `r` (114, r*); within hor* at index 3 `c` (99, crux) < `n` (110, net)). Common channel-group flags: `-o/--orderer-address`, `--ca-file`, `--client-cert`, `--client-key`, `--no-status`, `--help`. **`peer`** (state-machine with 5 top-level subcommands + nested sub-action groups + 11 global TLS/orderer flags): hyperledger/fabric main peer-node client. Top-level: `chaincode` (with `invoke`/`query` actions + flags `-C/--channelID`/`-c/--ctor`/`-n/--name`/repeatable `*--peerAddresses`/`--waitForEvent` for invoke; query adds `-x/--hex`/`-r/--raw`), `channel` (9 actions: `create` DEPRECATED/`fetch`/`getinfo`/`join`/`joinbysnapshot`/`joinbysnapshotstatus`/`list`/`signconfigtx`/`update` with per-action flags `-b/--blockpath` (join), `--snapshotpath` (joinbysnapshot), `--bestEffort` (fetch), `-f/--file` + `-t/--timeout` (create), `-c/--channelID` + `-f/--file` (getinfo/signconfigtx/update)), `node` (8 actions: `pause`/`rebuild-dbs`/`reset`/`resume`/`rollback`/`start`/`unjoin`/`upgrade-dbs` with `-c/--channelID` (pause/resume/unjoin), `-b/--blockNumber` + `-c/--channelID` (rollback), `--peer-chaincodedev` (start)), `lifecycle chaincode` (10 actions: `package`/`install`/`queryinstalled`/`getinstalledpackage`/`calculatepackageid`/`approveformyorg`/`queryapproved`/`checkcommitreadiness`/`commit`/`querycommitted`), `version`. Primary subcommands `run` (execute EVM bytecode), `blocktest`, `statetest`, `transition` (alias `t8n` — full state transition), `transaction` (alias `t9n` — transaction validation), `block-builder` (alias `b11r`), `verkle` (alias `vkt` — binary-trie helpers). Verkle sub-actions: `tree-keys` (alias `v`), `single-key` (alias `vk`), `code-chunk-key` (alias `vck`), `chunkify-code` (alias `vcc`), `state-root` (alias `vsr`). Plus 6 grouped sub-actions: `notes` (4-value enum `add`/`list`/`remove`/`show`), `replace` (`delete`/`list`), `stash` (`list`/`pop`/`push`), `submodule` (`add`/`init`/`list`/`update`), `worktree` (8-value enum `add`/`list`/`lock`/`move`/`prune`/`remove`/`repair`/`unlock`), `remote` (`add`). Total: 85 top-level verbs + 6 grouped sub-action sets covering ~100+ CLI entry points. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `duk` (6871) and `dum` (6872) for `dulwich` (within du* at index 2 `k` (107, k) < `l` (108, lwich) < `m` (109, m*)). - **corona-cli R34 — corona (1 stem; COVID-19 stats tracker)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream README at [github.com/ahmadawais/corona-cli](https://github.com/ahmadawais/corona-cli). Single-stem file `_corona` is `#compdef corona`. **Coverage**: ahmadawais/corona-cli tracks COVID-19 statistics globally (`npm i -g corona-cli`, invoked as `corona`). 8 flag pairs + 1 standalone (`-g`) + 9-field sort enum + 1 positional. Sort enum (`-s/--sort` with `-r/--reverse`): `country`/`cases`/`cases-today`/`deaths`/`deaths-today`/`recovered`/`active`/`critical`/`per-million`. Chart toggles `-b/--bar` (bar chart, max 10 countries), `-c/--chart` (line chart), `-g` (logarithmic scale). Output control `-l/--limit N` (entries displayed), `-m/--minimal` (bare-bones table), `-x/--xcolor` (single-color), `--help`. Positional 1 = target (`states` for US states, or country name like `china`/`usa`/etc). Data sources aggregate WHO/CDC. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `corkscrew` (4526) and `corosync` (4527) for `corona` (within cor* at index 3 `k` (107, kscrew) < `o` (111, ona/osync); within coro* at index 4 `n` (110, na) < `s` (115, sync)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — fortieth consecutive clean round.

Single-stem file `_insanely-fast-whisper` is `#compdef insanely-fast-whisper`. **Coverage**: Vaibhavs10/insanely-fast-whisper is a Flash Attention 2 optimized ASR CLI built on `openai/whisper-large-v3`. 14 well-documented flags: `--file-name FILE_OR_URL` (required), `--device-id` (CUDA ID 0/1/2/3 or `mps` for Mac, default 0), `--transcript-path FILE` (default output.json), `--model-name CHECKPOINT` (default `openai/whisper-large-v3`), `--task` 2-value enum (`transcribe`/`translate`, default transcribe), `--language LANG` (auto-detect if omitted), `--batch-size N` (default 24), `--flash` (enable Flash Attention 2), `--timestamp` 2-value enum (`chunk`/`word`, default chunk), and 5 speaker-diarization flags (`--hf-token TOKEN`, `--diarization_model MODEL` default `pyannote/speaker-diarization`, `--num-speakers N` exact count, `--min-speakers N`, `--max-speakers N`). Benchmarks: A100 GPU transcribes 150 minutes of audio in ~1m38s with Large-v3 + Flash Attention 2. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `inputlirc` (11971) and `insmod` (11972) for `insanely-fast-whisper` (within in* at index 2 `p` (112, putlirc) < `s` (115, s*); within ins* at index 3 `a` (97, anely-fast-whisper) < `m` (109, mod)). - **nihui ncnn+Vulkan family R32 — cain-ncnn-vulkan + dain-ncnn-vulkan + rife-ncnn-vulkan + srmd-ncnn-vulkan (4 stems via single multi-stem `_cain-ncnn-vulkan` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues image-processing harvest after R31. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/nihui/cain-ncnn-vulkan](https://github.com/nihui/cain-ncnn-vulkan), [github.com/nihui/dain-ncnn-vulkan](https://github.com/nihui/dain-ncnn-vulkan), [github.com/nihui/rife-ncnn-vulkan](https://github.com/nihui/rife-ncnn-vulkan), and [github.com/nihui/srmd-ncnn-vulkan](https://github.com/nihui/srmd-ncnn-vulkan). Sister-tools to the existing `_waifu2x-ncnn-vulkan` + R31's `_realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan`/`_realsr-ncnn-vulkan` coverage. Single multi-stem file `_cain-ncnn-vulkan` is `#compdef cain-ncnn-vulkan dain-ncnn-vulkan rife-ncnn-vulkan srmd-ncnn-vulkan`, dispatching on `$service`. All share the nihui common dialect: `-i`/`-o`/`-g`/`-j`/`-m`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`cain-ncnn-vulkan`** (8 flags): CAIN (Channel Attention Is All You Need) video frame interpolation — two modes (`-0 FRAME0 -1 FRAME1 -o OUT` 2-frame OR `-i INDIR -o OUTDIR` batch). Flags `-0`/`-1`/`-i`/`-o`/`-m MODEL_DIR`/`-g GPU_ID`/`-j LOAD:PROC:SAVE`/`-f PATTERN`. **`dain-ncnn-vulkan`** (11 flags): DAIN (Depth-Aware Video Frame Interpolation) — same dual-mode interface, adds `-n FRAME_COUNT` (default 2x input), `-s STEP` (0-1, default 0.5), `-t TILE_SIZE` (≥128, default 256). **`rife-ncnn-vulkan`** (12 flags): RIFE (Real-Time Intermediate Flow Estimation) supporting v1.2–v4.6 models incl. anime variants — same dual-mode, adds `-n FRAME_COUNT`, `-s STEP`, `-x` (spatial TTA), `-z` (temporal TTA), `-u` (UHD mode). **`srmd-ncnn-vulkan`** (9 flags + 3-scale enum): SRMD super-resolution + denoiser — single-image mode with `-i IMG -o OUT -n DENOISE_LEVEL` (-1..10, default 3) `-s SCALE` (`2`/`3`/`4`) `-t TILE_SIZE` `-x` (TTA). **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `cai` (2878) and `cairo-compile` (2879) for `cain-ncnn-vulkan` (within cai* at index 3 end-of-string < `n` (110, n*); within cai* at index 3 `n` (110, in-ncnn-vulkan) < `r` (114, ro-compile)); 1 between `dagu` (5610) and `dak` (5611) for `dain-ncnn-vulkan` (within da* at index 2 `g` (103, gu) < `i` (105, in-ncnn-vulkan) < `k` (107, k)); 1 between `rider` (22505) and `riff` (22506) for `rife-ncnn-vulkan` (within ri* at index 2 `d` (100, der) < `f` (102, fe/ff); within rif* at index 3 `e` (101, e-ncnn-vulkan) < `f` (102, f)); 1 between `srm` (24723) and `srptool` (24724) for `srmd-ncnn-vulkan` (within srm* at index 3 end-of-string < `d` (100, d-ncnn-vulkan); within sr* at index 2 `m` (109, m*) < `p` (112, ptool)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-eighth consecutive clean round.
- **Image-processing R31 — realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan + rembg (2 stems via single multi-stem `_realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/xinntao/Real-ESRGAN](https://github.com/xinntao/Real-ESRGAN) and [github.com/danielgatis/rembg](https://github.com/danielgatis/rembg). Single multi-stem file `_realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan` is `#compdef realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan rembg`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan`** (12 flags + 4-model enum + 3-scale enum + 3-format enum): xinntao/Real-ESRGAN portable NCNN+Vulkan image upscaler — `-h` (help), `-i input`, `-o output`, `-s SCALE` (`2`/`3`/`4`, default 4), `-t TILE_SIZE` (≥32, 0=auto), `-m MODEL_DIR`, `-n MODEL_NAME` (4-value enum: `realesr-animevideov3`/`realesrgan-x4plus`/`realesrgan-x4plus-anime`/`realesrnet-x4plus`), `-g GPU_ID` (auto), `-j LOAD:PROC:SAVE` (default 1:2:2), `-x` (TTA mode), `-f FORMAT` (`jpg`/`png`/`webp`), `-v` (verbose). **`rembg`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands + 16-model enum + 5-loglevel enum + per-subcommand flag sets): danielgatis/rembg CLI background remover (CPU/CUDA/ROCm variants) — subcommands `i` (single file with `-m MODEL`/`-om` mask-only/`-a` alpha matting/`-x JSON` extra params + 2 positionals), `p` (batch folder, adds `-w` watch mode), `s` (HTTP server with `--host`/`--port`/`--log_level`/`--no-ui`), `b` (RGB24 binary stream with `-o` printf-style + 2 positionals width/height). 16-model enum spanning u2net/u2netp/u2net_human_seg/u2net_cloth_seg/silueta/isnet-general-use/isnet-anime/sam/birefnet-general/birefnet-general-lite/birefnet-portrait/birefnet-dis/birefnet-hrsod/birefnet-cod/birefnet-massive/bria-rmbg. **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `readtapi` (22145) and `realm` (22146) for `realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan` (within rea* at index 3 `d` (100, dtapi) < `l` (108, l*); within real* at index 4 `e` (101, esrgan) < `m` (109, m)); 1 between `remarshal` (22297) and `remctl` (22298) for `rembg` (within rem* at index 3 `a` (97, ap/ark/arshal) < `b` (98, bg) < `c` (99, ctl)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-seventh consecutive clean round.
- **Nim koch R30 — koch (1 stem; build orchestrator for the Nim compiler)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream docs at [nim-lang.org/docs/koch.html](https://nim-lang.org/docs/koch.html). Single-stem file `_koch` is `#compdef koch`. **Coverage**: `koch` is Nim's build orchestrator for the compiler itself (separate from `nim`/`nimble`/`nimsuggest`/`nimpretty` which are already covered). State-machine dispatches on **7 subcommands** (one alias): `boot` (bootstrap), `csource` (build C sources for install), `temp` (build compiler as `nim_temp` for testing), `test` / `tests` (alias; compile + run test suite), `web` (RST→HTML docs), `pdf` (PDF docs). Per-verb flags: `boot`/`csource` accept 3 `-d:` define flags (`-d:release` for optimized vs debug, `-d:nimUseLinenoise` for linenoise interactive mode on non-Windows, `-d:leanCompiler` to omit JS codegen + docs generator); `web` accepts `--parallelBuild=N` (1 = serial). **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `koboldcpp` (13219) and `kodak2ti3` (13220) for `koch` (within ko* at index 2 `b` (98, boldcpp) < `c` (99, ch) < `d` (100, dak/de/di)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-sixth consecutive clean round.
- **Mixed-theme R29 — node-llama-cpp + safe-cli + safe-creator (3 stems via single multi-stem `_node-llama-cpp` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream README + docs at [github.com/withcatai/node-llama-cpp](https://github.com/withcatai/node-llama-cpp) (+ [node-llama-cpp.withcat.ai/cli](https://node-llama-cpp.withcat.ai/cli)) and [github.com/safe-global/safe-cli](https://github.com/safe-global/safe-cli). Single multi-stem file `_node-llama-cpp` is `#compdef node-llama-cpp safe-cli safe-creator`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`node-llama-cpp`** (state-machine with 7 subcommands; typically invoked via `npx --no node-llama-cpp <cmd>`): withcatai/node-llama-cpp is Node.js bindings for llama.cpp with GPU acceleration support (Metal/CUDA/Vulkan), JSON-schema output enforcement, function calling, embedding/reranking — subcommands `pull` (download from URL), `chat` (interactive), `init` (template scaffold), `complete`, `infill` (prefix/suffix-bracketed completion), `inspect` (GPU/GGUF/perf), `source` (manage underlying llama.cpp codebase). Global flags `-h`/`--help`, `-v`/`--version`. **`safe-cli`** (state-machine with 6 unattended subcommands + 2 positional + 5 boolean toggles): safe-global/safe-cli manages Ethereum Safe smart-contract accounts independently of the Safe{Core} API — positional 1 = Safe checksum address (or owner address with `--get-safes-from-owner`), positional 2 = Ethereum node URL. Flags `--history`/`--no-history` (default off for security), `--get-safes-from-owner`/`--no-get-safes-from-owner`, `--non-interactive` (unattended), `--help`. Unattended commands: `send-ether`, `send-erc20`, `send-erc721`, `send-custom` (custom tx data), `tx-builder` (JSON file), `version`. **`safe-creator`** (9 flags + 2 positionals): safe-global/safe-creator deploys new Safe contracts — positional 1 = node URL, positional 2 = deployer private key. Flags `--threshold N` (default deployer only), `--owners ADDRESSES`, `--safe-contract ADDR` (custom master copy), `--proxy-factory ADDR`, `--callback-handler ADDR`, `--salt-nonce NONCE` (deterministic addresses), `--without-events` (gas saving), `--generate-vanity-addresses` (preview without deploy), `--no-confirm`, `-v`/`--version`. **Blacklist additions (3) across 2 insertion blocks** (safe-cli + safe-creator share one block): 1 between `node-gyp` (17812) and `node-red` (17813) for `node-llama-cpp` (within node-* at index 5 `g` (103, gyp) < `l` (108, llama) < `r` (114, red)); **2 between `safe-bundle` (23154) and `safe-rm` (23155)** for `safe-cli` + `safe-creator` (within safe-* at index 5 `b` (98, bundle) < `c` (99, cli/creator); within safe-c* at index 6 `l` (108, cli) < `r` (114, creator); back to safe-* at index 5 `c` (99) < `r` (114, rm)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-fifth consecutive clean round.
- **Anchore sister-CLI harvest R28 — binny + chronicle + grant (3 stems via single multi-stem `_binny` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continuation of the Anchore Go/clio-based CLI family already represented by previously-covered `_grype-cli`/`_syft-cli`/`_cocogitto`/`_clio`/`_quill`. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/anchore/binny](https://github.com/anchore/binny), [github.com/anchore/chronicle](https://github.com/anchore/chronicle), and [github.com/anchore/grant](https://github.com/anchore/grant). Single multi-stem file `_binny` is `#compdef binny chronicle grant`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`binny`** (state-machine with 6 subcommands): anchore/binny manages a directory of binaries via `.binny.yaml` without a traditional package manager — `install` (deploy all), `check`, `update` (refresh pinned versions within constraints), `list`, `add`, `remove`. Acquisition methods: github-release, go-install, go-build, hosted-shell. Includes supply-chain cooldown for malicious-version detection. **`chronicle`** (5 flag pairs + positional + 4-format enum): anchore/chronicle is a GitHub-aware changelog generator — `-o/--output FORMAT` (`md`/`md-pretty`/`json`/`version`), `-s/--since-tag TAG` (default: last release), `-u/--until-tag TAG` (default: HEAD), `-n/--speculate-next-version` (guess next release), `-q` (suppress logging); positional 1 = repository path. **`grant`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands + repeatable target): anchore/grant is a license scanner for container images, SBOMs, and filesystems — `list` (enumerate licenses), `check` (validate against policy). Targets accept container image refs, `sbom:FILE`, `dir:PATH`, or filesystem paths. **Blacklist additions (3) across 3 insertion blocks**: 1 between `binmiscctl` (2204) and `binocle` (2205) for `binny` (within bin* at index 3 `m` (109, miscctl) < `n` (110, ny) < `o` (111, ocle)); 1 between `chronic` (3659) and `chronoctl` (3660) for `chronicle` (within chronic* at index 7 end-of-string < `l` (108, le); within chron* at index 5 `i` (105, ic*) < `o` (111, octl)); 1 between `granian` (line 10268, post-shift) and `granted` (10269) for `grant` (within gran* at index 4 `i` (105, ian) < `t` (116, t/ted); within grant* at index 5 end-of-string < `e` (101, ed)). Mid-edit slot-misordering caught + fixed: initial placement assumed `grant < granatier` but actual order is `granatier < granian < grant < granted` because at index 4 `granatier`'s `a` (97) precedes `grant`'s `t` (116) — `LC_ALL=C sort -c` caught it; one re-edit + re-verify. LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-fourth consecutive clean round.
- **Mixed-theme R27 — comfy + sloglint (2 stems; ComfyUI manager + Go log/slog linter)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Pivot out of pure Go-linter territory after R21-R26. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/Comfy-Org/comfy-cli](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/comfy-cli) and [github.com/go-simpler/sloglint](https://github.com/go-simpler/sloglint). Single multi-stem file `_comfy` is `#compdef comfy sloglint`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`comfy`** (state-machine with **12 top-level subcommands**, nested state-machines for `node`/`model`/`generate`/`manager`/`pr-cache`): Comfy-Org/comfy-cli is a Python-based ComfyUI manager (binary `comfy`, installed via `pip install comfy-cli`). Top-level: `install` (with `--skip-manager`/`--fast-deps`/`--workspace`/`--pr`), `launch` (with `--background`/`--frontend-pr`/`-- ARGS` passthrough), `env`, `run`, `set-default PATH`, `which`, `pr-cache` (list/clean sub-actions), `node` (13 sub-actions incl. show/install/update/reinstall/fix/uninstall/install-deps/deps-in-workflow/uv-sync/save-snapshot/restore-snapshot/bisect with show taking 7-value enum installed/enabled/all/disabled/not-installed/snapshot/snapshot-list and bisect taking 4-value action start/good/bad/reset), `model` (download with `--url`/`--set-civitai-api-token`/`--set-hf-api-token`, list, remove with `--model-names`), `generate` (3 utility sub-actions list/schema/resume plus **16 hosted partner models** Flux/Flux-Pro/Ideogram/DALL·E/Recraft/Stability/Runway/Kling/Luma/Pika/Vidu/Hailuo/Seedance/Gemini-Flash-Image; generate flags `--prompt`/`--download`/`--async`/repeatable `--image`/`--api-key`), `manager` (7 sub-actions disable/enable-gui/disable-gui/enable-legacy-gui/clear/migrate-legacy/uv-compile-default), `tracking disable`. Plus core workspace-selector flags `--workspace=PATH`/`--recent`/`--here` available on most subcommands, plus `--install-completion` for typer-style shell-completion install. **`sloglint`** (positional only): go-simpler/sloglint is a Go `log/slog` style linter (enforces global-logger detection, context requirement, static message validation, key-value vs. attribute consistency, key naming conventions snake_case/kebab-case/camelCase/PascalCase); standalone surface is minimal because configuration is golangci-lint primary. **Blacklist additions (2) across 2 insertion blocks**: 1 between `cometbft` (4261) and `comm` (4262) for `comfy` (within com* at index 3 `c` (99, cast/cetbft? no — `cometbft` index 3='e') < `e` (101, et/etbft) < `f` (102, fy) < `m` (109, m)); 1 between `slogkey` (24121) and `slogverify` (24122) for `sloglint` (within slog* at index 4 `k` (107, key) < `l` (108, lint) < `v` (118, verify)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-third consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter sweep R26 — bidichk + inamedparam + mnd + tagalign + tagliatelle + usetesting (6 stems via single multi-stem `_bidichk` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21-R25 sweep with 6 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/breml/bidichk](https://github.com/breml/bidichk), [github.com/macabu/inamedparam](https://github.com/macabu/inamedparam), [github.com/tommy-muehle/go-mnd](https://github.com/tommy-muehle/go-mnd), [github.com/4meepo/tagalign](https://github.com/4meepo/tagalign), [github.com/ldez/tagliatelle](https://github.com/ldez/tagliatelle), and [github.com/ldez/usetesting](https://github.com/ldez/usetesting). Single multi-stem file `_bidichk` is `#compdef bidichk inamedparam mnd tagalign tagliatelle usetesting`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`bidichk`** (1 flag + 9-rune enum + positional): breml/bidichk detects Trojan-Source Unicode bidirectional formatting characters (U+202A-U+202E + U+2066-U+2069) — `--disallowed-runes RUNES` (comma-separated; 9-value enum LEFT-TO-RIGHT-EMBEDDING/RIGHT-TO-LEFT-EMBEDDING/POP-DIRECTIONAL-FORMATTING/LEFT-TO-RIGHT-OVERRIDE/RIGHT-TO-LEFT-OVERRIDE/LEFT-TO-RIGHT-ISOLATE/RIGHT-TO-LEFT-ISOLATE/FIRST-STRONG-ISOLATE/POP-DIRECTIONAL-ISOLATE). **`inamedparam`** (1 flag + positional): macabu/inamedparam flags interface methods with unnamed parameters — `-skip-single-param` (exempt single-unnamed-param interfaces). **`mnd`** (4 flags + 6-check enum + positional): tommy-muehle/go-mnd is a magic-number detector — `-checks CHECKS` (6-value enum: `argument`/`assign`/`case`/`condition`/`operation`/`return`, comma-separated), `-ignored-numbers`, `-ignored-functions PATTERNS`, `-ignored-files PATTERNS`. Defaults exclude 0/1, test files, and `time.Date()`. **`tagalign`** (4 flags + positional): 4meepo/tagalign aligns and optionally sorts struct tags — `-fix`, `-sort`, `-order ORDER` (e.g. `json,xml`), `-strict` (dictionary-order grouped-names alignment). **`tagliatelle`** (positional only): ldez/tagliatelle is a struct-tag naming-convention linter; configuration is golangci-lint-primary (camelCase/PascalCase/kebab-case/snake_case/UPPER_SNAKE_CASE/Go-aware variants/header-case/upper/lower per tag), standalone surface is minimal. **`usetesting`** (positional only): ldez/usetesting suggests `t.TempDir`/`t.Setenv`/`t.Chdir`/`t.Context` replacements for `os.*` and `context.Background`/`context.TODO` calls in tests (Go 1.24+ for some); per-detection enable flags configured via golangci-lint primarily. **Blacklist additions (6) across 5 insertion blocks** (tagalign + tagliatelle share one block): 1 between `bicep` (2184) and `biff` (2185) for `bidichk` (within bi* at index 2 `c` (99, cep) < `d` (100, dichk) < `f` (102, ff)); 1 between `inadyn` (11885) and `inbucket` (11886) for `inamedparam` (within ina* at index 3 `d` (100, dyn) < `m` (109, medparam); within in* at index 2 `a` (97, a*) < `b` (98, bucket)); 1 between `mn` (16511) and `mneme` (16512) for `mnd` (within mn* at index 2 end-of-string < `d` (100, d) < `e` (101, eme)); **2 between `taffybar` (25501) and `taglib-config` (25502)** for `tagalign` + `tagliatelle` (within ta* at index 2 `f` (102, ffybar) < `g` (103, g*); within tag* at index 3 `a` (97, align) < `l` (108, liatelle/lib); within tagl* at index 4 `i` (105, both), at index 5 `a` (97, atelle) < `b` (98, b-config)); 1 between `usestdlibvars` (27358) and `usleep` (27359) for `usetesting` (within us* at index 2 `e` (101, e*) < `l` (108, leep); within use* at index 3 `s` (115, stdlibvars) < `t` (116, testing)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-second consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter sweep R25 — copyloopvar + dupword + exhaustivestruct + fatcontext + funcorder + promlinter + testableexamples (7 stems via single multi-stem `_copyloopvar` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21+R22+R23+R24 sweep with 7 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/karamaru-alpha/copyloopvar](https://github.com/karamaru-alpha/copyloopvar), [github.com/Abirdcfly/dupword](https://github.com/Abirdcfly/dupword), [github.com/mbilski/exhaustivestruct](https://github.com/mbilski/exhaustivestruct), [github.com/Crocmagnon/fatcontext](https://github.com/Crocmagnon/fatcontext), [github.com/manuelarte/funcorder](https://github.com/manuelarte/funcorder), [github.com/yeya24/promlinter](https://github.com/yeya24/promlinter), and [github.com/maratori/testableexamples](https://github.com/maratori/testableexamples). Single multi-stem file `_copyloopvar` is `#compdef copyloopvar dupword exhaustivestruct fatcontext funcorder promlinter testableexamples`, dispatching on `$service`. **Hunt-note**: `exhaustivestruct` is archived 2022-12-06 but preserved here for historical-codebase use via golangci-lint v1.x. **Per-stem coverage**: **`copyloopvar`** (positional only): karamaru-alpha/copyloopvar flags unnecessary loop-variable copies (`v := v`, `i := i`) addressable since Go 1.22 loop-variable semantics. **`dupword`** (3 flags + positional): Abirdcfly/dupword detects duplicate words in comments + strings — `-fix`, `-keyword WORDS` (target words comma-separated), `-test=true|false` (analyze test files). **`exhaustivestruct`** (1 flag + positional): mbilski/exhaustivestruct flags struct literals with uninitialized fields — `-struct_patterns PATTERNS` (comma-separated package+name matchers). *ARCHIVED 2022-12-06.* **`fatcontext`** (positional only): Crocmagnon/fatcontext detects nested contexts in loops or function literals (e.g. `ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, ...)` inside a loop without `:=` rebinding). **`funcorder`** (4 bool flags + positional): manuelarte/funcorder enforces function/method/constructor ordering per Uber style guide — `-constructor=true|false`, `-struct-method=true|false`, `-alphabetical=true|false`, `-function=true|false`. **`promlinter`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands): yeya24/promlinter is a Prometheus metrics-naming linter built on promlint — `list` (display discovered metrics) and `lint` with `--disable CHECKS` + `--strict`. **`testableexamples`** (positional only): maratori/testableexamples flags Example functions missing `// Output:` comments (without them, `go test` compiles but doesn't execute them — silent rot). **Blacklist additions (7) across 7 insertion blocks**: 1 between `copybara` (4491) and `copyparty` (4492) for `copyloopvar` (within copy* at index 4 `b` (98, bara) < `l` (108, loopvar) < `p` (112, party)); 1 between `duply` (6912) and `dura` (6913) for `dupword` (within dup* at index 3 `l` (108, ly) < `w` (119, word); within du* at index 2 `p` (112, p*) < `r` (114, ra)); 1 between `exhaustive` (7631) and `exif` (7632) for `exhaustivestruct` (within exhaustive* at index 10 end-of-string < `s` (115, struct); within ex* at index 2 `h` (104, haustive*) < `i` (105, if)); 1 between `fatbinary` (7860) and `fatlabel` (7861) for `fatcontext` (within fat* at index 3 `b` (98, binary) < `c` (99, context) < `l` (108, label)); 1 between `funclatency-bpfcc` (8601) and `funcslower` (8602) for `funcorder` (within func* at index 4 `l` (108, latency) < `o` (111, order) < `s` (115, slower)); 1 between `prometheus-config-reloader` (20804) and `prompt` (20805) for `promlinter` (within prom* at index 4 `e` (101, etheus) < `l` (108, linter) < `p` (112, pt)); 1 between `test_be` (25893) and `testament` (25894) for `testableexamples` (within test* at index 4 `_` (95, _be) < `a` (97, a*); within testa* at index 5 `b` (98, bleex) < `m` (109, ment)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirty-first consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter sweep R24 — forcetypeassert + gomoddirectives + interfacebloat + loggercheck + mirror + nestif + nilnil + rowserrcheck (8 stems via single multi-stem `_forcetypeassert` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21+R22+R23 sweep with 8 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/gostaticanalysis/forcetypeassert](https://github.com/gostaticanalysis/forcetypeassert), [github.com/ldez/gomoddirectives](https://github.com/ldez/gomoddirectives), [github.com/sashamelentyev/interfacebloat](https://github.com/sashamelentyev/interfacebloat), [github.com/timonwong/loggercheck](https://github.com/timonwong/loggercheck), [github.com/butuzov/mirror](https://github.com/butuzov/mirror), [github.com/nakabonne/nestif](https://github.com/nakabonne/nestif), [github.com/Antonboom/nilnil](https://github.com/Antonboom/nilnil), and [github.com/jingyugao/rowserrcheck](https://github.com/jingyugao/rowserrcheck). Single multi-stem file `_forcetypeassert` is `#compdef forcetypeassert gomoddirectives interfacebloat loggercheck mirror nestif nilnil rowserrcheck`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`forcetypeassert`** (positional only): gostaticanalysis/forcetypeassert flags unchecked type assertions, enforcing the 2-return form `value, ok := a.(int)`. **`gomoddirectives`** (3 flags + positional): ldez/gomoddirectives enforces policies on `go.mod` directives (retract/replace/exclude/ignore/tool/toolchain/godebug/go/module) — `-local` (allow local replacements), `-exclude` (forbid exclude directives), `-goversion PATTERN` (Go version regex). **`interfacebloat`** (positional only): sashamelentyev/interfacebloat flags interfaces with too many methods per Interface Segregation Principle. **`loggercheck`** (4 flags + 5-logger enum + positional): timonwong/loggercheck detects odd-arity key-value pairs in structured logging (`kitlog`/`klog`/`logr`/`slog`/`zap`) — `-requirestringkey`, `-disable LOGGERS` (5-value enum, comma-separated), `-fix`, `-rulefile FILE`. **`mirror`** (1 flag + positional): butuzov/mirror suggests mirror stdlib functions to avoid `[]byte`/`string` conversions (e.g. `MatchString` instead of `Match` after string→[]byte) — `--with-tests`. **`nestif`** (4 flags + positional): nakabonne/nestif is a cognitive-complexity-of-nested-if linter — `--min N` (threshold, default 1), `--top N` (default 10), `--json`, `--exclude-dirs DIRS`. **`nilnil`** (3 flags + 7-type enum + positional): Antonboom/nilnil flags `return nil, nil` patterns — `--checked-types TYPES` (7-value enum: `chan`/`func`/`iface`/`map`/`ptr`/`uintptr`/`unsafeptr`, comma-separated), `--detect-opposite` (also flag non-nil errors with valid values), `--only-two=true|false` (restrict to 2-return functions). **`rowserrcheck`** (positional only): jingyugao/rowserrcheck checks `sql.Rows.Err()` is called after iteration (forked from bodyclose, addresses silent data-integrity issues). **Blacklist additions (8) across 8 insertion blocks**: 1 between `force` (8382) and `forego` (8383) for `forcetypeassert` (within force* at index 5 end-of-string < `t` (116, type); within for* at index 3 `c` (99, c*) < `e` (101, ego)); 1 between `gomock` (9992) and `gomodifytags` (9993) for `gomoddirectives` (within gomod* at index 5 `d` (100, directives) < `i` (105, ifytags); within gom* at index 4 `c` (99, ock) < `d` (100, od*)); 1 between `interdiff` (12007) and `interlace` (12008) for `interfacebloat` (within inter* at index 5 `d` (100, diff) < `f` (102, facebloat) < `l` (108, lace)); 1 between `logger` (14966) and `login` (14967) for `loggercheck` (within logger* at index 6 end-of-string < `c` (99, check); within log* at index 5 `e` (101, ger*) < `i` (105, in)); 1 between `miri` (16261) and `mirrord` (16262) for `mirror` (within mir* at index 3 `i` (105, miri) < `r` (114, ror); within mirror* at index 6 end-of-string < `d` (100, rrord)); 1 between `nest-cli` (17433) and `nestjs` (17434) for `nestif` (within nest* at index 4 `-` (45, -cli) < `i` (105, if) < `j` (106, js)); 1 between `nilfs_cleanerd` (17663) and `nim` (17664) for `nilnil` (within nil* at index 3 `f` (102, fs*) < `n` (110, nil); within ni* at index 2 `l` (108, l*) < `m` (109, m)); 1 between `rover` (22674) and `rox-filer` (22675) for `rowserrcheck` (within ro* at index 2 `v` (118, ver) < `w` (119, ws*) < `x` (120, x*)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — thirtieth consecutive clean round (one slot misordering caught + fixed mid-edit; interfacebloat alphabetizes between interdiff and interlace, not between interpreter and interrogate, because `f` (102) precedes `l` (108) at index 5).
- **Go linter sweep R23 — canonicalheader + durationcheck + errchkjson + errname + exhaustive + gochecknoglobals + intrange + ireturn + spancheck (9 stems via single multi-stem `_canonicalheader` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21+R22 sweep with 9 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/lasiar/canonicalheader](https://github.com/lasiar/canonicalheader), [github.com/charithe/durationcheck](https://github.com/charithe/durationcheck), [github.com/breml/errchkjson](https://github.com/breml/errchkjson), [github.com/Antonboom/errname](https://github.com/Antonboom/errname), [github.com/nishanths/exhaustive](https://github.com/nishanths/exhaustive), [github.com/leighmcculloch/gochecknoglobals](https://github.com/leighmcculloch/gochecknoglobals), [github.com/ckaznocha/intrange](https://github.com/ckaznocha/intrange), [github.com/butuzov/ireturn](https://github.com/butuzov/ireturn), and [github.com/jjti/go-spancheck](https://github.com/jjti/go-spancheck). Single multi-stem file `_canonicalheader` is `#compdef canonicalheader durationcheck errchkjson errname exhaustive gochecknoglobals intrange ireturn spancheck`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`canonicalheader`** (positional only): lasiar/canonicalheader checks HTTP header names use canonical capitalization on `http.Header` Get/Set/Add/Del/Values methods. **`durationcheck`** (positional only): charithe/durationcheck detects erroneous `time.Duration` multiplications (e.g. `waitForSeconds(5 * time.Second)` producing ~1388 hours). **`errchkjson`** (1 flag + positional): breml/errchkjson analyzes types passed to `json.Marshal` for safe (basic types) / unsafe (float, interface{}) / forbidden (complex, chan, func, unsafe.Pointer) classification — `-omit-safe`. **`errname`** (positional only): Antonboom/errname enforces sentinel `ErrXxx`/`errXxx` + `XxxError` type naming conventions per Go stdlib. **`exhaustive`** (1 flag + positional): nishanths/exhaustive switch + map exhaustiveness checker for enum-like constant groups — `-check switch|map|switch,map`. **`gochecknoglobals`** (1 flag + positional): leighmcculloch/gochecknoglobals flags global variables (smart-ignores err vars, regex.Compile, embedded files, `_`) — `-test=true|false` (analyze test files). **`intrange`** (positional only): ckaznocha/intrange suggests Go 1.22 integer-range syntax for `for i := 0; i < n; i++ {}` patterns. **`ireturn`** (2 flags + positional + 5 shorthand keywords): butuzov/ireturn enforces "accept interfaces, return concrete types" — `--accept INTERFACES` / `--reject INTERFACES` (comma-separated; shorthand keywords `empty`/`anon`/`error`/`stdlib`/`generic`). **`spancheck`** (3 flags + positional): jjti/go-spancheck is an OpenTelemetry/OpenCensus span linter — `-checks end,set-status,record-error` (3-value enum), `-extra-start-span-signatures SIGS`, `-ignore-check-signatures SIGS`. **Blacklist additions (9) across 9 insertion blocks**: 1 between `canlogserver` (2956) and `canplayer` (2957) for `canonicalheader` (within can* at index 3 `l` (108, logserver) < `o` (111, onicalheader) < `p` (112, player)); 1 between `dura` (6912) and `durdraw` (6913) for `durationcheck` (within dura* at index 4 end-of-string < `t` (116, tion); within dur* at index 3 `a` (97, a*) < `d` (100, draw)); 1 between `errcheck` (7437) and `errinfo` (7438) for `errchkjson` (within errch* at index 5 `e` (101, check) < `k` (107, chkjson); within err* at index 4 `c` (99, check) < `i` (105, info)); 1 between `errinfo` (7438 → line shifted) and `errno` (7439) for `errname` (within err* at index 4 `i` (105, info) < `n` (110, name/no); within errn* at index 5 `a` (97, name) < `o` (111, no)); 1 between `exfatlabel` (7626) and `exif` (7627) for `exhaustive` (within ex* at index 2 `f` (102, fat) < `h` (104, haustive) < `i` (105, if)); 1 between `gocc` (9929) and `gochopchop` (9930) for `gochecknoglobals` (within goc* at index 4 end-of-string < `h` (104, check/hop); within goch* at index 5 `e` (101, check) < `o` (111, hop)); 1 between `intltoolize` (12007) and `inv` (12008) for `intrange` (within in* at index 3 `l` (108, ltool) < `r` (114, range) < `v` (118, v*)); 1 between `iredis` (12230) and `irexec` (12231) for `ireturn` (within ire* at index 3 `d` (100, dis) < `t` (116, turn) < `x` (120, xec)); 1 between `spamprobe` (24452) and `spark-beeline` (24453) for `spancheck` (within spa* at index 3 `m` (109, m*) < `n` (110, ncheck) < `r` (114, rk-*)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-ninth consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter sweep R22 — bodyclose + go-errorlint + maintidx + nonamedreturns + testpackage + usestdlibvars (6 stems via single multi-stem `_bodyclose` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Continues the R21 sweep with 6 more single-purpose analysis/passes-framework linters. Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/timakin/bodyclose](https://github.com/timakin/bodyclose), [github.com/polyfloyd/go-errorlint](https://github.com/polyfloyd/go-errorlint), [github.com/yagipy/maintidx](https://github.com/yagipy/maintidx), [github.com/firefart/nonamedreturns](https://github.com/firefart/nonamedreturns), [github.com/maratori/testpackage](https://github.com/maratori/testpackage), and [github.com/sashamelentyev/usestdlibvars](https://github.com/sashamelentyev/usestdlibvars). Single multi-stem file `_bodyclose` is `#compdef bodyclose go-errorlint maintidx nonamedreturns testpackage usestdlibvars`, dispatching on `$service`. Each tool is also runnable via `go vet -vettool=$(which TOOL) PKG/...` and through golangci-lint integration. **Per-stem coverage**: **`bodyclose`** (1 flag + positional): timakin/bodyclose verifies HTTP response bodies are properly closed for TCP connection reuse — `-check-consumption` (also verify body is consumed via io.Copy/io.ReadAll/JSON decoders/buffered readers). **`go-errorlint`** (1 flag + positional): polyfloyd/go-errorlint enforces Go 1.13 error-wrapping patterns (flags `%v` for errors → `%w`; `err == ErrFoo` → `errors.Is`; type assertions → `errors.As`) — `-fix` (apply autofixes). *NOTE: upstream archived; moved to Codeberg.* **`maintidx`** (1 flag + positional): yagipy/maintidx computes function maintainability index (cyclomatic + Halstead volume + LOC composite, Microsoft rebased scale) — `-under N` (show functions below threshold; default 20). **`nonamedreturns`** (1 flag + positional): firefart/nonamedreturns flags named-return values (variable shadowing / accidental modifications / unintended scope expansion concerns) — `-report-error-in-defer` (also report named errors used in defer; default false). **`testpackage`** (4 flags + positional): maratori/testpackage enforces separate `_test` packages for blackbox testing — `-skip-regexp PATTERN` (default `(export|internal)_test\.go`), `-allow-packages NAMES` (default `main`), `-test`, `-fix`. **`usestdlibvars`** (22 flags + positional): sashamelentyev/usestdlibvars suggests stdlib constants for hardcoded values across HTTP/time/crypto/OS/RPC/SQL/TLS domains — general `-V` (version), `-h` (help), `-flags` (JSON flags), `-json`, `-fix`, `-test`; 11 detection toggles `-http-method`, `-http-status-code`, `-time-layout`, `-time-month`, `-time-weekday`, `-os-dev-null`, `-crypto-hash`, `-constant-kind`, `-sql-isolation-level`, `-tls-signature-scheme`, `-rpc-default-path`; profiling/debug `-cpuprofile`, `-memprofile`, `-trace`, `-debug` (subset of `fpstv`), `-c N` (context lines, default -1). **Blacklist additions (6) across 6 insertion blocks**: 1 between `bochs` (2374) and `bodyfile.pl` (2375) for `bodyclose` (within bod* at index 4 `c` (99, close) < `f` (102, file)); 1 between `go-enum` (9896) and `go-feature-flag-relay-proxy` (9897) for `go-errorlint` (within go-e* at index 4 `n` (110, enum) < `r` (114, errorlint); within go-* at index 3 `e` (101, e*) < `f` (102, feature)); 1 between `maim` (15507) and `make` (15508) for `maintidx` (within m-a-* at index 2 `i` (105, maim/maintidx) < `k` (107, make); within mai* at index 3 `m` (109, maim) < `n` (110, maintidx)); 1 between `nona` (17815) and `nono` (17816) for `nonamedreturns` (within nona* at index 4 end-of-string < `m` (109, named); within non* at index 3 `a` (97, nona*) < `o` (111, nono)); 1 between `testkube` (25879) and `testparm` (25880) for `testpackage` (within test* at index 4 `k` (107, kube) < `p` (112, package/parm); within testp* at index 5 `a` (97, both); at index 6 `c` (99, package) < `r` (114, parm)); 1 between `users` (27328) and `usleep` (27329) for `usestdlibvars` (within us* at index 2 `e` (101, users/usestdlibvars) < `l` (108, usleep); within use* at index 3 `r` (114, users) < `s` (115, usestdlibvars)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-eighth consecutive clean round.
- **Go linter/analyzer sweep R21 — go-consistent + go-critic + perfguard + ruleguard + structlayout-optimize + structlayout-pretty + testifylint (7 stems via single multi-stem `_go-consistent` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs + main.go source files at [github.com/quasilyte/go-consistent](https://github.com/quasilyte/go-consistent), [github.com/go-critic/go-critic](https://github.com/go-critic/go-critic), [github.com/quasilyte/go-perfguard](https://github.com/quasilyte/go-perfguard), [github.com/quasilyte/go-ruleguard](https://github.com/quasilyte/go-ruleguard), [github.com/dominikh/go-tools/tree/master/cmd/structlayout-optimize](https://github.com/dominikh/go-tools/tree/master/cmd/structlayout-optimize) and [structlayout-pretty](https://github.com/dominikh/go-tools/tree/master/cmd/structlayout-pretty), and [github.com/Antonboom/testifylint](https://github.com/Antonboom/testifylint). Single multi-stem file `_go-consistent` is `#compdef go-consistent go-critic perfguard ruleguard structlayout-optimize structlayout-pretty testifylint`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`go-consistent`** (1 flag + positional): quasilyte/go-consistent identifies inconsistent code patterns where multiple equivalent expressions exist (e.g. `make(map[K]V)` vs `map[K]V{}`) — `-v` (verbose), `--help`, conventional Go-target positional (`std`, `./...`, files, packages). **`go-critic`** (state-machine with 3 subcommands): go-critic/go-critic is a Go linter with ~100 diagnostics — `check <targets>` with `-v` + `-enable CHECKERS_OR_TAGS` (filter via `#diagnostic`/`#style`/`#performance`/`#experimental`/`#opinionated`/`#security` tags) + `-disable`, `version`, `doc <checker>`. **`perfguard`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands): quasilyte/go-perfguard is a Go performance analyzer — `lint` (traditional static analysis) with `--fix`, `optimize` (profile-guided) with `--heatmap CPU_PROFILE` + `--fix`; both take Go-target positional. **`ruleguard`** (5 flags + positional): quasilyte/go-ruleguard is a pattern-based dynamic Go linter — `-rules FILES` (comma-separated ruleguard file paths), `-e RULE` (execute a single rule from a string), `-fix` (apply autofixes), `-c` (display offending lines with context), `-json` (output JSON). **`structlayout-optimize`** (3 flags): dominikh/go-tools cmd/structlayout-optimize reorders struct fields to reduce padding — `-json` (format as JSON), `-r` (break up structs + reorder fields freely), `-version`. **`structlayout-pretty`** (2 flags): dominikh/go-tools cmd/structlayout-pretty pretty-prints structlayout output with ASCII art — `-v` (do not compact consecutive bytes), `-version`. **`testifylint`** (4 flags + positional): Antonboom/testifylint is a testify usage-pattern linter with 25+ checkers — `--enable-all`, `--enable CHECKERS`, `--disable CHECKERS`, `--fix` (autofix). **Blacklist additions (7) across 5 insertion blocks** (go-consistent + go-critic share one block, structlayout-optimize + structlayout-pretty share another): **2 between `go-callvis` (9893) and `go-enum` (9894)** for `go-consistent` + `go-critic` (within go-c* at index 4 `a` (97, callvis) < `o` (111, consistent) < `r` (114, critic); within go-* at index 3 `c` (99, c*) < `e` (101, enum)); 1 between `perf` (19517 → line shifted) and `perfquery` (19518) for `perfguard` (within perf* at index 4 end-of-string < `g` (103, guard) < `q` (113, query)); 1 between `rulegen` (22882 → line shifted) and `rulesync` (22883) for `ruleguard` (within rule* at index 4 `g` (103, both gen/guard); at index 5 `e` (101, gen) < `u` (117, guard); within rul* at index 4 `g` (103, guard) < `s` (115, sync)); **2 between `structlayout` (24987 → line shifted) and `structurizr-cli` (24988)** for `structlayout-optimize` + `structlayout-pretty` (within structlayout* at index 12 end-of-string < `-` (45, layout-*); within structlayout-* at index 13 `o` (111, optimize) < `p` (112, pretty); within struct* at index 6 `l` (108, layout) < `u` (117, urizr)); 1 between `testgdbm` (25870 → line shifted) and `testj1939` (25871) for `testifylint` (within test* at index 4 `g` (103, gdbm) < `i` (105, ifylint) < `j` (106, j1939)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-seventh consecutive clean round.
- **Microsoft SARIF tooling R20 — sarif-multitool (1 stem; 19 verbs documented)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surface transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/microsoft/sarif-sdk docs/multitool-usage.md](https://github.com/microsoft/sarif-sdk). Continues the Microsoft security/analysis CLI sweep started in [R19](#) (`_appinspector`). Single-stem file `_sarif-multitool` is `#compdef sarif-multitool`. **Coverage**: `sarif-multitool` is a .NET-Global-Tool installed via `dotnet tool install --global Sarif.Multitool`, invoked as `sarif-multitool`. State-machine dispatches on **19 verbs** grouped into three families: **Primary log operations** (14 verbs): `absoluteuri` (relative→absolute URIs), `apply-policy`, `convert` (with `--tool` 13-value enum: AndroidStudio/ClangAnalyzer/ClangTidy/CppCheck/ContrastSecurity/Fortify/FortifyFpr/FxCop/PREfast/Pylint/SemmleQL/StaticDriverVerifier/TSLint), `export-validation-config`, `export-validation-rules`, `file-work-items`, `match-results-forward` (New/Absent/Unchanged result identification), `merge`, `page` (subset extraction), `query`, `rebaseuri`, `rewrite` (SARIF reformatter), `suppress`, `validate` (schema + correctness rules). **Event-log append flow** (5 verbs): `emit-init-run` (seed event log with SARIF run JSON), `add-result`, `add-notification`, `add-invocation`, `emit-finalize` (replay staged events into final SARIF). **Utility verbs** (2): `help`, `version`. Common `--log` parameter enum across many verbs: ForceOverwrite/Inline/PrettyPrint/Minify/Optimize. Per-verb flag enumeration is sparse upstream; primary verb-list + the convert tool enum + the log options are the well-documented surfaces. **Blacklist additions (1)**: 1 between `sarif` (23222) and `sash` (23223) for `sarif-multitool` (within sarif* at index 5 end-of-string < `-` (45, multitool); within sar* at index 4 `i` (105, sarif) < `m` (no — wait); at index 4 sarif/sarif-multitool both `i`; at index 5 end-of-string < `-`; at index 3 sarif/sarif-multitool/sash all `i` then `f` then end-vs-`-` vs `h`; sar* family ordering: sar < sar.sysstat < sarien < sarif < sarif-multitool < sash). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — twenty-sixth consecutive clean round.
- **Microsoft security/analysis CLI suite R19 — appinspector + devskim + 10x OSSGadget oss-* tools (12 stems via single multi-stem `_appinspector` file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs + wiki pages at [github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInspector](https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInspector), [github.com/microsoft/DevSkim](https://github.com/microsoft/DevSkim) (Analyze Command wiki), and per-tool [github.com/microsoft/OSSGadget wiki pages](https://github.com/microsoft/OSSGadget/wiki). **Hunt-defer log this round**: `oss-characteristic` (wiki page exists but enumerates no flags; defer per the gh-grep precedent), `oss-find-domain-squats` (wiki page not enumerated; defer per the gh-grep precedent), `oss-reproducible` (wiki page not enumerated; defer per the gh-grep precedent). Single multi-stem file `_appinspector` is `#compdef appinspector devskim oss-defog oss-detect-backdoor oss-detect-cryptography oss-diff oss-download oss-find-source oss-find-squats oss-health oss-metadata oss-risk-calculator`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`appinspector`** (state-machine with 7 subcommands; `analyze` carries 4 short flags from README + many additional flags deferred to wiki page 1): microsoft/ApplicationInspector is a .NET-Global-Tool static analyzer with 400+ rule patterns — subcommands `analyze`/`tagdiff`/`exporttags`/`verifyrules`/`packrules`/`help`/`version`; `analyze` carries `-s` (source, required), `-f` (format html/json/sarif/markdown), `-o` (output file), `-g` (glob exclude). **`devskim`** (state-machine with `analyze` subcommand carrying **~30 flags**): microsoft/DevSkim is a .NET-Global-Tool security analyzer with inline IDE integration — required `-I/--source-code PATH`, `-O/--output-file FILE`; rules `--options-json`, `-r`, `--rule-ids`, `--ignore-rule-ids`, `--languages`, `--comments`; output `-o/--output-format`, `-f/--file-format` (text/sarif), `-s/--severity`, `--confidence`, `-g/--ignore-globs`; processing `-d/--disable-supression`, `--disable-parallel`, `-i/--ignore-default-rules`, `-c/--crawl-archives`, `--skip-git-ignored-files`; paths `--base-path`, `--absolute-path`, `--skip-excerpts`; logging `-x/--console-verbosity`, `--disable-console`, `-v/--log-file-level`, `-l/--log-file-path`; utility `-E` (exit code = issue count). **`oss-defog`** (10 flags + PURL): microsoft/OSSGadget oss-defog finds Base64/hex-encoded obfuscated text — `--download-directory`, `--report-blobs`, `--minimum-hex-length N` (default 8), `--minimum-base64-length N` (default 1), three `--save-found-binaries-to`/`--save-archives-to`/`--save-blobs-to` directory flags, `--use-cache`, `--help`, `--version`. **`oss-detect-backdoor`** (6 flag pairs + PURL): oss-detect-backdoor flags potential malicious code (high false-positive rate per upstream) — `-d/--download-directory`, `-f/--format` (text/sarifv1/sarifv2), `-o/--output-file`, `-c/--use-cache`. **`oss-detect-cryptography`** (9 flags + PURL): oss-detect-cryptography finds cryptographic implementations — `--verbose`, `--custom-rule-directory`, `--disable-default-rules`, `--download-directory`, `--use-cache`, `--format`, `--output-file`. **`oss-diff`** (12 flag pairs + PURL): oss-diff compares two package versions — `-d/--download-directory`, `-c/--use-cache`, `-w/--crawl-archives` (default true), `-B/--context-before N`, `-A/--context-after N`, `-C/--context N` (-1 = all, overrides B/A), `-a/--added-only`, `-r/--removed-only`, `-f/--format`, `-o/--output-location`. **`oss-download`** (6 flag pairs + PURL): oss-download retrieves and optionally extracts packages — `-x/--download-directory` (default cwd), `-m/--download-metadata-only`, `-e/--extract`, `-c/--use-cache`. **`oss-find-source`** (5 flag pairs + PURL): oss-find-source locates source repositories — `-f/--format`, `-o/--output-file`, `-S/--single` (top possibility only). **`oss-find-squats`** (6 flag pairs + PURL): oss-find-squats detects typo-squatting candidates — `-o/--output-file`, `-f/--format`, `-q/--quiet`, `-s/--sleep-delay N` (ms between checks). **`oss-health`** (4 flag pairs + PURL): oss-health collects project health metrics — `-f/--format`, `-o/--output-file`. **`oss-metadata`** (3 flag pairs + PURL): oss-metadata fetches package metadata from deps.dev or libraries.io — `-o/--output-file`. **`oss-risk-calculator`** (9 flag pairs + PURL): oss-risk-calculator generates risk assessments — required `-d/--download-directory`, optional `-r/--external-risk N`, `-f/--format`, `-o/--output-file`, `-n/--no-health`, `--verbose`, `-c/--use-cache`. **Blacklist additions (12) across 3 insertion blocks**: 1 between `appimagetool` (1138) and `appium` (1139) for `appinspector` (within app* at index 3 'i' (105, both) consistent; at index 4 'm' (109, appimage*) < 'n' (110, appinspector) < 'u' (117, appium)); 1 between `devs` (6070 → line shifted) and `devspace` (6071) for `devskim` (within dev* at index 4 's' (115, all) consistent; at index 5 end-of-string < 'k' (107, devskim) < 'p' (112, devspace)); **10 between `osqueryi` (18725 → line shifted) and `ossec-agent` (18726)** for the entire oss-* family (within os* at index 2 'q' (113, osqueryi) < 's' (115, oss-*); within oss* at index 3 '-' (45, all oss-*) < 'e' (101, ossec); within oss-* the 10 stems alphabetize as `defog < detect-backdoor < detect-cryptography < diff < download < find-source < find-squats < health < metadata < risk-calculator`). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/anhsirk0/fetch-master-6000](https://github.com/anhsirk0/fetch-master-6000), [github.com/yuler/gh-download](https://github.com/yuler/gh-download), and [github.com/k1LoW/gh-setup](https://github.com/k1LoW/gh-setup). Single multi-stem file `_fetch-master-6000` is `#compdef fetch-master-6000 fm6000 gh-download gh-setup`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`fetch-master-6000`/`fm6000`** (single dispatch arm — `fm6000` is the actual binary name, `fetch-master-6000` is the project name and an occasional packaging alias; 25+ flags with a **9-way character-mutex** spanning short + long aliases): anhsirk0/fetch-master-6000 is a Perl-based neofetch-style system info display for Linux/BSD with selectable Dilbert characters — character mutex covers `-w/--wally`, `-dog/--dogbert`, `-al/--alice`, `-phb`, `-as/--asok`, `-r/--random`, `-f/--file` (load from file), `-rd/--random-dir DIR` (random from directory); custom-display flags `-o/--os`, `-k/--kernel`, `-d/--de`, `-sh/--shell`, `-u/--uptime`, `-p/--package`, `-nd/--not_de` (WM instead of DE), `-v/--vnstat`; styling `-c/--color` (17-value enum: 8 base + 8 bright + `random`), `-s/--say`, `-sf/--say-file`; layout `-m/--margin`, `-g/--gap` (default 10), `-l/--length` (default 13). **`gh-download`** (positionals only): yuler/gh-download downloads folders/files from a GitHub repo via the Git tree API — positionals accept `OWNER/REPO PATH` or a GitHub URL; paths ending with `/` are treated as folders. **`gh-setup`** (11 flags): k1LoW/gh-setup installs GitHub release assets (works as gh-extension, standalone CLI, and GitHub Action) — required `--repo OWNER/REPO`, `--version` (default latest), `--os` (linux/darwin/windows/freebsd enum), `--arch` (amd64/arm64/386/arm enum), `--bin-match PATTERN`, `--checksum`, `--force`, `--strict`, `--verify-attestation` (uses `gh attestation verify`), `--attestation-flags`, `--gh-setup-version`. **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `fetch-ebook-metadata` (7976) and `fetch_file` (7977) for `fetch-master-6000` (within fetch-* at index 6 `e` (101, ebook) < `m` (109, master); within fetch* at index 5 `-` (45, both `-`) < `_` (95, _file)); 1 between `fm` (8284) and `fmadm` (8285) for `fm6000` (within fm* at index 2 end-of-string < `6` (54, fm6000) < `a` (97, fmadm)); 1 between `gh-difftool` (9234) and `gh-drop` (9235) for `gh-download` (within gh-d* at index 4 `i` (105, difftool) < `o` (111, download) < `r` (114, drop)); - **gh-extension Round 17 — gh-branch + gh-grep + gh-label + gh-projects (4 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-branch` file; tooling-staple harvest — branch management, repo-search, label-CRUD, project-CRUD)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/mislav/gh-branch](https://github.com/mislav/gh-branch), [github.com/k1LoW/gh-grep](https://github.com/k1LoW/gh-grep), [github.com/heaths/gh-label](https://github.com/heaths/gh-label), and [github.com/heaths/gh-projects](https://github.com/heaths/gh-projects). **Hunt-note this round**: This round revisits `gh-grep` — earlier rounds had deferred it under what became known as the "gh-grep precedent" (no flag enumeration upstream). The current README documents 16 flags explicitly, so coverage is now appropriate; the precedent name itself stays useful as a label for the general pattern of deferring no-surface tools. Single multi-stem file `_gh-branch` is `#compdef gh-branch gh-grep gh-label gh-projects`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-branch`** (no flags, bare interactive): mislav/gh-branch is an fzf-based interactive branch switcher — bare invocation only, no documented CLI flags. **`gh-grep`** (16 flags): k1LoW/gh-grep searches text patterns across GitHub repos via the API — `-e/--strings PATTERN`, `--branch BRANCH`, `-c/--count`, `--exclude PATTERN`, `-h/--help`, `-i/--ignore-case`, `--include PATTERN` (default `**/*`), `-n/--line-number`, `--name-only`, `-o/--only-matching`, `--owner OWNER`, `--repo REPO`, `--repo-only`, `--tag TAG`, `--url`, `-v/--version`. **`gh-label`** (state-machine with 6 subcommands): heaths/gh-label is full CRUD for repo labels — `create <name>` with `--color` (hex, # optional; random if omitted) + `--description`, `edit <name>` with `--color`/`--description`/`--new-name`, `delete <name>`, `export <path>` and `import <path>` both with `--format csv|json` (use `-` for stdin/stdout), `list [substring-filter]`. **`gh-projects`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands): heaths/gh-projects is CRUD for Projects V2 — `clone` with `-t/--title NEW_TITLE` + `--private` + `-d DESC`, `edit` with `-d DESC` + `--public` + `--add-issue ISSUES` (comma-separated) + repeatable `-f FieldName=Value`, `list` with `--search QUERY`, `view`. **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-bofh` (9218) and `gh-changelog` (9219) for `gh-branch` (within gh-b* at index 4 `o` (111, bofh) < `r` (114, branch); within gh-* at index 3 `b` (98, b*) < `c` (99, changelog)); 1 between `gh-graph` (9242) and `gh-init` (9243) for `gh-grep` (within gh-g* at index 4 `r` (114, both); at index 5 `a` (97, graph) < `e` (101, grep); at index 3 `g` (103, g*) < `i` (105, init)); 1 between `gh-issue-estimate` (9246 — line shifted) and `gh-markdown-preview` (9247) for `gh-label` (within gh-* at index 3 `i` (105, issue) < `l` (108, label) < `m` (109, markdown)); 1 between `gh-project-item-move` (9261 — line shifted) and `gh-provision-envs` (9262) for `gh-projects` (within gh-project* at index 7 `-` (45, item-move) < `s` (115, projects); within gh-pro* at index 3-5 same `pro`; at index 6 `j` (106, project) < no — wait, at index 6 `e` (101, project*) < `v` (118, provision); `c` (99, project) — let me recheck — `gh-project` is index 6 `c`, `gh-projects` is index 6 `c`; consistent through `projects`; at index 7 `t` for projects-item-move vs `t` for projects; index 8 `-` vs `s`; '-' < 's' so project-item-move < projects; projects vs provision: at index 6 `c` (99, projects) < `v` (118, provision)).
