- **gh-extension Round 16 — gh-org-clone + gh-pr-repl + gh-provision-envs + gh-start + gh-submodule-add (5 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-org-clone` file; cross-language harvest — JS + Ruby; bulk-org-operation theme)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/crguezl/gh-org-clone](https://github.com/crguezl/gh-org-clone), [github.com/nobe4/gh-pr-repl](https://github.com/nobe4/gh-pr-repl), [github.com/AyogoHealth/gh-provision-envs](https://github.com/AyogoHealth/gh-provision-envs), [github.com/balvig/gh-start](https://github.com/balvig/gh-start), and [github.com/crguezl/gh-submodule-add](https://github.com/crguezl/gh-submodule-add). **Hunt-note this round**: `gh-start` here is the Ruby Cookpad-derived workflow tool from balvig/gh-start, distinct from jasonappah/gh-start (Rust WIP) which was deferred in round 167; the Ruby variant has documented surface so the completion targets it. Single multi-stem file `_gh-org-clone` is `#compdef gh-org-clone gh-pr-repl gh-provision-envs gh-start gh-submodule-add`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-org-clone`** (10 short/long flag pairs + `--default`): crguezl/gh-org-clone batch-clones all repos in an org via Node + optional fzf interactive selection — `-s/--search`, `-r/--regexp`, `-c/--csr` (comma-separated repo list), `-f/--file` (load repo list from file), `-k/--fork` 3-value enum (`true|only|false`), `-n/--dryrun`, `-o/--org`, `--default` (set org as default for future use), `-D/--depth` (shallow clone), `-p/--parallel`. **`gh-pr-repl`** (interactive REPL with positionals): nobe4/gh-pr-repl is a Ruby-based interactive REPL for managing PRs — bare invocation uses PR from current branch, positionals accept `OWNER/REPO/BRANCH` or PR URLs (one or many for multi-PR sessions). **`gh-provision-envs`** (2 flags): AyogoHealth/gh-provision-envs provisions environment variables + secrets from a JSON config — `-c CONFIG.json` (required) + `-R OWNER/REPO`; upstream documents the JSON shape uses an `environments` object with `variables` + `secrets` arrays of `{name,value}` pairs and will **overwrite** existing values. **`gh-start`** (1 positional): balvig/gh-start (Ruby Cookpad-derived) creates a branch and opens a draft PR — `gh start "description"` for free-text or `gh start [issue-url]` to auto-populate from a GitHub issue. **`gh-submodule-add`** (7 short/long flag pairs): crguezl/gh-submodule-add batch-adds repos as submodules via Node + optional fzf — `-C/--clone` (clone only, skip submodule setup), `-o/--org`, `-s/--search`, `-r/--regexp`, `-k/--fork`, `-p/--parallel` (default 2), `-D/--depth`. **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-notify` (9253) and `gh-ost` (9254) for `gh-org-clone` (within gh-* at index 3 `n` (110, notify) < `o` (111, org/ost); within gh-o* at index 4 `r` (114, org) < `s` (115, ost)); 1 between `gh-poi` (9256 — line shifted) and `gh-prism` (9257) for `gh-pr-repl` (within gh-p* at index 4 `o` (111, poi) < `r` (114, pr*); within gh-pr* at index 5 `-` (45, pr-repl) < `i` (105, prism)); 1 between `gh-project-item-move` (9259 — line shifted) and `gh-prpeek` (9260) for `gh-provision-envs` (within gh-pr* at index 5 `o` (111, project/provision); at index 6 `j` (106, project) < `v` (118, provision); within gh-pro* at index 6 `v` (118, provision) < `p` (112, prpeek)? no — `prpeek` is at index 5 `p` (112), at index 5 `o` (111, provision) < `p` (112, prpeek)); 1 between `gh-stack` (9283 — line shifted) and `gh-sub-issue` (9284) for `gh-start` (within gh-st* at index 5 `a` (97, both); at index 6 `c` (99, stack) < `r` (114, start); at index 4 `t` (116, start) < `u` (117, sub-issue)); 1 between `gh-sub-issue` (9284 — line shifted) and `gh-tidy` (9285) for `gh-submodule-add` (within gh-sub* at index 6 `-` (45, sub-issue) < `m` (109, submodule); within gh-s* at index 4 `u` (117, sub*) < no — at index 3 `s` (115, sub*) < `t` (116, tidy)). Single multi-stem file `_gh-ec2` is `#compdef gh-ec2 gh-init gh-rate-limit-status gh-reviewers-graph gh-secrets-migrator`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-ec2`** (state-machine with **8 subcommands**, instance-targeted ones take `INSTANCE_NAME` positional): matsui528/gh-ec2 is an AWS EC2 manager via gh CLI — `config` (interactive register to `~/.instance_list.json`, supports `--config FILE` for alternate paths), `list`, then 6 instance-targeted commands (`status`/`start`/`stop`/`ssh`/`nautilus`/`ip`) each taking a single instance-name positional. **`gh-init`** (5 short/long flag pairs): fsncps/gh-init creates a local + remote GitHub repo in one shot — `-p/--path DIR`, `-n/--name NAME`, `--no-gitignore`, `--no-readme`, `-P/--public` (default is private), `-d/--description TEXT`. **`gh-rate-limit-status`** (5 flags): gh-tui-tools/gh-rate-limit-status displays the gh API rate-limit status as a table — `-h` (help), `-a` (show all limits incl. low-volume + enterprise-only), `--json` (machine-readable output), `-w` (watch mode), `--warn N` (exit 1 if any limit drops below N%). **`gh-reviewers-graph`** (4 flags + 1 positional): gh-tui-tools/gh-reviewers-graph visualizes PR reviewer activity as standalone HTML — `--top N` (default 100), `--exclude LOGINS` (comma-separated), `--refresh` (bypass cache), `--output DIR` (report directory), positional 1 = `OWNER/REPO`. **`gh-secrets-migrator`** (14 flags): renan-alm/gh-secrets-migrator migrates secrets between GitHub organizations — required `--source-org`/`--source-repo`/`--target-org`, optional `--target-repo` (defaults to source-repo name); 4 token flags `--source-pat`/`--target-pat`/`--github-source-pat`/`--github-target-pat` for gh-gei-style auth; behavior toggles `--verbose`/`--skip-envs`/`--skip-overwrite`/`--org-to-org`; custom endpoints `--source-endpoint`/`--target-endpoint`. **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-drop` (9232) and `gh-f` (9233) for `gh-ec2` (within gh-* at index 3 `d` (100, drop) < `e` (101, ec2) < `f` (102, f)); Single multi-stem file `_gh-secret-sync` is `#compdef gh-secret-sync gh-runs-cleanup gh-repo-stats-plus`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-secret-sync`** (11 flags with **2 mutex groups**): Nick2bad4u/gh-secret-sync safely syncs secrets across repos/envs/orgs; default is dry-run, **`--confirm` ↔ `--yes` mutex** unlocks application; **4-way `--secret-value-{env|file|prompt|stdin}` mutex** encodes mutually exclusive secret-source channels (env-var, file, interactive prompt, stdin pipe); `--secret-name`, repeatable `*--set NAME=VALUE` + `*--set-env NAME=VALUE`, `--plan-file JSON_OR_CSV`, `--json` machine-readable output. **`gh-runs-cleanup`** (15 flags with **2 mutex groups**): Nick2bad4u/gh-runs-cleanup bulk-deletes GitHub Actions workflow runs; default is dry-run, **`--confirm` ↔ `--yes` mutex** unlocks deletion; **3-way `--json` ↔ `--verbose` ↔ `--quiet` mutex** for output verbosity; repeatable filters (`*--status` with 11-value enum, `*--workflow`), single filters (`--branch`/`--event` with 5-value enum/`--user`/`--commit`), safety controls (`--repo`, `--max-delete N`, `--fail-fast`). **`gh-repo-stats-plus`** (state-machine with **8 subcommands** + no per-subcommand flags surfaced): mona-actions/gh-repo-stats-plus is a comprehensive repo-stats collector — `repo-stats`/`project-stats`/`org-repos`/`combine-stats`/`post-process`/`rows-to-columns`/`package-stats`/`codespace-stats`. Per-subcommand flags are deliberately not surfaced because upstream README only lists subcommand names and purposes without enumerating flags — surfacing them would be vibe-coding. **Blacklist additions (3) across 2 insertion blocks** (gh-repo-stats-plus + gh-runs-cleanup share one block): **2 between `gh-repo-stats` (9256) and `gh-s` (9257)** for `gh-repo-stats-plus` + `gh-runs-cleanup` (within gh-repo-stats* at index 13 end-of-string < `-` (45, plus); within gh-r* at index 4 `e` (101, repo) < `u` (117, runs); within gh-r* at index 3 `r` (114, both) consistent and at index 4 `u` (117, runs) < `s` (115, s) — actually no, `s` (115) is at index 3 of `gh-s` not index 4; at index 3 `r` (114) < `s` (115) so gh-r* < gh-s); 1 between `gh-screensaver` (9259 — line shifted by this round's 2-block) and `gh-signoff` (9260) for `gh-secret-sync` (within gh-s* at index 4 `c` (99, screensaver) < `e` (101, secret) < `i` (105, signoff)). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/uzimaru0000/gh-board](https://github.com/uzimaru0000/gh-board), [github.com/daido1976/gh-default-branch](https://github.com/daido1976/gh-default-branch), [github.com/MH4GF/gh-prj](https://github.com/MH4GF/gh-prj), [github.com/balfons/gh-prpeek](https://github.com/balfons/gh-prpeek), and [github.com/Kesin11/gh-workflow-ls](https://github.com/Kesin11/gh-workflow-ls). **Hunt-skip log this round**: `gh-traffic` (yskszk63/gh-traffic — bare `gh traffic` only, no flags documented; skip per the bare-TUI-no-surface precedent), `gh-activity` (nogtk/gh-activity — interactive wrapper around `gh pr list` with no documented CLI flags; skip per the same precedent). Single multi-stem file `_gh-board` is `#compdef gh-board gh-default-branch gh-prj gh-prpeek gh-workflow-ls`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-board`** (2 flags + 1 positional): uzimaru0000/gh-board is a Rust TUI kanban board for GitHub Projects V2 — `--owner LOGIN` (organization/user; `@me` for current account), `--no-cache`, optional `<project-number>` positional (omitting both runs the interactive project picker). **`gh-default-branch`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands): daido1976/gh-default-branch shows or renames a repo's default branch — `show` (with `-n/--name-only` for just-the-name output), `rename <new-name>`. Upstream README explicitly warns rename does **not** re-target open PRs, update draft releases, move branch protection rules, redirect web requests, or notify contributors — surface it as plain CLI, no special warnings. **`gh-prj`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands + 2 global flags): MH4GF/gh-prj browses and inspects GitHub Projects — `list` / `view` subcommands + global `-h` (help) + `-V` (version). **`gh-prpeek`** (6 flags): balfons/gh-prpeek is a live PR-status monitor — `--repos OWNER/REPO[,...]`, `--interval SECS` (default 60), `--labels L1[,L2,...]`, `--notify` (enabled by default), `--hide-checks`, `--show-labels`. **`gh-workflow-ls`** (1 flag + 1 positional): Kesin11/gh-workflow-ls expands reusable-workflow and composite-action calls in a GitHub Actions run — `-t TOKEN` (overrides `$GITHUB_TOKEN`) + positional run URL of the shape `https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/actions/runs/RUN_ID`. **Blacklist additions (5) across 4 insertion blocks** (gh-prj + gh-prpeek share one block): 1 between `gh` (9214) and `gh-changelog` (9215) for `gh-board` (within gh-* at index 3 `b` (98, board) < `c` (99, changelog); at index 2 end-of-string for `gh` < `-` (45)); 1 between `gh-dash` (9226 — line shifted by round 165's `gh-difftool` insert) and `gh-difftool` (9227) for `gh-default-branch` (within gh-d* at index 4 `a` (97, dash) < `e` (101, default) < `i` (105, difftool)); **2 between `gh-prism` (9244 — line shifted) and `gh-pv` (9245)** for `gh-prj` + `gh-prpeek` (within gh-pr* at index 5 `i` (105, prism) < `j` (106, prj) < `p` (112, prpeek); within gh-p* at index 4 `r` (114, pr*) < `v` (118, pv)); 1 between `gh-user-stars` (9260 — line shifted) and `gh-worktree` (9261) for `gh-workflow-ls` (within gh-w* at index 7 `f` (102, workflow) < `t` (116, worktree); within gh-* at index 3 `u` (117, user) < `w` (119, w*)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — fifteenth consecutive clean round.
- **gh-extension Round 8 — gh-difftool + gh-prism + gh-releaser + gh-sizer + gh-sql (5 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-difftool` file; sourced from the TypeScript + Rust slices of topics/gh-extension)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/speedyleion/gh-difftool](https://github.com/speedyleion/gh-difftool), [github.com/kawarimidoll/gh-prism](https://github.com/kawarimidoll/gh-prism), [github.com/carlsberg/gh-releaser](https://github.com/carlsberg/gh-releaser), [github.com/timrogers/gh-sizer](https://github.com/timrogers/gh-sizer), and [github.com/KOBA789/gh-sql](https://github.com/KOBA789/gh-sql). **Hunt-defer log this round**: `gh-describe` (proudust/gh-describe — README documents GitHub Actions input names but not CLI flag names for the binary invocation; defer per the gh-grep precedent), `gh-news` (chmouel/gh-news — interactive TUI with only bare `gh news` and a config file documented, no CLI flags surface; skip per bare-TUI-no-surface precedent). Single multi-stem file `_gh-difftool` is `#compdef gh-difftool gh-prism gh-releaser gh-sizer gh-sql`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-difftool`** (3 named flags + 2 positionals): speedyleion/gh-difftool launches a visual difftool over PR changes — `-t/--tool TOOL` (override) + `--name-only` + `--skip-to FILE` + `--rotate-to FILE`; positional 1 = PR (number/URL/default current-branch's PR); files after `--` limit comparison scope. Also reads `GH_DIFFTOOL` env var and falls back to `git config diff.tool`/`merge.tool`. **`gh-prism`** (4 flags + 1 positional): kawarimidoll/gh-prism is a Rust+ratatui TUI for PR review — `--repo OWNER/REPO`, `--no-cache`, **`--light` ↔ `--dark` mutex** for theme override, `<PR_NUMBER>` positional. **`gh-releaser`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands): carlsberg/gh-releaser is a GitFlow release helper that creates branches + drafts releases + opens PRs — `start <tag>` (initiates the cycle), `close` (merges and publishes), `update <new tag>` (rename + update docs), `pr` (print current open release PR number). **`gh-sizer`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands): timrogers/gh-sizer wraps `git-sizer` over remote repos — `repo <owner/repo>` (clone+size+remove) and `generate-script <user-or-org>` (batch automation); both carry `--output-format text|json`. **`gh-sql`** (3 flag pairs + 2 positionals): KOBA789/gh-sql queries GitHub Projects via SQL (SELECT/DELETE/UPDATE with restrictions on Title/Assignees/Labels/Milestone/Repository) — `-e/--execute SQL` (non-interactive), `-o/--output table|json|initial` (3-value enum), `-oj` (shortcut for `--output json`), positional 1 = owner, positional 2 = project number. **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-dash` (9226) and `gh-f` (9227) for `gh-difftool` (within gh-d* at index 4 `a` (97, dash) < `i` (105, difftool); at index 3 `d` (100, dash/difftool) < `f` (102, f)); 1 between `gh-poi` (9241) and `gh-pv` (9242) for `gh-prism` (within gh-p* at index 4 `o` (111, poi) < `r` (114, prism) < `v` (118, pv)); 1 between `gh-pv` (9242) and `gh-repo-list` (9243) for `gh-releaser` (at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `r` (114, releaser/repo); within gh-re* at index 5 `l` (108, releaser) < `p` (112, repo-list/repo-stats)); 1 between `gh-signoff` (9248) and `gh-skyline` (9249) for `gh-sizer` (within gh-s* at index 4 `i` (105, signoff/sizer) consistent at 4; within gh-si* at index 5 `g` (103, signoff) < `z` (122, sizer); at index 4 within gh-s* `i` (105, sizer) < `k` (107, skyline)); 1 between `gh-slack` (9250) and `gh-stack` (9251) for `gh-sql` (within gh-s* at index 4 `l` (108, slack) < `q` (113, sql) < `t` (116, stack)). Single multi-stem file `_gh-contribs` is `#compdef gh-contribs gh-code-scanning gh-gonest gh-repo-list gh-worktree`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-contribs`** (8 short/long flag pairs — the deepest single-tool flag matrix in the recent rounds): MintArchit/gh-contribs is a terminal contribution-graph renderer forked from mislav's gh-contrib — `-s/--scheme` with 7-value enum (`gh_contrast|vibrant|dracula|nord|gold|sunset|mint`), `-g/--glyph` with 6-value enum (`square|dot|fisheye|diamond|plus|block`), `-u/--user` (display another user's graph), `-w/--weeks` (week range), `-p/--portrait`, `-l/--labels`, `-t/--total`, `-d/--double`. **`gh-code-scanning`** (state-machine with 3 subcommands + per-subcommand flags + `owner/repo` positional in each): advanced-security/gh-code-scanning automates Code Scanning with CodeQL — `enable` (`--force` overwrite + `--git-push` commit-directly + `--workflow FILE`), `alerts` (just the positional), `analyses` (`--delete` + `--ref BRANCH`). **`gh-gonest`** (2 flags): emmanuel-ferdman/gh-gonest cleans phantom GitHub notifications from deleted repos — `--dry-run` (safe preview) + `--after DATE` (date filter). **`gh-repo-list`** (1 required-flag enum): gh640/gh-repo-list lists repositories by `--type own|starred|viewer-status` (3-value enum, the flag itself is required per upstream). **`gh-worktree`** (state-machine with 6 subcommands + per-subcommand flags): bjester/gh-worktree manages Git worktrees with gh integration — `init`/`create`/`checkout` (PR number/URL/branch positional)/`remove` (`--force` for unmerged commits)/`install` (**`--gh-ext` ↔ `--path-bin` mutex** for gh-extension vs standalone install mode)/`version`. **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-clone-org` (9220) and `gh-combine-prs` (9221) for `gh-code-scanning` (within gh-co* at index 5 `d` (100, code) < `m` (109, combine); within gh-c* at index 4 `l` (108, clone) < `o` (111, co*)); 1 between `gh-combine-prs` (9221) and `gh-copilot` (9222) for `gh-contribs` (within gh-co* at index 5 `m` (109, combine) < `n` (110, contribs) < `p` (112, copilot)); 1 between `gh-fzf` (9225) and `gh-graph` (9226 — line shifted by round 163) for `gh-gonest` (at index 3 `f` (102, fzf) < `g` (103, gonest/graph); within gh-g* at index 4 `o` (111, gonest) < `r` (114, graph)); Single multi-stem file `_gh-cp` is `#compdef gh-cp gh-find-code gh-graph gh-user-stars`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-cp`** (3 positionals): mislav/gh-cp copies a single file from a GitHub repo without cloning — `<repo> <path> <dest>` (e.g. `gh cp cli/cli pkg/findsh/find_windows.go .`). **`gh-find-code`** (2 flags + 1 query positional): LangLangBart/gh-find-code is an fzf-driven code search via the GitHub REST API; CLI-documented flags are `-l N` (default 30, max 100) and `-h`; everything else is interactive (fzf keybindings `?`/`ctrl-b`/`ctrl-o`/`ctrl-p`/`ctrl-r`/`ctrl-space`/`ctrl-t`/`ctrl-x`/`enter`/`tab`/`esc`, all customizable via `GHFC_*_KEY` environment variables — completion only models the CLI surface). **`gh-graph`** (3 flags): kawarimidoll/gh-graph renders the contribution graph in the terminal — `--help`, `--pixel CHAR` (display character), `--scheme THEME` (color scheme; "unicorn" mentioned as one example theme). **`gh-user-stars`** (1 optional positional): korosuke613/gh-user-stars is an fzf-driven browser for the authenticated user's starred repositories — single optional `NUMBER` (batch size, default 50, max 100). **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-copilot` (9222) and `gh-dash` (9223) for `gh-cp` (within gh-co* at index 4 `o` (111, copilot) consistent at 4 but at index 5 `p` (112, copilot) vs end-of-string for gh-cp — and end < `p`, so gh-cp shares the `c` prefix but **at index 4 gh-cp's `p` (112) > gh-copilot's `o` (111), so gh-copilot < gh-cp**; at index 3 `c` (99, cp) < `d` (100, dash)); 1 between `gh-f` (9224) and `gh-fzf` (9225) for `gh-find-code` (within gh-f* at index 4 end-of-string < `i` (105, find) < `z` (122, fzf)); 1 between `gh-fzf` (9225) and `gh-install` (9226) for `gh-graph` (within gh-* at index 3 `f` (102, fzf) < `g` (103, graph) < `i` (105, install)); Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/davidraviv/gh-clean-branches](https://github.com/davidraviv/gh-clean-branches), [github.com/matt-bartel/gh-clone-org](https://github.com/matt-bartel/gh-clone-org), [github.com/rnorth/gh-combine-prs](https://github.com/rnorth/gh-combine-prs), [github.com/redraw/gh-install](https://github.com/redraw/gh-install), [github.com/andyfeller/gh-montage](https://github.com/andyfeller/gh-montage), and [github.com/mona-actions/gh-repo-stats](https://github.com/mona-actions/gh-repo-stats) — the last one resolves the round-160 `gh-repo-stats` deferral now that the correct owner-prefix `mona-actions` is known. **Hunt-defer log this round** — four real cases held back: `gh-pr-review` (agynio/gh-pr-review — README describes "command categories" (Review Management / Comment Threading / Thread Resolution) without naming specific subcommands or flags; defer per the gh-grep / gh-aw / gh-actions-importer precedent), `gh-branch` (mislav/gh-branch — bare `gh branch` interactive fzf switcher with no documented flags; defer), `gh-net` (github/gh-net — ARCHIVED 2023-10-20, README states it's bare `gh net` only; skip per archived-no-surface precedent), `gh-gei` (github/gh-gei — Migration CLI with 4 sub-extensions ado2gh/bbs2gh/gl2gh/gei and a `generate-script` subcommand; README enumerates environment variables but no specific CLI flag inventory; defer). Single multi-stem file `_gh-clean-branches` is `#compdef gh-clean-branches gh-clone-org gh-combine-prs gh-install gh-montage gh-repo-stats`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-clean-branches`** (3 flags): davidraviv/gh-clean-branches deletes local branches missing upstream and unpushed commits — `--dry-run`, `--force` (overrides safety via `git branch -D`), `--verbose`. **`gh-clone-org`** (6 flag pairs + ORG positional): matt-bartel/gh-clone-org clones all repos in an organization — `-t/--topic TOPIC` (filter), `-s/--search QUERY` (overrides `--topic`), `-p/--path PATH` (clone destination), `-y/--yes` (skip prompts), `-n/--dry-run`, `-h/--help`. **`gh-combine-prs`** (4 flags): rnorth/gh-combine-prs combines multiple PRs into a single review unit (e.g. Dependabot batches); ARCHIVED 2025-12-23 but surface stable enough to keep — `--query QUERY` (required), `--selected-pr-numbers` (comma-separated), `--limit N` (default 50), `--skip-pr-check` (combine regardless of CI status). **`gh-install`** (1 positional): redraw/gh-install installs binary/.deb releases from a GitHub repo — single `<user>/<repo>` positional; respects `$GH_BINPATH` env var (default `$HOME/.local/bin`). **`gh-montage`** (5 flag pairs + 1 positional): andyfeller/gh-montage creates avatar montages — `-a/--avatar-pixels N` (default 48), `-m/--montage-width N` (default 58), `-o/--output-file NAME`, `-f/--force`, `-d/--debug`; positional accepts `<organization>`, `<organization>/<team>`, or `<path/to/username_file>`; requires ImageMagick 7.1.0+. **`gh-repo-stats`** (1 flag): mona-actions/gh-repo-stats generates repository statistics for migrations — only `--org ORG_NAME` is explicitly named in the README; conservatively surfaces only what's documented (per the no-vibe-coding rule). **Blacklist additions (6) across 5 insertion blocks** (gh-clone-org + gh-combine-prs share one block): 1 between `gh-clean` (9216) and `gh-clean-forks` (9217 — line shifted by round 161) for `gh-clean-branches` (within gh-clean* at index 9 end-of-string < `-` (45); within gh-clean-* at index 10 `b` (98, branches) < `f` (102, forks)); **2 between `gh-cli2` (9218) and `gh-copilot` (9219)** for `gh-clone-org` + `gh-combine-prs` (within gh-c* at index 4 `l` (108, cli2/clone) < `o` (111, combine/copilot); within gh-cl* at index 5 `i` (105, cli2) < `o` (111, clone-org); within gh-co* at index 5 `m` (109, combine) < `p` (112, copilot)); 1 between `gh-fzf` (9222) and `gh-markdown-preview` (9223) for `gh-install` (within gh-* at index 3 `f` (102, fzf) < `i` (105, install) < `m` (109, markdown)); 1 between `gh-pv` (9230) and `gh-s` (9231) for `gh-repo-stats` (within gh-* at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `r` (114, repo) < `s` (115, s)). - **gh-extension Round 4 — gh-changelog + gh-screensaver + gh-sbom + gh-slack + gh-yule-log + gh-models (6 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-changelog` file; 6 more gh-extensions from the topics/gh-extension star-sorted index, page-2 band 128–432 stars with the correct upstream owner-prefixes resolved by re-fetching the index page itself)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/chelnak/gh-changelog](https://github.com/chelnak/gh-changelog), [github.com/vilmibm/gh-screensaver](https://github.com/vilmibm/gh-screensaver), [github.com/advanced-security/gh-sbom](https://github.com/advanced-security/gh-sbom), [github.com/rneatherway/gh-slack](https://github.com/rneatherway/gh-slack), [github.com/leereilly/gh-yule-log](https://github.com/leereilly/gh-yule-log), and [github.com/github/gh-models](https://github.com/github/gh-models). **Hunt-skip log this round** — three real-but-deferrable cases: `gh-actions-cache` (was `actions/gh-actions-cache`; ARCHIVED 2024-10-21, README explicitly states the functionality is now integrated into native `gh cache`, so a snowflake completion would advertise deprecated commands), `gh-profile` (was `gabe565/gh-profile`; ARCHIVED, README states "Official multi-account support was added to the `gh` cli in v2.40.0"), and `gh-grep` (real at `k1LoW/gh-grep` but the README enumerates capabilities — `--include`/`--exclude`/line-numbers/URLs/match-counts/filename-only/branch/tag/owner — without specific short-vs-long flag names; defer until exact flag names are upstream-documented). Single multi-stem file `_gh-changelog` is `#compdef gh-changelog gh-screensaver gh-sbom gh-slack gh-yule-log gh-models`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-changelog`** (state-machine with 3 subcommands + 4 flags on `new`): chelnak/gh-changelog is a Keep-a-Changelog generator — subcommands `new`/`show`/`config`; `new` carries `--next-version`, `--from-version`, `--latest`, and `--logger spinner|console` (2-value enum, auto-switches to `console` in CI). **`gh-screensaver`** (3 flags + per-screensaver extra args): vilmibm/gh-screensaver picks a random terminal animation by default, or `-s NAME` runs a specific one (5-value enum `fireworks|starfield|pipes|pollock|life`), `-l` lists available ones (mutex with `-s`); `--color full|off` (2-value enum) overrides color; per-screensaver options come after `--`. **`gh-sbom`** (2 flags): advanced-security/gh-sbom generates Software Bill of Materials — default SPDX format, `-c` switches to CycloneDX, `-l` enriches CycloneDX output with licensing info from ClearlyDefined. **`gh-slack`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands): rneatherway/gh-slack archives Slack conversations as markdown — `read` (1 positional Slack permalink), `send` (channel + message positionals), `api` (direct API call), `auth` (retrieve credentials). **`gh-yule-log`** (1 flag): leereilly/gh-yule-log is a festive Yule-log terminal animation — `--contribs` customizes the display around your GitHub contribution history. **`gh-models`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands): github/gh-models integrates AI model capabilities directly into the gh CLI — `list` (no args), `run MODEL PROMPT` (e.g. `openai/gpt-4o-mini`), `eval *.prompt.yml`, `generate` (PromptPex test-case auto-generation). **Blacklist additions (6) across 6 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh` (9214) and `gh-clean` (9215) for `gh-changelog` (within gh* at index 2 end-of-string < `-` (45); within gh-c* at index 4 `h` (104, changelog) < `l` (108, clean)); 1 between `gh-md-toc` (9224) and `gh-not` (9225) for `gh-models` (within gh-m* at index 4 `d` (100, md) < `o` (111, models); at index 3 `m` (109, md/models) < `n` (110, not)); 1 between `gh-s` (9230 — line shifted by round-160's `gh-s` insert) and `gh-signoff` (9231) for `gh-sbom` (within gh-s* at index 4 end-of-string < `b` (98, sbom) < `i` (105, signoff)); 1 between `gh-sbom` (9232 just-inserted) and `gh-signoff` (9233) for `gh-screensaver` (within gh-s* at index 4 `b` (98, sbom) < `c` (99, screensaver) < `i` (105, signoff)); - **gh-extension Round 3 — gh-stack + gh-s + gh-token + gh-f (4 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-stack` file; 4 more gh-extensions from the topics/gh-extension star-sorted index in the 380–432 star band)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/github/gh-stack](https://github.com/github/gh-stack), [github.com/gennaro-tedesco/gh-s](https://github.com/gennaro-tedesco/gh-s), [github.com/Link-/gh-token](https://github.com/Link-/gh-token), and [github.com/gennaro-tedesco/gh-f](https://github.com/gennaro-tedesco/gh-f). **Hunt-defer log this round**: `gh-repo-stats` and `gh-actions-cache` — both listed in the topics index but my speculated `owner/repo` paths 404'd; without the correct upstream owner-prefix the README is unreachable, so defer until the right owner surfaces. Single multi-stem file `_gh-stack` is `#compdef gh-stack gh-s gh-token gh-f`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-stack`** (state-machine with **19 documented subcommands** + 5 flags): github/gh-stack manages stacked PRs as ordered branch chains with linked GitHub PRs and metadata stored in `.git/gh-stack/*.json` — subcommands span 4 categories: Stack Management (`init`/`add`/`modify`/`submit`), Navigation (`up`/`down`/`top`/`bottom`/`trunk`/`switch`), Sync (`rebase`/`sync`/`push`), Utilities (`view`/`checkout`/`link`/`unstack`/`feedback`/`alias`); flags `--auto` (skip PR title prompts), `--force-with-lease` (safe force-push), `--numbered` (auto-increment `feat/01`/`feat/02`), and **`--downstack` ↔ `--upstack` mutex** for partial-stack rebases. **`gh-s`** (7 flag pairs + 1 positional): gennaro-tedesco/gh-s is an fzf-driven interactive repository search — `-l/--lang LANGUAGE`, `-d/--desc DESC`, `-u/--user USER`, `-t/--topic TOPIC`, `-c/--colour COLOUR`, `-L/--limit N` (default 20), `-E/--empty` (search by flags alone, no name prompt), plus a positional search term that can replace the interactive prompt; output is the selected repo's URL to stdout. **`gh-token`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands + per-subcommand flags): Link-/gh-token generates short-lived GitHub App installation tokens (1-hour expiry) — `generate` carries **`--key FILE` ↔ `--base64-key VALUE` mutex** plus `--app-id ID` + `--installation-id ID` + optional `--hostname HOST` (for GHES); `revoke` takes the token as a positional; `installations` lists GitHub App installations; `help` shows command docs. **`gh-f`** (11 single-letter subcommand aliases — mutex set + 11 long-form subcommand names): gennaro-tedesco/gh-f is an fzf-extension covering everyday Git/GitHub workflows — each subcommand has both a short alias (`-a`/`-p`/`-b`/`-l`/`-t`/`-R`/`-r`/`-g`/`-s`/`-m`/`-k`, all in a single `_arguments` mutex group) and a long name (`adds`/`prs`/`branches`/`logs`/`tags`/`releases`/`runs`/`greps`/`search`/`myissue`/`pick`); running `gh f` with no arg prints help. **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-dash` (9219) and `gh-fzf` (9220) for `gh-f` (within gh-f* at index 4 end-of-string < `z` (122, fzf); at index 3 `d` (100, dash) < `f` (102, f*)); 1 between `gh-pv` (9228) and `gh-signoff` (9229) for `gh-s` (within gh-s* at index 4 end-of-string < `i` (105, signoff); at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `s` (115, s*)); 1 between `gh-skyline` (9230) and `gh-sub-issue` (9231) for `gh-stack` (within gh-s* at index 4 `k` (107, skyline) < `t` (116, stack) < `u` (117, sub-issue)); 1 between `gh-todo` (9233) and `ghalint` (9234) for `gh-token` (within gh-to* at index 5 `d` (100, todo) < `k` (107, token); at index 2 within ghX `-` (45, gh-*) < `a` (97, ghalint)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — ninth consecutive clean round.
- **gh-extension Round 2 + poutine — gh-signoff + gh-markdown-preview + poutine (3 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-signoff` file; 2 more gh-extensions plus BoostSecurity's supply-chain scanner)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/basecamp/gh-signoff](https://github.com/basecamp/gh-signoff), [github.com/yusukebe/gh-markdown-preview](https://github.com/yusukebe/gh-markdown-preview), and [github.com/boostsecurityio/poutine](https://github.com/boostsecurityio/poutine). Candidates were sourced from the `github.com/topics/gh-extension` index (898 total public extensions at the time of the round); hunting against that index avoids the speculative-name failure mode of round 158's `gh-cm`/`gh-projects`/`gh-rollup`/`gh-prompt` deferrals. **Hunt-defer log this round**: `gh-aw` (github/gh-aw — Agentic Workflows, real 4.5k-star extension but README documents the workflow-markdown surface rather than CLI flags), `gh-actions-importer` (github/gh-actions-importer — real 1.2k-star CI/CD migrator but README only mentions `configure`/`update` invocations without flag details), `gh-eco` (jrnxf/gh-eco — real 475-star TUI extension but launches into Bubble Tea with no documented CLI flags) — all 3 deferred because their README surfaces are too vague to commit to without vibe-coding the specifics. Single multi-stem file `_gh-signoff` is `#compdef gh-signoff gh-markdown-preview poutine`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-signoff`** (state-machine with 2 subcommands + step positionals): basecamp/gh-signoff is a local-CI sign-off helper — bare invocation signs off the current PR; `status` shows completed signoffs; `install` enforces signoff requirements on PR merges; bare positionals are signoff step names (`gh signoff tests lint security`). Encoded as `_describe` over {status, install} with a fall-through to positional step names. **`gh-markdown-preview`** (7 flags + 1 optional file positional): yusukebe/gh-markdown-preview renders markdown with GitHub's native styling — **`--dark-mode` ↔ `--light-mode` mutex**, `-p/--port N` (default 3333), `--host HOSTNAME` (default localhost), `--disable-auto-open`, `--disable-reload`, `--markdown-mode` (plain markdown vs the default GFM), optional positional markdown file (auto-detects README if omitted; also accepts stdin). **`poutine`** (5 global flags + 3 `analyze_*` subcommands): supply-chain vulnerability scanner for build pipelines — global `--token TOKEN`, `--format pretty|json|sarif` (3-value enum), `--threads N`, repeatable `*--skip RULE`, `--fail-on-violation` (exit 10 on findings); subcommands `analyze_local` (1 positional dir), `analyze_repo` (1 positional `owner/repo` or URL), `analyze_org` (1 positional organization). Encoded with state-based dispatch from the global-flag layer into per-subcommand `_arguments` blocks. **Blacklist additions (3) across 3 insertion blocks**: 1 between `gh-fzf` (9220) and `gh-mcp` (9221) for `gh-markdown-preview` (within gh-m* at index 4 `a` (97, markdown) < `c` (99, mcp); at index 3 `f` (102, fzf) < `m` (109, m*)); 1 between `gh-pv` (9227 — line shifted by round 158's inserts) and `gh-skyline` (9228) for `gh-signoff` (within gh-s* at index 4 `i` (105, signoff) < `k` (107, skyline); at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `s` (115, signoff)); 1 between `pound` (20399) and `povray` (20400) for `poutine` (within pou* at index 3 `n` (110, pound) < `t` (116, poutine); at index 2 `u` (117, poutine) < `v` (118, povray)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — eighth consecutive clean round.
- **gh-extension Round 1 — gh-skyline + gh-sub-issue + gh-not + gh-tidy (4 stems via single multi-stem `_gh-skyline` file; companions to the pre-existing `_gh-poi`/`_gh-notify`/`_gh-dash`/etc gh-extension files)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream READMEs at [github.com/github/gh-skyline](https://github.com/github/gh-skyline), [github.com/yahsan2/gh-sub-issue](https://github.com/yahsan2/gh-sub-issue), [github.com/nobe4/gh-not](https://github.com/nobe4/gh-not), and [github.com/HaywardMorihara/gh-tidy](https://github.com/HaywardMorihara/gh-tidy). **Already-covered skip log**: `gh-poi` (at `more_src/_gh-poi`), `gh-notify` (at `more_src/_gh-notify`), `gh-dash` (at `more_src/_gh-dash`) — all 3 source-verified this round but pre-existing. **Source-404 deferrals**: `gh-eco`, `gh-cm`, `gh-projects`, `gh-screensaver`, `gh-asana`, `gh-rollup`, `gh-prompt` — all 404 on direct README fetch from their candidate GitHub repos; treated as likely-misremembered names (the gh-extension namespace has thousands of variants and many of my hunt-list candidates were speculative templates rather than real binaries). `gh-act` exists (verified at `github.com/nektos/gh-act`) but is just a wrapper around `act` (which is already covered), so its CLI surface is identical to act's — deliberately skipped to avoid a snowflake completion that would duplicate the act surface. Single multi-stem file `_gh-skyline` is `#compdef gh-skyline gh-sub-issue gh-not gh-tidy`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`gh-skyline`** (8 flag pairs, no positionals): generate a 3D-printable STL of a GitHub contribution graph — `-h/--help`, `-d/--debug`, `-f/--full` (from join year), `-o/--output FILE`, `-u/--user USERNAME`, `-y/--year YEAR_OR_RANGE` (2008..current), `-w/--web`, `-a/--art-only` (ASCII preview only). **`gh-sub-issue`** (state-machine with 4 subcommands, each with its own flag set): `add` links existing issue to parent (`-R/--repo` + 1+ issue positional); `create` builds new linked issue (`-p/--parent` required + `-t/--title` required + `-b/--body` + repeatable `*-l/--label` + repeatable `*-a/--assignee` + `-m/--milestone` + `--project` + `-R/--repo`); `list` shows sub-issues (`-s/--state open|closed|all` + `-L/--limit N` + `--json FIELDS` + `-w/--web` + `-R/--repo` + parent positional); `remove` unlinks without deletion (`-f/--force` + `-R/--repo` + sub-issue positional). **`gh-not`** (1 flag): rule-based GitHub notification manager — uses YAML config + jq-syntax rules so most behavior is config-file driven; CLI surface is minimal: `-h/--help` and `--config FILE`. Upstream notes the project is "mostly done" so the surface is stable. **`gh-tidy`** (2 flags): checkout main + gc + prune merged branches — `--auto-delete-merged` for non-interactive automation, `--trunk BRANCH` to override the default `main`/`master`. **Blacklist additions (4) across 2 insertion blocks** (gh-skyline + gh-sub-issue + gh-tidy share one block): 1 between `gh-md-toc` (9222) and `gh-notify` (9223) for `gh-not` (within gh-* at index 3 `m` (109, md) < `n` (110, not/notify); within gh-not* at index 6 end-of-string < `i` (105, notify)); **3 between `gh-pv` (9226) and `gh-todo` (9227)** for `gh-skyline` + `gh-sub-issue` + `gh-tidy` (within gh-* at index 3 `p` (112, pv) < `s` (115, skyline/sub-issue) < `t` (116, tidy/todo); within gh-s* at index 4 `k` (107, skyline) < `u` (117, sub-issue); within gh-t* at index 4 `i` (105, tidy) < `o` (111, todo)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — seventh consecutive clean round.
- **Post-git-extras pivot — tfnotify (1 stem standalone `_tfnotify` file; deliberate single-stem round after the tj/git-extras campaign closed at round 156)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). **Hunt-rate observation**: broad-scope hunts across Terraform/IaC extras (`tfsec-language-server`/`tflint-language-server`/`spacectl`/`tfctl` etc), observability extras (`promxy`/`vmctl`/`thanos-cli`/`perses-cli` etc), JS-tooling extras (`rolldown-vite`/`rspress`/`webcontainer` etc), and AI-CLI tools all turned up ~50 FRESH-tagged candidates but **none survived direct source verification** — most were either names I invented from category-knowledge templates rather than actual binary names, or already covered under their canonical names (e.g. `opa` covers `open-policy-agent`, `pulumi` covers `pulumi-cli`). Settling for a single solid stem is the correct outcome over force-padding the round with vibe-coded completions. The campaign-end state from round 156 + this single addition is sustainable: tj/git-extras is exhausted at 72 stems, and the harvest rate falls off naturally when the long tail consists of speculative-name guesses rather than verifiable upstream sources. Surface transcribed directly from upstream README at [github.com/mercari/tfnotify](https://github.com/mercari/tfnotify). Single standalone file `_tfnotify` is `#compdef tfnotify`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`tfnotify`** (3 global flags + 3 subcommands, stdin-driven): Mercari's Go-based wrapper that pipes Terraform output through template processing and posts to GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket as PR comments. Global flags `--config FILE` (default `./.tfnotify.y{,a}ml`), `--message TEXT` (custom template message), `--help`. Subcommands `plan` / `apply` / `fmt` correspond to the upstream Terraform operation type — each consumes stdin (pipe-driven via `terraform plan | tfnotify plan`) and accepts only the global flags (no per-subcommand flag space documented). Completion encodes subcommand `_describe` dispatch with state-based progression to subcommand-args state for `--config`/`--message` re-completion. **Blacklist addition (1)**: 1 between `tfmv` (25881) and `tfplugindocs` (25882) for `tfnotify` (within tf* at index 2 `m` (109, mv) < `n` (110, notify) < `p` (112, plugindocs)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly — sixth consecutive clean round.
- **git-extras Final Round 8 — git-browse-ci + git-paste + git-reauthor + git-repl + git-reset-file + git-unlock (6 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-browse-ci` file; **this round flushes the long-running tj/git-extras campaign at 64 stems** across rounds 150–156 + the pre-existing 8 standalone files for 72-stem total git-extras coverage out of the upstream-documented 74 subcommands)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). The canonical 74-name list was finally obtained this round from upstream `tj/git-extras/master/Commands.md` — cross-referencing against rounds 150–155 plus pre-existing files showed exactly 8 still-uncovered subcommands; 6 added in this round, the remaining 2 deferred. **Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages**: `man/git-browse-ci.1` / `man/git-paste.1` / `man/git-reauthor.1` / `man/git-repl.1` / `man/git-reset-file.1` / `man/git-unlock.1`. **Source-404 deferral**: `man/git-rscp.1` — listed in `Commands.md` but 404s in master, deferred per the source-404 precedent. **Already-covered skip log**: `git-sync` (at `more_src/_git-sync`) — source-verified this round but pre-existing. **Cumulative deferrals across the campaign**: `git-rscp` + 8 other 404s from round 155 + 6 404s from round 154 (`git-mailmap`/`git-merges`/`git-promote`/`git-psykick`/`git-line-summary`/`git-jira` — these names appeared in informal hunt lists but never had `.1` files in master; likely never existed) + `git-mv` (API-listing artifact). Single multi-stem file `_git-browse-ci` is `#compdef git-browse-ci git-paste git-reauthor git-repl git-reset-file git-unlock`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-browse-ci`** (1 optional positional): open the CI page for a remote in the browser; defaults to first remote. **`git-paste`** (0 flags, passthrough): all args passed directly to `git format-patch`; with no args defaults to `@{u}` (upstream tracking branch). **`git-reauthor`** (6 flag pairs): rewrite author/committer identities — `-a/--all`, `-c/--use-config`, `-e/--correct-email EMAIL` (empty string allowed), `-n/--correct-name NAME` (empty NOT allowed per upstream), `-o/--old-email EMAIL`, `-t/--type` with 3-value enum `author|committer|both` (default `both`). **`git-repl`** (no flags): interactive git REPL — recognises bare commands (`git <cmd>`), `!`-prefixed shell commands, `ls` shortcut for `git ls-files`, `exit`/`quit` to terminate; the surface is entirely stdin-driven so no CLI completion content. **`git-reset-file`** (2 positionals): `[<filename>] commit-hash` — reset a single file to a given commit (default `HEAD`). **`git-unlock`** (1 positional): undo `git lock` (re-enables tracking of the assume-unchanged file). **Blacklist additions (6) across 5 insertion blocks** (git-repl + git-reset-file share one block): 1 between `git-browse` (9331) and `git-brv` (9332) for `git-browse-ci` (within git-browse* at index 9 end-of-string < `-` (45, ci); at index 6 `o` (111, browse) < `v` (118, brv)); 1 between `git-pages-cli` (9433) and `git-pkgs` (9434) for `git-paste` (within git-pa* at index 6 `g` (103, pages) < `s` (115, paste); at index 5 `a` (97, pa*) < `k` (107, pkgs)); 1 between `git-quickfix` (9439) and `git-rebase-patch` (9440 was-9440-after-r155) for `git-reauthor` (at index 4 `q` (113, quickfix) < `r` (114, reauthor/rebase); within git-re* at index 6 `a` (97, reauthor) < `b` (98, rebase)); **2 between `git-rename-tag` (9452) and `git-review` (9453)** for `git-repl` + `git-reset-file` (within git-re* at index 6 `n` (110, rename) < `p` (112, repl) < `s` (115, reset) < `v` (118, review); within git-re-p/s entries at index 7 `l` (108, repl) — no further branching needed); 1 between `git-trim` (9489) and `git-upload-archive` (9490) for `git-unlock` (at index 4 `t` (116, trim) < `u` (117, unlock); within git-u* at index 5 `n` (110, unlock) < `p` (112, upload)). - **git-extras Round 7 — git-psykorebase + git-rebase-patch + git-rename-branch + git-rename-remote + git-sed + git-squash (6 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-psykorebase` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 58 stems across rounds 150–155 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`/`_git-get`/`_git-extras`/`_git-standup`/`_git-undo` via `_ugit`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-psykorebase.1` / `man/git-rebase-patch.1` / `man/git-rename-branch.1` / `man/git-rename-remote.1` / `man/git-sed.1` / `man/git-squash.1`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by `^#compdef` recheck before write): `git-extras` (at `more_src/_git-extras`) and `git-standup` (at `more_src/_git-standup`) — both source-verified this round but excluded from the multi-stem; `git-fork` also re-verified but was already added in round 151. **Source-404 deferrals**: `man/git-rscp.1`, `man/git-back.1`, `man/git-blame-someone-else.1`, `man/git-bug.1`, `man/git-chore.1`, `man/git-archive-status.1`, `man/git-archive-now.1`, `man/git-refactor.1` — all return HTTP 404 on direct fetch from `tj/git-extras` master despite some appearing in API listings. **API-listing-artifact deferral**: `man/git-mv.1` appears in the GitHub `contents` API page-2 response but the raw file URL 404s; treated as a listing-side artifact (and stock `git mv` covers the user-facing surface anyway). Single multi-stem file `_git-psykorebase` is `#compdef git-psykorebase git-rebase-patch git-rename-branch git-rename-remote git-sed git-squash`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-psykorebase`** (2 flags + 2 positionals): "psyko rebase" workflow — `--no-ff` forces a merge commit message even when conflicts are absent; `--continue` resumes after manual conflict resolution and is a `(- *)` mutex against everything else; positional 1 = target branch, positional 2 = optional feature branch (default current). **`git-rebase-patch`** (1 positional): apply a single patch file as a rebase. **`git-rename-branch`** (2 positionals): `[<old-branch>] <new-branch>` — with 1 arg the current branch is renamed to that arg, with 2 args the named branch is renamed. **`git-rename-remote`** (2 positionals): `<old-remote-name> <new-remote-name>`. **`git-sed`** (2 flags + variable positionals): bulk sed across tracked files — `-c` commits with a standard auto-message (fails on unstaged changes), `-f FLAGS` passes sed regex flags (e.g. `-f g` enables global-per-line); positionals are `<search> <replacement> [-- <pathspec>]`. **`git-squash`** (1-flag mutex + 2 positionals): `[--squash-msg] <source-branch|commit-ref> [<commit-message>]` — `--squash-msg` (concatenated squashed messages as the commit message) is explicitly mutually exclusive with a custom commit-message positional, encoded as `(--squash-msg 2)` in `_arguments` syntax so a 2nd-positional candidate disables `--squash-msg` and vice-versa. **Blacklist additions (6) across 5 insertion blocks** (git-rename-branch + git-rename-remote share one block): 1 between `git-pr` (9435) and `git-pull-request` (9436) for `git-psykorebase` (within git-p* at index 5 `r` (114, pr) < `s` (115, psykorebase) < `u` (117, pull)); 1 between `git-quickfix` (9439) and `git-rec` (9440) for `git-rebase-patch` (at index 4 `q` (113, quickfix) < `r` (114, rebase/rec); within git-re* at index 6 `b` (98, rebase) < `c` (99, rec)); **2 between `git-remote-hg` (9447) and `git-rename-tag` (9448)** for `git-rename-branch` + `git-rename-remote` (within git-re* at index 6 `m` (109, remote) < `n` (110, rename); within git-rename-* at index 11 `b` (98, branch) < `r` (114, remote) < `t` (116, tag)); 1 between `git-secrets` (9455) and `git-series` (9456) for `git-sed` (within git-se* at index 6 `c` (99, secret/secrets) < `d` (100, sed) < `r` (114, series)); 1 between `git-split-diffs` (9465) and `git-ssh` (9466) for `git-squash` (within git-s* at index 5 `p` (112, spice/split) < `q` (113, squash) < `s` (115, ssh)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly (no mid-round corrections — fourth consecutive clean round).
- **git-extras Round 6 — git-brv + git-clear + git-clear-soft + git-contrib + git-cp + git-create-branch + git-delete-branch + git-delete-squashed-branches + git-delete-submodule + git-delete-tag + git-force-clone + git-pull-request (12 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-brv` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 52 stems across rounds 150–154 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`/`_git-get`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; `git-get` is pre-existing at `more_src/_git-get` and was excluded from this round to avoid `#compdef` collision). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-brv.1` / `man/git-clear.1` / `man/git-clear-soft.1` / `man/git-contrib.1` / `man/git-cp.1` / `man/git-create-branch.1` / `man/git-delete-branch.1` / `man/git-delete-squashed-branches.1` / `man/git-delete-submodule.1` / `man/git-delete-tag.1` / `man/git-force-clone.1` / `man/git-pull-request.1`. **Source-404 deferrals this round**: `man/git-mailmap.1`, `man/git-merges.1`, `man/git-promote.1`, `man/git-psykick.1`, `man/git-line-summary.1`, `man/git-jira.1` — all listed in the project README but 404 on direct fetch from `tj/git-extras` master; defer per the source-404 precedent (same pattern as round 150's batch of `git-cleanup`/`git-hotfix`/`git-publish`/`git-unpublish`/`git-recall`/`git-since`). Single multi-stem file `_git-brv` is `#compdef git-brv git-clear git-clear-soft git-contrib git-cp git-create-branch git-delete-branch git-delete-squashed-branches git-delete-submodule git-delete-tag git-force-clone git-pull-request`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-brv`** (no flags): list branches sorted by last commit date. **`git-clear`** (2 flag pairs): combined hard reset + clean — `-f/--force` (no warning) + `-h/--help/?`. **`git-clear-soft`** (no flags): `git-clear` that respects `.gitignore`. **`git-contrib`** (1 optional positional): show commits by username or email. **`git-cp`** (2 positionals): copy a file preserving git history. **`git-create-branch`** (3 flags + branchname): `-r/--remote [name]` for remote tracking (defaults to `origin`) + `--from START` (defaults to current branch) + variable branch names. **`git-delete-branch`** (1+ positionals): delete one or more local + remote branches. **`git-delete-squashed-branches`** (1 flag pair + 1 optional positional): `-p/--proceed` to continue on errors + target branch (default current). **`git-delete-submodule`** (1 positional): full submodule removal by path. **`git-delete-tag`** (1+ positionals): delete one or more tags locally + remotely. **`git-force-clone`** (2 flags + 2 positionals): `--help` + `--branch=NAME` (post-clone checkout) + remote URL + destination path (replaces destination if it exists). **`git-pull-request`** (1 optional positional): file a PR against an optional target branch. **Blacklist additions (12) across 8 insertion blocks**: 1 between `git-browse` (9331) and `git-bug` (9332) for `git-brv` (within git-br* at index 6 `a` (97, branch) < `o` (111, browse) < `v` (118, brv)); **2 between `git-clang-format` (9340) and `git-cliff` (9341)** for `git-clear` + `git-clear-soft` (within git-cl* at index 6 `a` (97, clang) < `e` (101, clear) < `i` (105, cliff); within git-clear* at index 9 end-of-string < `-` (45, soft)); 1 between `git-conflict` (9347) and `git-count` (9348) for `git-contrib` (within git-con* at index 7 `f` (102, conflict) < `t` (116, contrib); at index 6 `n` (110, contrib) < `u` (117, count)); **2 between `git-count` (9348) and `git-credential-gcloud` (9349)** for `git-cp` + `git-create-branch` (at index 4 `o` (111, count) < `p` (112, cp/create) < `r` (114, credential); within git-cp* at index 5 end-of-string < `r` (114, create); at index 6 `a` (97, create) < `d` (100, credential)); 1 between `git-dag` (9356) and `git-delete-merged-branches` (9357) for `git-delete-branch` (within git-d* at index 5 `a` (97, dag) < `e` (101, delete); within git-delete-* at index 11 `b` (98, branch) < `m` (109, merged)); **3 between `git-delete-merged-branches` (9357) and `git-delta` (9358)** for `git-delete-squashed-branches` + `git-delete-submodule` + `git-delete-tag` (within git-delete-* at index 11 `m` (109, merged) < `s` (115, squashed/submodule) < `t` (116, tag); within git-delete-s* at index 12 `q` (113, squashed) < `u` (117, submodule); within git-d* at index 5 `e` (101, delete) < `e` consistent then at index 6 `l` (108, delete) < `l` consistent then at index 7 `e` (101, delete) < `t` (116, delta) — so `git-delete-*` < `git-delta`); 1 between `git-flow` (9365) and `git-foresta` (9366) for `git-force-clone` (within git-f* at index 5 `l` (108, flow) < `o` (111, force/foresta); within git-fo* at index 7 `c` (99, force) < `e` (101, foresta)); 1 between `git-pr` (9424) and `git-pulls` (9425) for `git-pull-request` (within git-p* at index 5 `r` (114, pr) < `u` (117, pull/pulls); within git-pull* at index 8 `-` (45, pull-request) < `s` (115, pulls)). - **git-extras Round 5 — git-gh-pages + git-ignore-io + git-lock + git-locked + git-local-commits + git-show-merged-branches + git-show-unmerged-branches + git-show-tree + git-stamp + git-utimes (10 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-gh-pages` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 40 stems across rounds 150–153 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-gh-pages.1` / `man/git-ignore-io.1` / `man/git-lock.1` / `man/git-locked.1` / `man/git-local-commits.1` / `man/git-show-merged-branches.1` / `man/git-show-unmerged-branches.1` / `man/git-show-tree.1` / `man/git-stamp.1` / `man/git-utimes.1`. Single multi-stem file `_git-gh-pages` is `#compdef git-gh-pages git-ignore-io git-lock git-locked git-local-commits git-show-merged-branches git-show-unmerged-branches git-show-tree git-stamp git-utimes`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-gh-pages`** (no flags): creates the gh-pages branch with a default initial index.html. **`git-ignore-io`** (7 flag pairs with 4 mutex groups): manages `.gitignore` via gitignore.io types — repeatable mutex `--append=TYPES` ↔ `--replace=TYPES` (both with short forms `-a`/`-r`), mutex `--list-in-table` ↔ `--list-alphabetically` (`-l`/`-L`), `-s/--search WORD`, `-t/--show-update-time`, `-u/--update-list` (each long-form ↔ short-form pair encoded as its own `_arguments` mutex). **`git-lock`** (1 positional): mark a file as locked via the assume-unchanged bit. **`git-locked`** (no flags): list all locked files. **`git-local-commits`** (no flags, all args passthrough): show commits not yet pushed; all args passed directly to `git log`. **`git-show-merged-branches`** / **`git-show-unmerged-branches`** / **`git-show-tree`** (each: no flags, no positionals): display merged/unmerged branch lists and the decorated commit-history graph respectively. **`git-stamp`** (1 flag + id + variable messages): append issue references to the last commit message — `git stamp [-r|--replace] <id> [<messages>]`; `-r` eliminates previous stamps sharing the same case-insensitive identifier. **`git-utimes`** (1 flag): restore file mtimes to last-commit dates; `--newer` only touches files newer than their last commit. **Blacklist additions (10) across 6 insertion blocks** (git-local-commits + git-lock + git-locked share one block; git-show-merged-branches + git-show-tree + git-show-unmerged-branches share another): 1 between `git-get` (9374) and `git-gone` (9375) for `git-gh-pages` (within git-g* at index 5 `e` (101, get) < `h` (104, gh) < `o` (111, gone)); 1 between `git-ignore` (9385) and `git-imerge` (9386) for `git-ignore-io` (within git-ignore* at index 10 end-of-string < `-` (45, io); at index 5 `g` (103, ignore) < `m` (109, imerge)); **3 between `git-lfs` (9396) and `git-machete` (9397)** for `git-local-commits` + `git-lock` + `git-locked` (within git-loc* at index 7 `a` (97, local) < `k` (107, lock/locked); within git-lock* at index 8 end-of-string < `e` (101, locked); at index 4 `l` (108, l*) < `m` (109, machete)); **3 between `git-shell` (9441) and `git-sim` (9442)** for `git-show-merged-branches` + `git-show-tree` + `git-show-unmerged-branches` (within git-sh* at index 6 `e` (101, shell) < `o` (111, show); within git-show-* at index 9 `m` (109, merged) < `t` (116, tree) < `u` (117, unmerged); within git-s* at index 5 `h` (104, shell/show) < `i` (105, sim)); 1 between `git-stack` (9447) and `git-standup` (9448) for `git-stamp` (within git-sta* at index 7 `c` (99, stack) < `m` (109, stamp) < `n` (110, standup)); 1 between `git-url-sub` (9465) and `git-vendor` (9466) for `git-utimes` (within git-u* at index 5 `r` (114, url) < `t` (116, utimes); at index 4 `u` (117, utimes) < `v` (118, vendor)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly.
- **git-extras Round 4 — git-magic + git-merge-repo + git-missing + git-obliterate + git-pr + git-rename-tag + git-root + git-scp + git-setup (9 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-magic` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 30 stems across rounds 150–152 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-magic.1` / `man/git-merge-repo.1` / `man/git-missing.1` / `man/git-obliterate.1` / `man/git-pr.1` / `man/git-rename-tag.1` / `man/git-root.1` / `man/git-scp.1` / `man/git-setup.1`. **Hunt-skip log this round**: `git-undo` shows `existing=more_src/_ugit` on the `^#compdef` recheck — the pre-existing `_ugit` completion (for the `ugit` interactive git tool) carries `git-undo` in its `#compdef` line, so the round-152 multi-stem excludes `git-undo` to avoid collision; the user already has `git-undo` completions via `_ugit` even though the upstream `tj/git-extras` shipping path is distinct. Single multi-stem file `_git-magic` is `#compdef git-magic git-merge-repo git-missing git-obliterate git-pr git-rename-tag git-root git-scp git-setup`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-magic`** (5 flags + `-h`): quick commit-and-push helper — `-a` adds untracked, repeatable `*-m msg` (concatenated as paragraphs per upstream), `-e` for editor-edit of the auto-message, `-p` for push, `-f` adds `-f` to the push. **`git-merge-repo`** (1 flag + 3 positionals): external-repo subtree merge — `<repo> <branch> <directory> [--squash]`; `--squash` collapses history to a single merge commit. **`git-missing`** (0 flags + 2 positionals + trailing `git log` opts): commit diff between branches — `[<first branch>] <second branch> [<git log options>]`; first defaults to current. **`git-obliterate`** (0 flags + files + trailing rev-list opts): erase files from entire history — `<files...> [-- <rev-list options...>]`; rev-list options after `--` per upstream's `git help rev-list`. **`git-pr`** (1-flag mutex + variable positional shapes): GitHub PR checkout — 4 synopsis shapes (number+remote, [remote]:number..., url..., `clean`) all dispatched via the same `*:` positional; `-m/--merge` switches between branch-tip vs merge-commit checkout. **`git-rename-tag`** (0 flags + 2 positionals): `<old-tag-name> <new-tag-name>` — local + remote tag rename. **`git-root`** (1 flag): `-r/--relative` for relative-from-root output, otherwise absolute root path. **`git-scp`** (1 flag + 2 positionals): secure-copy git changes via remote — `-h` for help; positional 1 = remote, positional 2+ = commits or paths; per upstream `git rscp` is the reverse-direction sibling that swaps the transfer direction (deliberately not stemmed by this completion since it's a separate command). **`git-setup`** (1 optional positional): `[<directory>]` — initialize a new repo in DIR (default cwd). **Blacklist additions (9) across 8 insertion blocks** (git-merge-repo + git-missing share one block): 1 between `git-machete` (9397) and `git-mediate` (9398) for `git-magic` (within git-ma* at index 5 `a` (97, both); at index 6 `c` (99, machete) < `g` (103, magic)); **2 between `git-merge-into` (9399) and `git-mob` (9400)** for `git-merge-repo` + `git-missing` (within git-merge-* at index 10 `i` (105, into) < `r` (114, repo); within git-m* at index 5 `e` (101, merge) < `i` (105, missing) < `o` (111, mob)); 1 between `git-number` (9408) and `git-octopus` (9409) for `git-obliterate` (at index 4 `n` (110, number) < `o` (111, both); within git-o* at index 5 `b` (98, obliterate) < `c` (99, octopus)); 1 between `git-pkgs` (9414) and `git-pulls` (9415) for `git-pr` (within git-p* at index 5 `k` (107, pkgs) < `r` (114, pr) < `u` (117, pulls)); 1 between `git-remote-hg` (9425) and `git-review` (9426) for `git-rename-tag` (within git-re* at index 6 `m` (109, remote) < `n` (110, rename) < `v` (118, review)); 1 between `git-revise` (9427) and `git-router` (9428) for `git-root` (within git-r* at index 5 `e` (101, revise) < `o` (111, root/router); within git-ro* at index 6 `o` (111, root) < `u` (117, router)); 1 between `git-router` (9428) and `git-secret` (9429) for `git-scp` (at index 4 `r` (114, router) < `s` (115, both scp/secret); within git-s* at index 5 `c` (99, scp) < `e` (101, secret)); 1 between `git-series` (9431) and `git-shell` (9432) for `git-setup` (within git-se* at index 6 `r` (114, series) < `t` (116, setup); within git-s* at index 5 `e` (101, setup) < `h` (104, shell)). LC_ALL=C sort verified directly (no mid-round corrections this round, in contrast to round 151's git-count fix).
- **git-extras Round 3 — git-abort + git-alias + git-authors + git-browse + git-bulk + git-coauthor + git-commits-since + git-count + git-fork + git-fresh-branch + git-graft + git-guilt + git-merge-into (13 more tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-abort` file; cumulative git-extras coverage now 21 stems across rounds 150–151 + the pre-existing `_git-changelog`/`_git-info`/`_git-ignore`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; `git-info` and `git-ignore` are both pre-existing at `more_src/_git-info` and `more_src/_git-ignore` and were excluded from this round to avoid `#compdef` collisions). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-abort.1` / `man/git-alias.1` / `man/git-authors.1` / `man/git-browse.1` / `man/git-bulk.1` / `man/git-coauthor.1` / `man/git-commits-since.1` / `man/git-count.1` / `man/git-fork.1` / `man/git-fresh-branch.1` / `man/git-graft.1` / `man/git-guilt.1` / `man/git-merge-into.1`. **GitHub API `man/` directory listing now used as the canonical source-of-truth** (the listing for `tj/git-extras/contents/man?ref=master` returns reliable file presence in 130+-entry pages; this round verified by cross-checking each candidate against that listing before fetching). Single multi-stem file `_git-abort` is `#compdef git-abort git-alias git-authors git-browse git-bulk git-coauthor git-commits-since git-count git-fork git-fresh-branch git-graft git-guilt git-merge-into`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`git-abort`** (no flags, no positionals): aborts the current operation per upstream "There are no options, it just aborts current operation." **`git-alias`** (2-flag mutex + variable positionals): `--global` ↔ `--local` mutex; 9 synopsis shapes collapse to (optional flag) + (search-pattern or alias-name + command). **`git-authors`** (3 flags): `-l/--list` and `--no-email`. **`git-browse`** (4 positionals): `[remote_name] [file_name] [line_1] [line_2]` — remote defaults to first remote when omitted; line_2 forms a range with line_1. **`git-bulk`** (10 flags + variable positionals — the most-complex surface of this round): 6 distinct synopsis shapes encoded as a single 7-way mutex `(-a -w --addworkspace --removeworkspace --addcurrent --purge --listall)` with the modifier flag `-g` (confirmation prompt) usable across all execute-modes and the auxiliary flag `--from URL_OR_FILE` only meaningful with `--addworkspace`. **`git-coauthor`** (2 positionals): `<co-author> <co-author-email>`. **`git-commits-since`** (1 optional positional): date string (defaults to "last week" when omitted). **`git-count`** (1 flag): `--all` switches between total-commit-count and per-author breakdown. **`git-fork`** (1 optional positional): GitHub repo URL — works without args inside an existing GitHub repo directory. **`git-fresh-branch`** (1 positional): branch name for the new orphan branch. **`git-graft`** (2 positionals): `<src-branch> <dest-branch>` — merge src into dest. **`git-guilt`** (4 flags + 2 optional positionals): `-h` for usage, `-e/--email`, `-w/--ignore-whitespace`, `-d/--debug`; positional `<since> [<until>]` revisions. **`git-merge-into`** (1 flag + 2 positionals): `--ff-only` + `[src] <dest>` — src defaults to current branch when omitted. **Blacklist additions (13) across 13 insertion blocks** — **with one mid-round sort fix** caught by `LC_ALL=C sort -c` for `git-count`: 1 between `git` (9316) and `git-absorb` (9317) for `git-abort` (within git-* at index 5 `b` (98, both abort/absorb) consistent; at index 6 `o` (111, abort) < `s` (115, absorb)); 1 between `git-ai` (9318) and `git-annex` (9319) for `git-alias` (within git-a* at index 5 `i` (105, ai) < `l` (108, alias) < `n` (110, annex)); 1 between `git-archive-file` (9324) and `git-autofixup` (9325) for `git-authors` (within git-a* at index 5 `r` (114, archive) < `u` (117, both authors/autofixup); within git-au* at index 6 `t` (116, both); at index 7 `h` (104, authors) < `o` (111, autofixup)); 1 between `git-branchless` (9327) and `git-bug` (9328) for `git-browse` (within git-b* at index 5 `r` (114, both branchless/browse); at index 6 `a` (97, branch) < `o` (111, browse) < `u` (117, bug from index 5)); 1 between `git-buildpackage` (9329) and `git-cal` (9330) for `git-bulk` (within git-bu* at index 6 `i` (105, build) < `l` (108, bulk); at index 4 `b` (98, bu*) < `c` (99, cal)); 1 between `git-clone` (9337) and `git-codereview` (9338) for `git-coauthor` (within git-c* at index 5 `l` (108, clone) < `o` (111, coauthor/codereview); within git-co* at index 6 `a` (97, coauthor) < `d` (100, codereview)); 1 between `git-cola` (9339) and `git-conflict` (9340) for `git-commits-since` (within git-co* at index 6 `l` (108, cola) < `m` (109, commits) < `n` (110, conflict)); **1 between `git-conflict` (9340) and `git-credential-gcloud` (9341)** for `git-count` (**initially incorrectly placed AFTER `git-credential-oauth`; the `sort -c` recheck reported "disorder: git-count at 9352"**; within git-co* at index 5 `o` (111, both); at index 6 `n` (110, conflict) < `u` (117, count) and `o` (111, count) < `r` (114, credential) — so `git-conflict` < `git-count` < `git-credential-*` — the original placement was off by ~10 entries); 1 between `git-foresta` (9358) and `git-format-staged` (9359) for `git-fork` (within git-fo* at index 6 `r` (114, both foresta/fork/format); at index 7 `e` (101, foresta) < `k` (107, fork) < `m` (109, format)); 1 between `git-fresh` (9360) and `git-ftp` (9361) for `git-fresh-branch` (within git-fresh* at index 9 end-of-string < `-` (45, branch); at index 5 `r` (114, fresh) < `t` (116, ftp)); 1 between `git-grab` (9366) and `git-graph` (9367) for `git-graft` (within git-gra* at index 7 `b` (98, grab) < `f` (102, graft) < `p` (112, graph)); 1 between `git-graph` (9367) and `git-hooks` (9368) for `git-guilt` (within git-g* at index 5 `r` (114, graph) < `u` (117, guilt); at index 4 `g` (103, g*) < `h` (104, hooks)); 1 between `git-mediate` (9386) and `git-mob` (9387) for `git-merge-into` (within git-m* at index 5 `e` (101, mediate/merge) < `o` (111, mob); within git-me* at index 6 `d` (100, mediate) < `r` (114, merge)).
