- **git-extras Round 2 — git-effort + git-summary + git-feature + git-release + git-mr + git-archive-file + git-touch (7 tj/git-extras subcommands via single multi-stem `_git-effort` file; extends the pre-existing `more_src/_git-changelog` to an 8-stem git-extras corpus, with Round 149's `_git-foresta`/`_git-stein` standalone files for adjacent git plugin coverage)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; the existing `_git-changelog` covers the eighth main git-extras subcommand and was specifically excluded from this round to avoid `#compdef` collision). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream manpages at [github.com/tj/git-extras](https://github.com/tj/git-extras) — `man/git-effort.1` / `man/git-summary.1` / `man/git-feature.1` / `man/git-release.1` / `man/git-mr.1` / `man/git-archive-file.1` / `man/git-touch.1`. **Source-404 deferrals**: `man/git-cleanup.1`, `man/git-hotfix.1`, `man/git-publish.1`, `man/git-unpublish.1`, `man/git-recall.1`, `man/git-since.1` — all 404 on direct fetch from the tj/git-extras `master` branch despite the subcommands being listed in the project README; defer per the source-404 precedent, will revisit if/when the man pages land. Single multi-stem file `_git-effort` is `#compdef git-effort git-summary git-feature git-release git-mr git-archive-file git-touch`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact SYNOPSIS line in the corresponding upstream `.1` manpage): **`git-effort`** (1 flag + path positionals + post-`--` git log opts): `git-effort [--above <value>] [<path>...] [-- [<log options>...]]` — `--above N` ignores files with commits ≤ N; the `--` separator marks the boundary between effort options and `git log` options (the "Commit Limiting" section of `git-log` is the relevant subset per upstream). **`git-summary`** (4 flags + optional committish/path): two synopsis shapes — `git-summary [--dedup-by-email] [--no-merges] [<committish>]` and `git-summary --line [<path>]` — encoded as a 3-way mutex: `--line` cannot combine with `--dedup-by-email` or `--no-merges`; `--output-style tabular|oneline` is a 2-value enum applied across both shapes. **`git-feature`** (6 flags + NAME or `finish`): two synopsis shapes — create form `git-feature [-a PREFIX] [-s SEP] [-r [REMOTE]] [--from START] NAME...` and finish form `git-feature [-a PREFIX] [-s SEP] finish [--squash] NAME...`; `-a/--alias` defaults to `feature`, `-s/--separator` defaults to `/`, `-r/--remote` defaults to `origin` when its optarg is omitted, `--from` defaults to current branch when omitted; the `finish` literal positional triggers `--squash` availability. **`git-release`** (9 flags + tagname or hook args): semver-aware release with pre/post-release hooks; `--semver major|minor|patch` (3-value enum) drives auto-increment; `--prefix STRING` prepends to semver tags (e.g. `r4.4.0` → `r4.5.0` with `--prefix r`); `-r REMOTE` push destination; `-m MESSAGE` custom commit message; `--no-empty-commit`; `-c` generates/updates changelog; `-s` creates signed annotated tag; `-u KEY-ID` GPG key for signed tag; any arguments after the `--` are passed to pre/post-release hooks. **`git-mr`** (0 flags, 3 shapes): GitLab merge-request checkout — `git-mr <number> [<remote>]` (remote defaults to `origin`), `git-mr <url>` (parses full GitLab MR URL), `git-mr clean` (removes local MR-checkout branches). **`git-archive-file`** (no options): creates a ZIP of the current HEAD; upstream manpage explicitly states "This command does not take any options." **`git-touch`** (1 positional): creates + stages a new file in one operation. **Blacklist additions (7) across 7 insertion blocks**: 1 between `git-archive-all` (9323) and `git-autofixup` (9324) for `git-archive-file` (within git-archive-* at index 12 `a` (97, all) < `f` (102, file); within git-a* at index 5 `r` (114, archive) < `u` (117, autofixup)); 1 between `git-delta` (9349) and `git-extras` (9350) for `git-effort` (at index 4 `d` (100, delta) < `e` (101, effort) and `e` < `e` then at index 5 within git-e* `f` (102, effort) < `x` (120, extras)); 1 between `git-fame` (9351) and `git-filter-repo` (9352) for `git-feature` (within git-fa* at index 5 `a` (97, fame) consistent; within git-f* at index 5 `a` (97, fame) < `e` (101, feature) < `i` (105, filter)); 1 between `git-modified-branches.sh` (9386) and `git-multi` (9387) for `git-mr` (within git-m* at index 5 `o` (111, modified) < `r` (114, mr) < `u` (117, multi)); 1 between `git-relation` (9404) and `git-remote-codecommit` (9405) for `git-release` (within git-rel* at index 7 `a` (97, relation) < `e` (101, release); within git-re* at index 6 `l` (108, release) < `m` (109, remote)); 1 between `git-suggest-coauthors` (9428) and `git-svn` (9429) for `git-summary` (within git-su* at index 6 `g` (103, suggest) < `m` (109, summary); within git-s* at index 5 `u` (117, su*) < `v` (118, svn)); 1 between `git-sync` (9430) and `git-town` (9431) for `git-touch` (within git-t* at index 5 `o` (111, both); within git-to* at index 6 `u` (117, touch) < `w` (119, town); within git-s* at index 5 `u` (117, sync) < git-t* `t` (116) — wait, **'s' (115) < 't' (116)** so git-sync < git-touch). All position-by-position; sort verified directly via `LC_ALL=C sort -c` (no mid-round corrections needed this round, in contrast to round 149). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **JtR final mop-up Round 6 + git-* extras Round 1 — telegram2john + ios7tojohn + itunes_backup2john (3 last JtR helpers in `_telegram2john` multi-stem) + git-foresta + git-stein (2 fresh standalone git extensions, each in its own file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). **JtR-side**: 3 stems verified via direct fetch of `run/telegram2john.py` / `run/ios7tojohn.pl` / `run/itunes_backup2john.pl`. This round flushes the long-running JtR campaign — the broader run/ directory listing on GitHub appears to have **substantial hallucinated entries** (the api/tree listings claim files like `openvpn2john`/`pgp2john`/`putty2john`/`raw2john`/`vdi2john`/`wallet2john`/`whirlpool2john`/`wincreds2john`/`sxc2john`/`dominosec2john`/`o5logon2john`/`odf2john` but every one of these 404s on direct raw fetch — the source-of-truth is the raw URL, not the listing API). **Source-404 deferrals**: all 12 of the above plus `racf2john` (deferred since round 145). **Already-covered skip log**: `truecrypt2john` (at `src/_truecrypt2john`) and `pwsafe2john` (at `src/_pwsafe2john`) — both source-verified this round but pre-existing in `src/`. **git-extras side**: 2 standalone tools picked from a broader hunt where most names 404 on upstream fetch (`git-bonsai`/`git-codeowners`/`git-friendly`/`git-tidy`/`git-tools`/`git-trial`/`git-clone-quick`/`git-lab` are all in the FRESH hit set but the first two 404 their READMEs on direct fetch; git-friendly's surface is 5 git-subcommands rather than a single binary so the JtR-style positional-file completion doesn't fit). Both verified surfaces transcribed from upstream READMEs: [github.com/takaaki-kasai/git-foresta](https://github.com/takaaki-kasai/git-foresta) and [github.com/sh5i/git-stein](https://github.com/sh5i/git-stein). **Per-stem coverage**: **`_telegram2john` multi-stem** `#compdef telegram2john ios7tojohn itunes_backup2john`. **`telegram2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positional files-or-dirs): Telegram Desktop hash extractor accepting either a Telegram data dir (recursively searched) or individual `userconfig.xml`/`map0`/`map1`/`key_datas` files; upstream strips trailing quote characters. Linux: `~/.local/share/TelegramDesktop`; Windows: `C:/Users/<Name>/AppData/Roaming/Telegram Desktop`. **`ios7tojohn`** (0 flags, 1+ `.plist` positionals): Apple iOS 7 restrictions-password plist parser; outputs PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA1 hashes; handles both single-line and multi-line XML `<data>` elements. **`itunes_backup2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `Manifest.plist` positionals): iTunes backup parser with two output formats by iOS version — pre-iOS 10 `$itunes_backup$*9*<wpky>*<iter>*<salt>**`, iOS 10.x+ `$itunes_backup$*10*<wpky>*<iter>*<salt>*<dpic>*<dpsl>`; WPKY/SALT/DPSL field lengths validated upstream (40/20/20 bytes). **`_git-foresta` single-stem**: 11 named flags — `--help`/`--version`, `--style 1|2|10|15` (4-value enum), `--svdepth=N` (default 2), `--graph-margin-left=N` (default 2), `--graph-margin-right=N` (default 1), 5 symbol-customization flags (`--graph-symbol-commit/merge/overpass/root/tip` with their respective `●`/`◎`/`═`/`■`/`○` defaults), `--no-status` — plus a final `*:git log argument:` catch-all because the script passes through to `git log` (and explicitly rejects `--pretty`/`--format` per upstream). **`_git-stein` single-stem**: 19 named flags + 1 source positional — covers all 3 documented modes (Overwrite = source only; Transform = source + `-o target`; Duplicate = source + `-o target` + `-d`); the completion encodes the `-o/--output`/`-d/--duplicate`/`-j/--jobs`/`-n/--dry-run`/`-q/--quiet`/`-v/--verbose` long-form ↔ short-form mutex pairs explicitly; `--log` carries a 9-value enum drawn from `java.util.logging.Level` (`OFF`/`SEVERE`/`WARNING`/`INFO`/`CONFIG`/`FINE`/`FINER`/`FINEST`/`ALL`). **Blacklist additions (5) across 5 insertion blocks** — and **one mid-round sort fix** caught by `LC_ALL=C sort -c` during the verification step: 1 between `git-flow` (9354) and `git-format-staged` (9355) for `git-foresta` (initially incorrectly placed AFTER `git-format-staged`; the `sort -c` recheck reported "disorder: git-foresta at 9356"; within git-f* at index 5 `l` (108, flow) < `o` (111, format/foresta); within git-fo* at index 7 `e` (101, foresta) < `m` (109, format) — so `git-foresta` < `git-format-staged`); 1 between `git-stats` (9421) and `git-submodule-move` (9422) for `git-stein` (also initially incorrectly placed BETWEEN `git-stack` and `git-standup`; `sort -c` reported "disorder: git-standup at 9422"; within git-st* at index 6 `a` (97, stack/standup/stats) < `e` (101, stein); within git-sta* at index 7 `c` (99, stack) < `n` (110, standup) < `t` (116, stats) so `git-stack` < `git-standup` < `git-stats` < `git-stein`; within git-s* at index 5 `t` (116, st*) < `u` (117, submodule)); 1 between `ios-deploy` (11890) and `ios_webkit_debug_proxy` (11891) for `ios7tojohn` (within ios* at index 3 `-` (45, deploy) < `7` (55, ios7tojohn) < `_` (95, webkit)); 1 between `itunes` (12159) and `itweb-settings` (12160) for `itunes_backup2john` (within it* at index 2 `u` (117, itunes) < `w` (119, itweb); within itunes* at index 6 end-of-string < `_` (95, backup)); 1 between `telegram-cli` (25556) and `telemetrygen` (25557) for `telegram2john` (within telegram* at index 8 `-` (45, telegram-cli) < `2` (50, john); at index 4 `g` (103, telegram) < `m` (109, telemetry)). The 2 mid-round sort fixes are documented as a worked example of the value of `LC_ALL=C sort -c` as a post-edit guardrail; in both cases the position-by-position reasoning was incorrect on first attempt and the sort-checker is what caught the off-by-one. LC_ALL=C sort verified after the corrections.
- **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 5 — bestcryptve2john + kdcdump2john + lion2john + lion2john-alt + mac2john-alt + mcafee_epo2john + money2john + mozilla2john + multibit2john + neo2john (10 more hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; cumulative JtR-helper coverage now 66 stems across rounds 144–148 + the pre-existing 23 dedicated single-stem files)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `run/bestcryptve2john.py` / `run/kdcdump2john.py` / `run/lion2john.pl` / `run/lion2john-alt.pl` / `run/mac2john-alt.py` / `run/mcafee_epo2john.py` / `run/money2john.py` / `run/mozilla2john.py` / `run/multibit2john.py` / `run/neo2john.py`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck before write): `libreoffice2john` (at `src/_libreoffice2john`), `luks2john` (at `src/_luks2john`), `mac2john` (at `src/_mac2john`), `mosquitto2john` (at `src/_mosquitto2john`), `openbsd_softraid2john` (at `src/_openbsd_softraid2john`), `enpass5tojohn` (at `src/_enpass5tojohn`), `keyring2john` (at `src/_keyring2john`) — 7 read from upstream source this round but found to be pre-existing single-stem files in `src/`, so excluded from the multi-stem. **Skipped without adding**: `mongodb2john.js` — the upstream source has no command-line argument parsing because the file is meant to be passed as a script to `mongosh` (e.g. `mongosh admin mongodb2john.js`), not invoked as a standalone command, so a binary-stem completion would never be triggered (the user never types `mongodb2john` at a shell prompt). `racf2john` — upstream `run/racf2john.py` 404s in the bleeding-jumbo branch; defer per the source-404 precedent. Single multi-stem file `_bestcryptve2john` is `#compdef bestcryptve2john kdcdump2john lion2john lion2john-alt mac2john-alt mcafee_epo2john money2john mozilla2john multibit2john neo2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact usage string in upstream source): **`bestcryptve2john`** (2 flags + 1 positional via argparse): Jetico BestCrypt Volume encryption extractor; `-h/--help` + `--offset=N` (byte offset to BestCrypt partition, default 0) + required 1st positional disk/partition image. **`kdcdump2john`** (0 flags, 1 positional): Kerberos KDC dump → JtR converter; identifies format types 23 (RC4-HMAC) and 18 (AES256). **`lion2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.plist` positionals): OS X 10.7 Lion salted-SHA-512 plist extractor; reads up to 1 MB per file, substitutes `UNKNOWN_USERNAME` when the username field cannot be parsed. **`lion2john-alt`** (0 flags, 1+ `.plist` positionals): alternative OS X Lion plist parser (`.pl` script, same surface as `lion2john`). **`mac2john-alt`** (0 flags, 1+ `.plist` positionals): Python 2 variant of `mac2john` for OS X / macOS 10.8+ ShadowHashData; the companion `src/_mac2john` covers the Python 3 mainline. **`mcafee_epo2john`** (0 flags, 1+ CSV positionals — `-` reads stdin): McAfee ePO OrionUsers CSV converter; CSV columns 1=username, 2=AuthURI, default separator comma. **`money2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): MS Money 2002-2007 / Money Plus encrypted-DB extractor; output type byte 1 = SHA1, 0 = MD5. **`mozilla2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `key3.db` positionals): Mozilla key3.db master-password extractor. **`multibit2john`** (0 flags, 1+ wallet positionals): MultiBit Classic or HD wallet extractor; accepts `.key` (Classic key backup), `.wallet` (Classic), or `mbhd.wallet.aes` (HD). **`neo2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.db3` positionals): Neo wallet SQLite extractor; queries Key table for rows where the first column equals `'PasswordHash'`. **Blacklist additions (10) across 9 insertion blocks** (lion2john and lion2john-alt share one block): 1 between `bestcrypt2john` (2132) and `bestls` (2133) for `bestcryptve2john` (within bestcrypt* at index 9 `2` (50, 2john) < `v` (118, ve2john); within best* at index 4 `c` (99, crypt) < `l` (108, ls)); 1 between `kdbx` (12734) and `kdcsetup` (12735) for `kdcdump2john` (within kd* at index 2 `b` (98, kdb) < `c` (99, kdc); within kdc* at index 3 `d` (100, kdcdump) < `s` (115, kdcsetup)); **2 between `linuxsampler` (13894) and `lipo` (13895)** for `lion2john` and `lion2john-alt` (within li* at index 2 `n` (110, linux) < `o` (111, lion) < `p` (112, lipo); within lion2john* at index 8 end-of-string < `-` (45, alt) so `lion2john` < `lion2john-alt`); 1 between `mac2john` (15274) and `mac2unix` (15275) for `mac2john-alt` (within mac2john* at index 8 end < `-` (45, alt) < `u` (117, unix from mac2unix)); 1 between `mcabber` (15643) and `mcap` (15644) for `mcafee_epo2john` (within mc* at index 2 `a` (97, mca-mcap) consistent; within mca* at index 3 `b` (98, mcabber) < `f` (102, mcafee) < `p` (112, mcap)); 1 between `monerod` (16421) and `mongo` (16422) for `money2john` (at index 3 within mon* `e` (101, money) < `g` (103, mongo); at index 4 within mone* `r` (114, monerod) < `y` (121, money)); 1 between `mozilla-xremote-client` (16585) and `mozjpeg` (16586) for `mozilla2john` (within mozilla* at index 7 `-` (45, mozilla-x) < `2` (50, john); at index 3 `i` (105, mozil) < `j` (106, mozjpeg)); 1 between `mullvad` (16819) and `multica` (16820) for `multibit2john` (within mu* at index 2 `l` (108, mullvad) < `l` then index 3 within mul* `l` (108, mull) < `t` (116, mult); within multi* at index 4 `b` (98, multibit) < `i` (105, multica)); 1 between `neo` (17238) and `neo4j` (17239) for `neo2john` (within neo* at index 3 end-of-string < `2` (50, john) < `4` (52, neo4j)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 4 — DPAPImk2john + adxcsouf2john + apop2john + diskcryptor2john + ejabberd2john + encdatavault2john + fvde2john + geli2john + gitea2john + ibmiscanner2john + iwork2john + keplr2john + known_hosts2john + krb2john (14 more hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; cumulative JtR-helper coverage now 56 stems across rounds 144–147 + the pre-existing 17 dedicated single-stem files)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `run/DPAPImk2john.py` / `run/adxcsouf2john.py` / `run/apop2john.py` / `run/diskcryptor2john.py` / `run/ejabberd2john.py` / `run/encdatavault2john.py` / `run/fvde2john.py` / `run/geli2john.py` / `run/gitea2john.py` / `run/ibmiscanner2john.py` / `run/iwork2john.py` / `run/keplr2john.py` / `run/known_hosts2john.py` / `run/krb2john.py`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck before write): `deepsound2john` (at `src/_deepsound2john`), `electrum2john` (at `src/_electrum2john`), `enpass2john` (at `src/_enpass2john`), `hccapx2john` (at `src/_hccapx2john`), `kirbi2john` (at `src/_kirbi2john`), `kwallet2john` (at `src/_kwallet2john`) — all 6 read from upstream source this round but found to be pre-existing single-stem files in `src/`, so excluded from the multi-stem to avoid `#compdef` collisions. Single multi-stem file `_DPAPImk2john` is `#compdef DPAPImk2john adxcsouf2john apop2john diskcryptor2john ejabberd2john encdatavault2john fvde2john geli2john gitea2john ibmiscanner2john iwork2john keplr2john known_hosts2john krb2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** — this round contains the **most-complex single surface added to the JtR helpers so far** (`DPAPImk2john`) plus several that are still simple positional extractors: **`DPAPImk2john`** (~6 flags, 0 positionals — the only round-147 stem with non-trivial flag handling): Windows DPAPI masterkey extractor with two mutually exclusive operating modes — the **Standard mode** requires all three of `-S/--sid SID` + `-mk/--masterkey FILE` + `-c/--context CONTEXT` (where CONTEXT is the 4-value enum `domain|domain1607+|domain1607-|local`), while the **Preferred mode** takes `-P/--preferred FILE` alone and the completion encodes mutual exclusion by listing `(-P --preferred)` on each Standard-mode flag and `(-mk --masterkey -S --sid -c --context)` on each Preferred-mode flag; shared across both modes: `-d/--debug` and `--password=PASSWORD`. **`adxcsouf2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `*.DAT` positionals): IBM/Toshiba 4690 OS ADXCSOUF.DAT/ADXEPW0F.DAT password-hash extractor; upstream prints "Tip: Maximum password length is 8 on IBM/Toshiba 4690 systems" to stderr. **`apop2john`** (0 flags, 1+ pcap positionals): APOP challenge/response extractor from packet captures; output format `username:$dynamic_1017$hash$HEX$salt`; requires `scapy`. **`diskcryptor2john`** (0 flags, 1+ device/raw-image positionals): DiskCryptor volume-header extractor; reads first 2048 bytes, calculates entropy, warns if <6 bits/byte unless env-var `FORCE=1` is set; cascaded ciphers explicitly not supported per upstream comment. **`ejabberd2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): parses files generated by `ejabberdctl dump`. **`encdatavault2john`** (0 flags, **1 directory** positional): ENCSecurity DataVault folder extractor — takes a directory not a file, so the completion uses `_files -/` to restrict completion to dirs. **`fvde2john`** (0 flags, exactly 1 positional via argparse): FileVault Drive Encryption disk-image extractor. **`geli2john`** (0 flags, 1+ disk-image positionals): FreeBSD GELI metadata extractor; seeks to last 1024 bytes, looks for `GEOM::ELI` magic, supports AES-XTS and AES-CBC, metadata versions 0-7. **`gitea2john`** (1 flag, **stdin-driven**): `-h/--help` plus stdin input format `username:hex_salt:hex_hash:algorithm` where algorithm follows `pbkdf2$iterations$keylen`; typical pipeline `sqlite3 gitea.db 'SELECT name,salt,passwd,passwd_hash_algo FROM user;' | gitea2john.py`. **`ibmiscanner2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): IBM iSeries (ibmiscanner output) → AS400-SHA converter; transforms `username:hexhash` → `username:$as400ssha1$hexhash$username`. **`iwork2john`** (0 flags, 1+ Apple-iWork positionals — `.key`/`.numbers`/`.pages`): handles both iWork '09 and 2013/2014 formats. **`keplr2john`** (0 flags, **2 positionals** — 1 required folder + 1 optional password): Keplr Chrome-extension LevelDB extractor; when the optional 2nd arg (password) is supplied the script attempts scrypt-KDF + AES-128-CTR decryption rather than just dumping; upstream comment requires that the first arg must **not** begin with a hyphen. **`known_hosts2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): hashed-entry extractor from SSH known_hosts files; lines starting `|1|` go to stdout as `$known_hosts$<data>`, non-hashed entries go to stderr. **`krb2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.pdml` positionals): Kerberos hash extractor from PDML (packet description markup language) files generated by `tshark -r capture.pcap -T pdml`. **Blacklist additions (14) across 14 insertion blocks** — verified position-by-position: 1 between `DP3` (69) and `DPPP` (70) for `DPAPImk2john` (within DP* at index 2 `3` (51) < `A` (65) < `P` (80) — the only round-147 insert in the uppercase block); 1 between `adwaita-1-demo` (637) and `aea` (638) for `adxcsouf2john` (within ad* at index 2 `v` (118, adv) < `w` (119, adw) < `x` (120, adx); at index 1 `d` (100, ad) < `e` (101, ae)); 1 between `apollo` (1125) and `app-sso` (1126) for `apop2john` (within apo* at index 3 `l` (108, llo) < `p` (112, pop); at index 2 `o` (111, apo) < `p` (112, app)); 1 between `diskard` (6276) and `diskinfo` (6277) for `diskcryptor2john` (within disk* at index 4 `a` (97, ard) < `c` (99, cryptor) < `i` (105, info)); 1 between `eix` (7165) and `ejabberdctl` (7166) for `ejabberd2john` (within e* at index 1 `i` (105, eix) < `j` (106, ejabb); within ejabberd* at index 8 `2` (50, john) < `c` (99, ctl)); 1 between `enca` (7286) and `encfs` (7287) for `encdatavault2john` (within enc* at index 3 `a` (97, enca) < `d` (100, encdata) < `f` (102, encfs)); 1 between `fuzztran` (8605) and `fvdeinfo` (8606) for `fvde2john` (within f* at index 1 `u` (117, fuzz) < `v` (118, fvde); within fvde* at index 4 `2` (50, john) < `i` (105, info)); 1 between `geli` (8986) and `gem` (8987) for `geli2john` (within g* at index 2 `l` (108, geli) < `m` (109, gem); 1 between `ibmgtsim` (11385) and `ibnetdiscover` (11386) for `ibmiscanner2john` (within ibm* at index 3 `g` (103, gtsim) < `i` (105, iscanner); at index 2 `m` (109, ibm) < `n` (110, ibn)); 1 between `iwmbtfw` (12164) and `iwpriv` (12165) for `iwork2john` (within iw* at index 2 `m` (109, mbtfw) < `o` (111, work) < `p` (112, priv)); - **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 3 — 1password2john + aem2john + androidbackup2john + ansible2john + apex2john + applenotes2john + aruba2john + atmail2john + authenticator2john + bestcrypt2john + bitshares2john + cardano2john + ccache2john + cisco2john + coinomi2john + cracf2john (16 more hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; brings cumulative JtR-helper coverage to 42 stems when combined with rounds 144–145 + the pre-existing `_john`/`_zip2john`/`_rar2john`/`_wpapcap2john`/`_ethereum2john`/`_lastpass2john`/`_dmg2john`/`_bitlocker2john`/`_encfs2john`/`_office2john`/`_aix2john`/`_andotp2john`/`_bks2john`/`_blockchain2john`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `run/1password2john.py` / `run/aem2john.py` / `run/androidbackup2john.py` / `run/ansible2john.py` / `run/apex2john.py` / `run/applenotes2john.py` / `run/aruba2john.py` / `run/atmail2john.pl` / `run/authenticator2john.py` / `run/bestcrypt2john.py` / `run/bitshares2john.py` / `run/cardano2john.py` / `run/ccache2john.py` / `run/cisco2john.pl` / `run/coinomi2john.py` / `run/cracf2john.py`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck before write): `aix2john` (at `src/_aix2john`), `andotp2john` (at `src/_andotp2john`), `bks2john` (at `src/_bks2john`), `blockchain2john` (at `src/_blockchain2john`) — all 4 read from upstream source this round but found to be pre-existing single-stem files in `src/`, so excluded from the multi-stem to avoid `#compdef` collisions. Single multi-stem file `_1password2john` is `#compdef 1password2john aem2john androidbackup2john ansible2john apex2john applenotes2john aruba2john atmail2john authenticator2john bestcrypt2john bitshares2john cardano2john ccache2john cisco2john coinomi2john cracf2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact `Usage:` string in upstream source): **`1password2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals — files OR directories): accepts 1Password Agile Keychain dirs, Cloud Keychain dirs, or `OnePassword.sqlite` files; `process_file()` auto-detects which shape based on whether the arg is a SQLite file or a directory. **`aem2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Adobe Experience Manager SSPR format converter; identifies `{SHA-256}` / `{SHA-512}` tagged lines with optional username prefix. **`androidbackup2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.ab` positionals): Android Backup file parser. **`ansible2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.yml`/`.yaml` positionals): Ansible Vault YAML parser; upstream source rejects anything other than AES256 cipher. **`apex2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Oracle APEX hash CSV parser (comma-delimited 3-field lines). **`applenotes2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.sqlite` positionals): Apple Notes SQLite parser; typical input lives at `~/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.notes/NoteStore.sqlite`. **`aruba2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): ArubaOS password-hash converter; output targets JtR dynamic format. **`atmail2john`** (0 flags, 0 positionals — **stdin-only**): atmail SSHA256 → JtR `$dynamic_62$` converter; reads stdin via `<>` so use shell redirection or pipe. **`authenticator2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Authenticator-app data-file parser; upstream notes "JtR doesn't have a format for cracking this yet" — converter exists for future format support. **`bestcrypt2john`** (0 flags, 1+ `.jbc` positionals): Jetico BestCrypt container parser. **`bitshares2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals — three input shapes): accepts SQLite databases, `.bin` backup wallet files, or `.ldb` LevelDB files; runtime detection chooses between SQL query, binary pattern matching, or hex conversion. **`cardano2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Cardano legacy secret-key extractor; requires `cbor2` library. **`ccache2john`** (0 flags, 1 positional): single Kerberos credential cache file (typically `/tmp/krb5cc_<uid>`); validates magic byte (`0x5` = standard, `0x76` = untested alt). **`cisco2john`** (1 flag, 0+ positionals — **stdin-fallback**): `cisco2john [-h] [cisco config file(s)]` — `-h` shows usage and exits; with 0 file args the script reads stdin via `while (<>)`. Output goes to stdout (extracted hashes) and stderr (plaintext + seed data for a wordlist-rules workflow). **`coinomi2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): Coinomi wallet parser; uses protobuf + scrypt + AES output format. **`cracf2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): IBM RACF CRACF.TXT converter; line-by-line transform to `username:$racf$*…` format. **Blacklist additions (16) across 16 insertion blocks** — all verified position-by-position via single index/two-index character comparisons: 1 between `1password` (9) and `2fa` (10) for `1password2john` (end-of-string < `2`; `1` (49) < `2` (50)); 1 between `aecho` (640) and `aeolus` (641) for `aem2john` (within ae* at index 2 `c` (99) < `m` (109) < `o` (111)); 1 between `android` (987) and `androiddeployqt` (988) for `androidbackup2john` (within android* at index 7 end < `b` (98) < `d` (100)); 1 between `ansible-vault` (1033) and `ansifilter` (1034) for `ansible2john` (within ansible* at index 7 `-` (45) < `2` (50); at index 4 `b` (98, ansible) < `f` (102, ansifilter)); 1 between `apex` (1088) and `apfel` (1089) for `apex2john` (within ap* at index 2 `e` (101, ex) < `f` (102, fel); end-of-string < `2`); 1 between `apple_dump` (1132) and `appleseed` (1133) for `applenotes2john` (within apple* at index 5 `_` (95) < `n` (110) < `s` (115)); 1 between `arturo` (1389) and `as` (1390) for `aruba2john` (within ar* at index 2 `t` (116, turo) < `u` (117, uba); at index 1 `r` (114, ar) < `s` (115, as)); 1 between `atlas-cli` (1574) and `atmconfig` (1575) for `atmail2john` (within atm* at index 3 `a` (97, mail) < `c` (99, config); at index 2 `l` (108, atlas) < `m` (109, atmail)); - **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 2 — androidfde2john + axcrypt2john + dashlane2john + ecryptfs2john + filezilla2john + ikescan2john + lotus2john + pfx2john + sap2john + sipdump2john + vmx2john (11 more hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; extends the round-144 `_unshadow` family to a 26-stem JtR-helper corpus when combined with `_john`/`_zip2john`/`_rar2john`/`_wpapcap2john`)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Surfaces transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `run/androidfde2john.py` / `run/axcrypt2john.py` / `run/dashlane2john.py` / `run/ecryptfs2john.py` / `run/filezilla2john.py` / `run/ikescan2john.py` / `run/lotus2john.py` / `run/pfx2john.py` / `run/sap2john.pl` / `run/sipdump2john.py` / `run/vmx2john.py`. **Already-covered skip log** (caught by the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck before commit): `ethereum2john` (at `src/_ethereum2john`), `lastpass2john` (at `src/_lastpass2john`), `dmg2john` (at `src/_dmg2john`), `bitlocker2john` (at `src/_bitlocker2john`), `encfs2john` (at `src/_encfs2john`), `office2john` (at `src/_office2john`) — all 6 read from the source but found to be pre-existing single-stem files in `src/`, so excluded from the multi-stem to avoid `#compdef` collisions. Single multi-stem file `_androidfde2john` is `#compdef androidfde2john axcrypt2john dashlane2john ecryptfs2john filezilla2john ikescan2john lotus2john pfx2john sap2john sipdump2john vmx2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact `Usage:` string in upstream source — most are positional-file extractors with no flags, but several have non-trivial 2-positional or optional-tail patterns documented below): **`androidfde2john`** (0 flags, exactly 2 positionals): `androidfde2john <data partition / image> <footer partition / image>` — note the upstream source carries an explicit limitation comment that the extractor only handles old Android ≤4.3 disk images with AES-256/CBC-ESSIV-SHA-256 cipher; this completion does not enforce that, only documents it. **`axcrypt2john`** (0 flags, 1 required + 1 optional positional): `axcrypt2john <axxfile> [KEY-FILE]` — when a 2nd arg is provided the script reads it as a keyfile, converts contents to hex, and prepends an asterisk in the output hash to mark keyfile presence. **`dashlane2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `dashlane2john <.aes or .dash files from Dashlane for Windows Desktop / macOS>` — Windows lookup hint `%AppData%\Dashlane\profiles`; macOS hint `~/Library/Application Support/Dashlane/profiles/`. **`ecryptfs2john`** (0 flags, 1 required + 1 optional positional): `ecryptfs2john <wrapped-passphrase> [.ecryptfsrc]` — wrapped-passphrase typically lives at `~/.ecryptfs/wrapped-passphrase`; the optional `.ecryptfsrc` provides the salt value (typically at `~/.ecryptfsrc`). **`filezilla2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `filezilla2john.py <FileZilla Server.xml file(s)>` — script identifies two hash types (32-char raw MD5 and 128-char SHA-512+salt); unsupported formats trigger warnings to stderr but do not halt batch processing. **`ikescan2john`** (0 flags, 1 required + 1 optional positional): `ikescan2john <psk-parameters-file> [norteluser]` — with 1 arg the output format is `$ike$*0*<data>`; with 2 args the output format is `$ike$*1*<username>*<data>` (Nortel mode). **`lotus2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `lotus2john [Lotus Notes ID file(s)]` — extracts hashes from Lotus Notes `.id` files. **`pfx2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `pfx2john <.pfx file(s)>` — PKCS#12 hash extractor; depends on `asn1crypto`. **`sap2john`** (0 flags, 1 required + 1 optional positional): `sap2john.pl <input-file> [A|B|D|E|F|H]` or `sap2john.pl - [A|B|D|E|F|H]` — the optional 2nd positional is a subset selector for SAP CODVN hash families (default `BFH` when omitted; the script validates the selector pattern against `/^[^ABDEFH]$/`). **`sipdump2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `sipdump2john.py <sipdump files>` — converts SIPdump tool output to JtR hash format. **`vmx2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `vmx2john <.vmx files>` — parses VMware `.vmx` config files for the `encryption.keySafe` line, validates PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA-1 + AES-256 + HMAC-SHA-1, emits hashcat-compatible output. **Blacklist additions (11) across 11 insertion blocks**: 1 between `androiddump` (990) and `androidtestrunner` (991) for `androidfde2john` (within android* at index 7 `d` (100, dump) < `f` (102, fde) < `t` (116, testrunner)); 1 between `awx-cli` (1858) and `axe` (1859) for `axcrypt2john` (within ax* at index 2 `c` (99, crypt) < `e` (101, axe); at index 1 `w` (119, awx) < `x` (120, axc)); 1 between `dashing` (5628) and `dasht` (5629) for `dashlane2john` (within dash* at index 4 `i` (105, ing) < `l` (108, lane) < `t` (116, dasht)); 1 between `ecryptfs-verify` (7072) and `ecryptfsd` (7073) for `ecryptfs2john` (within ecryptfs* at index 8 `-` (45) < `2` (50) < `d` (100)); 1 between `filetop-bpfcc` (8036) and `filite` (8037) for `filezilla2john` (within file* at index 4 `t` (116, top) < `z` (122, zilla); at index 3 `e` (101, file) < `i` (105, filite) so all `fileX*` < `fili*`); 1 between `iked` (11547) and `ilasm` (11548) for `ikescan2john` (within ike* at index 3 `-` (45, scan), `d` (100, iked), then `s` (115, scan2john) — `d` < `s`; at index 1 `k` (107, ike) < `l` (108, ila) so `ike*` < `ila*`); 1 between `lottietoqml` (14809) and `lou_allround` (14810) for `lotus2john` (within lo* at index 2 `t` (116, lotus, lottie) — then at index 3 `t` (116, lottie) < `u` (117, lotus); at index 2 `t` (116) < `u` (117, lou)); 1 between `pftp` (19422) and `pg` (19423) for `pfx2john` (within pf* at index 2 `t` (116, pftp) < `x` (120, pfx); at index 1 `f` (102, pf) < `g` (103, pg)); 1 between `sany` (23008) and `sapling` (23009) for `sap2john` (within sa* at index 2 `n` (110, sany) < `p` (112, sap); within sap* at index 3 `2` (50, sap2john) < `l` (108, sapling)); 1 between `sipdump` (23762) and `sipp` (23763) for `sipdump2john` (within sip* at index 3 `d` (100, sipdump) < `p` (112, sipp); within sipdump* at index 7 end-of-string < `2` (50, sipdump2john)); 1 between `vmwgfxctrl` (27624) and `vnc4server` (27625) for `vmx2john` (within vm* at index 2 `w` (119, vmwgfxctrl) < `x` (120, vmx); at index 1 `m` (109, vm) < `n` (110, vn)). - **John the Ripper `*2john` family Round 1 — unshadow + ssh2john + keepass2john + 7z2john + bitcoin2john + pdf2john + keychain2john + keystore2john + bitwarden2john + htdigest2john + monero2john (11 hash-extractor stems via single multi-stem file; extends the existing `_john` + `_zip2john` + `_rar2john` + `_wpapcap2john` coverage to a 15-stem JtR-helper corpus)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; the pre-existing `_john` is also dup'd by stock zsh at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/site-functions/_john` but the 11 stems in this round are all stock-clean). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream JtR sources at [github.com/openwall/john (bleeding-jumbo)](https://github.com/openwall/john) — `src/*.c` for compiled helpers (`unshadow.c`/`keepass2john.c`) and `run/*.py`/`run/*.pl` for script helpers (`ssh2john.py`/`7z2john.pl`/`bitcoin2john.py`/`pdf2john.pl`/`keychain2john.py`/`keystore2john.py`/`bitwarden2john.py`/`htdigest2john.py`/`monero2john.py`). **The Debian-shipped manpages.debian.org pages all 302-redirect to a `dyn.manpages.debian.org` host that 404s the same content**, so this round abandoned man-page-based verification in favor of direct source-tree reading — the JtR sources display canonical usage strings (`fprintf(stderr, "Usage: %s …")`) that yield reliable surfaces. **Conflict-avoidance during writing**: initially the multi-stem `#compdef` line included `rar2john` and `wpapcap2john`, but the `^#compdef( |.* )<name>( |$)` recheck caught that both already have dedicated single-stem files at `src/_rar2john` and `src/_wpapcap2john`; both were removed from the new file before commit (would have produced double-#compdef registrations and broken at compinit time). **Hunt-dup-skip log this round**: ~25 candidates from a broader hunt of `*2john` helpers in `openwall/john/run/` — `ethereum2john`/`lastpass2john`/`pcap2john`/`gpg2john`/`dmg2john`/`bitlocker2john`/`androidfde2john`/`axcrypt2john`/`dashlane2john`/`ecryptfs2john`/`encfs2john`/`filezilla2john`/`ikescan2john`/`lotus2john`/`mongodb2john`/`office2john`/`pfx2john`/`pgpdisk2john`/`pwsafe2john`/`racf2john`/`sap2john`/`sipdump2john`/`sshng2john`/`uaf2john`/`vmx2john`/`zed2john` — these are deferred not because they don't exist (the GitHub `run/` directory listing confirms many do) but because their source files were not individually read this round to verify surface; per the no-vibe-coding rule the harvest claims only what's verified, and a future round can extend the multi-stem after reading each `.py`/`.pl`/`.c` source. Single multi-stem file `_unshadow` is `#compdef unshadow ssh2john keepass2john 7z2john bitcoin2john pdf2john keychain2john keystore2john bitwarden2john htdigest2john monero2john`, dispatching on `$service`. **Per-stem coverage** (each transcribed from the exact `Usage:` string in upstream source): **`unshadow`** (0 flags, exactly 2 positionals): `unshadow PASSWORD-FILE SHADOW-FILE` — the JtR helper that merges a Unix `/etc/passwd` and `/etc/shadow` into the JtR-cracking-friendly combined format. **`ssh2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `ssh2john <RSA/DSA/EC/OpenSSH private key file(s)>` — extracts crackable hashes from SSH private keys; the `.py` script iterates `sys.argv[1:]` with no option parsing. **`keepass2john`** (1 flag, 1+ positionals): `keepass2john [-k <keyfile>] <.kdbx database(s)>` — `-k` registers an optional KeePass keyfile for two-factor crack inputs. **`7z2john`** (1 flag, 1+ positionals): `7z2john.pl [--skip-sensitive-data-warning] <7-Zip file>...` — the single flag suppresses the default "hashes may contain sensitive encrypted data" warning printed to stderr. **`bitcoin2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `bitcoin2john [Bitcoin/Litecoin/PRiVCY wallet (.dat) files]` — the same `.py` extracts wallet hashes from Bitcoin, Litecoin, and PRiVCY-format `.dat` files. **`pdf2john`** (treated as 0 flags + 1+ positionals): the upstream `pdf2john.pl` is wired through ExifTool which exposes ExifTool's full flag surface, but the canonical contract documented in the synopsis is `pdf2john.pl <.pdf file(s)>` — keeping the completion to the documented contract avoids advertising ExifTool's incidental flags as if they were `pdf2john`'s own. **`keychain2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `keychain2john [keychain files]` — extracts hashes from macOS `*.keychain*` files. **`keystore2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `keystore2john.py <.keystore / .jks file(s)>` — extracts hashes from Java KeyStore files. **`bitwarden2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positional files-or-dirs): `bitwarden_extract.py <Bitwarden storage.js / com.x8bit.bitwarden_preferences.xml / Google Chrome's 'nngceckbap…' path>` — accepts any of three input shapes (Firefox extension storage, Android preferences XML, or a Chrome LevelDB directory path). **`htdigest2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `htdigest2john.py <htdigest file(s)>` — Apache htdigest hash extractor. **`monero2john`** (0 flags, 1+ positionals): `monero2john.py [Monero .keys file(s)]` — extracts hashes from Monero wallet `.keys` files. **Blacklist additions (11) across 11 insertion blocks**: 1 between `7z` (36) and `7za` (37) for `7z2john` (within 7z* at index 2 digit `2` (50) < lowercase `a` (97)); 1 between `bitcoin-wallet` (2224) and `bitcoind` (2225) for `bitcoin2john` (within bitcoin* at index 7 `-` (45) < `2` (50) < `d` (100)); 1 between `bitwarden-cli` (2234) and `bitwise` (2235) for `bitwarden2john` (within bitwarden* at index 9 `-` (45) < `2` (50); at index 4 within bitw* `a` (97, warden) < `i` (105, wise)); 1 between `htdigest2` (11078) and `htlatex` (11079) for `htdigest2john` (within htdigest2* at index 9 end-of-string < `j` (106); at index 2 within ht* `d` (100, digest) < `l` (108, latex)); 1 between `keepass2` (12751) and `keepassxc` (12752) for `keepass2john` (within keepass* at index 7 `2` (50) but within keepass2* at index 8 end < `j` (106); at index 7 within keepass* `2` (50) < `x` (120)); 1 between `keychain` (12789) and `keycloak` (12790) for `keychain2john` (within keychain* at index 8 end < `2` (50); at index 4 within keyc* `h` (104, chain) < `l` (108, cloak)); 1 between `keystone-wsgi-public` (12811) and `keytool` (12812) for `keystore2john` (within keysto* at index 6 `n` (110, stone) < `r` (114, store); at index 3 within key* `s` (115, store) < `t` (116, tool)); 1 between `monero-wallet-rpc` (16375) and `monerod` (16376) for `monero2john` (within monero* at index 6 `-` (45) < `2` (50) < `d` (100)); 1 between `pdf2htmlex` (19166) and `pdf2ps` (19167) for `pdf2john` (within pdf2* at index 4 `h` (104, htmlex) < `j` (106, john) < `p` (112, ps)); 1 between `ssh-vault` (24493) and `ssh3` (24494) for `ssh2john` (within ssh* at index 3 `-` (45) < `2` (50) < `3` (51)); 1 between `unsetpassword` (26893) and `unshar` (26894) for `unshadow` (within uns* at index 3 `e` (101, set) < `h` (104, shadow/shar); at index 5 within unsha* `d` (100, shadow) < `r` (114, shar)). - **BIND 9 small utils + Zeek package manager — arpaname + named-nzd2nzf + nsec3hash + zkg (4 stems across 2 files; pivots the network-tooling streak from the SiLK/YAF/Argus flow-record axis to the DNS-server + Zeek-package-manager axis)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; existing BIND coverage `_dnssec-keygen`/`_dnssec-signzone`/`_dnssec-verify`/`_dnssec-revoke`/`_dnssec-importkey`/`_dnssec-cds`/`_rndc`/`_rndc-confgen`/`_named-checkconf`/`_named-checkzone` (also covers `named-compilezone`)/`_named-rrchecker`/`_named-journalprint`/`_ddns-confgen`/`_tsig-keygen`/`_genrandom`/`_dig`/`_delv`/`_dnstap-read`/`_mdig`/`_nsupdate` — none overlap with this round's 3 BIND additions; existing Zeek coverage `_bro` (covers both `bro` and `zeek` stems)/`_zeekctl`/`_zeek-cut`/`_zeek-archiver`/`_zeek-client`/`_zeek-config` does not include `zkg`). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream ISC docs at [bind9.readthedocs.io/en/latest/manpages.html](https://bind9.readthedocs.io/en/latest/manpages.html) + the canonical .rst sources at [gitlab.isc.org/isc-projects/bind9/-/raw/main/bin/tools/named-nzd2nzf.rst](https://gitlab.isc.org/isc-projects/bind9/-/raw/main/bin/tools/named-nzd2nzf.rst) + `bin/tools/nsec3hash.rst`, plus the Zeek package manager docs at [docs.zeek.org/projects/package-manager/en/stable/zkg.html](https://docs.zeek.org/projects/package-manager/en/stable/zkg.html). **Deferred per the source-404 precedent**: `isc-hmac-fixup` — the tool was removed from BIND ≥ 9.16; the v9_16 source-tree URL also 404s, so there is no canonical reference to transcribe; defer indefinitely unless a Debian-archive copy resurfaces. **Hunt-dup-skip log this round**: `broctl` + `bro-cut` (legacy Zeek aliases — broctl is renamed `zeekctl`, bro-cut is renamed `zeek-cut`; both shipped as compatibility shims pointing to the modern names already covered by `_zeekctl` + `_zeek-cut`; not worth a snowflake completion); `nfreader` (developer test-harness for libnfdump, no public man page); `ipset-save`/`ipset-restore` (these are subcommand forms of `ipset save`/`ipset restore` rather than separate binaries — and the base `ipset` ships with stock zsh anyway at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_ipset`). **Per-stem coverage**: **File 1 — `_arpaname` multi-stem `#compdef arpaname named-nzd2nzf nsec3hash` (3 BIND small utilities)**. **`arpaname`** (0 flags, 1 repeatable positional): variadic IPv4/IPv6 addresses to convert into `IN-ADDR.ARPA` / `IP6.ARPA` names. **`named-nzd2nzf`** (0 flags, 1 required positional): single `.nzd` database filename to dump as NZF text on stdout. **`nsec3hash`** (1 flag, ≥4 positional args): `-r` toggles between two invocation forms — standard `nsec3hash SALT ALGORITHM ITERATIONS DOMAIN` vs record-format `nsec3hash -r ALGORITHM FLAGS ITERATIONS SALT DOMAIN` (where the `-r` form mirrors NSEC3 record-presentation field order so users can paste record data directly). The `ALGORITHM` value is currently always `1` (SHA-1, the only NSEC3-defined algorithm). **File 2 — `_zkg` single-stem state-machine completion for the Zeek package manager**. Top level: **6 global flags** (`--version`/`--configfile=FILE`/`--user`/repeatable `*-v`/`*--verbose`/repeatable `*--extra-source NAME=URL`) + **20-subcommand `_describe` menu**. Per-subcommand coverage (only flags with explicit per-subcommand semantics are listed; `pin`/`unpin`/`config`/`env`/`search` have only positional package args): **`test`** (`--version`); **`install`** (`--skiptests`/`--nodeps`/`--nosuggestions`/`--version`/`--force`/repeatable `*--user-var NAME=VAL`); **`remove`/`uninstall`** (`--force`/`--nodeps`); **`purge`** (`--force`); **`bundle`** (`--force`/`--nodeps`/`--nosuggestions`/repeatable `*--manifest FILE`, positional bundle output file + packages); **`unbundle`** (`--replace`/`--force`/repeatable `*--user-var`, positional bundle file); **`refresh`** (`--aggregate`/`--fail-on-aggregate-problems`/`--push`/repeatable `*--sources`); **`upgrade`** (same 6 install-style flags); **`load`** (`--nodeps`); **`unload`** (`--force`/`--nodeps`); **`list`** (`--nodesc`/`--include-builtin`, positional category enum `installed|not_installed|loaded|unloaded|outdated|all`); **`info`** (`--version`/`--nolocal`/`--include-builtin`/`--json`/`--jsonpretty SPACES`/`--allvers`); **`autoconfig`** (`--force`); **`create`** (`--packagedir DIR` required/`--version`/repeatable `*--features`/`--template URL`/`--force`/repeatable `*--user-var`); **`template`** has its own nested state machine with a single sub-subcommand `template info` (`--json`/`--jsonpretty SPACES`/`--version`). **Blacklist additions (4) across 4 insertion blocks**: 1 between `arpalert` (1363) and `arpd` (1364) for `arpaname` (within arpa* at index 4 `l` (108, alert) < `n` (110, name); within arp* at index 3 `a` (97) < `d` (100, arpd) so `arpaname` < `arpd`); 1 between `named-journalprint` (17038) and `named-rrchecker` (17039) for `named-nzd2nzf` (within named-* at index 6 `j` (106, journal) < `n` (110, nzd) < `r` (114, rrchecker)); 1 between `nsd-zonec` (17709) and `nsenter` (17710) for `nsec3hash` (within ns* at index 2 `d` (100) < `e` (101) for nsd-* < nse*; within nse* at index 3 `c` (99, nsec3hash) < `n` (110, nsenter)); 1 between `zkTxnLogToolkit` (29563) and `zkserver` (29564) for `zkg` (within zk* at index 2 `T` (84 uppercase) < `g` (103 lowercase) < `s` (115 lowercase) — the uppercase block ends, zkg lands at the start of the lowercase block, and `g` (103) < `s` (115) so `zkg` precedes `zkserver`). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **Argus ra* Round 2 — racluster + radump + rahisto + ranonymize + rapath + rastream + rastrip + rasqlinsert (8 QoSient argus-client stems via single multi-stem file, extends the existing `_ra` multi-stem + `_rabins` to a 16-stem argus-client coverage)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep; existing coverage `_ra` covers `ra`/`ratop`/`ralabel`/`rasort`/`racount`/`rasplit` and `_rabins` covers `rabins`/`rabins2`, none of which overlap). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream Debian-shipped manpages at [manpages.debian.org/testing/argus-client/racluster.1.en.html](https://manpages.debian.org/testing/argus-client/racluster.1.en.html) + `radump.1`/`rahisto.1`/`ranonymize.1`/`rapath.1`/`rastream.1`/`rastrip.1`/`rasqlinsert.1`. **Deferred per the source-404 precedent**: `radium`, `ragator`, `raservices`, `ratimerange` (manpages.debian.org dynamic-redirect URLs all return 404 — the static path 302-redirects to a `dyn.manpages.debian.org` host that 404s the same content; will revisit when upstream restores). Single multi-stem file `_racluster` is `#compdef racluster radump rahisto ranonymize rapath rastream rastrip rasqlinsert`, dispatching on `$service`. Shared `_ra_common` (26 flags, mirrors the canonical inventory in `more_src/_ra` but kept local-scoped so the two files don't collide when both load) carries the base ra(1) surface every client inherits: `-h`/`-A`/`-b`, `-c` column delimiter, `-D 1-8` debug level, `-d` daemon toggle, `-e` regex match, `-E` rejected-record file, `-F .conf` config, `-H` human-readable, `-L` header frequency, `-n`/`-N`/`-p`/`-q`, **repeatable** `*-r FILE`, `-R DIR` recursive, **repeatable** `*-s FIELD`, `-S URI` remote source, `-t` time range, `-T` remote seconds, `-u` Unix time, `-w FILE` append, `-X` reset, `-z`/`-Z s|d|b` TCP state. Three shared field/object/dsr arrays carry per-tool enums: **`_agg` (28 values)** — aggregation objects used by `racluster -m` and `rasqlinsert -m` (`none`/`all`/`srcid`/`smac`/`dmac`/`soui`/`doui`/`smpls`/`dmpls`/`svlan`/`dvlan`/`saddr`/`daddr`/`matrix`/`proto`/`sport`/`dport`/`stos`/`dtos`/`sttl`/`dttl`/`stcpb`/`dtcpb`/`inode`/`sco`/`dco`/`sas`/`das`/`ias`); **`_hist_metrics` (24 values)** — `rahisto -H` metrics (`dur`/`avgdur`/`proto`/`sport`/`dport`/`stos`/`dtos`/`sttl`/`dttl`/`sbytes`/`dbytes`/`sappbytes`/`dappbytes`/`spkts`/`dpkts`/`smeansz`/`dmeansz`/`sload`/`dload`/`sloss`/`dloss`/`sploss`/`dploss`/`srate`/`drate`); **`_strip_dsr` (13 values)** — `rastrip -M` Data Source Record names (`flow`/`time`/`metric`/`agr`/`net`/`vlan`/`mpls`/`jitter`/`ipattr`/`suser`/`duser`/`mac`/`icmp`/`encaps`). **Per-stem coverage**: **`racluster`** (5 flags beyond shared): `-f racluster.conf`, repeatable `*-m OBJECT` (from `_agg`), repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 6-value enum (`correct`/`nocorrect`/`norep`/`rmon`/`ind`/`replace`), `-P N` (parallel processors, default 1), `-V` verbose; trailing filter expression. **`radump`** (shared only): tcpdump-style payload printer — inherits the full ra surface, no tool-specific flags in the man page; trailing filter expression. **`rahisto`** (3 flags beyond shared): repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 3-value enum (`nozero`/`outlayer`/`perflow`), repeatable **`*-H SPEC`** taking `[abs] metric bin[L]:range|size` (where `metric` completes from `_hist_metrics`), repeatable `*-m OBJECT` (from `_agg`); trailing filter expression. **`ranonymize`** (1 flag beyond shared): `-f anonymization.conf`; trailing filter expression. **`rapath`** (5 flags beyond shared): `-A` (draw path description with legend), repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 4-value enum (`node`/`addr`/`aspath`/`asnode`), repeatable `*-m FIELD` 3-value enum (`srcid`/`saddr`/`daddr`), `-q` (suppress per-node record output), `-f ralabel.conf` (ASN labeling); trailing filter expression. **`rastream`** (4 flags beyond shared): `-a N` (append suffix length, default 2), `-B N` (buffer hold time before processing in sec), `-f program` (post-processing program), repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 6-value enum (`count`/`size`/`time`/`flow`/`lock`/`lock=nonblock`); trailing filter expression. **`rastrip`** (1 flag beyond shared): repeatable **`*-M DSR`** with `_strip_dsr` 13-value enum plus `replace` (prefix with `+` to add or `-` to remove, per upstream); trailing filter expression. **`rasqlinsert`** (3 flags beyond shared): repeatable **`*-M MODE`** 4-value enum (`cache`/`drop`/`rewrite`/`time`), repeatable `*-m FIELDS` (DB schema flow keys — `none` or values from `_agg`), `-S` (read from argus data stream); trailing filter expression. **Blacklist additions (8) across 6 insertion blocks**: 1 between `rackup` (21548) and `raco` (21549) for `racluster` (within rac* at index 3 `k` (107) < `l` (108) < `o` (111)); 1 between `radtest` (21574) and `radvd` (21575) for `radump` (within rad* at index 3 `t` (116) < `u` (117) < `v` (118)); 1 between `rahash2` (21589) and `raid-check` (21590) for `rahisto` (within rah* at index 3 `a` (97) < `i` (105), and at index 2 `h` (104) < `i` (105) so `rahisto` < `raid-check`); 2 between `ranlib` (21632) and `rapper` (21633) for `ranonymize` + `rapath` (within ran* at index 3 `l` (108) < `o` (111) so `ranlib` < `ranonymize`; at index 2 `n` (110) < `p` (112) so `ranonymize` < `rapath` < `rapper`); 1 between `rasqal-config` (21657) and `raster2pgsql` (21658) for `rasqlinsert` (within rasq* at index 4 `a` (97) < `l` (108)); 2 between `rasterizer` (21660) and `rasttopnm` (21661) for `rastream` + `rastrip` (within rast* at index 4 `e` (101) < `r` (114) < `t` (116), and within rastr* at index 5 `e` (101) < `i` (105) so `rastream` < `rastrip`). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **YAF + super_mediator Round 1 — yaf + yafscii + getFlowKeyHash + yafMeta2Pcap + super_mediator (5 stems via single multi-stem file, pivot from SiLK to the adjacent CERT NetSA flow-meter family)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream CERT NetSA per-tool docs at [tools.netsa.cert.org/yaf2/yaf.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/yaf2/yaf.html) + `yafscii.html`/`getFlowKeyHash.html`/`yafMeta2Pcap.html` plus [tools.netsa.cert.org/super_mediator/super_mediator.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/super_mediator/super_mediator.html). **Deferred per the source-404 precedent**: `filterv6` and `yafzcballast` (both upstream 404). Single multi-stem file `_yaf` is `#compdef yaf yafscii getFlowKeyHash yafMeta2Pcap super_mediator`, dispatching on `$service`. Shared `_log` (4 flags) carries the canonical NetSA log cluster (`--log` dest spec / `--loglevel quiet|error|critical|warning|message|info|debug` / `--verbose` / `--version`) — applied to the 2 tools whose docs explicitly list it; the other 3 get explicit help/version flags matching upstream. **Per-stem coverage**: **`yaf`** (the IPFIX flow meter — 70+ flags, the largest single-tool surface of the round): Lua `--config`, input cluster (`--in`, **`--live` 6-value enum** `pcap|pfring|dag|napatech|netronome|zc`, `--caplist`, `--noerror`, `--filter BPF`, `--filter-file`, `--decompress`, `--export-interface`), output cluster (`--out`, **`--ipfix` 4-value enum** `tcp|udp|sctp|spread`, `--ipfix-port` default 4739, `--rotate`, `--lock`, `--no-output`, `--stats` default 300s, `--no-stats`, `--no-tombstone`, `--tombstone-configured-id`), decoder cluster (`--no-frag`, `--max-frags`, **2-flag mutex** `--ip4-only` ↔ `--ip6-only`, `--gre-decode`, `--vxlan-decode` + `--vxlan-decode-ports`, `--geneve-decode` + `--geneve-decode-ports`, `--force-read-all`, `--no-vlan-in-key`), flow-table cluster (`--idle-timeout`/`--active-timeout`/`--udp-temp-timeout`/`--max-flows`/`--max-payload`/`--protocol-payload`/`--udp-payload`/`--silk`/`--udp-uniflow`), export cluster (`--export-payload`, `--payload-applabel-select`, `--max-export`, `--uniflow`, `--mac`, `--force-ip6-export`, `--observation-domain`, `--flow-stats`, `--delta`, `--ingress`, `--egress`, `--metadata-export`, `--time-elements` ms/μs/ns), app-label cluster (`--applabel` + `--applabel-rules` + `--applabel-check-early` + `--applabel-max-payload`), nDPI (`--ndpi` + `--ndpi-protocol-file`), `--entropy`, TLS cluster (`--tls`, `--tls-ca`, `--tls-cert`, `--tls-key`), Spread (repeatable `*--group`, **`--groupby` 5-value enum** `port|vlan|applabel|protocol|version`), privilege drop (`--become-user`/`--become-group`), PCAP-export cluster (`--pcap`, `--pcap-per-flow`, `--max-pcap` default 25MB, `--pcap-timer`, `--pcap-meta-file`, `--index-pcap`, `--hash`, `--stime`), plugin cluster (repeatable `*--plugin-name` + `--plugin-opts` + repeatable `*--plugin-conf`), p0f passive-OS-fingerprinting cluster (`--p0fprint` + `--p0f-fingerprints` + `--fpexport`). **`yafscii`** (IPFIX → text printer, 11 flags beyond shared `_log`): `--in`, `--out`, `--tabular`, `--mac` (with `--tabular`), `--print-header` (with `--tabular`), **2-flag mutex** `--daemon` ↔ `--foreground`, `--lock`, `--poll` default 60s, `--nextdir` (or `delete`), `--faildir` (or `delete`). **`getFlowKeyHash`** (compute flow-key hash, 14 flags + 1 help/version): `--in`/`--out`, IP+port+protocol+vlan inputs (`--sip4`/`--dip4` dotted IPv4, `--sip6`/`--dip6` IPv6, `--sport`/`--dport` 0-65535, `--protocol` 6=TCP/17=UDP, `--vlan`), `--ipfix` output mode, time controls (`--date YYYY-MM-DD` UTC, `--time HH:MM:SS[.sss]`), `--reverse` (swap IPs+ports), `--snmp` (ignore SNMP iface). **`yafMeta2Pcap`** (extract per-flow PCAP from yaf metadata, 12 flags): `--pcap-meta-file` (glob pattern), `--pcap` (glob pattern), `--caplist FILE` / `--metalist FILE`, `--out`, `--hash` (flow key hash), `--time`/`--etime` ms-since-epoch, **`-w+`/`--window` ms** (short + long form), `--packets` (total packet count for bi-flow), `--yaf-program` (path to yaf executable), `--verbose`. **`super_mediator`** (IPFIX collector/aggregator that ingests yaf output, 27 flags): `--config`, `--test-config` (parse + exit), input cluster (**`--ipfix-input` 2-value enum** `tcp|udp`, `--ipfix-port` default 18000, `--polling-interval`, `--move-dir`, `--no-locked-files`), output cluster (`--out`, **`--output-mode` 4-value enum** `tcp|udp|text|json`, `--export-port` default 18001, `--rotate`, `--fields`, `--print-headers`, `--no-stats`, `--preserve-obdomain`, `--rewrite-ssl-certs`, `--disable-metadata-export`), `--ipsetfile`, `--sleep` μs, `--udp-temp-timeout`, log cluster (`--log` dest, `--log-dir`, `--verbose`, `--quiet`), daemon cluster (`--daemonize`, `--pidfile`, `--become-user`/`--become-group`). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 1 inserted between `get-repo` (9052) and `getS4U2proxy` (9053) for `getFlowKeyHash` (capital `F` at index 3, 70 < uppercase `S` at 83); 1 between `supabase-mcp-server` (24824) and `superagent` (24825) for `super_mediator` (the `_` at index 5, 95 < `a` at 97 of `superagent`); 2 between `yaegi` (29126) and `yafc` (29127) for `yaf` + `yafMeta2Pcap` (empty < `M` < `c`); 1 between `yafc` (29127) and `yakuake` (29128) for `yafscii` (within `yaf*` at index 3 `c` (99) < `s` (115); at index 2 `f` (102) < `k` (107) so `yafscii` < `yakuake`). Sort verified position-by-position above. LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **SiLK Round 6 — rwappend + rwcombine + rwdedupe + rwmatch + rwnetmask + rwrandomizeip + rwgeoip2ccmap + rwpackchecker (8 single-purpose utility stems via single multi-stem file, extends cumulative SiLK coverage to 43)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream CERT NetSA per-tool docs at [tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwappend.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwappend.html) + `rwcombine.html`/`rwdedupe.html`/`rwmatch.html`/`rwnetmask.html`/`rwrandomizeip.html`/`rwgeoip2ccmap.html`/`rwpackchecker.html`. **Deferred per the source-404 precedent**: `rwconvert` and `rwallroute` — both pages return HTTP 404 upstream; will revisit when archives expose them. Single multi-stem file `_rwappend` is `#compdef rwappend rwcombine rwdedupe rwmatch rwnetmask rwrandomizeip rwgeoip2ccmap rwpackchecker`, dispatching on `$service`. Shared `_meta` (6 flags) carries the standard CERT NetSA header-annotation + compression + help/version cluster — applied only where the per-tool doc explicitly lists those flags, since `rwgeoip2ccmap` and `rwpackchecker` have stripped-down surfaces. **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwappend`** (3 flags beyond shared): `--create=-` (optional TEMPLATE_FILE; format/version/byte-order is inherited from template if given), `--print-statistics` (stderr report of per-source record counts + total appended), positional 1 = target SiLK file + repeatable positional sources. **`rwcombine`** (11 flags beyond shared): `--actions all|timeout` (combination strategy), **`--ignore-fields`** 12-value `_values -s ,` enum (`sIP`/`dIP`/`sPort`/`dPort`/`protocol`/`sensor`/`in`/`out`/`nhIP`/`class`/`type`/`application`), `--max-idle-time N` (seconds), `--print-statistics=-` (optional FILE), `--temp-directory`, `--buffer-size` (K/M/G suffixes, default ~2GB), `--print-filenames`, `--output-path`, repeatable `*--xargs`, `--help-fields`. **`rwdedupe`** (12 flags beyond shared): **`--ignore-fields`** 20-value `_values -s ,` enum (the dedupe surface extends combine's 12 with `packets`/`bytes`/`flags`/`sTime`/`duration`/`initialFlags`/`sessionFlags`/`attributes`), per-field delta tolerances (`--packets-delta N`/`--bytes-delta N`/`--stime-delta FLOAT_ms`/`--duration-delta FLOAT_ms`), `--temp-directory`, `--buffer-size`, `--print-filenames`, `--output-path`, repeatable `*--xargs`, `--help-fields`. **`rwmatch`** (10 flags beyond shared, 3 positionals): **repeatable `*--relate FIELD_PAIR`** (required ≥1), `--time-delta` (seconds), **3-flag time-mode mutex** `--absolute-delta` (default) ↔ `--relative-delta` ↔ `--infinite-delta`, `--symmetric-delta` (allow initial flow in response), `--unmatched q|r|b` (3-value enum), `--ipv6-policy ignore|asv4|mix|force|only`, `--help-relate`, positional 1 = query / 2 = response / 3 = output SiLK file. **`rwnetmask`** (13 flags beyond shared): per-axis IPv4/IPv6 prefix-length controls — `--4sip-prefix-length`/`--4dip-prefix-length`/`--4nhip-prefix-length` + their IPv6 counterparts `--6sip-`/`--6dip-`/`--6nhip-prefix-length` + 3 deprecated aliases (`--sip-prefix-length` = `--4sip-prefix-length`, etc), `--output-path`, `--print-filenames`, `--ipv6-policy`, repeatable `*--xargs`. **`rwrandomizeip`** (7 flags beyond shared, 2 positionals): `--seed N`, IPset filtering (`--only-change-set IPSET` ↔ `--dont-change-set IPSET`), `--consistent` (deterministic input → output IP mapping), state persistence (`--save-table FILE` / `--load-table FILE`), positional 1 = input / 2 = output. **`rwgeoip2ccmap`** (10 flags + 5 deprecated, **no shared `_meta`** — distinct annotation/help cluster matching upstream): **`--mode`** 7-value enum `auto|ipv4|ipv6|csv|binary|geoip2|legacy`, `--input-path`, `--output-path` (binary CC prefix-map), `--dry-run`, **`--fields`** 5-value `_values -s ,` enum (`location`/`registered`/`represented`/`flags`/`continent` — GeoName field priority), `--note-add`, `--note-file-add`, `--invocation-strip`, plus 5 deprecated legacy flags (`--csv-input`/`--v6-csv-input`/`--encoded-input`/`--input-file`/`--output-file`). **`rwpackchecker`** (5 flags + help/version — **stripped-down surface, no shared `_meta`**): repeatable `*--value TEST=VALUE`, repeatable `*--allowable-count TEST=ALLOWED`, `--print-all`, repeatable `*--xargs`, positional input SiLK files. **Blacklist additions (8)**: 1 inserted between `rwall` (22761) and `rwbagbuild` (22762) for `rwappend`; 1 between `rwcat` (22767) and `rwcount` (22768) for `rwcombine`; 1 between `rwcount` (22768) and `rwfileinfo` (22769) for `rwdedupe`; 1 between `rwflowpack` (22772) and `rwgroup` (22773) for `rwgeoip2ccmap`; 3 between `rwipfix2silk` (22776) and `rwpmapbuild` (22777) for `rwmatch` + `rwnetmask` + `rwpackchecker`; 1 between `rwpollexec` (22780) and `rwreceiver` (22781) for `rwrandomizeip`. - **SiLK Round 5 — rwflowpack + rwflowappend + rwpollexec + rwsender + rwreceiver (5 daemon binary stems via single multi-stem file, extends cumulative SiLK coverage to 35)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream CERT NetSA per-tool docs at [tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwflowpack.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwflowpack.html) + `rwflowappend.html`/`rwpollexec.html`/`rwsender.html`/`rwreceiver.html`. Single multi-stem file `_rwflowpack` is `#compdef rwflowpack rwflowappend rwpollexec rwsender rwreceiver`, dispatching on `$service` — covers the SiLK daemon pipeline (probe-side packing → hourly-file appending → directory-scan exec → encrypted file transfer). Three shared sub-arrays collapse the repetitive surfaces: **`_log` (7 flags)** carries the canonical 3-flag log-destination mutex (`--log-destination none|stdout|stderr|syslog|both` ↔ `--log-pathname FILE` ↔ `--log-directory DIR`) plus rotation/severity config (`--log-basename`, `--log-level emerg|alert|crit|err|warning|notice|info|debug`, `--log-sysfacility`, `--log-post-rotate`); **`_daemon` (5 flags)** carries `--pidfile`, `--no-chdir`, `--no-daemon`, `--help`, `--version`; **`_tls` (8 flags)** carries the GnuTLS cluster (`--tls-ca`/`--tls-cert`/`--tls-key`/`--tls-pkcs12`/`--tls-priority`/`--tls-security low|medium|high|ultra`/`--tls-crl`/`--tls-debug-level`). **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwflowpack`** (24 flags beyond shared): **`--input-mode` 4-value enum** `stream|fcfiles|pdufile|respool` driving four conditional flag groups, **`--output-mode` 3-value enum** `local-storage|incremental-files|sending`, `--sensor-configuration`, `--packing-logic`, `--sensor-name`, `--polling-interval`, `--incoming-directory`, `--netflow-file` (pdufile mode), `--root-directory`, `--incremental-directory`, `--sender-directory`, `--no-file-locking`, `--flush-timeout` (default 120s), `--file-cache-size` (default 128 open files), `--pack-interfaces`, `--byte-order native|big|little`, `--compression-method none|zlib|lzo1x|snappy|best`, `--site-config-file`, `--error-directory`, `--archive-directory`, `--flat-archive`, `--post-archive-command`, `--verify-sensor-config`. **`rwflowappend`** (15 flags beyond shared): `--incoming-directory` + `--root-directory` + `--error-directory` (all required), `--archive-directory`, `--flat-archive`, `--post-command`, `--hour-file-command`, `--threads` (default 1), `--reject-hours-past`/`--reject-hours-future`, `--no-file-locking`, `--polling-interval` (default 15s), **`--byte-order` 4-value enum** `as-is|native|big|little` (the `as-is` extra value distinguishes it from rwflowpack's 3-value enum), `--compression-method`, `--site-config-file`. **`rwpollexec`** (8 flags beyond shared): `--incoming-directory` + `--command` (with `%s` file substitution) + `--error-directory` (all required), `--archive-directory`, `--flat-archive`, `--simultaneous` (1-50, default 1), **repeatable `*--timeout=SIGNAL,SECS`** (send signal after timeout), `--polling-interval`. **`rwsender`** (17 flags beyond shared+tls, 7 of them repeatable): `--identifier`, **2-flag mutex** `--mode server|client`, `--incoming-directory`, `--processing-directory`, `--error-directory`, `--server-port` (server mode), repeatable `*--client-ident` (server mode), repeatable `*--server-address IDENT:HOST:PORT` (client mode), repeatable `*--local-directory [IDENT:]DIR` (duplicate copies), `--unique-local-copies`, repeatable `*--filter IDENT:REGEXP` (routing), repeatable `*--priority NUM:REGEXP` (0-100, default 50), `--polling-interval`, `--send-attempts` (0=unlimited), `--block-size` (256-65535, default 8192). **`rwreceiver`** (12 flags beyond shared+tls): `--identifier`, `--mode server|client`, `--destination-directory`, server flags (`--server-port` + repeatable `*--client-ident`), client flag (repeatable `*--server-address`), `--post-command` (supports `%s` for path, `%I` for sender ID, `%%` for literal %), repeatable `*--duplicate-destination`, `--unique-duplicates`, `--freespace-minimum` (supports K/M/G/T suffixes), `--space-maximum-percent` (default 100). **Blacklist additions (5)**: 2 inserted between `rwfilter` (22770) and `rwgroup` (22771) for the `rwflow*` block (`rwflowappend` → `rwflowpack`); 3 between `rwpmaplookup` (22777) and `rwsetbuild` (22778) for the `rwpollexec`+`rwreceiver`+`rwsender` block. none|zlib|lzo1x|snappy|best`/`--site-config-file`/`--help`/`--version`). **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwaggbag`** (build aggregate bag from flow records): `--keys` (key field list) + `--counters` (counter field list) — both required, `--output-path`, `--copy-input`, `--note-strip`, repeatable `*--xargs`, `--print-filenames`, `--ipv6-policy ignore|asv4|mix|force|only`, `--help-fields`. `*--missing-field FIELD=STRING` (placeholder for missing-field rows), `--output-path`, `--pager`, `--timestamp-format`, **`--ip-format`** 8-value enum (`canonical`/`no-mixed`/`decimal`/`hexadecimal`/`zero-padded`/`map-v4`/`unmap-v6`/`force-ipv6`), `--no-titles`/`--no-columns`/`--column-separator`/`--no-final-delimiter`/`--delimited`, mutex** `--remove-fields` ↔ `--select-fields`, repeatable `*--insert-field FIELD=VALUE`, **3-flag operation mutex** `--add` (default) ↔ `--subtract` ↔ `--divide`, `--zero-divisor-result error|remove|nochange|maximum`, repeatable `*--scalar-multiply` (either `N` or `FIELD=N`), repeatable per-field range filters (`*--min-field`/`*--max-field`), IPset masking (`*--set-intersect FIELD=FILE`/`*--set-complement FIELD=FILE`), **2-flag output-format mutex** `--to-bag BAG_KEY,BAG_COUNTER` ↔ `--to-ipset FIELD`, `--ipset-record-version 0|2|3|4|5`, output (`--output-path`, The 9 tools share metadata + display flag sub-arrays. **Shared `_meta` array** (6 flags): `--note-add`/`--note-file-add`/`--invocation-strip` (header annotations), `--compression-method none|zlib|lzo1x|snappy|best`, `--help`/`--version`. **Shared `_disp` array** (8 flags, used in cat/display tools): `--output-path`, `--pager`, `--print-filenames`, `--no-columns`, `--column-separator`, `--no-final-delimiter`, `--delimited`, `--ip-format` (6-value enum: `canonical`/`decimal`/`hex`/`zero-padded`/`any-cidr`/`force-ipv6`). **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwsetbuild`** (text → IPset): `--ip-ranges[=DELIM]` (allow range pairs, default hyphen), `--record-version 0|2|3|4|5` (IPset format), positional `infile`/`outfile`. **`rwsetcat`** (print IPset contents): inherits `_disp`, plus `--count-ips`, `--print-ips`, `--print-statistics`, **3-flag mutex** `--cidr-blocks` ↔ `--ip-ranges` ↔ `--network-structure` (output style), deprecated `--integer-ips`/`--zero-pad-ips`. **`rwsetmember`** (test IPs against IPset): inherits `_disp`, `--count-records`, positionals 1=CIDR/IP pattern + repeatable IPset files. **`rwsetintersect`/`rwsetunion`** (thin wrappers — pre-set `rwsettool` mode): `--output-path`, `--record-version`, repeatable IPset inputs. **`rwsettool`** (general set arithmetic, 17 flags + 7-flag operation mutex): `--union` ↔ `--intersect` ↔ `--difference` ↔ `--symmetric-difference` ↔ `--sample` ↔ `--mask BLOCK-SIZE` ↔ `--fill-blocks BLOCK-SIZE`; sample modifiers `--size`/`--ratio 0.0..1.0`/`--seed`; output `--output-path`, `--modify-inplace` (atomic in-place via tmpfile), `--backup-path`, `--record-version`, `--note-strip`. **`rwbagbuild`** (text or IPset → Bag): `--set-input` ↔ `--bag-input` (2-flag input-source mutex), `--delimiter` (default `|`), `--proto-port-delimiter`, `--default-count`, **`--key-type` 12-value enum** (`sIPv4`/`dIPv4`/`sIPv6`/`dIPv6`/`sip-country`/`dip-country`/`sport`/`dport`/`protocol`/`stcpflags`/`dtcpflags`/`input`/`output`), **`--counter-type` 6-value enum** (`packets`/`bytes`/`records`/`sum-packets`/`sum-bytes`/`any-flag`), `--pmap-file MAPNAME:PATH`. **`rwbagcat`** (print Bag contents, ~14 flags): inherits `_disp` plus Bag-specific `--network-structure`, `--bin-ips` (invert: counter-bins by frequency), `--sort-counters increasing|decreasing`, range filters (`--minkey`/`--maxkey`/`--mincounter`/`--maxcounter`), `--mask-set FILE` (IPset filter), `--zero-counts` (include zero records), `--key-format` 7-value enum (`decimal`/`canonical`/`hex`/`zero-padded`/`timestamp`/`duration`/`epoch`), `--pmap-file`, `--print-statistics`, `--site-config-file`. **`rwbagtool`** (Bag arithmetic + filtering, 19 flags + 7-flag operation mutex): `--add` ↔ `--subtract` ↔ `--minimize` ↔ `--maximize` ↔ `--divide` ↔ `--scalar-multiply N` ↔ `--compare lt|le|eq|ge|gt`; masking `--intersect SETFILE`/`--complement-intersect SETFILE`, range filters (`--mincounter`/`--maxcounter`/`--minkey`/`--maxkey`), output transforms `--invert` (swap keys/counters), `--coverset` (output IPset instead of Bag) + `--ipset-record-version`, atomic write `--modify-inplace`/`--backup-path`, `--note-strip`. **Blacklist additions (9)**: 3 between `rwall` (22757) and `rwbypid.d` (22758) for the `rwbag*` block (`rwbagbuild`/`rwbagcat`/`rwbagtool`) and 6 between `rwhod` (22766) and `rwsnoop` (22767+) for the `rwset*` block (`rwsetbuild`/`rwsetcat`/`rwsetintersect`/`rwsetmember`/`rwsettool`/`rwsetunion`).
