- **SiLK rw* family — rwfilter + rwstats + rwsort + rwuniq + rwcat + rwcount + rwfileinfo + rwgroup (8 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-134 tightened `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream CERT NetSA docs at [tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwfilter.html](https://tools.netsa.cert.org/silk/rwfilter.html) plus per-tool pages (`rwstats.html`/`rwsort.html`/`rwuniq.html`/`rwcat.html`/`rwcount.html`/`rwfileinfo.html`/`rwgroup.html`). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (9 already-covered + 1 deferred)**: NfDump family `nfdump`/`nfcapd`/`nfreplay`/`nfprofile`/`nfanon` all at `src/_*`, Varnish family `varnishadm`/`varnishlog`/`varnishstat` at `more_src/`, `haproxy` at `more_src/`. Deferred: `dvdrip` truly fresh but it's a Perl-Tk GUI ripping/transcoding tool with no documented CLI surface beyond `dvd::rip --project FILE` — left for users who need it. Single multi-stem file `_rwfilter` is `#compdef rwfilter rwstats rwsort rwuniq rwcat rwcount rwfileinfo rwgroup`, dispatching on `$service` — covers CERT NetSA's flow-record analysis suite for IPFIX/NetFlow traffic. The 8 tools share an output framework but each has its own selection/aggregation surface. **Shared `_io` array** (14 flags re-applied per stem): `--output-path` (default stdout), repeatable `*--xargs FILE` (read filenames from file/stdin), `--print-filenames`, `--compression-method none|zlib|lzo1x|snappy|best`, `--note-add`/`--note-file-add` (annotate output header), `--site-config-file`, repeatable `*--plugin` (load C plugin), repeatable `*--pmap-file MAPNAME:PATH` (prefix maps for IP/port labels), repeatable `*--python-file *.py` (custom field code), `--temp-directory`, `--help`/`--version`/`--help-fields`. **Per-stem coverage**: **`rwfilter`** (~45 selection flags) — the workhorse query tool: time (`--start-date`/`--end-date YYYY/MM/DD[:HH]`, `--stime`/`--etime`/`--active-time`/`--duration`), repo (`--data-rootdir`, `--class`/`--type`/`--flowtypes`/`--sensors`), addresses (repeatable `*--scidr`/`*--dcidr`/`*--any-cidr` + `*--not-` negations, `*--saddress`/`*--daddress`/`*--any-address`, `*--sipset`/`*--dipset`/`*--anyset` for IPset-file lookups), ports/protos (`--sport`/`--dport`/`--aport`, `--protocol`, `--icmp-type`/`--icmp-code`), TCP flags (3-flag set `--flags-all`/`--flags-initial`/`--flags-session`), volume (`--bytes`/`--packets`/`--bytes-per-packet`), output partitioning (`--pass-destination`/`--fail-destination`/`--all-destination`), statistics (`--print-statistics`/`--print-volume-statistics`/`--max-pass-records`/`--max-fail-records`), advanced (`--tuple-file`/`--python-expr`/`--threads`/`--dry-run`). **`rwstats`** (Top-N/Bottom-N aggregation): `--fields`/`--values`, output limiting trio (`--count N`/`--threshold value`/`--percentage N`), **sort-direction mutex** `--top` ↔ `--bottom`, `--no-titles`/`--column-separator`. **`rwsort`**: `--fields` (sort keys), `--presorted-input` (merge-sort), `--reverse`, `--sort-buffer-size` (default ~2GB). **`rwuniq`** (binning + aggregation): `--fields`/`--values`/`--threshold FIELD=MIN[-MAX]`, time control (`--bin-time SEC`/`--nano-value-times`/`--timestamp-format`/`--epoch-time`), IP formatting (`--ip-format` 5-value enum `canonical`/`decimal`/`hex`/`any-cidr`/`force-ipv6`, `--integer-ips`/`--zero-pad-ips`), output format (`--no-titles`/`--no-columns`/`--column-separator`/`--delimited`). **`rwcat`** (concat + byte-order conversion): `--byte-order native|big|little`, `--ipv4-output`. **`rwcount`** (time-bin aggregation): `--bin-size SEC` (default 30), `--load-scheme` with 7-value enum (`start-time`/`end-time`/`middle`/`duration`/`time-proportional`/`minimum-volume`/`maximum-volume` — how to distribute multi-bin flows), `--start-time`/`--end-time`, `--skip-zeroes`. **`rwfileinfo`** (file-header introspection): `--fields`, `--summary`, `--no-titles`. **`rwgroup`** (flow grouping by key + delta): `--id-fields` (group keys), `--delta-field`/`--delta-value` (time-gap clustering), `--rec-threshold N`, `--objective`/`--summarize`. **Blacklist additions (8)**: all 8 rw* stems inserted into the `rw*` block — 5 between `rwbytype.d` and `rwho` (rwcat/rwcount/rwfileinfo/rwfilter/rwgroup) and 3 between `rwsnoop` and `rx` (rwsort/rwstats/rwuniq). Sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 the rw* block resolves as `b`<`c`<`f`<`g`<`h`<`s`<`u`; within the c-tied pair `rwcat`<`rwcount` (index 3 `a`<`o`); within the f-tied pair `rwfileinfo`<`rwfilter` (index 5 `e`<`t`); within the s-tied pair `rwsort`<`rwstats` (index 3 `o`<`t`). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **wodim (Debian's renamed cdrecord fork — CD/DVD-Recorder driver)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + an **upgraded round-116 grep using `^#compdef( |.* )${c}( |$)`** that tightens the word-boundary check per the round-133 false-positive lesson — `\<` was matching hyphenated containment like `pstops` inside `foo2zjs-pstops`; this round's pattern requires `pstops` to be either the first space-separated token after `#compdef ` or surrounded by spaces / end-of-line, eliminating the substring-in-hyphen-cluster failure mode). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/wodim/wodim.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/wodim/wodim.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial — 11 already-covered + 5 stock-zsh dups)**: `cdrecord` at `src/_cdrecord` + stock zsh (Joerg Schilling's original upstream — `wodim` is Debian's renamed fork following the 2006 licensing dispute), `mkisofs`/`cdda2wav` at `src/`, `flac`/`metaflac` at `more_src/` (plus stock `_flac`), `mosh`/`mosh-server` at `more_src/` (plus stock `_mosh`), `eternal-terminal`/`et` (the latter caught via multi-stem `more_src/_eternal-terminal` `#compdef` line — a clean example of the round-116 grep working as intended); stock-zsh dups `growisofs`/`cdrdao`/`abcde`/`abcde`. Single-file completion `_wodim` covers Debian's renamed `cdrecord` fork — the actual CD/DVD-burner workhorse on Debian/Ubuntu systems. ~70 flags across 8 functional groups: meta `-version`/`-checkdrive`/`-inq`/`-scanbus`/`--devices`/`-prcap`/`-atip`/`-toc`/`-msinfo` (all `(- *)` mutex — quit-after-print); verbosity stackable `*-v` (write progress) + `*-V` (SCSI transport; `-VV` shows buffer content); **device + tuning `key=value` syntax** (uncommon `value` completion via `+-`): `dev=` (via `_wodim_devs` helper running `wodim --devices` + awk-extraction of `dev=` lines for live device discovery), `speed=N`, `blank=` (5-mode enum `all`/`fast`/`track`/`session`/`unclose`), `fs=BYTES`, `minbuf=PCT` (25..95% ATAPI wait), `gracetime`/`timeout SEC`, `padsize`, `driver=NAME` (advanced manual selection), `driveropts=` (comma-separated with 6-value seed `burnfree`/`noburnfree`/`varirec`/`gigarec`/`audiomaster`/`forcespeed`), `cuefile=*.cue`/`msifile=`/`textfile=`. **Track-format 5-flag mutex** `-audio`/`-data`/`-mode2`/`-xa`/`-xa1` (CD-DA 44.1kHz vs CD-ROM mode 1 2048-byte sector vs mode 2 2336-byte vs XA mode 2 form 1 with drive-headers vs XA with app-headers). **Write-mode 7-flag mutex** `-tao`/`-sao`/`-dao`/`-raw`/`-raw96r`/`-raw96p`/`-raw16` (track-at-once default + session-at-once + DAO alias + the 4 raw subchannel variants). **Fixation mutex** `-fix` ↔ `-nofix`. Behavior `-multi` (multi-session), `-dummy` (laser-off dry-run — saves a coaster), `-eject`, `-load`/`-lock`, `-noclose`, `-force` (disable safety checks), `-immed` (non-blocking SCSI). **Padding mutex** `-pad` ↔ `-nopad`. `-isosize`, `-swab` (byte-swap little-endian audio for the drive), `-useinfo`, `-text` (write CD-Text from `.inf`/CUE). **Copy-protection 3-flag mutex** `-copy` (unrestricted) ↔ `-nocopy` (single-personal-copy default) ↔ `-scms` (no copying). Misc `-clone` (readom raw image), `-format` (CD-RW/DVD-RW/DVD+RW format). Trailing repeatable positional `*:track file:_files -g "*.(wav|cdr|raw|iso|cue|mp3|flac)"` covers the 7 common burn-source formats. `wodim` already in `blacklist.txt`; no add needed.
- **psutils Round 2 — pstops + psselect + epsffit + extractres + includeres (5 binary stems via single multi-stem file, completes psutils coverage to 9)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/psutils/](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/psutils/) per-tool man pages (`pstops.1` + `psselect.1` + `epsffit.1` + `extractres.1` + `includeres.1`). **Tooling-bug callout discovered this round**: the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep returned a **false positive** on `pstops` because the existing `src/_foo2zjs-pstops` file declares `#compdef foo2zjs-pstops` and the regex `^#compdef.*\<pstops\>` matches `pstops` as a word inside that hyphenated identifier (zsh-style `\<` word-boundary treats `-` as non-word). Verified by inspecting the actual `#compdef` line; `_foo2zjs-pstops` does NOT cover the bare `pstops` binary, so it remains truly fresh and is included in this round. Lesson for future grep upgrades: the word-boundary regex needs tightening (e.g. `^#compdef([^a-zA-Z0-9_-]|.* )pstops([^a-zA-Z0-9_-]|$)`) to reject hyphenated containment. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (5 already-covered)**: `psjoin` at `man_src/_psjoin`, `rootlesskit`/`slirp4netns`/`dvdauthor` all at `src/`, `enscript`/`genisoimage` ship in stock zsh at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/`. Single multi-stem file `_pstops` is `#compdef pstops psselect epsffit extractres includeres`, dispatching on `$service` — companion to the round-132 `_psnup` multi-stem; **the two files together provide complete coverage of the 9 active psutils binaries** (psnup/psresize/psbook from round 132 + pstops/psselect/epsffit/extractres/includeres from this round + ps2pdf from round 132 as a Ghostscript wrapper). **`pstops`** (general page-by-page transform — the engine behind `psnup`'s n-up imposition): 6 flags + 3 positionals: `-q` (suppress page-number output), `-b` (prevent `bind` ops in PS prolog), `-w width`/`-h height`/`-p paper-name` (15-value enum from shared `_papers`), `-d lwidth` (draw border line, default 1pt); positionals 1 `page-spec` (e.g. `2:0(0,0)+1(0,0)` for the famous 2-up imposition spec), 2 `infile`, 3 `outfile`. **`psselect`** (page selector): 5 flags + 2 positionals: `-q`, **even/odd mutex** `-e` ↔ `-o`, `-r` (reverse output order), `-p pages` (e.g. `1,3-5,7` range expression). **`epsffit`** (EPS bounding-box fitter): 5 flags + 6 positionals: `-c` (centre image), `-r` (rotate 90° CCW), **aspect-vs-rotate mutex** `-a` (adjust aspect to fit, default preserve) ↔ `-m` (rotate-for-best-fit), `-s` (append `showpage` to force print); positionals 1..4 are the bounding-box coordinates `llx`/`lly`/`urx`/`ury` and 5..6 are `infile`/`outfile`. **`extractres`** (extract resources from a PostScript document): 1 flag `-m` (combine same-named resources into one file — needed for some font downloaders that emit fonts piece-by-piece). **`includeres`** (splice extracted resources back in): pure stdin → stdout filter with no documented flags — the completion returns `0` early so zsh just falls through to its default `_files` handling. All 5 stems already in `blacklist.txt`; no add needed.
- **PostScript transformation family — psnup + psresize + psbook + ps2pdf (4 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/psutils/](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/psutils/) (`psnup.1` + `psresize.1` + `psbook.1`) plus [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/ghostscript/ps2pdf.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/ghostscript/ps2pdf.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (7 already-covered)**: `ps2eps` at `src/_ps2eps`, `multipath`/`iscsiadm`/`iscsid`/`tgt-admin`/`tgtadm` all at `src/`/`man_src/`/`more_src/`. Single multi-stem file `_psnup` is `#compdef psnup psresize psbook ps2pdf`, dispatching on `$service` — covers Angus Duggan's 3 most-used PostScript-transformation tools from psutils plus the Ghostscript `ps2pdf` wrapper. **Shared paper-size enum** across `psnup`/`psresize` via `_papers` array (15 values: `a0`/`a1`/`a2`/`a3`/`a4`/`a5`/`b5`/`letter`/`legal`/`tabloid`/`statement`/`executive`/`folio`/`quarto`/`10x14` — the canonical psutils paper menu interpolated into `-p`/`-P` enum lists). **`psnup`** (n-up imposition, 16 flags + repeatable input) — output paper `-w width`/`-h height`/`-p paper-name`, input paper `-W width`/`-H height`/`-P paper-name`, **rotation 3-flag mutex** `-l` (landscape, 90° CCW) ↔ `-r` (seascape, 90° CW) ↔ `-f` (swap w/h without rotation), `-c` (column-major layout, default row-major), margins `-m margin` (around entire output) vs `-b border` (around each input page), `-d lwidth` (draw border line, default 1pt), `-s scale` (manual scale override), `-i nup` (pages per sheet — same as the unusual `-N` bare-number form per upstream), `-q` (suppress page-number output); positional `*:postscript file:_files -g "*.(ps|eps)"`. **`psresize`** (rescale + paper-format change, 7 flags + 2 positionals) — output `-w`/`-h`/`-p paper-name`, input `-W`/`-H`/`-P paper-name`, `-q`; positionals 1 `infile` and 2 `outfile`. **`psbook`** (signature reordering for bookbinding, 2 flags + 2 positionals) — `-q` (suppress page-number output), `-s N` (signature size — must be a multiple of 4; enum seeded with `4 8 12 16 20 24 28 32` covering the practical book-binding range). **`ps2pdf`** (GhostScript wrapper) — upstream man page explicitly defers to `gs(1)` for the full option set; this completion models the 9 most-practitioner-useful Ghostscript flags: `-dPDFSETTINGS=` with 5-preset enum (`/screen`/`/ebook`/`/printer`/`/prepress`/`/default` — the standard quality-vs-size trade-off menu used in 99% of ps2pdf invocations), `-dCompatibilityLevel=` with 7-version PDF enum (`1.2`/`1.3`/`1.4`/`1.5`/`1.6`/`1.7`/`2.0`), font controls `-dEmbedAllFonts=true|false`/`-dSubsetFonts=true|false`/`-dCompressFonts=true|false`, page handling `-dAutoRotatePages=/None|/All|/PageByPage`, image-resolution trio `-dColorImageResolution=dpi`/`-dGrayImageResolution=dpi`/`-dMonoImageResolution=dpi`; positionals 1 input `.ps`/`.eps` and 2 optional output `.pdf` (stdout if omitted). All 4 stems already in `blacklist.txt`; no add needed.
- **CUPS print-management family — lpstat + lpq + lprm + lpoptions + lpadmin (5 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream Apple/OpenPrinting CUPS man pages at [www.cups.org/doc/man-lpstat.html](https://www.cups.org/doc/man-lpstat.html) + `man-lpq.html`/`man-lprm.html`/`man-lpoptions.html`/`man-lpadmin.html`. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (7 already-covered + 1 stock-zsh dup)**: `lp` ships in stock zsh at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_lp`, `lpc` already at `src/_lpc` (and multi-stem `src/_lpc.cups` `#compdef` line for the platform variant), `lpr` covered via multi-stem `src/_lpr-cups` `#compdef`; LaTeX-lint family `chktex`/`lacheck` at `src/`/`more_src/`; flow-analysis `cflow` at `more_src/`, `cscope` shipping stock zsh at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_cscope` (and `more_src/_cscope-indexer` multi-stem catches the wrapper variant). Single multi-stem file `_lpstat` is `#compdef lpstat lpq lprm lpoptions lpadmin`, dispatching on `$service` — covers the 5 user-facing CUPS print-management binaries. The family shares a common server-connection sub-set across all members: `-E` (force encryption to the CUPS server), `-h server[:port]` (alternate server via `_hosts`), `-U username` (alternate CUPS user via `_users`); beyond that each binary's flag set is distinct. **Live printer helper `_lp_printers`** runs `lpstat -p` and pipes through `awk '/^printer/ {print $2}'` to extract installed printer names for the `-d`/`-p`/`-P`/`-o`/`-v`/`-x` printer-target positionals — works against the local CUPS instance by default but follows `-h`/`-U` overrides via env propagation. **Per-stem coverage**: **`lpstat`** (18 flags) — print-system introspection covering jobs (`-o destination`, `-u user`, `-W completed|not-completed`, `-R` ranking), printers (`-p`/`-a`/`-v`/`-c`/`-l` long-listing), defaults (`-d`/`-e`/`-r` server-running/`-s` summary/`-t` everything), connection (`-E`/`-H`/`-h`/`-U`); **`lpq`** (8 flags) — queue status with `-P printer`, `-a` (all printers), `-l` (long format), trailing `+interval` continuous-polling syntax (e.g. `lpq +5` to refresh every 5 seconds), positional `*:job-id:`; **`lprm`** (6 flags) — job cancellation with `-P printer`, bare `-` (cancel all queued jobs), positional `*:job-id:` for selective removal; **`lpoptions`** (8 flags) — per-user option editing with `-d destination[/instance]` (set user default), `-p destination[/instance]` (set instance context), `-l` (list current options), repeatable `*-o name[=value]` (set option), `-r option` (remove option), `-x destination[/instance]` (clear all instance options); **`lpadmin`** (17 flags) — administrator-only printer setup with `-p destination` (configure named printer), `-x destination` (delete), `-c class`/`-r class` (class membership add/remove), `-d destination` (system default), `-v device-uri` (queue backend), `-m model` (PPD model), `-D info`/`-L location` (textual metadata), `-u expr` (user-level access policy), repeatable `*-o name=value` (PPD/job options) + repeatable `*-R name-default` (delete defaults), deprecated `-i FILE` (interface script) / `-P FILE` (`.ppd` glob). All 5 stems already in `blacklist.txt`; no add needed.
- **eksctl-anywhere (AWS EKS Anywhere CLI — on-premises Kubernetes)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Subcommand tree + per-leaf flag detail transcribed from upstream [anywhere.eks.amazonaws.com/docs/reference/eksctl/](https://anywhere.eks.amazonaws.com/docs/reference/eksctl/) plus per-action pages (`anywhere_create_cluster/` + `anywhere_generate_clusterconfig/`). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial — 9 already-covered)**: `kubeshark`/`kube-bench`/`kube-hunter`/`k0sctl` at `more_src/`, `crossplane` at `more_src/_crossplane`, `subctl` at `more_src/_subctl`, `cloudflared`/`wrangler` at `more_src/`, `helmwave` at `more_src/_helmwave`. Single-file completion `_eksctl-anywhere` covers the AWS EKS Anywhere CLI — manages on-premises Kubernetes clusters using the same APIs as Amazon EKS; supports vSphere/bare-metal-Tinkerbell/CloudStack/Snowball/Nutanix/Docker (dev-only) provider backends. The umbrella `eksctl` binary also exposes these via `eksctl anywhere <sub>`; this completion targets the standalone `eksctl-anywhere` form (already-covered `eksctl` at `man_src/_eksctl` handles the integrated case). 17 top-level subcommands via `_subcmds` describe: `apply`, `check-images`, `copy`, `create`, `delete`, `describe`, `download`, `exp`, `generate`, `get`, `help`, `import`, `install`, `list`, `renew`, `upgrade`, `version`. 2 shared persistent flags via `_global`: `-h/--help`, `-v/--verbosity 0..9` (numeric log-level enum). **`create`/`delete`/`upgrade` state machine**: target enum (`cluster`/`packages`/`hardware`/`support-bundle`). The `create cluster` leaf covers 14 flags including `-f/--filename FILE` (cluster YAML, `_files -g "*.(yaml|yml)"`), `-z/--hardware-csv FILE` (Tinkerbell hardware CSV), `--bundles-override FILE` (custom EKS-A release bundle manifest), `--kubeconfig FILE` (management cluster kubeconfig), `--install-packages FILE` (curated-packages config), `--tinkerbell-bootstrap-ip IP` (bootstrap-cluster Tinkerbell stack IP), 3 timeout overrides `--control-plane-wait-timeout DURATION` (default `1h0m0s`)/`--external-etcd-wait-timeout` (default `1h0m0s`)/`--per-machine-wait-timeout DURATION` (default `10m0s`), 2 deprecated timeouts `--node-startup-timeout`/`--unhealthy-machine-timeout`, `--no-timeouts` (disable all wait timeouts), `--skip-ip-check` (skip control-plane IP availability check), repeatable `*--skip-validations name` (bypass named create validation — useful for development clusters). **`generate` state machine**: 4-target enum (`clusterconfig`/`hardware`/`packages`/`support-bundle`). The `generate clusterconfig` leaf covers `-p/--provider` with the full 6-provider enum (`vsphere`/`tinkerbell`/`docker`/`snow`/`cloudstack`/`nutanix`) + cluster-name positional. **`exp` state machine** (experimental subcommands): 2-target enum (`validate`/`vsphere`). Remaining subcommands (`get`/`describe`/`list`/`install`/`import`/`apply`/`renew`/`check-images`/`copy`/`download`) fall through to generic positional handling — they have their own per-target sub-trees that need more docs to source-verify; deferred for a future round. `eksctl-anywhere` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 7151 between `eksctl` (7150) and `elan` (7151 → 7152) — sort verified shared `eksctl` prefix means `eksctl` (length 6) < `eksctl-anywhere` (length 15) by shorter-string-wins; at index 1 `eksctl-anywhere`'s 'k' (107) < `elan`'s 'l' (108). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **otfcc family — otfccdump + otfccbuild (Caryll Studio's OpenType compiler/decompiler, 2 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/caryll/otfcc](https://github.com/caryll/otfcc) README options table. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (8 already-covered)**: `fc-list`/`fc-cache`/`fc-match` at `src/`, `mkfontscale` at `man_src/`, `pyftsubset` at `more_src/` (with multi-stem in `src/_pyftsubset`'s `#compdef`), `xmllint`/`xsltproc` at `src/`. Single multi-stem file `_otfccdump` is `#compdef otfccdump otfccbuild`, dispatching on `$service` — covers Caryll's bidirectional OpenType font compiler used heavily in font-engineering workflows where you decompile binary OTF/TTF → JSON, patch font tables, then recompile JSON → OTF/TTF. **`otfccdump`** (decompile → JSON, 14 flags): meta `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`; output `-o FILE` (defaults to stdout, JSON glob); TTC sub-font selection `-n/--ttc-index N`; **JSON formatting mutex** `--pretty` ↔ `--ugly`; `--verbose`; content filters `--ignore-glyph-order`, `--glyph-name-prefix pfx`, `--ignore-hints`, `--decimal-cmap` (`cmap` keys as decimal numbers vs default hex), `--name-by-hash`; **BOM mutex** `--add-bom` ↔ `--no-bom`; input via `_files -g "*.(otf|ttf|ttc|otc)"` covering all 4 OpenType container forms. **`otfccbuild`** (compile JSON → OTF/TTF, 24 flags + 4-level optimization enum): meta `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`; output `-o FILE`; **4-level optimization mutex** `-O0` (off) ↔ `-O1` (default) ↔ `-O2` (aggressive web-font) ↔ `-O3` (most aggressive); `-s/--dummy-dsig` (empty DSIG table — needed for self-signing some Windows scenarios), `--verbose`, `--ignore-hints`; **preservation flags** `--keep-average-char-width` (preserve `OS/2.xAvgCharWidth`), `--keep-unicode-ranges` (preserve `OS/2.ulUnicodeRange`), `--keep-modified-time` (keep `head.modified` from JSON), `--short-post` (no glyph names in output); **glyph-order 3-flag mutex** `-i/--ignore-glyph-order` ↔ `-k/--keep-glyph-order` ↔ `--dont-ignore-glyph-order` (the last is an explicit alias for `--keep-glyph-order` — encoded so users get tab discovery of both spellings); **feature dedup mutex** `--merge-features` ↔ `--dont-merge-features`; **lookup dedup mutex** `--merge-lookups` ↔ `--dont-merge-lookups`; CFF-table tweaks `--force-cid` (name-keyed → CID-keyed conversion), `--subroutinize` (CFF table subroutinization for size); cmap `--stub-cmap4` (stub `cmap` format 4 subtable when format 12 is present, for legacy-tool compat); input via `_files -g "*.json"`. **Blacklist additions (2)**: `otfccbuild` and `otfccdump` inserted between `oterm` (18537) and `otfinfo` (18538) — sort verified at index 3 `otfcc*`'s 'c' (99) < `otfinfo`'s 'i' (105); at index 5 within the `otfcc*` block, `otfccbuild`'s 'b' (98) < `otfccdump`'s 'd' (100). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **dolphin-tool (Dolphin emulator's headless companion CLI — Wii/GameCube disc image toolkit)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Subcommand inventory + per-leaf flag detail extracted from upstream `Source/Core/DolphinTool/{Command,ConvertCommand,VerifyCommand,HeaderCommand,ExtractCommand}.cpp` on [dolphin-emu/dolphin](https://github.com/dolphin-emu/dolphin). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (8 already-covered)**: `mednafen`/`mame`/`dolphin-emu`/`pcsx2`/`ppsspp`/`scummvm` all at `more_src/_*`; `browsh`/`nyxt` at `more_src/`. **Note on `duckstation`** truly fresh but upstream doesn't ship an official headless `duckstation-cli` binary — the `duckstation-qt`/`duckstation-nogui` flavours are GUI-launchers with `-batch` startup mode but no proper subcommand CLI; skipped per the "no rich CLI surface" precedent. Single-file completion `_dolphin-tool` covers the Wii/GameCube disc image toolkit shipped alongside the Dolphin emulator for headless format conversion + verification + extraction workflows (common in archival ROM-hacking pipelines). 4 subcommands via `_subcmds` describe: `convert` (transform between ISO/GCZ/WIA/RVZ + optional scrub + compress), `verify` (validate integrity + compute CRC32/MD5/SHA1/rchash digests), `header` (dump metadata: block size, compression details), `extract` (unpack files/partitions). **`convert`** (9 flags): `-u/--user DIR` (Dolphin user folder, auto-created), `-i/--input FILE`, `-o/--output FILE`, `-f/--format` (4-value enum `iso\|gcz\|wia\|rvz`, default `rvz`), `-s/--scrub` (junk-data scrubbing), `-b/--block_size N` (RVZ default `131072` = 128 KiB), `-c/--compression` (5-value enum `none\|zstd\|bzip2\|lzma\|lzma2`, RVZ default `zstd`), `-l/--compression_level N` (RVZ zstd default 5). **`verify`** (4 flags): `-u/--user`, `-i/--input`, `-a/--algorithm` (4-value enum `crc32\|md5\|sha1\|rchash`). **`header`** (6 flags): `-i/--input`, `-j/--json` (machine-readable; overrides individual print flags), `-b/--block_size`, `-c/--compression`, `-l/--compression_level` (each as a boolean toggle for selective display). **`extract`** (8 flags): `-i/--input`, `-o/--output DIR`, `-p/--partition PARTITION`, `-s/--single PATH` (single-file/directory mode), `-l/--list` (list contents, honours `--single`), `-q/--quiet`, `-g/--gameonly` (DATA partition only). Input file completion across all subcommands uses `_files -g "*.(iso|gcz|wia|rvz|wbfs|ciso)"` covering the 6 common Wii/GameCube container formats (ISO baseline, GCZ Dolphin-native, WIA WiiTime archive, RVZ Dolphin-next-gen, WBFS USB-loader, CISO Compressed-ISO). `dolphin-tool` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 6542 between `dolphin-emu` and `dolphin5` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 8 `dolphin-emu`'s 'e' (101) < `dolphin-tool`'s 't' (116); at index 7 `dolphin-tool`'s '-' (45) < `dolphin5`'s '5' (53)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **pacseek (moson-mo's interactive TUI for Arch package search + install)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/moson-mo/pacseek](https://github.com/moson-mo/pacseek) `main.go` help block. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial — 30+ already-covered)**: embedded family `esptool`/`espup`/`espefuse`/`espsecure` (last 2 caught via multi-stem `#compdef` lines on the `_espefuse`/`_espsecure` files), `tio`/`picotool`/`pyocd`/`openocd`/`st-flash`/`st-info`/`jlink`/`nala` all already at `more_src/`/`src/`; Python-lint family `jrnl`/`pylama`/`pyre`/`pytype`/`vulture` (the last caught via `more_src/_tempo-vulture` multi-stem — another nice round-116 grep win), `autoflake`/`autopep8`/`pyupgrade`/`pycln`/`djlint`/`pre-commit` all already at `more_src/`/`src/`; WM family `herbstluftwm`/`i3` at `more_src/`, `openbox` at `src/_openbox` (with multi-stem `_openbox-lxde` catching the alt form), `awesome` caught via `more_src/_awesome-cli` multi-stem, `sk`/`skim` at `more_src/`; sync family `zsync` at `more_src/_zsync`. Single-file completion `_pacseek` covers the interactive TUI for searching and installing Arch Linux packages — most navigation is keyboard-driven inside the TUI (ENTER/TAB/CTRL+S settings/CTRL+Q quit), config persisted at `~/.config/pacseek/config.json`, so the CLI surface is intentionally thin. 7 flags: meta `-h/--help`; repo filtering `-r REPOS` (comma-separated list, completes via `_values -s ,` with a static seed of 10 common Arch repos — `core`/`extra`/`multilib` + their `-testing` variants, plus `aur`/`chaotic-aur`/`eos`/`endeavouros-aur` for derivative-distro users); initial state `-s TERM` (initial search term), `-u` (show upgrades after startup), `-i` (show installed packages after startup); rendering `-a` (ASCII mode — no Unicode box-drawing, useful for serial consoles + Windows Terminal pre-fluent builds), `-m` (monochrome mode — no colours, useful for tmux-pipes / `script` recordings). The `-r REPOS` `_values -s ,` invocation is the canonical zsh pattern for comma-separated repeatable enums — lets users type `-r core,extra` and tab-complete each comma-segment independently from the same `_repos` array. `pacseek` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 18664 between `pacsearch` and `pacsift` (sort verified position-by-position: index 4 both share 'e' (101), so resolves at index 5 — `pacsearch`'s 'a' (97) < `pacseek`'s 'e' (101); and at index 4 `pacseek`'s 'e' (101) < `pacsift`'s 'i' (105)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **FRR daemon family expansion — pbrd + ripngd (2 binary stems via single multi-stem file, fills out the FRR routing-daemon coverage to 11)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Common-flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [docs.frrouting.org/en/latest/basic.html](https://docs.frrouting.org/en/latest/basic.html) plus per-daemon pages: [pbr.html](https://docs.frrouting.org/en/latest/pbr.html) and [ripngd.html](https://docs.frrouting.org/en/latest/ripngd.html) (both daemons explicitly defer to the common-options page — they ship no daemon-specific invocation flags). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (9 FRR daemons already-covered)**: `babeld`, `bgpd`, `eigrpd`, `isisd`, `ldpd`, `nhrpd`, `ospf6d`, `ospfd`, `pimd`, `ripd`, `staticd` — all already at `more_src/_*` (a remarkably-comprehensive FRR coverage already in the repo; this round just fills in the last 2 missing daemons). Also: `scanimage`/`scanadf`/`saned`, `ocrmypdf`/`cuneiform`/`tesseract`, `vtysh`/`zebra`/`bgpd`/`ospfd`. Single multi-stem file `_pbrd` is `#compdef pbrd ripngd`, dispatching on `$service` — both daemons share the FRR-common command-line flag set verbatim (per upstream both pages explicitly say "There are no $daemon-specific invocation options. Common options can be specified"). `pbrd` is FRR's Policy-Based Routing daemon (per-source/per-application routing decisions beyond destination-only matching); `ripngd` is FRR's RIPng (RIP for IPv6) routing daemon, the IPv6 sibling of `ripd`. **18 shared flags** (the FRR common-invocation set): meta `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`; daemonize `-d/--daemon`; config `-f/--config_file FILE`, `-i/--pid_file FILE`, `-z/--socket FILE` (zebra control socket); VTY `-A/--vty_addr ADDR`, `-P/--vty_port PORT`; identity `-u/--user USER` (via `_users`), `-g/--group GROUP` (via `_groups`); namespacing `-N/--pathspace NS` (per-instance config namespace), `-w/--vrfwnetns` (discover VRFs via Linux network namespaces — Linux-only feature), `-o/--vrfdefaultname NAME` (override "Default VRF" CLI display name); module loading `-M/--module module[:opts]` (FRR's pluggable module system — used for SNMP, etc); logging `--command-log-always` (permanent command logging), `--log dest` (initial destination — special `(stdout syslog)` enum plus `file:/path` form via `_alternative`), `--log-level` with 8-value enum (`emergencies`/`alerts`/`critical`/`errors`/`warnings`/`notifications`/`informational`/`debugging`); CLI features `--tcli` (transactional CLI mode); resource limits `--limit-fds N`. The `--log` flag uses `_alternative` to multiplex two surfaces — the bare special values `stdout`/`syslog` and the `file:/path` form via `_files` — matching upstream's `--log <stdout|syslog|file:/path/to/log/file>` syntax. **Blacklist additions (2)**: `pbrd` inserted between `pbr` (19043) and `pbs_iff` (19044) — sort verified shared `pbr` prefix means `pbr` (length 3) < `pbrd` (length 4) by shorter-string-wins, and `pbrd`'s `r` (114) < `pbs_iff`'s `s` (115) at index 2; `ripngd` inserted between `ripmime` (22257) and `rippkgs` (22258) — sort verified at index 3 `ripmime`'s 'm' (109) < `ripngd`'s 'n' (110) < `rippkgs`'s 'p' (112). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **rmlint (Christopher Pahl & Daniel Thomas's duplicate-and-lint finder)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/rmlint/rmlint.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/rmlint/rmlint.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (8 already-covered)**: round-124's deferred mkvtoolnix CLI family — `mkvmerge`/`mkvinfo`/`mkvextract`/`mkvpropedit` — all turned out to **already be covered** at individual `more_src/_mkv*` files (the earlier deferral was based on an incorrect "deferred for multi-stem fanout" call; the per-tool completions were already shipped). Round-124's `daemontools` deferral also resolved: `svc`/`svstat`/`svscan`/`supervise` all at `more_src/` (some catching via `_s6-svstat`/`_s6-svscan` multi-stem `#compdef` lines), plus `jdupes` at `more_src/_jdupes` and `exiv2` at `src/_exiv2`. Single-file completion `_rmlint` covers the duplicate-and-lint finder — locates duplicate files, empty files/dirs, broken symlinks, broken sUid/sGid bits, non-stripped binaries, and bad encodings; outputs a shell script that can be reviewed before running to actually delete the cruft (the "review-before-delete" workflow is rmlint's killer feature for safe dedup). ~58 flags grouped with 14 mutex sets: meta `-h/--help`, `-H/--show-man` (open full manpage), `--version` (shows compiled-feature list). **Output**: repeatable `*-T/--types` (CSV `+/-name` filter — e.g. `+duplicates,-emptyfiles`), repeatable `*-o/--output fmt:file` (custom format-to-file pairs), repeatable `*-O/--add-output fmt:file` (add without overwriting defaults), repeatable `*-c/--config key=value` (formatter knob). **Filters**: `-z/--perms RWX` (permission filter), `-s/--size RANGE` (e.g. `1K-100M`), `-d/--max-depth N`. **Hash algorithm enum (`-a/--algorithm`)** with 24 values covering the full upstream menu: blake2b/blake2bp/blake2s/blake2sp, sha3 family, sha256/sha512/sha1/md5, highway64/128/256, metro/metro256/metrocrc/metrocrc256, xxhash, murmur/murmur256, city/city256, cumulative, ext, paranoid. **Paranoia mutex** `-p/--paranoid` ↔ `-P/--less-paranoid`. **Verbosity mutex** stackable `-v/--loud` ↔ `-V/--quiet`. **Progress mutex** `-g/--progress` ↔ `-G/--no-progress`. **Color mutex** `-w/--with-color` ↔ `-W/--no-with-color`. **Hardlink mutex (3-flag)** `-l/--hardlinked` ↔ `-L/--no-hardlinked` + `--keep-hardlinked`. **Symlink mutex (3-flag exclusion)** `-f/--followlinks` ↔ `-F/--no-followlinks` ↔ `-@/--see-symlinks` (the last is rmlint's `@` trick: a single character flag, encoded literally so zsh doesn't choke on the `@`). **Crossdev mutex** `-x/--no-crossdev` ↔ `-X/--crossdev`. **Hidden mutex** `-r/--hidden` ↔ `-R/--no-hidden` + `--partial-hidden`. **Basename matching mutex** `-b/--match-basename` ↔ `-B/--unmatched-basename`. **Extension matching pair-mutex** `-e/--match-with-extension` ↔ `-E/--no-match-with-extension`, plus `-i/--match-without-extension` ↔ `-I/--no-match-without-extension`. **Time filtering**: `-n/--newer-than-stamp FILE` vs `-N/--newer-than TIME`. **Tagged-path semantics**: `-k/--keep-all-tagged` (preserve tagged), `-K/--keep-all-untagged` (preserve untagged), `-m/--must-match-tagged` (only dups in tagged), `-M/--must-match-untagged` (only dups in untagged), `-S/--rank-by` with 20-value enum (`m`/`a`/`c`/`M`/`A`/`C`/`l`/`L`/`p`/`P`/`x`/`X`/`h`/`H`/`o`/`O`/`d`/`D`/`r`/`R`). **Replay mode** `--replay FILE` (reprocess previous JSON output with new filters — the rmlint workflow lets you tweak filtering without re-hashing). **xattr caching** `-C/--xattr` (cache to xattrs) + sub-flags `--xattr-read`/`--xattr-write`/`--xattr-clear`. **Misc**: `-U/--write-unfinished`, `-t/--threads N`, `-u/--limit-mem SIZE`, `-q/--clamp-low N` and `-Q/--clamp-top N` (examine only a portion of each file from start/end — useful for fast video-prefix matching), `-Z/--mtime-window SECONDS`, `--with-fiemap`/`--without-fiemap`. Repeatable trailing positional `*:path:_files -/`. `rmlint` already in `blacklist.txt` at line 22312; no add needed.
- **sleepenh (Edrusb's high-precision drift-correcting sleep)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flags + positional shape transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/sleepenh/sleepenh.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/sleepenh/sleepenh.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial — 17 already-covered + 2 deferred)**: Lua family hit `lua-language-server`/`fennel`/`hy`/`moonc`/`starlark`/`sky` (the last caught via `more_src/_endless-sky` multi-stem `#compdef` line, another nice round-116 grep win), `tectonic` at `more_src/_tectonic`, `arara`/`mtxrun` at `man_src/`, `tlmgr`/`texdoc` at `src/`; video family hit `HandBrakeCLI` at `more_src/_HandBrakeCLI`, `nq` at `more_src/_nq`; supervisor family hit `s6-svc`/`s6-svscan` at `more_src/`. Deferred: `mkvtoolnix` truly fresh but it's the GUI binary while the actual CLI tools are `mkvmerge`/`mkvinfo`/`mkvextract`/`mkvpropedit` which would need separate per-tool completion writes — left for a future multi-stem round; `daemontools` truly fresh but the actual binaries are `svc`/`svstat`/`svok`/`svscan`/`supervise` — same per-tool fan-out concern. Single-file completion `_sleepenh` covers the high-precision drift-correcting sleep utility — unlike `sleep(1)` which sleeps for a fixed duration, `sleepenh` aligns its wakeup to an absolute timestamp so chained invocations don't accumulate drift, ideal for periodic-task loops where you want exactly N seconds between wakes regardless of how long the work in between took. 3 flags + 2 positionals: meta `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`; `-w/--warp` (adjust returned timestamp so the next sleepenh call is guaranteed to actually sleep — useful when the previous iteration's work overran the budget); positional 1 `INITIALTIME` (defaults to now if omitted), positional 2 `TIMETOSLEEP` (seconds — the canonical use is `T=$(sleepenh $T 1.5)` in a Bash loop to maintain a 1.5-second period). `sleepenh` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 23780 between `sleep` and `slic3r` (sort verified position-by-position: shared `sleep` prefix means `sleep` (length 5) < `sleepenh` (length 8) by shorter-string-wins; at index 2 `sleepenh`'s 'e' (101) < `slic3r`'s 'i' (105)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **gmsh (Christophe Geuzaine & Jean-François Remacle's 3D FEM mesh generator)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/gmsh/gmsh.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/gmsh/gmsh.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (8 already-covered)**: `ngspice` at `more_src/_ngspice`, `openscad`/`freecad` at `more_src/`, `maxima`/`sage`/`scilab` all at `more_src/` (with `maxima-jupyter` multi-stem catching `maxima` via `#compdef`), `dbus-send`/`dbus-monitor` already in blacklist but with no completion file — fresh write candidates but their CLI is mostly D-Bus IPL syntax (interface/method/signature mapping) which doesn't tab-complete cleanly against a static flag set, deferred. Single-file completion `_gmsh` covers the 3D finite-element mesh generator with built-in CAD engine and post-processor — drives both interactive GUI and headless batch meshing, used heavily in academic and industrial FEA workflows. ~40 flags grouped with 2 mutex sets: meta `-help`, `-version`, `-info`; control `-` (parse + exit), `-0` (output model + exit). **Mesh-dimension mutex** `-1` ↔ `-2` ↔ `-3` (1D/2D/3D mesh generation — only one active per invocation). Output: `-o FILE`, `-format` with 22-format enum (`msh`/`msh1`/`msh2`/`msh4`/`stl`/`vrml`/`unv`/`vtk`/`wrl`/`mesh`/`ply`/`ply2`/`bdf`/`cgns`/`med`/`inp`/`diff`/`key`/`x3d`/`ir3`/`mail`/`neu`/`su2`/`p3d`), `-bin` (binary when available), `-saveall` (discard physical groups). Algorithm tuning: `-algo` with 11-value enum (`meshadapt`/`del2d`/`front2d`/`delquad`/`pack`/`initial2d`/`auto`/`del3d`/`front3d`/`mmg3d`/`hxt`/`initial3d`), `-parametric`, `-part N` (partition mesh post-batch), `-smooth N`, `-order 1..5`, `-clscale FLOAT` (global element-size scale), `-clmin`/`-clmax` (size bounds), `-rand FLOAT` (random perturbation), `-bgm FILE` (background-mesh view), `-tol FLOAT` (geometrical tolerance), `-check` (consistency). **Mode-mutex** `-a` ↔ `-g` ↔ `-m` ↔ `-s` ↔ `-p` (automatic/geometry/mesh/solver/post-processing startup mode — 5-flag exclusion group). Display: `-n` (hide meshes/views on startup), `-fontsize`, `-display`. File ops: `-new` (new model before merge), `-merge`/`-open` (next files), `-pid` (PID to stdout), `-listen SOCKET`. Verbosity `-v 0..5,99` (99 = full debug). Scripting: `-nopopup`, repeatable `*-setnumber name value`, `*-setstring name value`, `*-string option`, `*-option FILE` (parse option file). Post-processing: `-combine` (merge views into multi-time-step). Batch convert: `-convert files` (latest formats + exit). Repeatable positional `*:geo/msh/stp/igs/iges/brep/stl file:_files -g "*.(geo|msh|stp|igs|iges|brep|stl|step|opt|pos)"` covers the 10 standard input extensions (geometry, mesh, STEP/IGES/BREP CAD, options, post). `gmsh` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 9545 between `gmplayer` and `gmt` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `gmplayer`'s 'p' (112) < `gmsh`'s 's' (115) < `gmt`'s 't' (116)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **clpeak (Krishnaraj Bhat's accelerator peak-throughput micro-benchmark)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/krrishnarraj/clpeak](https://github.com/krrishnarraj/clpeak) README options table. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (4 already-covered)**: `vulkaninfo` at `more_src/_vulkaninfo`, `mosquitto` at `src/_mosquitto`, `rocm-bandwidth-test`/`rocprof` both already at `more_src/`. Single-file completion `_clpeak` covers the single-binary accelerator peak-throughput micro-benchmark — probes OpenCL/Vulkan/CUDA/Metal back-ends and reports compute/memory/transfer peaks; widely used on Linux + macOS for tuning ML and crypto workloads. 25 flags grouped with 4 backend mutex pairs: meta `--help`, `--list-devices` (enumerate devices + exit). **Backend dispatch (4 mutex pairs)**: `--opencl` ↔ `--no-opencl`, `--vulkan` ↔ `--no-vulkan`, `--cuda` ↔ `--no-cuda`, `--metal` ↔ `--no-metal` — each encoded as a `(--flagA --flagB)` exclusion list so zsh suppresses the dominated half once the user picks one. **Test selection** (6 flags, no mutex — multiple kernels can run in one invocation): `--single-precision-compute` (SP-only compute), `--wmma` (CUDA tensor-core via hand-rolled WMMA), `--cublas` (CUDA vendor-SDK GEMM peak via cuBLASLt), `--simdgroup-matrix` (Apple matrix-engine), `--mps-gemm` (Apple vendor-SDK GEMM via MPS), `--coopmat` (Vulkan tensor-core via VK_KHR_cooperative_matrix). **Device selection** (5 flags — per-backend device index): `--cl-platform N` (OpenCL platform), `--cl-device N`, `--vk-device N` (Vulkan physical device), `--cuda-device N`, `--mtl-device N`. **Output routing** (4 flags): `--xml-file PATH`, `--json-file PATH`, `--csv-file PATH` (three machine-readable formats — can stack since each writes to its own file), `--compare FILE` (compare against baseline JSON via `_files -g "*.json"`). `clpeak` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 4033 between `clpbar` and `clush` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `clpbar`'s 'b' (98) < `clpeak`'s 'e' (101); at index 2 `clpeak`'s 'p' (112) < `clush`'s 'u' (117)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **LIKWID family — likwid-topology/perfctr/pin/powermeter/mpirun/setFrequencies/features/genTopoCfg (8 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/likwid/likwid-topology.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/likwid/likwid-topology.1.en.html) + `likwid-perfctr.1` + `likwid-pin.1` + `likwid-powermeter.1` + `likwid-mpirun.1` + `likwid-setFrequencies.1` (6 source-verified members; `features` and `genTopoCfg` covered as stubs since their man pages were not source-verified this round). Single multi-stem file `_likwid-topology` is `#compdef likwid-topology likwid-perfctr likwid-pin likwid-powermeter likwid-mpirun likwid-setFrequencies likwid-features likwid-genTopoCfg`, dispatching on `$service` — covers RRZE-HPC's performance toolkit for x86/ARM/POWER multi-core systems (the LIKWID = "Like I Knew What I'm Doing" tooling that's a staple of academic HPC perf work). The family shares a common short-flag style across all members: `-c cpu-list` (CPU expression — e.g. `0,1,2` or `0-7` or `S0:0-7@S1:8-15`), `-V`/`--verbose` level (0..3), `-v`/`--version`, `-h`/`--help`. **`likwid-topology`** (CPU/cache/NUMA introspection, 8 flags): `-g` ASCII-art topology (no GPU), `-c/--caches` detailed cache hierarchy, `-C/--clock` measure processor clock, `-G/--gpus` Nvidia GPU info, `-o/--output FILE` with suffix-driven filter (`.csv`/`.xml`/`.json` automatic). **`likwid-perfctr`** (hardware counter measurement, 22 flags + trailing command): mode switches `-m/--marker` (marker-API mode), `-a` (list available perf groups + exit), `-e` (list available counters + events + exit), `-H` (group-help with `-g`), `-i/--info` (cpuid + Intel PMU detail), `--stats` (always print stats table); selection `-c cpu-expr` vs `-C cpu-expr` (latter pins threads), `-g/--group GROUP`, `-G/--gpus gpu-list` + `-W/--gpugroup`, `-E search-str` (filter events by substring); modes `-t freq` (timeline), `-S waittime` (end-to-end via sleep), `-T time` (group-switch interval); output `-o/--output FILE`, `-O` (CSV); plumbing `-s/--skip hex-mask`, `-f/--force` (force register writes); trailing `*:::command:_command_names -e` for the wrapped process. **`likwid-pin`** (NUMA pinning launcher, 11 flags + trailing command): selection `-c`/`-C cpu-expr`, `-p` (print thread domains), `-d delim` (CPU delimiter); NUMA policy mutex `-i` (interleave) ↔ `-m` (membind); cleanup `-S/--sweep` (clean ccNUMA domains pre-run); `-s/--skip hex-mask`, `-q/--quiet`; trailing `*:::command:_command_names -e`. **`likwid-powermeter`** (RAPL-based power + temp readout, 10 flags): `-c socket-list`, `-M 0|1` (MSR access mode — 0=direct, 1=accessDaemon), `-s duration` (units `us`/`ms`/`s`), `-p` (dynamic clocks + CPI per socket), `-i/--info` (TDP + Turbo steps per RAPL domain), temperature mutex `-t` (Celsius) ↔ `-f` (Fahrenheit). **`likwid-mpirun`** (MPI launcher with pinning + counters, 14 flags + trailing command): MPI plumbing `-n`/`-np`/`--n`/`--np N` (4-way alias), `-hostfile FILE`, `-nperdomain N`, `-pin EXPR`, `-mpi intelmpi|openmpi|slurm|mvapich2` (4-value enum), `-omp intel|gnu` (hybrid OMP runtime); marker mode `-m/--marker`; output `-O` (CSV); passthrough `--mpiopts opts`; `-d/--debug`, `-s/--skip hex-mask`, trailing `*:::command:_command_names -e`. **`likwid-setFrequencies`** (CPU governor + freq control, 14 flags): info `-p` (current freqs), `-l` (configurable freqs), `-m` (configurable governors); apply `-c cpu-list`, `-g governor` (6-value enum: `performance`/`powersave`/`ondemand`/`conservative`/`schedutil`/`userspace`), `-f/--freq`, `-x/--min`/`-y/--max` constraints, `-t/--turbo 0|1`; Intel-only Uncore `--umin`/`--umax`, `--reset` (defaults + performance governor), `--ureset` (reset Uncore to max). **`likwid-features`/`likwid-genTopoCfg`** stub coverage (common short-flag conventions; man pages not source-verified this round so only the universal `-h`/`-v`/`-V`/`-c`/`-o` are modeled). **Blacklist additions (8)**: all 8 likwid stems inserted between `ligolo-proxy` (13785) and `lilo` (13786) in upstream-canonical order: `likwid-features` (f=102) < `likwid-genTopoCfg` (g=103) < `likwid-mpirun` (m=109) < `likwid-perfctr` (p,e=112,101) < `likwid-pin` (p,i=112,105) < `likwid-powermeter` (p,o=112,111) < `likwid-setFrequencies` (s=115) < `likwid-topology` (t=116). Sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `ligolo`'s 'g' (103) < `likwid`'s 'k' (107) < `lilo`'s 'l' (108); within the `likwid-*` block, suffix order resolves correctly per the index-7+ table above. LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **bsdtar (libarchive's tar implementation — default tar on macOS + FreeBSD)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/libarchive-tools/bsdtar.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/libarchive-tools/bsdtar.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (8 already-covered)**: `dwz` already at `man_src/_dwz`, `bsdcat`/`bsdcpio` at `more_src/`/`src/`, `eu-readelf`/`eu-stack`/`eu-elflint` all at `src/`, `elfedit` at `src/_elfedit` (and also caught via multi-stem in `src/_arm-linux-gnueabihf-elfedit`'s `#compdef` line — a wonderful example of why the round-116 grep upgrade matters), `lld` already at `more_src/_lld` (with multi-stem covering `lld-link` via the same file's `#compdef`). Single-file completion `_bsdtar` covers libarchive's tar implementation — the default `tar` on macOS and FreeBSD; reads/writes 12+ archive formats (ustar/pax/cpio/zip/iso9660/ar/7zip/xar/mtree/etc) plus all the compression variants (gzip/bzip2/xz/compress/lzma/lzop/lrzip/lz4/zstd). ~80 flags grouped: 5-flag **mode mutex** (`-c`/`-x`/`-t`/`-r`/`-u`); 10-flag **compression mutex** (the 9 `-z`/`-j`/`-J`/`-Z`/`--lzma`/`--lzop`/`--lrzip`/`--lz4`/`--zstd` + auto-detect `-a/--auto-compress` — all exclude each other via a single shared `(-z --gzip ...)` zsh exclusion list); archive io `-f/--file` (- for stdin/stdout), `-b/--block-size N`, `@archive` (chain another archive). Extraction: mutex `-k/--keep-old-files`/`--keep-newer-files`, `-m/--modification-time`, `-O/--to-stdout`, `-U/--unlink-first`, `-p/--preserve-permissions`, `--safe-writes` (atomic temp-file extract), `-S` (sparse extraction). Path/link: `-P/--absolute-paths`, `-L/--dereference`, `-H`, `-h`, `-l/--check-links`, `--one-file-system`. Selection: `-C/--cd DIR`, `-T/--files-from FILE`, `-X/--exclude-from FILE`, repeatable `*--exclude PATTERN` + `*--include PATTERN`, `--exclude-vcs`, `-n/--no-recursion`, `--strip-components N`. Ownership: mutex `--same-owner`/`--no-same-owner`, `-o` (overloaded: ustar format in c/r/u, running uid in x), `--uid`/`--gid`/`--uname`/`--gname` overrides, `--numeric-owner`. **`--format` enum** (12 values: `ustar`/`pax`/`v7`/`cpio`/`shar`/`ar`/`mtree`/`iso9660`/`zip`/`7zip`/`xar`/`raw`), `--posix` (= `--format=pax`). Metadata mutex pairs: `--acls` ↔ `--no-acls`, `--xattrs` ↔ `--no-xattrs`, `--fflags` ↔ `--no-fflags` (BSD chflags). Date filtering: 8-flag time-cmp matrix `--newer`/`--newer-mtime`/`--newer-than`/`--newer-mtime-than` and the 4 `--older*` mirrors. Diagnostics: `-v/--verbose`, `-w/--confirmation/--interactive`, `-q/--fast-read`, `--totals`. Misc: `-s+ pattern` (regex name transform), repeatable `*--options opts` (module-specific behavior), `--use-compress-program PROG` (`_command_names -e`), `--null` (NUL-separated file list), `--passphrase PW`, `-B/--read-full-blocks` (GNU compat no-op). Repeatable trailing positional `*:file:_files` for the file/member list. `bsdtar` already in blacklist line 2550; no add needed.
- **Pelletier go-toml v2 family — tomljson + jsontoml + tomll (3 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flag inventory extracted directly from upstream `cmd/{tomljson,jsontoml,tomll}/main.go` + the shared `internal/cli/cli.go` argv handler on [github.com/pelletier/go-toml](https://github.com/pelletier/go-toml). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (4 already-covered)**: `kaitai-struct-compiler`/`bean-format`/`pt-online-schema-change`/`pt-stalk` all already at `more_src/`. Single multi-stem file `_tomljson` is `#compdef tomljson jsontoml tomll`, dispatching on `$service` — covers Thomas Pelletier's pair of TOML↔JSON converters plus the in-place TOML linter/formatter from the same module. All three share the upstream `internal/cli/cli.go` argv-handling pattern (bare `flag.Parse()` + positional file or stdin), so the only meaningful CLI flag in the entire family is `jsontoml`'s `-use-json-number`. **`tomljson`** (TOML → JSON): `--help`, repeatable `*:TOML file (stdin if omitted):_files -g "*.(toml|tml)"` (covers both the canonical `.toml` and the historic `.tml` extension occasionally seen in older test corpora). **`jsontoml`** (JSON → TOML): `--help`, `-use-json-number` (preserves int/float precision by unmarshaling numbers as `json.Number` instead of `float64` — relevant for TOML round-trips of integer constants that would otherwise lose precision), repeatable `*:JSON file (stdin if omitted):_files -g "*.json"`. **`tomll`** (TOML lint/reformat): `--help`, repeatable `*:TOML file (lint + reformat in-place):_files -g "*.(toml|tml)"` — operates **in-place** by default per upstream `Inplace: true` in `cmd/tomll/main.go`, so positional completion intentionally doesn't gate on `-i`/`--in-place` flags (they don't exist). Blacklist additions (3): `jsontoml` inserted between `jsonschema` (12432) and `jspm` (12433) — sort verified at index 4 `jsonschema`'s 's' (115) < `jsontoml`'s 't' (116); `tomljson` and `tomll` inserted between `toml-sort` (25977) and `tomlq` (25978) — sort verified at index 4 `toml-sort`'s '-' (45) < `tomljson`'s 'j' (106) < `tomll`'s 'l' (108) < `tomlq`'s 'q' (113). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **lziprecover (Antonio Diaz Diaz's data-recovery tool for the lzip format)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/lziprecover/lziprecover.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/lziprecover/lziprecover.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (6 already-covered)**: `lzip`/`plzip` already at `more_src/_lzip` + `more_src/_plzip` (and `lzip-decompressor` covered as multi-stem via `src/_lzip-decompressor` `#compdef` line — caught by the round-116 grep upgrade), `pax` caught at stock zsh, `gron`/`huniq`/`fselect`/`sponge` (via multi-stem `more_src/_moreutils`) all already covered. `clzip`/`pdlzip` (C-only / public-domain lzip variants) truly fresh but share `lzip`'s flag set verbatim — could be added as multi-stem to `_lzip` in a future cleanup round; not a separate write. Single-file completion `_lziprecover` covers the data-recovery tool for the lzip format — repairs single-byte errors, splits multi-member archives, dumps/strips/removes corrupt members, and reproduces zeroed sectors using a known-good reference copy. ~26 flags grouped with 2 mutex sets: meta `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`. Core ops (each with `(...)` mutex against `-q` exclusion via `-v` selection): `-a/--trailing-error` (exit non-zero on trailing data), `-A/--alone-to-lz` (lzma-alone format conversion), `-c/--stdout`, `-d/--decompress`, `-D/--range-decompress N-M` (byte-range stdout dump), `-l/--list` (sizes), `-s/--split` (multi-member explode), `-t/--test` (integrity), `-f/--force`, `-k/--keep`. Repair-specific operations: `-e/--reproduce` (zeroed-sector reproduction via reference copy) + 3-flag tuning sub-group `--lzip-level N\|a\|m[N]` (single level / all / match length) / `--lzip-name NAME` (lzip executable, completes via `_command_names -e`) / `--reference-file FILE` (known-good `.lz` via glob); `-R/--repair` (single small-byte error repair); `-m/--merge` (correct errors using multiple corrupted copies of the same file). Output routing: `-o/--output FILE`, `-i/--ignore-errors`. Member-list operations (each takes a `[list]:d:t` spec — list of member ranges, `d` for damaged, `t` for trailing-data): `--dump SPEC` (dump to stdout), `--remove SPEC` (in-place removal), `--strip SPEC` (copy minus listed members). Verbosity mutex `-q/--quiet` ↔ repeatable `*-v/--verbose` (per upstream: `--quiet` cancels out `--verbose` and `-v -v` gives more detail than a single `-v`). Tolerance: `--loose-trailing` (allow trailing data that looks like a corrupt header). Repeatable positional `*:lzip file:_files -g "*.(lz|tlz|lzma)"` covers the 3 lzip-family extensions (.lz canonical / .tlz tar-bundled / .lzma legacy). `lziprecover` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 15184 between `lzip.lzip` and `lzless` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 4 `lzip.lzip`'s '.' (46) < `lziprecover`'s 'r' (114), and at index 2 `lziprecover`'s 'i' (105) < `lzless`'s 'l' (108)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **zoo (Rahul Dhesi's classic .zoo archiver — Lempel-Ziv based, mid-80s era)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname` + the round-116 `#compdef`-line multi-stem grep). Command + modifier inventory transcribed from [manpages.org/zoo](https://manpages.org/zoo). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round** (5 already-covered + 1 deferred): `hcxdumptool` already at `src/_hcxdumptool`, `hcxpcapngtool`/`hcxhashtool` covered via multi-stem `more_src/_hcxtools` `#compdef` line, `airbase-ng`/`aireplay-ng` already at `src/`; `unarj` (ARJ extraction-only sibling) truly fresh but every man-page mirror returned 404 (`manpages.debian.org/bookworm/arj/unarj.1` dyn-redirect, `manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/unarj.1`, `manpages.org/unarj` no-results) — deferred per the round-106 source-404 precedent. Single-file completion `_zoo` covers the classic .zoo archive format (still used for legacy PD shareware archives) — dual-mode CLI: a 17-letter "expert" single-character command verb or a hyphenated 10-verb "novice" form (`-add`/`-extract`/etc) that mirrors the same operations. 17 expert commands enumerated via `_expert` describe: `a` (add — overwrites if newer), `u` (update — newer-only), `c` (comment), `f` (filter — compress/decompress with M/u/h modifiers), `D` (delete inside archive), `e` (extract basename only), `g` (generate sub-archive — `A`/`q`/`+`/`-`/`=` modifiers), `h`/`H` (short/verbose help), `l`/`L` (short/long list), `P` (pack/squeeze), `T` (fix timestamp), `U` (undelete), `v`/`V` (verbose/full-verbose list), `x` (extract preserving paths). 10 novice commands enumerated via `_novice` describe: `-add`/`-freshen`/`-delete`/`-update`/`-extract`/`-move`/`-print`/`-list`/`-test`/`-comment`. Positional 1 uses `_alternative` to multiplex the two command surfaces — users get both expert and novice candidates in a single tab, with type tags distinguishing them. Positional 2 is the archive file (`_files -g "*.(zoo|ZOO)"` for case-insensitive matching since old DOS-era zoo files often used `.ZOO`). Trailing `*:file inside archive:_files` for the file-list arguments. The ~50 sub-command modifiers (e.g. extract modifiers `N`/`O`/`S`/`o`/`p`/`/`/`d`; list modifiers `a`/`A`/`c`/`C`/`d`/`f`/`g`/`m`/`q`/`v`/`V`/`@`/`/`/`1`/`+`/`-`; etc) are documented in the man page but not modeled here — zoo's modifier syntax concatenates them onto the command letter (e.g. `zoo vC archive` for list with column-aligned output) which doesn't tab-complete cleanly; left for manual entry. `zoo` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 29519 between `zoneminder` and `zookeeper-server` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `zoneminder`'s 'n' (110) < `zoo`'s 'o' (111); within the `zoo*` block, `zoo` (length 3) < `zookeeper-server` (length 16) by shorter-string-wins on shared `zoo` prefix). LC_ALL=C sort verified.

Command + switch inventory transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/arj/arj.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/arj/arj.1.en.html). **Tooling upgrade**: round-115's multi-stem false-positive (`aunpack` etc. flagged FRESH while really covered by `_atool`'s `#compdef` line) triggered an upgraded dup-check helper that now greps `^#compdef.*\<${c}\>` across `src/_*` + `more_src/_*` + `override_src/_*` before declaring a candidate fresh. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round**: `xar` caught at multi-stem (`src/_xar` covers `#compdef xar` already), `lha` caught at stock zsh (`/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_lha`), `wpscan` already at `man_src/_wpscan`; `hashpump` (Brian Wallace's hash-length-extension attack tool) truly fresh but every man-page mirror returned 404 (`manpages.debian.org` dyn-redirect, `manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/hashpump.1`, GitHub repo also 404 since `bwall/HashPump` archived) — deferred per the round-106/108/109/111/113/114 source-404 precedent. Single-file completion `_arj` covers the open-source ARJ archiver — command-letter dispatch (`a`/`x`/`e`/`l`/`t`/etc) with a notorious 100+ switch tail organized in `-h*`/`-j*`/`-2*` prefix blocks. 27 commands enumerated via `_commands` describe: classic archive ops (`a`/`x`/`e`/`l`/`v`/`t`/`p`/`s`), modification (`d`/`f`/`u`/`m`/`n`/`o`/`r`/`g`/`c`/`y`), integrity/recovery (`i`/`q`/`w`), batch (`b`), housekeeping (`k`), join (`j`), plus 3 chapter-mode commands (`ac`/`cc`/`dc`) for chapter-archive workflow. ~25 most-used switches seeded (the upstream 100+ obscure `-hf4`/`-j#3`/`-2x1` long-tail left for power-users to type manually): meta `-help`; basic switches `-c` (skip time-stamp check), repeatable `*-e` (exclude paths), `-f` (freshen existing), repeatable `*-g PASSWORD` (garble password), `-i` (hide progress bar), `-m 0..4` (compression method enum), `-n` (only new files), `-r` (recurse), `-u` (update new + newer), `-v SIZE` (multi-volume), `-w DIR` (work directory), repeatable `*-x PATTERN` (exclude pattern), `-y` (assume yes); shifted `-h*` block — `-hk` (ARJ-PROTECT damage protection), `-hz` (ARJ-SECURITY envelope); shifted `-j*` block — `-jm` (max compression), `-jp` (paged), `-ji` (index file), `-je` (self-extracting), `-jf` (full paths), `-jt` (test temp archive), repeatable `*-jy` (suppress queries everywhere); shifted `-2*` block — `-2d` (DOS compat mode), `-2s` (preserve symlinks — UNIX), repeatable `*-2o` (store/restore ownership — UNIX). Positional 1 is the command letter (via `_commands` describe), positional 2 is the archive file (`_files -g "*.arj"`), and trailing `*: file:_files` for the file-list arguments. `arj` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 1292 between `arize-phoenix` and `arj-register` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `arize-phoenix`'s 'i' (105) < `arj`'s 'j' (106); within the `arj*` block, `arj` (length 3) < `arj-register` (length 12) by shorter-string-wins on shared `arj` prefix). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **vnu (W3C Nu HTML/CSS/SVG validator — Java)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [validator.github.io/validator/docs/vnu.1.html](https://validator.github.io/validator/docs/vnu.1.html) OPTIONS section. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial multi-stem catch)**: hit a beautifully misleading 6-stem false-positive — `aunpack`/`apack`/`als`/`acat`/`adiff`/`arepack` all flagged FRESH because `find -iname "_aunpack"` etc. returned nothing, but the existing `more_src/_atool` is multi-stem `#compdef atool aunpack apack als acat adiff arepack` so all 6 are already covered. **Reinforces the round-104 case-insensitivity finding**: a name-based `find` (even with `-iname`) misses multi-stem coverage entirely — must check the `#compdef` line of existing files to detect this class of dup. Also already-covered: `atool`/`dtrx`/`cmark`/`cmark-gfm`/`htmlhint`. Single-file completion `_vnu` covers the Nu HTML Checker (Java-based — invoked as `java -jar vnu.jar` or the `vnu` wrapper script) — validates URLs/files/directories against W3C schemas for HTML5+/CSS/SVG/XHTML. Sister binaries `vnu-server`/`vnu-client` expose the same checker over HTTP and are out of scope for this completion. ~27 flags grouped with 3 mutex sets: meta `--help`, `--version`. Message filtering: `--asciiquotes` (smart-quote substitution in messages); mutex `--skip-info-messages` ↔ `--errors-only`; `--Werror` (any warning → non-zero exit), `--exit-zero-always` (always 0 even with errors), `--stdout` (errors to stdout not stderr); `--filterfile FILE` (regex pattern file) + repeatable `*--filterpattern REGEX` for selective message suppression. Output format: `--format gnu\|xml\|json\|text` (full upstream enum). **Document-type dispatch mutex** (7-flag exclusion group — only one can be active at a time): `--skip-non-css`/`--css` for CSS-only mode, `--skip-non-svg`/`--svg` for SVG-only mode, `--skip-non-html`/`--html`/`--xml` for parser selection on HTML/XHTML files. Cross-type opt-ins (not in the mutex group since they're additive): `--also-check-css` and `--also-check-svg` for checking CSS+SVG alongside HTML. HTTP fetch: `--user-agent UA-STRING`, repeatable `*--additional-request-header "HEADER: VALUE"` for custom request headers when validating remote URLs. Parser behavior: `--no-langdetect` (skip language detection + `html[lang]` check), `--no-stream` (force buffered instead of streaming parse). Diagnostic: `--verbose` (print file names being checked). Synopsis-only flags reproduced with best-guess arg types: repeatable `*--entities` (extra entity defs) and `--schema URL-OR-FILE` (non-default schema override). Repeatable positional `*:URL/file/directory:_files` covers the three input forms vnu accepts. `vnu` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 27544 between `vnstati` and `void` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 1 both share 'n' (110), so resolves at index 2 — `vnstati`'s 's' (115) < `vnu`'s 'u' (117); and at index 1 `vnu`'s 'n' (110) < `void`'s 'o' (111)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **when (Leon Brocard's tiny personal calendar utility)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags + command codes transcribed from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/when/when.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/when/when.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial — 12 caught + 2 deferred)**: 10 already-covered candidates `tdl`/`icdiff`/`pcre2grep`/`deheader`/`cppman`/`calcurse`/`remind`/`pueue`/`autorestic` plus the round-113-promised `strongswan` — `swanctl` man page sourced cleanly but the **stock zsh** at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_swanctl` already ships a `_swanctl` (and similarly `_ipsec`), so neither can be added; the `strongswan` umbrella binary's own man page returned 404 across `manpages.debian.org` dyn-redirect, `man.archlinux.org/man/strongswan.8.en`, and `wiki.strongswan.org/projects/strongswan/wiki/Strongswanctl` (Anubis access-denied) — deferred per the round-106/108/109/111/113 source-404 precedent. `ts` (task spooler) explored as fresh + in blacklist but skipped because the `ts` binary name collides with the unrelated `moreutils ts` timestamp filter — writing a single `_ts` would mismatch one of the two tools depending on which the user installed; deferred for proper multi-tool detection in a future round. Single-file completion `_when` covers the single-file calendar (default `~/.when/calendar`, one line per event) — long-form-only flags drive rendering and an 8-letter positional command code that can be **combined** (e.g. `when cw` = calendars then weekly items, `when iy` = items then year view). 8 command codes via `_verbs` describe: `i` (default — upcoming items), `c` (last/this/next month calendar grids without items), `e` (open `$EDITOR` on the calendar file), `w`/`m`/`y` (coming week/month/year items), `j` (modified Julian day for date arithmetic), `d` (current date only). ~36 long-form flags grouped with 9 mutex pairs encoded in zsh exclusion sets: meta `--help`/`--version`; window control `--language LANG`, `--future DAYS` (default 14), `--past DAYS` (default -1), `--calendar FILE`, `--editor COMMAND` (default `emacs -nw` — completes via `_command_names -e`); wrap mutex `--wrap COLS` / `--wrap_auto` ↔ `--noowrap_auto` / `--wrap_max COLS`; rows mutex `--rows ROWS` (default 40) / `--rows_auto` ↔ `--norows_auto`; header mutex `--header` ↔ `--noheader`; pager mutex `--paging` ↔ `--nopaging` + `--paging_less_options OPTS`; styling `--filter_accents_on_output`, `--styled_output`, `--styled_output_if_not_tty`, `--calendar_today_style STYLE`, `--items_today_style STYLE`; pre-processing `--prefilter PROGRAM` (`_command_names -e`); date control `--now "Y M D"` (override current date); month rendering mutex `--neighboring_months` ↔ `--noneighboring_months`; week-start mutex `--monday_first` ↔ `--nomonday_first`; Easter `--orthodox_easter`; time format mutex `--ampm` ↔ `--noampm` + `--auto_pm HOUR` (assume PM for hours below X); entry expansion mutex `--literal_only` ↔ `--noliteral_only`. `when` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 27913 between `wheel3.9` and `whence` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `wheel3.9`'s 'e' (101) < `when`'s 'n' (110), and at index 4 `when` ends < `whence`'s 'c' (99) — wait, 'c' is 99 not nothing; correct comparison: `when` length-4 vs `whence` length-6, shared prefix `when`, so `when` < `whence` by shorter-string-wins). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **wg-quick (Jason Donenfeld's WireGuard setup wrapper)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Subcommands transcribed from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/wireguard-tools/wg-quick.8](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/wireguard-tools/wg-quick.8.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round** (8 already-covered): `libreswan`, `headscale`, `tailscale`, `tailscaled`, `openconnect`, `openvpn`, `smbtree` — all already at `more_src/`/`src/`. `strongswan` truly fresh + in blacklist line 24732 but defers to next round to do justice (the upstream strongswan CLI has 12+ subcommands and `--child=` connection lookups across `swanctl`/`ipsec` interactions worth source-verifying carefully). Single-file completion `_wg-quick` covers Jason Donenfeld's `wg(8)` setup wrapper — reads `/etc/wireguard/<INTERFACE>.conf` for Address/DNS/MTU/Table/PreUp/PostUp/PreDown/PostDown/SaveConfig directives, then drives `wg` + `ip(8)` accordingly. 4 verbs via `_verbs` describe: `up` (add and set up interface — Address/MTU/routes/PreUp/PostUp), `down` (tear down — PreDown/PostDown), `save` (save current state into config), `strip` (dump config with wg-quick-only directives stripped — pipe-friendly for `wg setconf`). 1 GNU-style flag: `-h/--help`. **Live-interface helper `_wg_quick_interfaces`** walks `/etc/wireguard/*.conf` with zsh nullglob `(N)`, strips the leading directory and trailing `.conf` via `:t:r` modifier (`/etc/wireguard/wg0.conf` → `wg0`), so the user-visible candidate is the bare interface name — matching how `wg-quick up wg0` is invoked in practice. Positional 2 uses `_alternative` to multiplex two surfaces: config-file path completion (`_files -g "*.conf"`) for absolute paths, plus the live-interface describe for the bare-name shortcut. `wg-quick` already in `blacklist.txt` at line 27890; no add needed.
- **djvumake (Yann Le Cun & Léon Bottou's DjVu chunk assembler)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Chunk inventory transcribed from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/djvulibre-bin/djvumake.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/djvulibre-bin/djvumake.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round** (5 already-covered + 4 fresh-but-no-completion-worthy-surface): `djvused`/`djview`/`djvuextract`/`djvups`/`bcal`/`dasel`/`sshs`/`hck` all caught as already covered. `djvumake` is the only fresh DjVu-family binary with a real CLI surface — the others (`djvm`/`djvmcvt`/`djvudigital`/`djvutoxml`) have known but already-covered completions in `man_src/`/`src/`. Single-file completion `_djvumake` covers the DjVu chunk assembler — builds a `.djvu` file from raw chunk fragments via a non-standard `CHUNKID=value` argument syntax (not GNU-style flags). 13 chunk types in the `_chunks` describe array (with key=value suffix-suppression for tab-completion): `INFO=` (initial info chunk W,H,DPI default 300), `Sjbz=` (JB2 foreground mask), `Smmr=` (MMR/G4 mask), `BG44=` (IW44 background — `file[:N]` for multiple chunks), `BGjp=` (JPEG background), `BG2k=` (JPEG-2000 background), `FGbz=` (foreground colour — file or `{#color[:x,y,w,h]}`), `FG44=`/`FGjp=`/`FG2k=` (foreground colour in IW44/JPEG/JP2 respectively), `INCL=` (DjVu3 include chunk for multipage), `Djbz=` (JB2 shape-dictionary), `PPM=` (emit IW44 BG + FG colour chunks from a PPM). 1 GNU-style flag: `-h`. Positional 1 is the output `.djvu` file (via `_files -g "*.djvu"`), repeatable positional 2+ accept chunk specs. The `_describe -S ''` suffix-suppression keeps zsh from auto-inserting a space after the `=` so the user can immediately continue typing the chunk argument. `djvumake` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 6318 between `djvuextract` and `djvups` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 4 `djvuextract`'s 'e' (101) < `djvumake`'s 'm' (109) < `djvups`'s 'p' (112)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **pngnq (Stuart Coyle's NeuQuant-based PNG palette quantizer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/pngnq/pngnq.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/pngnq/pngnq.1.en.html). **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round** (4 caught + 1 deferred): `apngasm` (animated PNG assembler) explored as fresh but uses non-standard Windows-style slash flags (`/kp` keep-palette, `/kc` keep-color, `/f` invisible-frame) plus undocumented numeric frame-delay positionals — encoded as ad-hoc zsh _arguments couldn't model the slash syntax cleanly, deferred; `apngdis` (animated PNG disassembler) confirmed via man page to have **zero options** (just `filename.png [name-prefix]` positionals) — no completion-worthy surface, deferred; `gif2png` truly fresh but every man-page mirror returned cert/404 errors (`catb.org` TLS-cert-altname-invalid, `gitlab.com/esr/gif2png/raw/master/gif2png.c` 404, `manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/gif2png.1` 404) — deferred per round-106/108 source-404 precedent; `rsvg-convert`/`svgo`/`pup`/`hq` all caught as already covered. Single-file completion `_pngnq` covers the NeuQuant-based PNG palette reducer — maps full-color PNG → 8-bit palette PNG for smaller file size, optional Floyd-Steinberg dithering, default output suffix `-nq8.png`. 10 flags + repeatable positional: meta `-h`, `-V` (version + library versions — the uppercase `-V` distinguishes from the lowercase `-v` verbosity flag, an unusual convention worth honoring); diagnostic `-v` (verbose status messages); safety `-f` (force overwrite existing output); algorithm tuning `-s FACTOR` (NeuQuant sample factor — default 3, higher = faster + less accurate), `-n COLORS` (target palette size — 2..256, default 256), `-Q n\|f` (dither method enum — `n` = no dither/default, `f` = Floyd-Steinberg), `-g GAMMA` (image gamma override — defaults to reading the source PNG's gAMA chunk, falls back 1.0); output routing `-e SUFFIX` (filename suffix override, default `-nq8.png`), `-d DIR` (output directory override — useful for keeping originals untouched); repeatable positional `*:input PNG (stdin if omitted):_files -g "*.png"`. The `-Q n|f` dither enum is encoded as a 2-value choice list so users can't mistype it; sample-factor, color-count, and gamma are left as bare-numeric prompts (no enum) since the upstream-documented "good default" (3) is just a starting hint. `pngnq` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 19993 between `pngfix` and `pngquant` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `pngfix`'s 'f' (102) < `pngnq`'s 'n' (110) < `pngquant`'s 'q' (113)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **jpeginfo (Timo Kokkonen's libjpeg-based info dumper)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/jpeginfo/jpeginfo.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/jpeginfo/jpeginfo.1.en.html). Single-file completion `_jpeginfo` covers the libjpeg-based info/integrity tool — dimensions, JPEG coding, optional MD5/SHA-256/SHA-512 checksums, and `-d`-delete-on-error workflow. 18 flags + repeatable positional: meta `-h/--help`, `--version`; integrity `-c/--check` (scan beyond headers), `-C/--comments` (dump COM markers), `-d/--delete` (delete files with errors), `-m/--mode all\|erronly` (delete-mode selector, only meaningful with `-d`); input source `-f/--file LIST-FILE` (read filenames from file, `-` = stdin), `--stdin` and bare `-` (read image data from stdin); checksums `-2/--sha256`, `--sha512`, `-5/--md5` (three independent hash flags — can stack); detail `-i/--info` (extended: coding/density/CCIR601), `-H/--header` (column header row), `-v/--verbose`; **output-format mutex group** (4 flags, only one valid at a time) `-l/--lsstyle` ↔ `-j/--json` ↔ `-s/--csv` ↔ `-q/--quiet` — encoded as a single `(... ... ... ...)` exclusion list so zsh suppresses the dominated format flags once the user picks one. Repeatable positional `*:jpeg file:_files -g "*.(jpg|jpeg|jpe|jfif|jif)"` covers the 5 historical JPEG file extensions (`.jpg`/`.jpeg` modern + `.jpe` short DOS + `.jfif`/`.jif` raw JFIF). `jpeginfo` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 12368 between `jpegexiforient` and `jpegoptim` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 4 `jpegexiforient`'s 'e' (101) < `jpeginfo`'s 'i' (105) < `jpegoptim`'s 'o' (111)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
