Flag transcribed directly from upstream [g2p/bcache-tools](https://github.com/g2p/bcache-tools) `probe-bcache.c` `getopt(argc, argv, "o:")` call. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round**: tpm2-tools family hunt resolved as `tpm2_seal` truly fresh but every man-page mirror returned 404 (`manpages.debian.org` dyn-redirect → 404, `manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man1/tpm2_seal.1`, `tpm2-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/man/tpm2_seal.1/`, `raw.githubusercontent.com/tpm2-software/tpm2-tools/master/man/tpm2_seal.1.md` — all 404) — deferred per the round-106/108 source-404 precedent. The remaining 10 `tpm2_*` siblings (`tpm2_create`/`tpm2_pcrread`/`tpm2_unseal`/`tpm2_eventlog`/`tpm2_pcrextend`/`tpm2_load`/`tpm2_getrandom`/`tpm2_evictcontrol`/`tpm2_flushcontext`/`tpm2_getcap`) all already covered via `man_src/` auto-generated completions. Also caught `bcache-super-show`, `make-bcache` as already covered (`man_src/`/`src/` respectively). Single-file completion `_probe-bcache` covers the bcache-tools superblock-probe binary used by udev rules to autostart bcache once both cache and backing devices show up in `/dev/`. 1 flag + repeatable positional: `-o FORMAT` (only `udev` is recognized — emits `ID_FS_UUID`/`ID_FS_UUID_ENC`/`ID_FS_TYPE` for udev consumption; without `-o` defaults to a human-readable UUID/TYPE one-liner); positional `*: block device:_files -g "/dev/*"` (repeatable — multiple devices probed in one invocation). The single-format enum is encoded as `(udev)` value table so users get tab-completion of the only valid spelling rather than mistakenly typing variants like `udev-style` or `ID_FS_UUID`. `probe-bcache` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 20503 between `prm` and `probe-rs` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `prm`'s 'm' (109) < `probe-bcache`'s 'o' (111); within the `probe-*` block, index 6 `probe-bcache`'s 'b' (98) < `probe-rs`'s 'r' (114)). - **binbloom (Quarkslab's raw-firmware base-address + endianness finder)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/quarkslab/binbloom](https://github.com/quarkslab/binbloom) README usage table. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round (substantial)**: hit a 7-tool wall of already-covered candidates (toot, bottles, spotdl, beets, gallery-dl, yt-dlp, calibre — all at `more_src/_$tool`) plus 8 stems-already-tracked-as-aliases (rad, erd, erdtree, tre, fossil, pijul, ty, pyrefly), plus `aursync`/`aursearch` were truly fresh but their canonical man-page sources returned 404 across `man.archlinux.org`/`manpages.debian.org`/`raw.githubusercontent.com/aurutils/aurutils/master/man1/aursync.1` (different repo path than the README suggested) — deferred rather than guessed per the round-106 "frida-compile" precedent. Single-file completion `_binbloom` covers Quarkslab's raw-firmware analyzer — auto-detects endianness and the loading base address of an unstructured firmware blob (no ELF/PE headers present), used in embedded-firmware RE before loading into Ghidra/IDA. 7 flags + 1 positional: meta `-h`; arch-detection overrides `-a 32|64` (word-size enum, default 32) and `-e le|be` (force endianness when auto-detect is unreliable); `-b HEX-ADDRESS` (supplied base address, skip auto-detect); performance `-t N` (thread count for parallel address search), `-d` (deep-search mode — slower, finds addresses in rare cases); diagnostic `*-v` (stackable verbosity); positional `1:firmware blob:_files`. All numeric/hex flag positions are uppercase short — encoded as `-a+`/`-b+`/`-e+`/`-t+` (next-word argument) to match the GNU short-arg style binbloom uses. `binbloom` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 2171 between `bin2obj` and `bind9-config` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `bin2obj`'s '2' (50) < `binbloom`'s 'b' (98) < `bind9-config`'s 'd' (100)). - **Joern family — joern + joern-cli + joern-parse + joern-export + joern-scan + joern-slice + joern-flow + joern-vectors + joern-stats (9 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed directly from upstream Scala scopt argument parsers under [joernio/joern](https://github.com/joernio/joern) `joern-cli/src/main/scala/io/joern/joerncli/` — `JoernParse.scala`, `JoernExport.scala`, `JoernScan.scala`, `JoernSlice.scala`, `JoernFlow.scala`, `JoernVectors.scala`. Single multi-stem file `_joern` is `#compdef joern joern-cli joern-parse joern-export joern-scan joern-slice joern-flow joern-vectors joern-stats`, dispatching on `$service` — covers Whitespace Engineering's static code-analysis platform built around CPGs (Code Property Graphs). **`joern`/`joern-cli`** (interactive Scala REPL launcher): `--help`, `--script FILE` (run script instead of REPL, `.sc`/`.scala` glob), `*--param key=value`, `--import-script FILE`, `--server`/`--server-host HOST`/`--server-port PORT` (JSON-RPC server mode), `--nocolors`. **`joern-parse`** (source → CPG): `-o/--output FILE`, `--language` with 14-frontend enum (`c`/`cpp`/`java`/`jvm`/`javasrc`/`go`/`python`/`php`/`csharp`/`kotlin`/`js`/`javascript`/`ruby`/`swift`/`ghidra`), `--list-languages`, `--namespaces NS,NS`, mutex `--nooverlays` ↔ `--overlaysonly`, `--max-num-def N` (data-flow definition limit), trailing frontend-args after `--`. **`joern-export`** (CPG → graph format): `-o/--out DIR`, `--repr` (7-value: ast/cfg/ddg/cdg/pdg/cpg14/cpg/all, default cpg14), `--format` (4-value: dot/neo4jcsv/graphml/graphson). **`joern-scan`** (run query scans): `--overwrite`, `--store`, `--dump`/`--dump-to FILE`, `--list-query-names`, `--updatedb`/`--dbversion`, `--names NAMES,NAMES` and `--tags TAGS,TAGS` query-set filters, `--depth N` interprocedural call depth, `--language`/`--list-languages` (12-frontend enum). **`joern-slice`** (CPG slicing — state-machine sub-tree with 2 modes): common flags `-o/--out`, `--dummy-types`, `--file-filter`/`--method-name-filter`/`--method-parameter-filter`/`--method-annotation-filter` regex filters, `-p/--parallelism N`; **`data-flow` mode** adds `--slice-depth N` (default 20), `--sink-filter REGEX`, `--end-at-external-method`; **`usages` mode** adds `--min-num-calls N` (default 1), `--exclude-operators`, `--exclude-source`. **`joern-flow`** (data-flow query): 3 positionals `src-regex`/`dst-regex`/`cpg-file`, plus `--src-param N`/`--dst-param N`/`--depth N`/`--verbose`. **`joern-vectors`** (CPG → vectors): `-o/--out DIR`, `--features` flag, positional CPG. **`joern-stats`** (minimal flag set — scopt config not exposed): `--help` + CPG positional. **Blacklist additions (9)**: `joern`, `joern-cli`, `joern-export`, `joern-flow`, `joern-parse`, `joern-scan`, `joern-slice`, `joern-stats`, `joern-vectors` inserted between `joe` and `john` — sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `joe`'s 'e' (101) = `joern`'s 'e' (101), so `joe` < `joern` by shorter-string-wins (length 3 < 5); within the `joern-*` block, suffix order resolves as `cli` (c=99) < `export` (e=101) < `flow` (f=102) < `parse` (p=112) < `scan` (sc=115,99) < `slice` (sl=115,108) < `stats` (st=115,116) < `vectors` (v=118); finally at index 2 `joern`'s 'e' (101) < `john`'s 'h' (104). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **apkleaks (dwisiswant0's APK secret scanner)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` via `-iname`). Flags transcribed from upstream [github.com/dwisiswant0/apkleaks](https://github.com/dwisiswant0/apkleaks) README usage table. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log this round**: `entr` (Eric Radman's fs-watcher — ships in stock zsh at `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/_entr`) and `capa` (Mandiant capability matcher — already at `more_src/_capa`) both caught at dup-check before reaching write; `frida-compile` truly fresh but upstream `bin/compile.ts` returned 404 across multiple URL forms so flag inventory couldn't be source-verified — deferred rather than guessed. Single-file completion `_apkleaks` covers the jadx-backed APK static scanner that surfaces URIs, endpoints, and embedded credentials via a JSON-defined regex set. 7 flags: meta `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`; input `-f/--file APK` (completes via `_files -g "*.(apk|xapk|aab)"` covering standard APK + Aurora Store split-APK bundles + Android App Bundles); output `-o/--output FILE` (random tmp file if omitted), `--json` (JSON format instead of plain text); customization `-p/--pattern FILE` (custom regex pattern JSON via `_files -g "*.json"`), `-a/--args STRING` (extra args forwarded to jadx — e.g. `--deobf --log-level DEBUG`). `apkleaks` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 1110 between `apkeep` and `apko` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `apkeep`'s 'e' (101) < `apkleaks`'s 'l' (108) < `apko`'s 'o' (111)). - **shellcraft (pwntools' shellcode generator with hierarchical shellcode catalog)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` — used the upgraded `-iname` (case-insensitive) dup-check this round following last round's macOS HFS+/APFS case-insensitivity gotcha). Flags transcribed directly from upstream [docs.pwntools.com/en/stable/commandline.html](https://docs.pwntools.com/en/stable/commandline.html) options table plus the `pwn` global-flag table. Single-file completion `_shellcraft` covers Gallopsled's standalone `shellcraft` binary (also reachable as `pwn shellcraft <name>`); generates pre-built shellcodes from a hierarchical catalog (architecture `.` os `.` payload — e.g. `amd64.linux.sh`, `i386.linux.dupsh`, `arm.linux.execve`, `aarch64.linux.sh`, `mips.linux.sh`, `thumb.linux.sh`) with per-name argument lists, plus encoding/output transformations applied after generation. ~20 flags grouped: meta `-h/--help`, `-?/--show` (per-name doc), `-l/--list` (catalog browse, optional filter), `--syscalls` (list all syscalls for selected arch+os, exit); output `-o/--out FILE`, `-f/--format` with the full 18-value upstream enum table (`r`/`raw`, `s`/`str`/`string`, `c`, `h`/`hex`, `a`/`asm`/`assembly`, `p`, `i`/`hexii`, `e`/`elf`, `d`/`escaped`, `default`); debug `-d/--debug` (auto-launch GDB), `-b/--before` and `-a/--after` (insert debug-trap markers around emitted code), `--delim` (multi-shellcode separator); bad-byte encoding `-v/--avoid HEX-BYTES`, `-n/--newline`, `-z/--zero` (NULL-byte avoid — the three classic shellcode-encoding constraints); execution `-r/--run` (run the generated shellcode in-process for quick smoke-test), `--address HEX` (load-address override); ELF emission `-s/--shared` (build shared-library ELF instead of raw blob); pwn-global overrides `--arch` (11-value enum `i386`/`amd64`/`arm`/`aarch64`/`thumb`/`mips`/`mipsel`/`mips64`/`mips64el`/`powerpc`/`sparc`), `--os` (5-value enum `linux`/`freebsd`/`windows`/`baremetal`/`android`); color mutex `--color` ↔ `--no-color`. **`_names` catalog** seeds the positional argument with 21 of the most common CTF picks (amd64+i386+arm+aarch64+mips+thumb x sh/execve/dupsh/read/write/open/connect/listen/cat) plus common synthetic `common.{nop,infloop,debugbreak}` — users still tab through arbitrary names since the upstream catalog is much larger than this seed. Positional argument 2+ is the shellcode-specific argument list (e.g. `i386.linux.read 3 0x8048000 100` for read(fd, buf, len)) — left as free-form `*:shellcode argument:` since arg semantics depend entirely on which shellcode is selected. `shellcraft` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 23534 between `shellcheck` and `shellharden` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 5 both `shellcheck` and `shellcraft` share 'c' (99), so resolves at index 6 — `shellcheck`'s 'h' (104) < `shellcraft`'s 'r' (114); and `shellcraft`'s index-5 'c' (99) < `shellharden`'s index-5 'h' (104)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **one_gadget (David Wang's libc "one gadget RCE" finder)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/david942j/one_gadget](https://github.com/david942j/one_gadget) README options table. Single-file completion `_one_gadget` covers the Ruby tool that locates the single-instruction `execve("/bin/sh", NULL, NULL)` shortcut inside a libc image — the bread-and-butter primitive for libc-leak exploits in pwn CTF. **Candidate-hunt dup-skip log**: round 104 fresh-check turned up `ROPgadget` (Jonathan Salwan's ROP gadget finder) which was correctly skipped — existing `more_src/_ROPgadget` (canonical filename `_ropgadget` on disk; macOS HFS+/APFS case-insensitive resolves both spellings) already declares `#compdef ROPgadget ropgadget` with comprehensive flag coverage including `--rawArch`/`--rawMode`/`--rawEndian` capstone enums and `--mipsrop` MIPS-specific dispatch. Note for future fresh-checks: case-insensitive macOS filesystems make `find -name` (case-sensitive) lie about what's already on disk — the file walker resolved the file as `_ropgadget` even though the dup-check name was `_ROPgadget`. 12 flags: meta `-h/--help`, `--version`, `--info BuildID` (show version info for a specific libc BuildID without lookup). Input source: `-b/--build-id BuildID` (SHA1 BuildID lookup against bundled db) versus libc-file positional via `_files -g "*libc*"` — repeatable; mutex `-f/--force-file` ↔ `--no-force-file` controls precedence between file-first vs BuildID-first lookup. Output: `-l/--level 0|1|2|3` (verbosity — higher reveals more conditional/marginal gadgets), `-o/--output-format pretty|raw|json` and `-r/--raw` shorthand (both wired into the same mutex group so zsh suppresses the dominated flag); `-n/--near FUNCTIONS-OR-FILE` orders results by proximity to specified ELF functions or GOT entries (completes via `_files`). Exploit-integration: `-s/--script EXPLOIT-SCRIPT` (execute script per-gadget with the offset as `$1` — heavily used in CTF auto-pwn pipelines); `--base HEX-ADDRESS` (libc base override, default 0). `one_gadget` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 18150 between `ondir` and `oneagent` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `ondir`'s 'd' (100) < `one_gadget`/`oneagent` shared 'e' (101), then at index 3 `one_gadget`'s '_' (95) < `oneagent`'s 'a' (97) — underscore precedes lowercase letters in ASCII so `one_gadget` slots ahead of the `onea*` block). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **pwninit (Andrew Otto's CTF binary-exploit challenge initializer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags extracted directly from upstream [github.com/io12/pwninit](https://github.com/io12/pwninit) `src/opts.rs` (structopt definitions that generate clap args). Single-file completion `_pwninit` covers the auto-detection + patchelf + pwntools-template-emission flow used at the start of every pwn CTF challenge. 11 flags: meta `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`. Auto-detect overrides: `--bin FILE` (challenge binary), `--libc FILE` (challenge libc, completes via `_files -g "*libc*"`), `--ld FILE` (linker for libc preload, completes via `_files -g "*ld*"`). Template customization: `--template-path FILE` (custom pwntools solve-script template, completes via `_files -g "*.py"`), `--template-bin-name NAME` (binary-variable name in emitted template, default `exe`), `--template-libc-name NAME` (default `libc`), `--template-ld-name NAME` (default `ld`). Disable switches: `--no-patch-bin` (skip patchelf), `--no-template` (skip solve-script emission). All file-completion specs use targeted globs — `*libc*` matches the typical CTF naming convention (`libc.so.6`, `libc-2.31.so`, `libc6_2.27-3ubuntu1_amd64.deb`-extracted), `*ld*` matches `ld-linux-x86-64.so.2`/`ld-2.31.so`/etc, `*.py` matches the pwntools template. `pwninit` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 20894 between `pwned` and `pwpolicy` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 `pwned`'s 'e' (101) < `pwninit`'s 'i' (105), and at index 2 `pwninit`'s 'n' (110) < `pwpolicy`'s 'p' (112) so it remains at the end of the `pwn*` block before `pwp*` begins). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **chartmuseum (Helm chart-repository server — Go binary)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [chartmuseum.com/docs/](https://chartmuseum.com/docs/) ("Cache, Multitenancy, Storage Configuration" tables) plus chartmuseum/chartmuseum README. The candidate hunt also turned up `curl-impersonate` as fresh — correctly skipped as a drop-in curl replacement whose flag surface is dominated by 100+ overlapping curl flags (the impersonation-profile binaries `curl_chrome116`/`curl_ff117` etc. expose only the profile name, not new flags). Single-file completion `_chartmuseum` covers ~70 flags (every flag has an equivalent UPPERCASE env var — this completion covers the flag side only). Meta `--help`/`--version`/`--config FILE` (yaml/yml/json/toml glob). Core server: `--port`, `--listen-host` (via `_hosts`), `--debug`, `--log-json` (structured logging), `--log-health`, `--log-latency-integer`, `--read-timeout SECONDS`, `--write-timeout SECONDS`. Auth/security: `--basic-auth-user`/`--basic-auth-pass`, `--auth-anonymous-get` (allow anon GET even with auth set), `--bearer-auth` + `--auth-realm URL` + `--auth-service NAME` + `--auth-cert-path *.pem` + `--auth-actions-search-path JMESPath`, TLS trio `--tls-cert`/`--tls-key`/`--tls-ca-cert`, `--cors-allow-origin`. **`--storage BACKEND` with 10-value enum** (`local`/`amazon`/`google`/`microsoft`/`oracle`/`alibaba`/`baidu`/`tencent`/`openstack`/`etcd`) — covers every cloud-provider object store ChartMuseum supports. Per-backend flag families: local (`--storage-local-rootdir DIR`); AWS S3 (`--storage-amazon-bucket`/`-prefix`/`-region` with 17-region enum aws-east/west/eu/asia/etc/`-endpoint`/`-sse AES256\|aws:kms`); GCS (`-bucket`/`-prefix`); Azure (`-microsoft-container`/`-prefix`); Oracle (`-bucket`/`-prefix`/`-region`/`-compartmentid`); Alibaba OSS (`-bucket`/`-prefix`/`-endpoint`/`-sse AES256\|KMS`); Baidu BOS, Tencent COS (each `-bucket`/`-prefix`/`-endpoint`); OpenStack (`-container`/`-prefix`/`-region`/`-auth v1\|auto`/`-cacert`); etcd (`-endpoint`/`-prefix`/`-cafile`/`-certfile`/`-keyfile`); plus `--storage-timestamp-tolerance DURATION`. Chart management: `--chart-url`, `--chart-post-form-field-name`, `--prov-post-form-field-name`, `--max-upload-size` (default 20971520 = 20 MiB), `--max-storage-objects`, `--allow-overwrite`, `--disable-force-overwrite`, `--disable-delete`, `--disable-api` (read-only mode). Indexing/caching: `--gen-index` (emit-and-exit), `--disable-statefiles`, `--index-limit`, `--cache redis` + `--cache-redis-addr host:port`/`-db`/`-password`, `--cache-interval`. Multitenancy/routing: `--depth`/`--depth-dynamic`, `--context-path`. Monitoring: `--enable-metrics` (Prometheus). `chartmuseum` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 3479 between `chartex` and `chartread` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 5 `chartex`'s 'e' (101) < `chartmuseum`'s 'm' (109) < `chartread`'s 'r' (114)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **kots + kubectl-kots (Replicated kOTS — Kubernetes-Off-The-Shelf, 2 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand tree + per-leaf flag detail extracted from upstream [docs.replicated.com/reference/kots-cli-getting-started](https://docs.replicated.com/reference/kots-cli-getting-started) navigation menu plus per-command pages (`kots-cli-install`, `kots-cli-admin-console-index`, `kots-cli-get-index`). Single multi-stem file `_kots` is `#compdef kots kubectl-kots` because Replicated ships kots as both a standalone binary and a kubectl plugin (the `kubectl-kots` convention auto-attaches it under `kubectl kots …`). 20 top-level subcommands: `install`, `admin-console`, `backup`, `docker`, `download`, `enable-ha`, `get`, `identity-service`, `pull`, `remove`, `reset-password`, `reset-tls`, `restore`, `set`, `upload`, `upstream`, `velero`, `version`, `completion`, `help`. 4 shared persistent flags via `_global`: `-h/--help`, `-n/--namespace NS` (Admin Console namespace), `--kubeconfig FILE`, `--context CONTEXT`. **`install`** carries 35 flags (largest single subcommand in the repo): air-gap (`--airgap`, `--airgap-bundle` with `.tar`/`.tgz` glob), labels/annotations (`--additional-annotations`, `--additional-labels`), config (`--app-version-label`, `--config-values *.yaml`, `--license-file *.yaml`, `--local-path` for local-render test, `--name`), networking (`--copy-proxy-env`, `--http-proxy`, `--https-proxy`, `--no-proxy`, `--port` default 8800, `--no-port-forward`), registry (`--disable-image-push`, `--kotsadm-namespace`, `--kotsadm-registry host/ns`, `--registry-username`, `--registry-password`, `--repo helm-uri`), security (`--ensure-rbac`, `--private-ca-configmap`, `--shared-password`, `--strict-security-context`, `--use-minimal-rbac`, `--skip-rbac-check`, `--skip-registry-check`, `--skip-compatibility-check`, `--skip-preflights`), storage (`--with-minio`, `--storage-class`), timeouts (`--preflights-wait-duration`, `--wait-duration`). **`admin-console` state machine** (5 sub-sub: `garbage-collect-images`/`generate-manifests`/`push-images`/`upgrade` plus the implicit port-forward variant), with `push-images` carrying `--kotsadm-registry`+`--registry-username`/`--registry-password`+ images-bundle positional, `generate-manifests` carrying `--rootdir`/`--shared-password`/`--with-minio`. **`get` state machine** (5 leaves: `apps`/`backups`/`config`/`restores`/`versions`), each with `-o/--output json`; `config`/`versions` add `--decrypt` and require an app-slug positional. **`set` state machine** (`config`/`license`) targeting an app-slug positional. **`pull`/`upload`/`download`** triplet for source-bundle round-tripping with `--rootdir`/`--source`/`--dest` and `--airgap-bundle` air-gap variant. **`backup`/`restore`** with `--wait` flags and Velero/`--velero-namespace` integration. **`upstream` state machine** (`upgrade`/`download`) with airgap-bundle + `--deploy` flags. **`velero` state machine** (8 store-config variants: `configure-aws-s3`/`configure-other-s3`/`configure-azure`/`configure-gcs`/`configure-nfs`/`configure-internal` plus `ensure-permissions` and `print-fs-instructions`). **`identity-service` state machine** (`configure`/`enable-shared-password`). **`docker ensure-secret`** with `--dockerhub-username`/`--dockerhub-password`. **Live-resource helper `_kots_apps`** runs `kots get apps 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | awk '{print $1}'` for installed app-slug positionals across `remove`/`set config`/`set license`/`download`/`upload`/`upstream upgrade`/`upstream download`/`get config`/`get versions`. **Blacklist additions (2)**: `kots` between `kotlinc-jvm` (13024) and `koyeb` (13025) — sort verified at index 3 `kotlinc-jvm`'s 'l' (108) < `kots`'s 's' (115), and at index 2 `kots`'s 't' (116) < `koyeb`'s 'y' (121); `kubectl-kots` between `kubectl-karmada` (13261) and `kubectl-krew` (13262) — sort verified at index 9 `kubectl-karmada`'s 'a' (97) < `kubectl-kots`'s 'o' (111) < `kubectl-krew`'s 'r' (114). - **kp (kpack-cli — K8s-native OCI image builder using Cloud Native Buildpacks)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand inventory + per-leaf flag detail extracted from upstream [buildpacks-community/kpack-cli](https://github.com/buildpacks-community/kpack-cli) `docs/kp.md` plus per-subcommand pages (`docs/kp_image.md`, `docs/kp_build.md`, `docs/kp_secret.md`, `docs/kp_config.md`) generated from the live cobra command tree by `go run cmd/docs/main.go`. The repo also turned up a `cr` candidate (Helm chart-releaser) in candidate hunt — correctly skipped because the existing `more_src/_cr` already covers it; `chart-releaser` itself isn't shipped as a separate binary by upstream. Single-file completion `_kp` covers VMware Tanzu's kpack CLI — a K8s-native image builder driven by Cloud Native Buildpacks specs. 14 top-level subcommands via `_subcmds`: `build`, `builder`, `buildpack`, `clusterbuilder`, `clusterbuildpack`, `clusterlifecycle`, `clusterstack`, `clusterstore`, `completion`, `config`, `image`, `import`, `secret`, `version`, `help`. 6 shared persistent flags via `_global` re-applied per leaf: `-h/--help`, `--kubeconfig FILE`, `--context CONTEXT`, `-n/--namespace NS`, `--registry-ca-cert-path FILE`, `--registry-verify-certs`. **Shared 6-action CRD pattern** `_crd_actions` (create/delete/list/patch/save/status) reused across builder/buildpack/clusterbuilder/clusterbuildpack/clusterlifecycle/clusterstack/clusterstore — single shared array means a future kpack API addition (e.g. a new clusterlifecycle action) cascades to all CRD-style subtrees. **Per-subtree specialization**: `image` adds a 7th action `trigger` (force a new build), with `create`/`save` carrying 16 flags (source-trio mutex via `--git`/`--blob`/`--local-path` plus `--git-revision`, `--registry`, `--cluster-builder`/`--builder`, `--service-account`, `--sub-path`, repeatable `*--env key=value` + `*--delete-env`, repeatable `*--cache-size`, `--additional-tag`, `--wait`, `--dry-run`, `--output yaml|json`); `build` collapses to 3 actions (list/logs/status) and `logs` adds `--build N`, `-f/--follow`, `--timestamps`; `secret create` carries auth mixins for DockerHub (`--dockerhub user`), GCR (`--gcr file`), generic registry (`--registry url`/`--registry-user user`), git SSH (`--git-ssh-key file`) + git HTTPS (`--git-user user`), with `--service-account` attachment; `config` is a 2-key K/V tree (`default-repository` and `default-service-account`); `import` covers `-f/--filename FILE`, `--show-changes` (dry-run-style preview), `--force` (overwrite); `completion` takes bash/fish/powershell/zsh. **Live-resource helper `_kp_resources`** runs `kubectl get <CRD-fully-qualified-kind> -o name` per CRD family — `images.kpack.io`, `builders.kpack.io`, `clusterbuilders.kpack.io`, `clusterbuildpacks.kpack.io`, `clusterlifecycles.kpack.io`, `clusterstacks.kpack.io`, `clusterstores.kpack.io`, `buildpacks.kpack.io` — and strips the `<kind>/` prefix so resource-name positionals auto-complete from the live cluster. **Blacklist additions (2)**: `kp` and `kpack` inserted between `koyeb` and `kpackagetool5` (sort verified: at index 1 'o' < 'p', then shorter-string-wins gives `kp` (length 2) < `kpack` (length 5) < `kpackagetool5` (length 13)). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **Two TUI/GTK file managers in one round — clex (Vlado Potisk's ncurses FM) + spacefm (Ignorantguru's PCManFM fork)** (both dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — **clex** flags transcribed from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/clex/clex.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/clex/clex.1.en.html); **spacefm** flags from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/spacefm/spacefm.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/spacefm/spacefm.1.en.html). **`_clex`** (3 flags, behaviour entirely keymap-driven inside the TUI): `--help` and `--version` (both marked `(- *)` mutex so zsh suppresses further suggestions after either is typed), `--log=LOGFILE` (append session log; completes via `_files`). **`_spacefm`** (19 flags + repeatable folder positional): tab/window-target mutex group `-t/--new-tab` ↔ `-r/--reuse-tab` ↔ `-w/--new-window` (default `-t`), `-n/--no-saved-tabs`, `-p/--panel N` (N=1..4 enum) for SpaceFM's multi-panel feature; desktop manager `--no-desktop` (compat no-op), `--desktop` (launch daemon), `--desktop-pref [N]` (preferences dialog, optional tab number); daemon + config `-d/--daemon-mode`, `-c/--config-dir DIR` (via `_files -/`); features `-f/--find-files` (search dialog), `--set-wallpaper FILE` (completes via image glob `_files -g "*.(jpg|jpeg|png|gif|bmp|svg|webp|tif|tiff)"`); IPC `-g/--dialog` (custom dialog spec, see help), `-s/--socket-cmd` (send socket command); compat `--profile PROFILE` (no-op); X11 `--display`; meta `-h/--help`, `--version`. The man-page has overlapping `-p` listings for `--panel` and `--profile` — encoded `-p` as the active `--panel` meaning since profile is documented as compat-only. Trailing repeatable positional `*:folder:_files -/` for paths to open. **Blacklist additions (2)**: `clex` inserted at line 3922 between `clevis-luks-unlock` and `cli` (sort verified: at index 3 `clevis`'s 'v' (118) < `clex`'s 'x' (120), and at index 2 `clex`'s 'e' (101) < `cli`'s 'i' (105)); `spacefm` inserted at line 24110 between `spacectl` and `spacelift` (sort verified: at index 5 `spacectl`'s 'c' (99) < `spacefm`'s 'f' (102) < `spacelift`'s 'l' (108)). A round-99 fresh-check turn-up of `clojure` was correctly skipped because the existing `src/_clj` already declares `#compdef clj clojure` — multi-stem coverage is invisible to a name-based grep for `_clojure`.
- **ptw (pytest-watch — fs-watch-and-rerun harness for pytest)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/joeyespo/pytest-watch](https://github.com/joeyespo/pytest-watch) README options table. Single-file completion `_ptw` covers Joe Esposito's pytest-watch — watches the filesystem and re-runs pytest on change. 19 flags total; unknown extra args pass through to pytest. Meta `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`. Watcher filtering: repeatable `*--ignore DIR` (file-set walker excludes), `--ext exts` (comma-separated `.py`/`*.pyx`/etc that trigger re-runs — default `.py`, `*` allows any), `--config FILE` (override implicit config-file lookup). Trigger control: `-c/--clear` (clear screen before each run), `-n/--nobeep`, `-w/--wait` (let a run finish before re-triggering — opposed to default-interrupt-on-fs-event), `--spool ms` (re-run delay default 200ms — lets fs events queue up), `-p/--poll` (polling instead of inotify-style events — useful in VMs/Docker bind mounts). Hook commands (each completes via `_command_names -e`): `--beforerun` (pre-run), `--afterrun` (post-run; gets pytest exit code as `$1`), `--onpass`, `--onfail`, `--onexit`, `--runner` (override the default `pytest` command). Debugger: `--pdb` (start pdb on errors, also implies `--wait` to prevent pdb interruption). Verbosity mutex `-q/--quiet` ↔ `-v/--verbose` (with `--quiet` taking precedence per upstream — encoded as a single mutex group so zsh suppresses the dominated flag once the user has selected). Trailing `*:pytest path or pytest arg:_files` passes through to pytest's own argument parser. `ptw` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 20750 between `ptunnel` and `ptweens` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 2 `ptunnel`'s 'u' (117) < `ptw`'s 'w' (119), and at index 3 `ptw` ends while `ptweens`'s 'e' (101) is present — shorter-string-wins gives `ptw` < `ptweens`). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **hiawatha (Hugo Leisink's secure-by-default web server)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags extracted directly from upstream `hiawatha.c` `main()` argument parser on [gitlab.com/hsleisink/hiawatha](https://gitlab.com/hsleisink/hiawatha/-/raw/master/src/hiawatha.c) (no kingpin/getopt — hand-rolled C argv scanner). Single-file completion `_hiawatha` covers the secure-by-default forking web server. Defaults: daemonize + read from compiled `$CONFIG_DIR` (usually `/etc/hiawatha`). 7 flags total — almost all server behavior is config-driven (see virtual-host + reverse-proxy directives in `hiawatha.conf`), so the CLI surface is intentionally minimal: meta `-h` (help), `-v` (version + copyright), `-m` (list enabled modules and exit); validation/inspection `-k` (config-validate-and-exit — does not start the server), `-s` (sandbox info, only built when compiled with `ENABLE_SANDBOX`); config dir `-c DIR` (overrides compiled `$CONFIG_DIR`, completes via `_files -/`); foreground `-d` (do not daemonize — useful for systemd unit `Type=simple` or container PID 1). All quit-after-printing flags (`-h`/`-v`/`-m`/`-s`/`-k`) marked with `(- *)` mutex so zsh suppresses follow-up flag suggestions. `hiawatha` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 10861 between `hh` and `hid2hci` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 1 `hh`'s 'h' (104) < `hiawatha`'s 'i' (105), and at index 2 `hiawatha`'s 'a' (97) < `hid2hci`'s 'd' (100) — so `hiawatha` is the lexicographically-first `hi*` entry, preceded only by the `hh*` block). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **byzanz family — byzanz-record + byzanz-playback (2 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/byzanz/byzanz-record.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/byzanz/byzanz-record.1.en.html) + sibling `byzanz-playback.1`. Single multi-stem file `_byzanz-record` is `#compdef byzanz-record byzanz-playback`, dispatching on `$service` — covers Benjamin Otte's GNOME-based X11 screencast recorder + the debug-recording-to-final-format converter. **byzanz-record** (14 flags + 1 positional): GTK help trio `-?/--help`, `--help-all`, `--help-gtk`; capture pipeline `-a/--audio` (only honored when output format supports audio), `-c/--cursor` (capture mouse pointer); timing `-d/--duration SECONDS` (default 10), `--delay SECONDS` (default 1); subprocess `-e/--exec COMMAND` (record until command exits — completes via `_command_names -e`); X11 `--display DISPLAY`; recording rectangle `-x/--x`, `-y/--y`, `-w/--width`, `-h/--height` (all pixels); output `-v/--verbose`; positional output file via `_files -g "*.(gif|byzanz|flv|ogv|ogg|webm)"` (format inferred by extension — `.gif` for the canonical animated-GIF case, `.byzanz` for the debug-recording format consumed by `byzanz-playback`, `.flv`/`.ogv`/`.ogg`/`.webm` for muxed audio+video). **byzanz-playback** (1 flag + 2 positionals): minimal — `-h/--help`, positional 1 byzanz debug recording (`_files -g "*.byzanz"`), positional 2 output file (format inferred — `_files -g "*.(gif|flv|ogv|ogg|webm)"`). Multi-stem keeps both binaries in one file because they share the same package and the playback side has too few flags to justify its own file. Blacklist additions (2): `byzanz-playback`, `byzanz-record` inserted between `bytebase` and `byzanz-window` (sort verified: at index 2 't' < 'z', then suffix order `playback < record < window` by index-7 'p' < 'r' < 'w'). - **ssh-vault (Nicolas Bigaouette's SSH-key asymmetric encryption tool)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [github.com/ssh-vault/ssh-vault](https://github.com/ssh-vault/ssh-vault) `cmd/ssh-vault/main.go` (Go std `flag` package — no kingpin/cobra here). Verified `ssh-vault.com` for subcommand naming. Single-file completion `_ssh-vault` covers the small-secret asymmetric-encryption flow: encrypt to a SSH public key (file path or live GitHub-user lookup) and decrypt with the corresponding private key. 5 flags + 2 positionals: `-h` (help), `-v` (version), `-f` (boolean — create using key matching fingerprint, OR print fingerprint when fingerprint subcommand selected), `-k KEY-OR-INDEX` (public ssh key file path or numerical index when `-u` returns multiple keys; default `~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub`; completes via `_files -g "*.pub"`), `-o FILE` (output file — only honored by `view` subcommand), `-u USER` (GitHub username or URL — fetches all that user's published pubkeys). 8 subcommand aliases via `_ops` describe (4 long + 4 single-letter short): `create`/`c`, `edit`/`e`, `view`/`v`, `fingerprint`/`f`. Positional 1 = operation (via `_ops`), positional 2 = vault file (`_files -g "*.(vault|sshv)"` for the common vault extensions). `ssh-vault` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 24388 between `ssh-mitm` and `ssh3` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 '-' = '-', then index 4 'm' (109) < 'v' (118), and ssh-vault's index-3 '-' (45) < ssh3's index-3 '3' (51) preserves the `ssh-*` block before the `ssh3`-onward block). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **in-toto family completion — verify + sign + mock + match-products (4 binary stems via single multi-stem file)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flag inventory extracted directly from upstream [in-toto/in-toto](https://github.com/in-toto/in-toto) Python argparse sources: `in_toto/in_toto_verify.py`, `in_toto/in_toto_sign.py`, `in_toto/in_toto_mock.py`, `in_toto/in_toto_match_products.py`, plus `in_toto/common_args.py` (shared `VERBOSE_ARGS`/`QUIET_ARGS`/`GPG_HOME_ARGS`/`DSSE_ARGS`/`LSTRIP_PATHS_ARGS`) and `pyproject.toml` `[project.scripts]` entry-points table (which confirmed `in-toto-keygen` is no longer an upstream binary — Python source removed from develop branch — so the candidate-hunt fresh hit for `in-toto-keygen` was correctly skipped). Single multi-stem file `_in-toto-verify` is `#compdef in-toto-verify in-toto-sign in-toto-mock in-toto-match-products`, dispatching on `$service`. Complements the pre-existing `_in-toto-run` and `_in-toto-record` files (the other 2 upstream entry-points). Shared `_shared` array (4 flags re-applied in every stem branch): `--version`, mutex `-v/--verbose` ↔ `-q/--quiet`, `-h/--help`. Per-stem flag coverage: **in-toto-verify** (`-l/--layout LAYOUT` root layout, repeatable `*--verification-keys FILE` for rsa/ed25519/ecdsa key formats, repeatable `*-g/--gpg KEYID` for GPG keyring lookups, `--gpg-home DIR`, `--link-dir DIR` for step link metadata, default `.`, `--inspection-timeout SECONDS` max subprocess timeout); **in-toto-sign** (`-f/--file FILE` link/layout to operate on, repeatable `*-k/--key FILE` for PEM/PKCS8, `-p/--prompt` for decryption-password TTY prompt, repeatable `*-g/--gpg KEYID`, `--gpg-home DIR`, `-o/--output FILE`, `-a/--append` to add signatures instead of replacing, `--verify` mode-switch to verify-using-key-or-gpg instead of sign); **in-toto-mock** (`-n/--name NAME` for `<name>.link` output filename, `--use-dsse` experimental DSSE metadata generation, trailing `*::command and args:_command_names -e` for the wrapped subprocess); **in-toto-match-products** (`-l/--link FILE` link metadata to match against, repeatable `*-p/--paths PATH` local artifact paths defaulting to cwd, repeatable `*-e/--exclude PATTERN` gitignore-style filters, repeatable `*--lstrip-paths PREFIX` prefix-strip controls). Blacklist additions (4): `in-toto-match-products`, `in-toto-mock`, `in-toto-sign`, `in-toto-verify` inserted between `in-cli` and `in.tftpd` to preserve sort (LC_ALL=C verified position-by-position: at index 2 '-' < '.', then suffix order match < mock < record < run < sign < verify). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **witness (in-toto / TestifySec supply-chain attestation collector)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand + flag inventory extracted directly from upstream [in-toto/witness](https://github.com/in-toto/witness) Cobra command tree: `cmd/root.go` (subcommand registry + `ro.AddFlags`), `options/root.go` (4 persistent flags), `options/run.go` (15 flags), `options/sign.go` (4 + signer/KMS-provider mixins), `cmd/verify.go` (17 verifier flags). Single-file completion `_witness` covers the modern Cobra CLI used in CI/CD pipelines to attest builds (SLSA Provenance v1, sigstore artifact metadata, SBOM/VEX/SARIF, GitHub/GitLab/Jenkins runtime claims, AWS IID + GCP IIT, lockfile + system-packages snapshots) with sigstore/fulcio signing, RFC 3161 timestamping, and Archivista storage. 8 top-level subcommands via `_subcmds`: `run` (root operation — exec subprocess + collect attestations + sign envelope), `sign` (sign arbitrary policy/data file), `verify` (verify a witness-policy-signed artifact), `attestors` (list/info/schema/subjects/run-type sub-tree), `policy` (generate/validate/sign/verify/check sub-tree), `completion` (bash/fish/powershell/zsh), `version`, `help`. 5 persistent root flags via `_global`: `-c/--config FILE` (witness config yaml/json), `-l/--log-level debug|info|warn|error`, `--debug-cpu-profile-file FILE`, `--debug-mem-profile-file FILE`, `-h/--help`. **Shared signer-flag array `_signer`** reused across `sign` and `run` (13 flags): key+cert (`--signer-key-path`, `--signer-cert-path`, `--signer-intermediate-paths`), Fulcio (`--signer-fulcio-url`, `--signer-fulcio-token`, `--signer-fulcio-token-issuer-url`, `--signer-fulcio-token-client-id`, `--signer-fulcio-token-grant-flow device-code|authcode`), KMS (`--signer-kms-ref awskms:/...|gcpkms:/...`, `--signer-kms-region`, `--signer-kms-hash-type SHA256|SHA384|SHA512`), TUF (`--signer-tuf-mirror`, `--signer-tuf-root`). **Attestor catalog `_attestors`** (21 attestor names + one-line descriptions, sourced from upstream `attestation/` package): `aws-iid`, `commandrun`, `environment`, `gcp-iit`, `git`, `github`, `gitlab`, `jenkins`, `jwt`, `lockfile`, `material` (auto), `maven`, `oci`, `product` (auto), `sarif`, `sbom`, `secretscan`, `slsa`, `sigstore`, `system-packages`, `vex`. **Per-subcommand handling**: `run` covers `-d/--workingdir DIR`, repeatable `*-a/--attestations attestor` (using `_attestors` describe), repeatable `*--dirhash-glob`, repeatable `*--hashes sha256|sha384|sha512|sha1|md5`, `-o/--outfile`, `-s/--step`, `-r/--trace`, repeatable `*-t/--timestamp-servers URL`, env-sensitive controls (`--env-filter-sensitive-vars` for filter-vs-obfuscate, `--env-disable-default-sensitive-vars`, repeatable `*--env-add-sensitive-key key|glob`, repeatable `*--env-allow-sensitive-key key` no-globs), Archivista trio (`--enable-archivista`, `--archivista-server URL` default `https://archivista.testifysec.io`, repeatable `*--archivista-headers`), and trailing `*::command and args:_command_names -e` so the wrapped subprocess autocompletes against `$PATH`. `sign` covers `-t/--datatype URI` (default `https://witness.testifysec.com/policy/v0.1`), `-o/--outfile`, `-f/--infile`, repeatable `*--timestamp-servers`. `verify` covers `--policy-ca` (deprecated) + `*--policy-ca-root`/`*--policy-ca-intermediate`, `-p/--policy FILE`, `-k/--publickey FILE`, repeatable `*-a/--attestations FILE`, `-f/--artifactfile FILE`, `-d/--artifactdirectory DIR`, repeatable `*--subjects DIGEST`, repeatable `*--policy-timestamp-server`, cert constraint group (`--policy-common-name CN`, repeatable `*--policy-dns-name`, `*--policy-email`, `*--policy-organization`, `*--policy-uri`, `*--policy-fulcio-cert-extension OID=value`), Archivista pair. `attestors` state machine: `list`/`info`/`schema`/`subjects`/`run-type` with attestor positional from `_attestors`. `policy` state machine: `generate`/`validate`/`sign`/`verify`/`check` with `-o/--outfile`/`-f/--infile`. `completion` takes `bash`/`fish`/`powershell`/`zsh`. `witness` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 27989 between `withsctp` and `witnessme` (sort verified position-by-position: at index 3 'h' < 'n' and at index 7 end-of-string < 'm').
- **vnstat (Teemu Toivola's network statistics monitor)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/vnstat/vnstat.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/vnstat/vnstat.1.en.html). Single-file completion `_vnstat` for the per-interface byte/packet counter that persists to a local sqlite-style db. 36 flags grouped: meta `-?/--help`, `--longhelp` (full options list), `-v/--version`, `-D/--debug`; selection `-i/--iface IFACE` (with live `_net_interfaces`), `-q/--query` (force query mode), `-s/--short`; time-resolution stats `-5/--fiveminutes [N]` (5-min stats for last N hours), `-h/--hours [N]`, `-hg/--hoursgraph` (24-hour bar graph), `-d/--days [N]`, `-m/--months [N]`, `-y/--years [N]`, `-t/--top [N]` (all-time top traffic days); range filtering `-b/--begin DATE`, `-e/--end DATE`; live mode `-l/--live [mode 0\|1\|2]`; on-demand calc `-tr/--traffic [SECONDS]` (measure traffic over next N sec); units `-ru/--rateunit [mode 0\|1]` (swap configured rate unit); db management `--add`, `--remove`, `--rename NAME`, `--setalias ALIAS`; listing `--dbiflist [mode 0\|1]`, `--iflist [mode 0\|1]`; config `--config FILE`, `--dbdir DIR`, `--showconfig`, `--style 0..5`, `--locale LOC` (e.g. C, en_US.UTF-8), `--limit N` (max list entries); machine-readable `--oneline [mode b]`, `--json [mode a\|d\|f\|h\|m\|s\|t\|y]`, `--xml [mode a\|d\|f\|h\|m\|s\|t\|y]`; alerts `--alert spec` (output exit type condition limit unit — free-form). 2-letter short flag handling (`-hg`, `-ru`, `-tr`) encoded as separate `_arguments` entries with explicit mutex parentheses against their long equivalents so zsh's option parser handles both the fused (`-hg`) and split (`--hoursgraph`) forms correctly. `vnstat` already in blacklist line 27507 (sibling `vnstatd` daemon at 27508 and `vnstati` image variant at 27509 are separate binaries with their own flag sets, not covered by this completion); no add needed.
- **cppcheck (Daniel Marjamäki's C/C++ static analyzer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/cppcheck/cppcheck.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/cppcheck/cppcheck.1.en.html) plus the cppcheck-2.10 reference. Single-file completion `_cppcheck` covers the largest open-source C/C++ static analyzer. ~50 flags grouped: meta `-h/--help`, `--version`, `--errorlist` (dump every possible diagnostic id as XML); analysis modes `--check-config` (config-only run), `--check-library` (incomplete-library reports), `--check-level reduced|normal|exhaustive`, `--inconclusive`; **repeatable** `*--enable=` / `*--disable=` over check groups `all`/`warning`/`style`/`performance`/`portability`/`information`/`unusedFunction`/`missingInclude`; preprocessor repeatable `*-D macro` / `*-U macro`; language `--std c89|c99|c11|c17|c23|c++03|c++11|c++14|c++17|c++20|c++23`, `--language c|c++`, `--platform unix32|unix64|win32A|win32W|win64|native|unspecified`; includes repeatable `*-I dir`, `--includes-file FILE`, repeatable `*--include FILE`, repeatable `*-i path`; libraries `*--library` with full upstream value table (`std`/`posix`/`gnu`/`microsoft_sal`/`microsoft_atl`/`microsoft_unittest`/`boost`/`qt`/`python`/`sdl`/`gtk`/`wxwidgets`/`opengl`/`openssl`/`windows`/`zlib`/`bsd`/`libcerror`/`libcurl`/`libstdcpp`/`libsigc++`/`ruby`/`tinyxml2`/`emscripten`/`googletest`/`catch2`); output `--template` (simple/gcc/vs/edit/daca2/cppcheck1), `--template-location`, `--xml`, `--xml-version 2`, `--output-file FILE`, `-q/--quiet`, `-v/--verbose`, `--report-progress`, `--error-exitcode N`; suppressions repeatable `*--suppress id:file:line`, `--suppressions-list FILE`, `--suppress-xml FILE`, `--inline-suppr`, `--exitcode-suppressions FILE`; parallelism `-j N`, `-l LOAD`, `--max-configs N` (default 12), `--max-ctu-depth N` (default 2), `--max-template-recursion N`; input `--file-list FILE`, `--force`; config exclusion `--config-exclude PATH`, `--config-excludes-file FILE`; rules repeatable `*--rule REGEX`, repeatable `*--rule-file XMLFILE`; addons repeatable `*--addon misra|threadsafety|y2038|cert|naming`, repeatable `*--addon-python` (interpreter completed via `_command_names -e`); Clang interop `--plist-output DIR`, `--cppcheck-build-dir DIR` (whole-program cache); project `*--project FILE` (Visual Studio `.sln`/`.vcxproj` or CMake `compile_commands.json` via `_files -g "*.(sln|vcxproj|json|cppcheck)"`), `--project-configuration NAME`; debug `--debug-warnings`, `--debug-simplified`, `--debug-symdb`, `--showtime file|file-total|summary|top5|top5_file|top5_summary`; analysis modes `--bug-hunting` (premium), `--safety`; positional `*:source file or directory:_files`. `cppcheck` already in blacklist line 4582 (between `cpp-9` and `cppcheck-htmlreport` neighborhood); no add needed.

- **flutter (Google's cross-platform UI toolkit CLI)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand tree + per-command flag inventory extracted from upstream [docs.flutter.dev/reference/flutter-cli](https://docs.flutter.dev/reference/flutter-cli) (top-level reference + per-command pages). Single-file completion `_flutter` covers the largest mobile-dev CLI in the repo — 30 top-level subcommands, full build-target dispatch, pub sub-dispatch, custom-devices sub-dispatch, and live device discovery. 30 top-level subcommands: `analyze`, `assemble`, `attach`, `bash-completion`, `build`, `channel`, `clean`, `config`, `create`, `custom-devices`, `daemon`, `debug-adapter`, `devices`, `doctor`, `downgrade`, `drive`, `emulators`, `gen-l10n`, `generate`, `ide-config`, `install`, `logs`, `packages` (deprecated), `precache`, `pub`, `run`, `screenshot`, `symbolize`, `test`, `upgrade`. 14 shared global flags in `_global` array re-applied at every state machine layer: `-v/--verbose`, `--version`, `-h/--help`, `-d/--device-id DEV` (with `_flutter_devices` helper), `--suppress-analytics`, `--disable-analytics`/`--enable-analytics`, `--machine`, `--color`/`--no-color` (mutex pair), `--packages FILE`, `--device-timeout SEC`, `--version-check`/`--no-version-check` (mutex pair). `build` state machine: 12 build targets (`apk`/`appbundle`/`aar`/`ios`/`ios-framework`/`ipa`/`macos`/`macos-framework`/`linux`/`windows`/`web`/`bundle`) → `_build_common` (19 flags: mutex `--release`/`--debug`/`--profile`, `-t/--target FILE`, `--target-platform` android-arm/arm64/x64, `--build-name`, `--build-number`, `--analyze-size`, `--tree-shake-icons`, repeatable `*--dart-define key=val`, repeatable `*--dart-define-from-file JSON`, `--obfuscate`, `--split-debug-info DIR`, `--source-maps`, `--no-pub`, `--web-renderer` auto/html/canvaskit/skwasm, `--csp`, `--flavor`). `run` covers same mode mutex + `--hot`/`--no-hot`, `--enable-software-rendering`, `--trace-skia`, `--start-paused`, `--use-application-binary`, `--web-port`, `--web-hostname` (via `_hosts`). `test` covers `--coverage`/`--coverage-path`, repeatable `*--name PATTERN`, `*--plain-name SUBSTR`, `*--tags`/`*--exclude-tags`, `--update-goldens`, `--reporter` (compact/expanded/json/silent/github), `--concurrency`, `--start-paused`. `create` covers `--org`, `-t/--template` (app/module/package/plugin/plugin_ffi), `--project-name`, `--description`, `--ios-language` objc/swift, `--android-language` java/kotlin, repeatable `*--platforms` (android/ios/linux/macos/web/windows), `--overwrite`, `--sample`, `--empty`. `pub` state machine: 15 pub subcommands (`get`/`add`/`remove`/`upgrade`/`downgrade`/`outdated`/`deps`/`cache`/`publish`/`run`/`global`/`token`/`login`/`logout`/`pub`) — `add` accepts `--dev` + package, `remove` accepts repeatable packages, `run` takes executable + script args, `global` takes action (activate/deactivate/list/run), `cache` takes action (add/clean/repair/preload/list), `deps` takes `--style` compact/tree/list. `custom-devices` state machine: list/add/delete/reset — `add` accepts `--json FILE` + `--check`, `delete` takes device-id. `emulators` covers `--launch ID`, `--cold`, `--create`, `--name`. `doctor` covers `--android-licenses`, `--check-for-remote-artifacts`. `channel` takes `stable`/`beta`/`dev`/`master`/`main`. `analyze` covers `--write FILE`, `--congratulate`/`--no-congratulate`, `--preamble`/`--no-preamble`. `screenshot` covers `-o/--out`, `--type` device/rasterizer/skia, `--vm-service-url`. `symbolize` covers `-i/--input`, `-o/--output`, `-d/--debug-info`. `gen-l10n` covers `--arb-dir`, `--template-arb-file`, `--output-localization-file`, `--output-class`, `--header-file`, `--use-deferred-loading`. `precache` covers per-platform flags (`--all-platforms`/`--android`/`--ios`/`--web`/`--linux`/`--windows`/`--macos`/`--fuchsia`/`--universal`/`--force`). `upgrade`/`downgrade` cover `-f/--force` + `--continue`. `config` covers 14 persistent-setting flags including `--build-dir`, `--enable-{web,android,ios,linux-desktop,macos-desktop,windows-desktop}`, `--clear-features`, `--android-sdk`, `--android-studio-dir`, `--jdk-dir`. Live device discovery `_flutter_devices` runs `flutter devices --machine 2>/dev/null` and extracts JSON `"id":"..."` fields via `grep -oE` + `sed` for portable parsing without jq dependency. `flutter` already in blacklist line 8220; no add needed.
- **tmuxinator + mux (Ruby YAML-driven tmux session manager, 2 binary stems)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh` — verified on this host that no shipped `_tmuxinator` is present, even though upstream documents installing one at `/usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions/_tmuxinator` from the gem) — subcommand inventory and config-file priority list extracted from the upstream [tmuxinator/tmuxinator README](https://github.com/tmuxinator/tmuxinator). Single-file completion `_tmuxinator` is multi-stem `#compdef tmuxinator mux` (the `mux` binary is upstream's short alias, same Thor app behind both names; `mux` was already at blacklist line 16805 with no file). 14 verbs covered with all aliases enumerated as separate `_describe` entries: `new`/`n`, `open`/`o`, `edit`/`e`, `start`/`s`, `copy`/`c`/`cp`, `list`/`l`/`ls`, `delete`/`rm`, `stop`, `stop-all`, `implode`/`i`, `doctor`, `debug`, `help`/`h`, `version` → 25 dispatch keys. Per-verb argument handling: `new`/`open` accept `-l/--local` (creates `.tmuxinator.yml` in cwd) plus project name; `edit` takes project name; `start`/`s` covers `-p/--project-config FILE` (explicit YAML via `_files -g "*.(yml|yaml)"`), `-n/--name` (session-name override), `--suppress-tmux-version-warning`, project positional, plus repeatable `*:override key:value` for Thor template substitution at launch; `copy`/`c`/`cp` takes 2 positionals (source project, new name); `delete`/`rm` accepts repeatable `*:project name`; `stop` takes one project; `debug` adds mutex `--cmd`/`--no-cmd` plus project + overrides; `doctor`/`implode`/`list`/`stop-all`/`version` take no positionals. Live-project helper `_tmuxinator_projects` walks all 4 documented config roots in upstream priority order — `$TMUXINATOR_CONFIG` (custom env), `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tmuxinator`, `$HOME/.config/tmuxinator`, `$HOME/.tmuxinator` — globs `*.yml`/`*.yaml` (zsh nullglob `(N)`), strips `:t:r` to bare project name, deduplicates with `${(u)…}`, and appends a synthetic `[local: ./.tmuxinator.yml]` marker entry when a local-project YAML is present. `tmuxinator` already at blacklist line 25890 and `mux` already at line 16805; no add needed.
- **ponymix (Sean Pringle's PulseAudio command-line mixer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — verb/flag inventory extracted directly from upstream [github.com/falconindy/ponymix/blob/master/bash-completion](https://github.com/falconindy/ponymix/blob/master/bash-completion) source script (authoritative for the action surface). Single-file completion `_ponymix` covers the libpulse-direct mixer (not a pactl wrapper). 12 flags: `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, `-c/--card CARD` (with `_ponymix_cards` from `ponymix list-cards-short`), `-d/--device DEVICE` (with `_ponymix_devices` device-name + numerical-id alternative — live from `ponymix --<type> list-short`), `-t/--devtype TYPE` (sink/source/sink-input/source-output), 6 shortcut aliases `--source`/`--input` (devtype=source), `--sink`/`--output` (devtype=sink), `--sink-input`, `--source-output` — encoded as mutex set against `-t/--devtype`, `-N/--notify` (desktop-notify on action). 24 verbs from upstream `verbs=` array via `_verbs` describe: `help`, `defaults`, `set-default`, `list`, `list-short`, `list-cards`, `list-cards-short`, `get-volume`, `set-volume`, `get-balance`, `set-balance`, `adj-balance`, `increase`, `decrease`, `mute`, `unmute`, `toggle`, `is-muted`, `move`, `kill`, `list-profiles`, `list-profiles-short`, `get-profile`, `set-profile`. Pre-scan loop over `$words` detects devtype hint from any `--sink`/`--source`/`-t`/`--devtype=`/`--sink-input`/`--source-output` already on the command line so live device discovery matches user intent. Per-verb argument completion: `set-volume`/`increase`/`decrease` accept `+N`/`-N`/`N` percent (free-form `_message`); `set-balance`/`adj-balance` take signed value (-100..100); `set-default`/`kill` complete via `_ponymix_devices`; `move` takes two positionals (source then target, both via `_ponymix_devices`); `set-profile` completes via `_ponymix_profiles` (live `ponymix list-profiles-short`); other verbs (`mute`/`unmute`/`toggle`/`is-muted`/`get-volume`/`get-balance`/`get-profile`/list/defaults/help) emit "no further arguments". `ponymix` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 20148 between `ponyc` and `ponysay` (lexicographic-by-position-4: 'c' < 'm' < 's'). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **resperf (DNS resolver perf testing tool — DNS-OARC dnsperf package)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/dnsperf/resperf.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/dnsperf/resperf.1.en.html). Single-file completion `_resperf` covers DNS-OARC's resolver-side load tester (sister to the authoritative-server-side `dnsperf` already in repo at `more_src/_dnsperf`). resperf ramps up query rate over a configurable ramp-up window, optionally sustains constant load after, and finds the throughput threshold under a target acceptable loss percentage. 27 flags covered: sources `-d FILE` (query input, `_files`; stdin if omitted), `-R` (reopen datafile if exhausted); target `-s HOST` (server, default 127.0.0.1, via `_hosts`), `-p PORT`, `-M MODE` (`udp`/`tcp`/`dot`/`doh`), `-f FAMILY` (`inet`/`inet6`/`any`); rate model `-m QPS` (target max, default 100000), `-r SECONDS` (ramp-up, default 60), `-c SECONDS` (constant-rate hold after ramp, default 0), `-L PERCENT` (max acceptable loss for throughput determination), `-q N` (max outstanding per client, default 64k), `-F N` (fall-behind limit, default 1000; 0=disable); client model `-C N` (clients with separate sockets), `-a ADDR` (local source addr via `_hosts`), `-x PORT` (local source port, default wildcard 0); EDNS/DNSSEC `-D` (DNSSEC OK bit, enables EDNS0), `-e` (EDNS0 OPT only); auth `-y [alg:]name:base64-secret` (TSIG); buffers `-b kB` (socket buffer); plotting `-i SECONDS` (data-point interval, default 0.5), `-P FILE` (output, default `resperf.gnuplot`); protocol-specific `*-O option=value` (repeatable, for DoT/DoH params); timeout `-t SECONDS` (default 45); output `-v` (verbose), `-W` (warnings to stdout); help `-h`. `resperf` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 22089 between `respberry` and `responder` (lexicographic-by-position-4: 'b' < 'e' < 'o'). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **powerstat (Canonical's power + thermal + CPU C-state monitor)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags transcribed directly from upstream [manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man8/powerstat.8](https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/man8/powerstat.8.html). Single-file completion `_powerstat` for Colin King's power-monitoring tool that reads either battery discharge rate or RAPL (Running Average Power Limit) domain counters. 19 flags covered: roll-up `-a` (= `-c -f -t -H`); sampling controls `-b` (redo if <98% CPU idle), `-d SECONDS` (start delay — defaults 180s on battery / 0s on RAPL), `-i PERCENT` (idle threshold for forced re-sample), `-p` (redo on any fork/exec/exit), `-r` (redo only if not-idle + fork/exec/exit), `-S` (standard averaging instead of rolling); data collection `-c` (per-CPU C-state activity), `-D` (all power-domain readings), `-f` (avg frequency across on-line cores), `-g` (Intel i915 GPU power), `-H` (histogram of power measurements), `-R` (switch from battery to RAPL source), `-t` (thermal-zone temperatures), `-z` (ignore zero readings); output `-n` (no column headings); log `-s` (dump fork/exec/exit log at end); `-h` (help). Positional args: `1: delay seconds`, `2: number of samples`. `powerstat` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 20239 between `powershell-empire` and `powertop` (lexicographic-by-position-6: 'h' < 's' < 't'). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **pacapt (universal pacman-style package-manager wrapper)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — operation/flag inventory extracted directly from upstream [github.com/icy/pacapt](https://github.com/icy/pacapt) README. Single-file completion `_pacapt` for the pacman-API wrapper that translates `-S/-R/-Q/-U` operations into the host system's native package manager (apt/dnf/yum/zypper/brew/portage/pkg/etc). Top-level operation enumeration uses `_alternative` to multiplex two surfaces: **33-op pacman-style fused flag tree** via `_ops` describe (`-S` install, `-Sy` refresh DB, `-Su` upgrade, `-Suy`/`-Syu` refresh+upgrade, `-Ss` search, `-Si`/`-Sii` info/detailed-info, `-Sg` groups, `-Sl` list-repo, `-Sc`/`-Scc`/`-Sccc` cache cleanup graduations, `-Sw` download-only, `-R` remove, `-Rs` remove+deps, `-Rn` remove+configs, `-Rns` remove+deps+configs, `-Rdd` force-skip-dep-check, `-Q` query, `-Qs` query-by-pattern, `-Qi`/`-Qii` info, `-Ql` list-files, `-Qo` owner-of-file, `-Qm` foreign packages, `-Qu` upgradeable, `-Qd` dependencies, `-Qe` explicit-installs, `-Qc` changelog, `-Qk` integrity-verify, `-U` local-file install, `-P` list-supported-ops); **7-alias long-form tree** via `_aliases` describe (`install`/`remove`/`search`/`upgrade`/`update`/`autoremove`/`clean`). Shared `_global` array (6 modifier flags) re-applied per-op: `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, `--noconfirm`, `-q/--quiet`, `-y` (auto-yes alias), `-w` (download-only alias). Per-op argument handling: install/upgrade/sw/info/list ops accept package args via `_pacapt_remote` helper (deferred — no remote lookup, pacapt already translates underneath); search/`-Ss`/`-Qs` accept free-form pattern; remove/`-Q*` query ops complete from live `pacapt -Qq` via `_pacapt_installed` helper; `-Qo` completes file paths via `_files`; `-U` completes local package files via `_files -g "*.(deb|rpm|pkg.tar.{zst,xz,gz}|apk|tgz)"` glob covering Debian/RPM/Arch-zst/Arch-xz/Arch-gz/Alpine/tarball; cache cleanup ops (`-Sc`/`-Scc`/`-Sccc`/`autoremove`/`clean`) and `update`/`-P` take no positional args. `pacapt` added to `blacklist.txt` at line 18571 between `pac` and `pacaptr` (Rust rewrite — distinct binary already covered). LC_ALL=C sort verified.
- **gm (GraphicsMagick — single-binary image-processing suite)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand inventory + flag coverage extracted from [manpages.debian.org/bookworm/graphicsmagick/gm.1](https://manpages.debian.org/bookworm/graphicsmagick/gm.1.en.html) and the upstream [graphicsmagick.org reference](http://www.graphicsmagick.org/GraphicsMagick.html). Single-file completion `_gm` dispatches the 14-subcommand utility tree: `animate` (X11 animation viewer), `batch` (linear gm-script executor), `benchmark` (perf-iteration meta), `compare` (diff statistics across metrics), `composite` (blend/merge), `conjure` (MSL — Magick Scripting Language interpreter), `convert` (format conversion + transform pipeline), `display` (X11 viewer), `help` (per-subcommand help), `identify` (image format/characteristics report), `import` (X11 window capture), `mogrify` (in-place transform — overwrites originals), `montage` (tiled composite), `time` (CPU+wall-time meta), `version` (release + features report). Two shared flag arrays for cascading future flag adds: `_common_flags` (40 flags shared by `convert`/`mogrify`/`composite`/`montage`/`compare` — repeatable `*-resize`, `*-quality`, `*-rotate`, `*-crop`, `*-format` (with `_formats` value table — 50 supported encodings: JPEG/PNG/GIF/BMP/TIFF/PDF/PS/EPS/SVG/WEBP/HEIC/HEIF/AVIF/JP2/JXR/PSD/DDS/DPX/EXR/MIFF/MNG/MVG/WMF/RGB/CMYK/HISTOGRAM/etc), `*-colorspace` (RGB/CMYK/GRAY/YUV/YIQ/XYZ/Lab/HSL/HSV/HCL/OHTA/CMY/Rec601Luma/Rec709Luma/sRGB/Transparent), `*-density`, `*-background`, `-flatten`, `-strip`, `*-thumbnail`, `*-gravity` (9 anchors), `*-compose` (22 operators), `*-border`, `*-depth` (1/4/8/16/32), `*-extent`, `*-filter` (20 resampling kernels: Point/Hermite/Cubic/Box/Gaussian/Quadratic/Triangle/Mitchell/Sinc/Bessel/Hanning/Hamming/Blackman/Catrom/Lanczos/Bohman/Parzen/Kaiser/Welsh/Cosine), `*-fuzz`, `*-gamma`, `-monochrome`, `-normalize`, `*-profile`, `*-sharpen`, `*-size`, `-trim`, `*-type` (10 image types), `*-units` (3 resolution units), `-verbose`, `*-compress` (10 codecs: None/BZip/Fax/Group4/JPEG/JPEG2000/Lossless/LZW/RLE/Zip), `*-frame`, `*-geometry`, `*-interlace` (7 schemes), `*-modulate`, `-negate`, `*-page`, `*-roll`, `*-sample`, `*-scale`, `*-shave`, `*-tile`, `-version`); `_x_flags` (10 X11 flags shared by `display`/`animate`/`import`: `*-display`, `*-window`/`-window-id`/`-window-group`, `*-iconic`, `*-name`, `*-title`, `*-update`, `*-visual` (6 X visual classes), `*-colormap` private/shared). Per-utility specialization: `identify` (own subset — `-format` escape-string, `-ping`, `-regard-warnings`, `-size`, `-depth`, `-debug`); `display`/`animate` (X11 + animation timing: `*-delay`, `*-pause`, `*-loop`, `*-magnify`, `*-immutable`, `*-treedepth`); `import` (X11 capture: `*-crop`, `-frame`, `-border`, `-delay`, `-mosaic`, `-screen`, `-silent`, `-snaps`); `benchmark` (`-duration`, `-iterations`, `-stepthreads`, `-rawcsv`, recursive subcommand dispatch via `->sub` state); `time` (recursive subcommand dispatch); `conjure` (MSL: repeatable `*-set name=value`, `-monitor`, `-verbose`, `-debug`, `*.msl` file glob); `batch` (`-echo`, `-feedback`, `-pass`, `-fail`, `-prompt`, `-escape` none/windows, `-stop-on-error`). Image input completion uses `_files -g "*.(#i)(jpg|jpeg|png|gif|bmp|tif|tiff|webp|heic|heif|avif|svg|pdf|psd|tga|xpm|xbm|pnm|ppm|pgm|pbm|png|ico|jp2|jxr|jxl|dds|dpx|exr|hdr|miff|mng|sgi|yuv|xcf|wmf|mvg)"` for case-insensitive multi-format matching. `gm` already at blacklist line 9519 (between `glx-utils` and `gm2`); no add needed.
- **tkn (Tekton Pipelines CLI)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — subcommand tree extracted from upstream [tektoncd/cli docs/cmd/tkn*.md](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/tree/main/docs/cmd) cobra reference markdown plus [tekton.dev/docs/cli/](https://tekton.dev/docs/cli/). Single-file completion `_tkn` covers the full CRD-style management tree: 13 top-level subcommands (with aliases — `pipeline`/`p`, `pipelinerun`/`pr`/`pipelineruns`, `task`/`t`/`tasks`, `taskrun`/`tr`/`taskruns`, `eventlistener`/`el`/`eventlisteners`, `clustertriggerbinding`/`ctb`/`clustertriggerbindings`, `triggerbinding`/`tb`/`triggerbindings`, `triggertemplate`/`tt`/`triggertemplates`, `customrun`/`cr`/`customruns`, plus `bundle`, `hub`, `completion`, `version`, `help`). Persistent global flags via shared `_global` array (5 flags): `-c/--context KUBECTX`, `-k/--kubeconfig FILE`, `-n/--namespace NS`, `-C/--no-color`, `-h/--help` — applied at top level and re-applied inside every sub-sub state machine. Sub-sub command groups (kept as shared arrays so future flag additions cascade): `_sub_pipeline` (delete/describe/export/list/logs/sign/start/verify), `_sub_pipelinerun` (cancel/delete/describe/export/list/logs), `_sub_task` (delete/describe/list/logs/sign/start/verify), `_sub_taskrun` (cancel/delete/describe/export/list/logs), `_sub_bundle` (list/push), `_sub_hub` (install/check-upgrade/downgrade/get/info/reinstall/search/upgrade — last 7 are upstream-deprecated, kept for back-compat), `_sub_eventlistener` (delete/describe/list/logs), `_sub_trigger_simple` (delete/describe/list — shared by ctb/tb/tt/customrun), `_sub_completion` (bash/fish/powershell/zsh). Each sub-sub leaf gets per-action flag coverage: `list` actions get `-o/--output json|yaml|name|go-template|jsonpath` + `--all-namespaces` + (for runs) `--limit N` + `--reverse`; `delete` gets `-f/--force` + (for runs) `--keep N` + (for pipeline/task) `--prs`/`--trs` cascade flags; `start` (pipeline/task) covers repeatable `*-p/--param key=value`, repeatable `*-w/--workspace`, repeatable `*-s/--serviceaccount`, repeatable `*-l label=value`, `--showlog`, `--use-param-defaults`, `--filename FILE`, `--last`, repeatable `*--use-pipelinerun`/`*--use-taskrun`; `describe`/`logs` for runs add `-f/--follow` for live streaming; `bundle push` covers OCI auth (`--ctx`, `--remote-bearer`, `--remote-username`, `--remote-password`, `--remote-skip-tls`); `hub install`/`get`/`info` accept `--from CATALOG` + `--version VER` + positional `kind` (task|pipeline) + `name`; `hub search` covers `--kinds`, `--tags`, `--categories`, `--platforms`; `version` accepts `--component client|tektoncd|dashboard|triggers|operator|pipeline`. Live-resource completion helper `_tkn_k_resources` calls `kubectl get <kind> -o name` and strips the `<kind>/` prefix — used for all `describe`/`logs`/`delete`/`cancel`/`start` resource-name positionals across all 9 CRD families (`pipelines.tekton.dev`, `pipelineruns.tekton.dev`, `tasks.tekton.dev`, `taskruns.tekton.dev`, `eventlisteners.triggers.tekton.dev`, `clustertriggerbindings.triggers.tekton.dev`, `triggerbindings.triggers.tekton.dev`, `triggertemplates.triggers.tekton.dev`, `customruns.tekton.dev`). `tkn` already at blacklist line 25849 (between previously-installed `tknewsbiff` neighborhood); no add needed.
- **limactl (Lima — Linux VMs on Mac CLI)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags derived directly from upstream `cmd/limactl/*.go` cobra command definitions in [lima-vm/lima](https://github.com/lima-vm/lima) plus [lima-vm.io/docs/reference/limactl/](https://lima-vm.io/docs/reference/limactl/). Single-file completion `_limactl` covers the full subcommand tree from `newApp()` (32 subcommands enumerated): `create`, `start`, `stop`, `restart`, `shell`, `copy` (alias `cp`), `list` (alias `ls`), `delete` (alias `rm`), `validate`, `prune`, `hostagent`, `guest-install`, `info`, `show-ssh`, `debug`, `edit`, `factory-reset`, `disk` (sub-sub: create/delete/list/resize/unlock), `usernet`, `gen-doc`, `gen-schema`, `snapshot` (sub-sub: create/delete/apply/list), `protect`/`unprotect`, `tunnel`, `template` (sub-sub: copy/ls/validate), `sudoers`, `start-at-login`, `network` (list/start/stop/reload/sudoers), `clone`, `rename`, `watch`, `yq-restrictions-help`. Plugin commands dynamically discovered via `addPluginCommands()` are not enumerated. Global persistent flags via shared `_global` array (5 flags): `--log-level` (trace/debug/info/warn/error), `--log-format` (text/json), `--debug`, `--tty` (auto-detected from stdout), `-y/--yes` (alias of `--tty=false`). Per-subcommand flag coverage: `start`/`create` (`--name`, `--list-templates`, `--list-drivers`, repeatable `*--set yq-expr`, `--foreground`, `--timeout`, `--progress`), `shell` (`--shell` via `_command_names -e`, `--workdir`, `--reconnect`, `--preserve-env`, `--start`, `--sync host-path`), `list` (`-f/--format` json/yaml/table/go-template, `--json` legacy alias, `--list-fields`, `--all-fields`, `-q/--quiet` table-only, repeatable `*--yq`, repeatable `*-l/--filter` AND-combined), `copy`/`cp` (`-r/--recursive`), `stop` (`-f/--force`), `delete` (`-f/--force`), `show-ssh` (`-f/--format` cmd/args/options/config), `validate` (`--fill`), `prune` (`--keep-referred`), `snapshot` (state machine: create/delete/apply/list with `--tag`), `template` (state machine: copy/ls/validate), `disk` (state machine with `--size`, `--format` qcow2/raw), `network`, `sudoers --check`, `start-at-login --enabled`, `gen-doc --type man/markdown/rest/yaml + --output`, `gen-schema --output`, `tunnel --ssh-flag`. Helper `_limactl_instances` runs `limactl list --quiet` for live instance-name completion across `shell`/`stop`/`delete`/`restart`/`copy`/`edit`/`show-ssh`/`protect`/`unprotect`/`clone`/`rename`/`tunnel`/`factory-reset`/`start-at-login`. `limactl` was already in `blacklist.txt` line 13768 (between `lima` and `limit`) but no completion file existed; this round fills the gap. The `_lima` wrapper completion already exists in more_src.
- **git-revise (pure-Python git history rewriter)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — flags derived from upstream `gitrevise/tui.py` argparse plus [git-revise.readthedocs.io/en/latest/man.html](https://git-revise.readthedocs.io/en/latest/man.html). Single-file completion `_git-revise` for Michael Sullivan's efficient non-interactive history editor (update/split/rearrange commits without rebase mechanics). Three mutually exclusive groups encoded in `_arguments`: **autosquash** (`--autosquash` / `--no-autosquash`, controls `revise.autoSquash` config), **index** (`-a/--all` stage tracked / `-p/--patch` interactive hunks / `--no-index` ignore staged), **mode** (`-i/--interactive` edit commit stack / `-c/--cut` split commit / `-m/--message` reword non-interactively). Standalone flags: `-e/--edit` open editor for message, `--reauthor` reset author to current user, `--ref REF` target branch (defaults `HEAD`, completes with `__git_references`). `-m/--message` uses `append` action so it is repeatable for multi-line messages. Positional arg is target commit completed via `__git_revisions`. `--version` shows version and exits. `git-revise` was already reserved in `blacklist.txt` (line 9362, between `git-review` and `git-router`) by an earlier harvest run but the completion file was missing — this round fills the gap.
- **rtla (Real-Time Linux Analysis meta-tool, 4 binary stems)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — from `docs.kernel.org/tools/rtla/`. `_rtla` is a single multi-stem file `#compdef rtla rtla-osnoise rtla-timerlat rtla-hwnoise` covering the Linux kernel's real-time analysis meta-tool. Dispatches on `$service` for direct sub-binaries vs the `rtla` umbrella. 3 tools (`osnoise`/`timerlat`/`hwnoise`) × 2 display modes (`top` real-time stream / `hist` histogram). 22 shared flags from `_shared` array: `-a/--auto`, `-p/--period US`, `-r/--runtime US`, `-s/--stop US` and `-S/--stop-total US` stopping thresholds, `-T/--threshold US` (default 5us), `-t/--trace FILE` save stopped trace, `-c/--cpus`/`-H/--house-keeping` CPU placement, `-d/--duration` time-unit suffixes, `-D/--debug`, repeatable `*-e/--event sys:event` + `*--filter` + `*--trigger`, `-P/--priority o:prio|r:prio|f:prio|d:runtime:period`, `-C/--cgroup`, `--warm-up SEC`, `--trace-buffer-size kB`, `--on-threshold`/`--on-end` action hooks, `-q/--quiet`, `-h/--help`. timerlat-specific `_timerlat_only` array (12 flags): `-i/--irq US`, `--thread US`, `--stack US`, 3-way thread mutex `-k/--kernel-threads` / `-u/--user-threads` (default) / `-U/--user-load`, `--dma-latency`, `--deepest-idle-state N`, `--bpf-action BPF-PROG`, `--stack-format truncate|skip|full`, `--no-aa`, `--aa-only US`. hist-only `_hist_only` array (6 flags): `-b/--bucket-size` (1), `-E/--entries` (256), `--no-header`/`--no-summary`/`--no-index`, `--with-zeros`. The `case $service in` dispatches to direct-binary mode (1 positional for top/hist) or umbrella `rtla` mode (2 positionals: tool then mode). 4 binaries added to blacklist.
- **rdisc6 (IPv6 Router Discovery) + tracepath (MTU-aware tracer) + iptables save/restore** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — all from man.archlinux.org / manpages.debian.org. `rdisc6` (ICMPv6 Router Discovery — send Router Solicitation + print Router Advertisements. 10 flags: `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, 2-way mutex `-1/--single` (exit on first) / `-m/--multiple` (wait + print all — default), `-n/--numeric` no DNS resolution, 2-way mutex `-q/--quiet` (only prefixes) / `-v/--verbose` (default), `-r/--retry N` retries (default 3), `-s/--source IP` source address, `-w/--wait MS` per-retry wait (default 4000); positional network interface), `tracepath` + `tracepath6` (multi-stem `#compdef tracepath tracepath6` — trace path discovering MTU along the way; no admin privileges required. 8 flags: `-V`, 2-way mutex `-4`/`-6`, 2-way mutex `-n` numeric / `-b` both, `-l BYTES` initial packet size (default 65535 IPv4 / 128000 IPv6), `-m HOPS` max TTL (default 30), `-p PORT` start probe port; positional `<host> [port]`), `iptables-save` + `ip6tables-save` (multi-stem `#compdef iptables-save ip6tables-save` — dump current rules. 4 flags: `-M PATH`/`--modprobe`, `-f FILE`/`--file` (default stdout), `-c/--counters` include packet/byte counters, `-t TABLE`/`--table` filter/nat/mangle/raw/security), `iptables-restore` + `ip6tables-restore` (multi-stem — restore rules from file. 9 flags: `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, `-c/--counters`, `-n/--noflush` preserve existing, `-t/--test` parse+construct no-commit, stackable `*-v/--verbose`, `-w/--wait [SEC]` xtables-lock wait, `-M/--modprobe`, `-T/--table NAME` restore only this table; positional file). All four binaries already in blacklist as part of the 8-iptables + 8-ip6tables family — no add needed.
- **ndppd (IPv6 NDP proxy daemon) + nft (nftables CLI)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — both from man.archlinux.org / manpages.debian.org. `ndppd` (IPv6 Neighbor Discovery Protocol Proxy Daemon. 4 flags: `-c FILE` config (default `/etc/ndppd.conf`), `-d` daemonize + syslog, `-p FILE` PID file, stackable `*-v` verbosity), `nft` (nftables — modern replacement for iptables/ip6tables/arptables/ebtables. 24 flags grouped: general (`-h`/`-v`/`-V` extended version + compile config), ruleset input (`-f FILE` read from file or "-" for stdin, `-i` interactive readline shell, repeatable `*-D name=value` define variables, repeatable `*-I DIR` include search path, `-c` check-only no-apply, `-o` optimize ruleset), output formatting (`-a` show object handles, `-s` stateless, `-t` terse no-set-contents, `-S` port→service names, `-N` reverse DNS, `-u` UID/GID→names, `-n` fully numeric, `-y` numeric chain priorities, `-p` numeric protocols, `-T` numeric time), command output (`-e` echo add/insert/replace notifications, `-j` JSON output, `-d LEVEL` debug — scanner/parser/eval/netlink/mnl/proto-ctx/segtree/all)). `nft` already in blacklist; `ndppd` added.
