- **X11 utilities continuation: xwd + xwud + xfontsel** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — all from man.archlinux.org. `xwd` (X Window Dumper — dump an X11 window into a file in XWD format. 15 flags: `-debug`, `-d`/`-display`, `-help`, `-version`, `-nobdrs` exclude border pixels, `-out FILE` (default stdout), `-xy` use XY format (color displays only), `-add SIGNED-VAL` adjust pixel values, `-frame` include WM frame, 3-way target mutex `-root` / `-id WDID` / `-name WDNAME`, `-icmap` use first-installed colormap, `-screen` capture from root window (overlapping/popups), `-silent`), `xwud` (X Window UnDumper — display an XWD image from xwd. 16 flags: `-help`, `-version`, `-bg COLOR`/`-fg COLOR` for "0"/"1" bits in bitmaps, `-display`, `-dumpheader` print XWD header only, `-geometry GEOM`, `-in FILE` (default stdin), `-new` force new colormap, `-noclick` disable click-to-close, `-plane N` show single bit-plane, `-raw` use current screen colors, `-rv` reverse video, `-scale` enable resize+scaling, `-std MAPTYPE` Standard Colormap, `-vis VIS` particular visual), `xfontsel` (interactive X11 font selector — browse fonts, view samples, retrieve XLFD names. 8 flags: `-help`, `-version`, `-pattern FONTNAME` filter, `-print` write selected font specifier to stdout on quit, `-sample TEXT`/`-sample16 TEXT16`/`-sampleUCS TEXTUCS` for linear/matrix/UTF-8-ISO10646 sample text, `-scaled` enable arbitrary-size scaled fonts). All three already in blacklist — no add needed.
- **X11 utilities: xprop + xsel** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — both from man.archlinux.org. `xprop` (display + modify X11 window/font properties; part of `x11-utils`. 18 flags: window/font selection (`-id ID`, `-name NAME`, `-root`, `-font FONT`, `-frame` for WM-frame hand-select), display control (`-len N`, `-notype`, `-fs FILE` extra format file, repeatable `*-f atom format [dformat]` custom format), property modification (repeatable `*-set property-name value`, repeatable `*-remove property-name`), `-spy` continuous property-change monitor, info (`-help`, `-grammar`, `-version`), `-display DISPLAY`; positional property atom), `xsel` (manipulate X11 selections from CLI — PRIMARY/SECONDARY/CLIPBOARD. 18 flags grouped: 7-way action mutex `-a/--append` (append stdin) / `-f/--follow` (append as stdin grows) / `-i/--input` (read stdin) / `-o/--output` (write to stdout) / `-c/--clear` / `-d/--delete` / `-x/--exchange` (swap PRIMARY+SECONDARY), 3-way selection mutex `-p/--primary` (default) / `-s/--secondary` / `-b/--clipboard`, `-k/--keep` (PRIMARY+SECONDARY persist after exit), `--display`, `--windowName`, `-t/--selectionTimeout MS`, `-l/--logfile`, `-n/--nodetach`, `-v/--verbose`, `-h/--help`, `--version`). Both already in blacklist — no add needed.
- **Poppler PDF tools: pdfimages + pdffonts** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — both from man.archlinux.org. `pdfimages` (extract images from a PDF — outputs PPM/PBM/JPEG/JP2/JBIG2/CCITT. 23 flags grouped: page selection (`-f FIRST`/`-l LAST`), output format 7-way mutex (`-png`/`-tiff`/`-j` native JPEG/`-jp2` native JP2/`-jbig2`/`-ccitt`/`-all` all-native with CMYK→TIFF + others→PNG), CCITT-specific (3-way encoding mutex `-1` 1D-G3 / `-2` 2D-G3 / `-4` Group 4; alignment mutex `-A`/`-P`; `-X N` width; bit-polarity mutex `-W` black=1 / `-B` black=0; `-M` MSB-first bit fill), info/output (`-list` list-only no-write, `-print-filenames` echo written paths, `-p` include page in filename), security (`-opw`/`-upw` owner/user passwords), `-q` quiet, `-v`/`-h`. Positional: `<PDF> [image-root-prefix]`), `pdffonts` (list fonts used in a PDF. 7 flags: `-f`/`-l` page range, `-subst` show substitute fonts poppler uses for non-embedded, `-opw`/`-upw` passwords (owner password bypasses security), `-v`/`-h`. Positional PDF file or `-` for stdin). Both already in blacklist — no add needed.
- **mlmmj family completion (3 admin/internal tools)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — all from mlmmj.org. `mlmmj-process` (process mail in mlmmj list: access control, header mgmt, list-control ops, moderation. 5 flags: `-L PATH` list dir, `-m FILE` mail file, `-P` skip mlmmj-send (debug), `-h`/`-V`), `mlmmj-maintd` (mlmmj maintenance daemon; runs as daemon by default. 3 flags: 2-way mutex `-d DIR` (recurse all subdirectories) / `-L PATH` (single list), `-F` non-daemon mode for cron (one cycle then exit; recommend every-2h schedule)), `mlmmj-bounce` (handle bounced messages for an mlmmj list. 7 flags: `-L PATH` REQUIRED list dir, 2-way address mutex `-a ADDR` (bouncing address string e.g. `user=domain.org`) / `-d` (parse mail to extract via DSN RFC1891), 2-way action mutex `-n NUM` (archive message number) / `-p` (send out a probe), `-h`/`-V`). Combined with previous rounds covers all 9 user-facing+internal mlmmj binaries: list/make-ml/sub/unsub + receive/send + process/maintd/bounce.
- **mlmmj mailing list manager family expansion (4 user-facing tools)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — all from mlmmj.org. `mlmmj-make-ml` (interactive script to create a new mailing list directory structure. 6 flags: `-h` help, `-L NAME` list name, `-s SPOOLDIR` (default `/var/spool/mlmmj`), `-a` create /etc/aliases entries, `-c USER` chown spool dir, `-z` placeholder; tool requires interactive setup — flags alone cannot fully configure a list), `mlmmj-sub` (subscribe email address; switches subscription if already-subscribed to a different version. 15 flags: `-L PATH` REQUIRED list dir, `-a EMAIL`, `-m STR` moderation string, version mutex `-d` digest / `-n` nomail, confirmation mutex `-c` welcome mail / `-C` mail confirmation, `-f` force-no-moderation, `-q` no owner notification, `-s` silent if already subscribed, `-r`/`-R` simulate email-arrival (internal), `-U` skip UID switch, `-h`/`-V`), `mlmmj-unsub` (unsubscribe email address. 14 flags: `-L`/`-a` REQUIRED, version-target 3-way mutex `-d`/`-n`/`-N` (default all), notify mutex `-c` goodbye / `-C` confirmation, `-q` no owner notify, `-s` silent if not subscribed, `-r`/`-R` simulate email, `-U` skip UID, `-h`/`-V`), `mlmmj-list` (show subscriber/moderator/owner info. 8 flags: `-L PATH` REQUIRED, 5-way mutex `-s` regular (default) / `-d` digest / `-n` nomail / `-m` moderators / `-o` owners, `-c` show count, `-V` version).
- **muchsync (Joey Hess notmuch DB sync) + mlmmj binaries (mailing list manager)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `muchsync` (muchsync.org — bidirectional sync of notmuch maildirs + tag DB between machines, by Joey Hess. 14 flags: `-C/--config FILE` notmuch config (default `$NOTMUCH_CONFIG` or `~/.notmuch-config`), `-F` check for modified files (default assumes maildir files never edited), `-r PATH` muchsync path on server (non-standard install), `-s CMD` ssh command line (default `ssh -CTaxq`), stackable `*-v`, `--init MAILDIR` clone remote mailbox locally, `--nonew` skip `notmuch new` before sync, `--noup`/`--noupload` receive-only (download but no upload), `--upbg` download in foreground + fork background upload, `--self` print 64-bit replica ID + exit, `--newid` change replica identifier (use after DB copy), `--version`, `--help`; positional remote `host` or `user@host`), `mlmmj-receive` (mlmmj.org — entry point for mail delivered to mlmmj mailing lists. 5 flags: `-L PATH` REQUIRED list directory, `-h` help, `-V` version, `-P` skip mlmmj-process for debug, `-F` no fork/background for debug), `mlmmj-send` (mlmmj.org — distribute mail through mlmmj mailing lists; "not supposed to be used by humans" per upstream docs but real binary. 13 flags: `-L PATH` list dir, `-l 1-6` sending mode, `-m FILE` mail file, `-a` skip archive, `-D` preserve mail file, `-o ADDR` exclude recipient, `-r IP` relay host (default `127.0.0.1`), `-F MAIL-FROM`, `-R REPLY-TO`, `-T RCPT-TO`, `-s SUBSCRIBERS-FILE`, `-h`/`-V`).
- **afew (notmuch mail auto-tagger)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `afew` (afewmail/afew from afew/commands.py argparse — automated tagging tool for notmuch mail; runs after `notmuch new` to apply filters. 13 flags: `-V/--version`, `-h/--help`, 3-way mode mutex `-t/--tag` (run tag filters) / `-w/--watch` (continuously monitor mailbox via pyinotify — Linux only) / `-m/--move-mails` (relocate maildir files), 2-way scope mutex `-a/--all` (operate on all messages) / `-n/--new` (operate on new messages), `-C/--notmuch-config FILE`, `-e/--enable-filters CSV` filter classes, `-d/--dry-run` (preview only), `-R/--reference-set-size N` (default 1000), `-T/--reference-set-timeframe DAYS` (default 30), stackable `*-v/--verbose`, `-N/--notmuch-args` args for `notmuch new` in move mode, positional `query` (notmuch query string). Existing `afew` entry already in blacklist — no add needed. NOTE: skipped `artha` (Ubuntu/Arch manpages confirm artha has no real CLI options — GUI-only thesaurus with global hotkey). Hunt this round had a lot of -cli alias false-positives.
- **skarnet s6 TLS wrappers + s6-linux-init-maker (3 NEW)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `s6-tlsserver` (TLS-wrapped TCP server runner — combines s6-tcpserver + s6-tlsd + access control. ~39 flags grouped: server core (`-L` PROXY protocol, `-1` print port, `-c`/`-C`/`-b` connection caps + backlog), TCP access mutex pairs (verbosity `-v0`/`-v2`, DNS/IDENT strictness `-w`/`-W`, Nagle `-d`/`-D`, IDENT `-r`/`-R`, paranoid DNS `-p`/`-P`, /etc/hosts `-h`/`-H`), TCP misc (`-l NAME`, `-B BANNER`, `-t TIMEOUT`, `-i RULESDIR`/`-x RULESFILE` access-rules CDB), TLS-specific mutex pairs (env vars `-Z`/`-z`, close notify `-S`/`-s`, peer-close-notify-fail `-J`/`-j`, client cert mode `-Y`/`-y`), TLS knobs (`-K TIMEOUT`, `-k SNI-LEVEL`), privilege (`-u`/`-g`/`-G`/`-U`); positional `<bind-IP> <port> <program> [args]`), `s6-tlsclient` (TLS-wrapped TCP client runner — combines s6-tcpclient + s6-tlsc. ~27 flags: inherits s6-tcpclient verbosity/IP/NODELAY/host/qual/IDENT/timeout mutex set + `-i`/`-p`/`-l` bind tweaks; adds TLS mutex pairs `-Z`/`-z` env, `-S`/`-s` close-notify, `-J`/`-j` peer-fail, `-Y`/`-y` send-client-cert; plus `-k SERVERNAME` SNI server name + `-K MS` handshake timeout. Positional `<host> <port> <program> [args]`), `s6-linux-init-maker` (generate complete stage-1 init system for Linux box using s6-rc. 22 flags: boot config `-V 0-4` verbosity (default 1), `-c BASEDIR` (default `/etc/s6-linux-init/current`), `-u USER` logger owner (default root), `-G TTY` early-getty for boot failures, `-1` copy to /dev/console, `-L` add logouthookd, `-p PATH` (default `/usr/bin:/bin`), `-m UMASK` (default 022), `-t 0/1/2/3` timestamp style (none/TAI64N/ISO8601/both, default 1), `-d DEVTMPFS_DIR`, `-s KERNEL_ENV_DIR`, repeatable `*-e key=value` global env, `-q MS` SIGKILL grace (default 3000), `-D RUNLEVEL` (default "default"), mutex `-n`/`-N` /run mount handling, `-f SKEL_DIR`, `-R LIMITS`, `-C` container mode, `-B` disable catch-all logger, `-S` sync on container halt, `-W` wait-fd-EOF. Positional output dir).
- **skarnet s6-rc + s6-networking + s6-dns continuation (5 NEW files, 6 binaries)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — all from skarnet.org software docs. `s6-rc-compile` (compile s6-rc service definitions into binary DB; 3 flags `-v 0-4` verbosity (default 1), `-h USER` s6-fdholder-daemon owner (default supervision tree owner), `-b` make s6-rc-oneshot-run wait instead of fail on lock contention; positional `<compiled-dir> <source-dir...>`), `s6-rc-bundle` (define/delete bundle services in compiled DB; 4 flags `-c COMPILED` operate on compiled DB instead of live, `-l LIVE` live state location default `/run/s6-rc`, `-f` force (no error on missing-delete or existing-add), `-b` blocking lock; 3 subcommands `add bundle service1 service2...`, `delete bundle...`, `multiple` (reads stdin lines `bundle s1 s2...`)), `s6-tcpclient` (TCP client runner — connects to host:port and execs program with stdin/stdout connected; 18 flags grouped into 5 mutex sets: verbosity (`-q`/`-Q` default/`-v`), IP version (`-4`/`-6`), TCP_NODELAY (`-d` default/`-D`), host resolution (`-h` /etc/hosts before DNS / `-H` skip DNS), host qualification (`-n`/`-N` default), IDENT (`-r` get TCPREMOTEINFO / `-R` default); plus `-t TIMEOUT` global, `-T x+y` connect timeouts (default 2+58), `-i LOCALIP`/`-p LOCALPORT` bind, `-l NAME` TCPLOCALHOST; positional `<host> <port> <program> [args]`), `s6-dnsip4` + `s6-dnsip6` (multi-stem `#compdef s6-dnsip4 s6-dnsip6` — DNS resolver clients (IPv4 / IPv6) — print resolved addresses to stdout; 5 flags `-q` qualify domain per /etc/resolv.conf suffixes (env `$DNSQUALIFY`), mutex `-h`/`-H` /etc/hosts use (default `-H`), `-r` random-permute results, `-t MS` timeout (exit 99 if exceeded); repeatable positional domain), `s6-dnsname` (reverse DNS — IP → name; auto-detects IPv4/v6 from input; 6 flags: mutex `-4`/`-6` force version, mutex `-h`/`-H` /etc/hosts (default `-H`), `-r` random-permute, `-t MS` timeout). NOTE: existing previous-round `_s6-applyuidgid`/`_s6-envuidgid`/`_s6-setuidgid`/`_s6-softlimit`/`_s6-tai64n`/`_s6-tcpserver4` still active. `s6-applyuidgid` (drop privileges then exec; 5 flags `-u UID`, `-g GID`, `-G CSV-gidlist`, `-U` read UID/GID/GIDLIST env vars (cmdline overrides), `-z` unexport env vars), `s6-envuidgid` (set UID/GID/GIDLIST env vars from an account name then exec; 6 flags 3-way mutex `-u`/`-g`/`-B` user/group/user:group interpretation, `-n` numeric fallback, `-i` insist (exit 1 if unknown), `-D uid:gid:gidlist` default values), `s6-setuidgid` (switch to account's UID/GID/GIDLIST then exec — NO FLAGS upstream; takes only account positional + program args), `s6-softlimit` (apply setrlimit() limits then exec; 14 flags — modifier mutex `-H` set hard+soft / `-h` hard only; resource limits `-a` total memory, `-c` core file size, `-d` heap, `-f` file size, `-l` locked memory, `-m` all memory, `-o` open FDs, `-P` RT priority, `-p` procs per user, `-r` physical memory, `-s` stack, `-t` CPU seconds; each numeric option accepts `=` to mean "set to hard limit"), `s6-tai64n` + `s6-tai64nlocal` (multi-stem `#compdef s6-tai64n s6-tai64nlocal` — filters that prepend TAI64N timestamps to stdin lines; NO FLAGS upstream; `s6-tai64nlocal` converts TAI64N → local time), `s6-tcpserver4` + `s6-tcpserver6` (multi-stem TCP server runners IPv4/IPv6, exec per-connection program; 9 flags: `-1` print bound port to stdout for readiness, `-v 0/1/2` verbosity, `-c` max concurrent connections default 40 max 1000, `-C` per-IP cap default 40, `-b` backlog, `-G`/`-g`/`-u`/`-U` privilege dropping (root only)). Positional `<bind-IP> <port> <program> [args...]`.
- **execlineb (skarnet execline script interpreter)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `execlineb` (skarnet.org/software/execline/execlineb.html — script interpreter for the execline language; designed for run scripts in s6/s6-rc service supervision. 10 flags: `-c SCRIPT` execute script directly (skip file lookup), `-e` ignored (kept for `$SHELL -ec script` compatibility), 3-way strictness mutex `-q` (silent — EXECLINE_STRICT unset) / `-w` (warning — EXECLINE_STRICT=1) / `-W` (error/exit — EXECLINE_STRICT=2), positional-handling mutex `-p` (bypass push phase — overwrite positional params) / `-P` (bypass positional handling completely — no env push), substitution mutex `-S NMIN` (substitute positionals up to N — no push, no env) / `-s NMIN` (like -S but define `$@` as remaining cmdline after N args). Positional script file (when `-c` not used) + arguments. **NOTE**: existing `_sqlfluff` in more_src/ verified against docs.sqlfluff.com — already covers all 8 subcommands (`lint`/`fix`/`format`/`parse`/`render`/`dialects`/`rules`/`version`) with 26 SQL dialects in `-d` completion, 4 templater choices, full per-subcommand flag dispatch incl. `lint`'s `--format human/json/yaml/sarif/github-annotation*`, `fix`'s `--FIX-EVEN-UNPARSABLE`/`-x/--fixed-suffix`, `parse`'s `-c/--code-only`/`-m/--include-meta`. No rewrite needed.
- **textual (Textualize TUI dev CLI) + acme-dns (ACME DNS-01 server) + redo multi-stem extension** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `textual` (Textualize/textual-dev from src/textual_dev/cli.py click — development tools CLI for Textual TUI framework, shipped via separate `textual-dev` PyPI package. 8 subcommands with rich flag sets: `run` (`--dev` connect to console, `--host`/`--port` console connection defaults DEVTOOLS_HOST/PORT, `--press CSV` simulate keys, `--screenshot DELAY`/`--screenshot-path`/`--screenshot-filename`, `-c/--command` cmd-not-file, `-r/--show-return`, positional FILE or FILE:APP + extra args), `serve` (`-h/--host` localhost default, `-p/--port` 8000 default, `-t/--title` default "Textual App", `-u/--url` public URL, `--dev`, `-c/--command`, positional + extras), `console` (`--port`, `-v/--verbose`, repeatable `*-x/--exclude EVENT|DEBUG|INFO|WARNING|ERROR|SYSTEM|LOGGING|PRINT` log groups), and 5 explorer subcommands: `borders`/`easing`/`colors`/`keys`/`diagnose`), `acme-dns` (joohoi/acme-dns from main.go — limited DNS-01 challenge server. Intentionally minimal CLI: single `-c FILE` flag (default `/etc/acme-dns/config.cfg`) — all operational config is .cfg-driven), `redo` (apenwarr/redo — djb-style incremental build system. EXTENDED multi-stem from `#compdef redo` to `#compdef redo redo-ifchange redo-ifcreate redo-always redo-stamp redo-targets redo-ood redo-sources redo-log` — these sibling helpers are called inside `.do` scripts and share the same option parser. Also added mutex group on `--color`/`--no-color`. 15 flags total: `-j/--jobs N`, `-d/--debug`, `-v/--verbose` (passes `-v` to /bin/sh), `-x/--xtrace` (passes `-x` to /bin/sh), `-k/--keep-going`, `--debug-locks`, `--debug-pids`, `--shuffle`, `--no-log`, `--no-details`, `--no-status`, `--no-pretty`, mutex `--color`/`--no-color`, `--version`).
- **lsmod (Linux kmod module list) + niv (Nix dependency manager)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `lsmod` (Linux kmod from man/lsmod.8 — list currently loaded Linux kernel modules. 4 flags only: `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, `-s/--syslog` (send errors to syslog instead of stderr), `-v/--verbose` (print what the program is doing — normally silent unless something goes wrong)), `niv` (nmattia/niv from README + src/Niv/Cli.hs — Haskell-based Nix dependency manager; maintains `nix/sources.json` + `nix/sources.nix`. 6 subcommands: `init` (bootstrap with `--latest` for niv branch override), `add` (with `-n/--name`, `-b/--branch`, `-v/--version`, `-t/--template URL`, `-T/--type file|tarball`, repeatable `-a/--attribute k=v`, positional `owner/repo` or URL — supports private GitHub via SSH/`.netrc`), `update` (same flags as add; omit package name to update all; supports `NIV_OVERRIDE_<name>` env vars for local dev), `drop` (positional package), `modify` (change attributes without fetching), `show` (positional package, omit = show all). Shared root flags: `-s/--sources-file` alternate sources.json path, `--no-colors`, `-h/--help`).
- **Sysinternals ProcMon for Linux + rsign/rsign2 Rust Minisign** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `procmon` (Microsoft Sysinternals/ProcMon-for-Linux from src/configuration/procmon_configuration.cpp — Linux port of the famous Windows Sysinternals Process Monitor. Requires sudo. 7 flags: `-p/--pids CSV` PIDs (capped at MAX_PIDS), `-s/--storageEngine` backend (validated against supported options), `-e/--events CSV` syscalls (default: trace all), `-c/--collect [FILE]` headless mode w/ optional output file (default pattern `procmon_[date]_[time].db`), `-f/--file` load existing trace, `-l/--log` debug trace file path, `-h/--help`. Note: the `-c` and `-f` flags are incompatible with curses UI), `rsign` + `rsign2` (jedisct1/rsign2 — Rust implementation of Minisign for signing files + verifying signatures. Pure Rust, minimal deps. Multi-stem `#compdef rsign rsign2` since binary ships as both names. 3 subcommands: `generate` (-p public-key path default `rsign.pub`, -s secret-key path default `~/.rsign/rsign.key`, mutex -W/--unencrypted password-less + no-encrypt, -f overwrite existing), `sign` (-s secret-key, -x signature output default `<input>.minisig`, -t trusted comment SIGNED, -c untrusted comment, -W password-less, file positional), `verify` (mutex -p public-key-file vs -P public-key-string-arg, -x signature file, -q quiet, -o output content on success, file positional). Global `-h/--help`/`-V/--version`).
- **donut (PIC shellcode generator) + microsocks (tiny SOCKS5 proxy)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `donut` (TheWover/donut from donut.c usage block — generate position-independent shellcode from .NET assemblies, EXEs, DLLs, and scripts; authorized security-testing tool present in many pentest distributions. 21 flags grouped: module options (`-n/--modname`, `-s/--server host[user:pass]`, `-e/--entropy 1=none|2=random-names|3=random-names+encryption`), PIC options (`-a/--arch 1=x86|2=amd64|3=both`, `-o/--output` default `loader.bin`, `-f/--format 1=bin|2=base64|3=c|4=ruby|5=python|6=powershell|7=csharp|8=hex`, `-y/--fork OFFSET` thread-at-offset, `-x/--exit 1=thread|2=process|3=block`), file options (`-c/--class` REQUIRED for .NET DLL, `-d/--domain` AppDomain, `-i/--input` file, `-m/--method` REQUIRED for .NET DLL, `-p/--params` args, `-w/--unicode` Unicode for unmanaged DLL, `-r/--runtime` CLR version, `-t/--thread` EXE entrypoint as thread), additional (`-z/--compress 1=none|2=aPLib|3=LZNT1|4=Xpress`, `-b/--bypass 1=none|2=abort|3=continue` AMSI/WLDP/ETW, `-k/--headers 1=overwrite|2=keep` PE headers, `-j/--decoy` optional decoy module)), `microsocks` (rofl0r/microsocks from sockssrv.c getopt — tiny SOCKS5 proxy server (<1000 SLOC, single binary). 8 short flags only: `-1` auth_once mode (auth'd IPs added to whitelist for passwordless reconnects), `-q` quiet, `-i` listening IP (default `0.0.0.0`), `-p` listening port (default 1080), `-u`/`-P` user/pass (must be used together), `-b` bind outgoing connections to specific IP, `-w` CSV whitelist of IPs that bypass auth).
- **nfdump tool family deep-dive: 4 NEW + 3 in-place REWRITES in src/** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — all from phaag/nfdump man/*.1 manpages. NEW: `nfpcapd` (pcap-to-nfdump converter; live-capture or read-file mode. 25 flags: input (`-i` iface, `-r` pcap input, `-s` snaplen, `-B` cache size, `-e active,inactive` flow expiry), output (`-w` flow dir, `-p` pcap dir, `-H host[/port]` forward, `-S 0-15` subdir layout, `-t` rotation sec, `-z lzo|bz2|lz4|zstd` with optional levels), daemon (`-D`, `-d`, `-v`, `-u`/`-g` drop-privs, `-P` pidfile), processing (`-I` ident, repeatable `*-o` option CSV, `-W` workers)), `ft2nfdump` (flow-tools-to-nfdump converter — 7 flags: `-r` ft input, `-w` nf output, `-z` compress, `-E` show flows on screen, `-c N` stop after N records, `-h`/`-V`), `geolookup` (GeoIP/ASN lookup helper — 3 flags: `-d DIR` CSV input dir to build DB, `-w` output DB file, `-G` use existing binary DB for queries; positional IP/AS queries), `torlookup` (Tor exit-node lookup helper — 3 flags: `-d DIR` CSV input dir, `-w` output torDB, `-H` use existing torDB; positional IP queries). REWRITES in-place in src/ (moved updated content from more_src/ → src/ to avoid shadow duplication): `nfanon` (was missing `-K` encryption key + `-v`/`-W` flags; had `-M`/`-R` that don't exist upstream — fixed to 6 real flags: `-h`/`-r`/`-w`/`-K`/`-v`/`-W`), `nfexpire` (was missing `-T` runtime/`-p` print profiles/`-Y` confirm-all — added 11 real flags incl. `-l`/`-r`/`-e` dir-modes, `-s`/`-t`/`-w` size/age/watermark thresholds, `-u` collector log path), `nfreplay` (existing had typo `-H /ip>`, missing `-S` source spoof/`-u` usec delay/`-B` buffer/`-z` time-scaling/`-v 5|9|ipfix` version; fixed to 14 real flags). NOTE: existing `_nfprofile` left untouched; existing `_nfcapd` in src/ also untouched (covers main nfdump capture daemon — different from nfpcapd which is pcap-based).
- **Network-flow accounting daemons + LTTng crash recovery** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `sfcapd` (phaag/nfdump from man/sfcapd.1 — sFlow v4/v6 collector daemon paired with nfdump. 28 short flags: storage (`-w` flow dir, `-C` extra-config, `-z lzo|bz2|lz4|zstd` compress, `-S 0-15` subdir layout, `-t` rotation sec), daemon (`-D` daemonize, `-u`/`-g` drop-privs, `-P` pidfile), network (`-p` UDP port default 9995, `-d` ERSPAN iface, `-I` ident, `-b` bind, mutex `-4`/`-6`, `-j` multicast group, `-R` packet repeater target + `-A` spoof mode, `-B` buffer size), exporters (repeatable `*-n` netflow-source spec, `-M` dynamic exporter dir, `-i`/`-m` flow-metric exporter), processing (`-o gre` options, `-e` auto-expire, `-x` rotation cmd, repeatable `*-X` extensions, `-W` worker threads, `-v 0-4` verbosity)), `sfacctd` (pmacct/pmacct from src/sfacctd.c — pmacct sFlow accounting daemon collecting v2/v4/v5 samples and routing to plugins. 30 short flags: network (`-L` bind IP, `-l` UDP port), aggregation (`-a` list primitives, `-c` aggregation method, `-n` networks/ASNs file, `-t` ports file, repeatable `*-P memory|print|mysql|pgsql|sqlite3|amqp|kafka|tee` plugins), daemon (`-D`, `-d` debug, `-S auth|mail|daemon|kern|user|local0-7` syslog, `-F` pidfile), processing (`-R` renormalize, `-u` numeric protos, `-I` pcap savefile + `-Z`/`-W`/`-Y` replay control, `-T config|setup` dry-run, memory plugin tuning `-p`/`-b`/`-m`/`-s`, `-r` refresh, `-O formatted|csv|json|avro`/`-o`/`-M`/`-A`/`-E` output)), `uacctd` (pmacct/pmacct from src/uacctd.c — pmacct user-space accounting daemon for Netlink NFLOG packets. 26 short flags: same shape as sfacctd MINUS sFlow-network flags, PLUS Netlink-specific (`-g` NFLOG group, `-L` snapshot length); plugin list adds `nfprobe`/`sfprobe`), `lttng-crash` (lttng.org/man/1/lttng-crash/v2.13/ — LTTng v2.13 tool to recover + view traces from shared memory after a crashed system. 6 flags: `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, `--list-options`, `-x/--extract DIR` extract-only (skip reader), stackable `*-v/--verbose` (up to 3), `-e/--viewer babeltrace|babeltrace2`. Required positional `SHMDIR` (shared-memory dir from `--shm-path`)).
- **LTTng tracing daemons: lttng-sessiond + lttng-relayd** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `lttng-sessiond` (LTTng v2.13 session daemon — manages tracing sessions, coordinates kernel + 32/64-bit userspace consumers — from lttng.org/man/8/lttng-sessiond/v2.13/. 30+ flags across categories: daemon lifecycle (mutex `-b/--background` keep-FDs vs `-d/--daemonize` close-FDs, `-f/--config *.ini`, `-g/--group` Unix tracing-group default `tracing`, `-l/--load` session config path, `-S/--sig-parent` USR1-on-ready), kernel tracing (`--no-kernel`, `--kmod-probes CSV`, `--extra-kmod-probes CSV`), socket paths (`--agent-tcp-port`, `-a/--apps-sock`, `-c/--client-sock`, full `--consumerd32-{path,libdir}` + `--consumerd64-{path,libdir}` + `--kconsumerd-{cmd,err}-sock` + `--ustconsumerd{32,64}-{cmd,err}-sock` matrix), event notifier (`--event-notifier-error-buffer-size-{kernel,userspace}` slots), verbosity (mutex `-q/--quiet` vs stackable `*-v/--verbose` up to `-vvv`, `--verbose-consumer`), program info (`-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, `--list-options`)), `lttng-relayd` (LTTng relay daemon — receives trace data over network + writes to local storage. 14 flags: lifecycle (mutex `-b/--background` vs `-d/--daemonize`, `-f/--config *.ini`, `-x/--disallow-clear`, `--fd-pool-size`, `-g/--group`, `-w/--working-directory`, stackable `*-v/--verbose`), output grouping (mutex `-p/--group-output-by-host` vs `-s/--group-output-by-session`, `-o/--output DIR`), network ports (`-C/--control-port` default `tcp://0.0.0.0:5342` / `-D/--data-port` default `tcp://0.0.0.0:5343` / `-L/--live-port` default `tcp://localhost:5344`), program info (`-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, `--list-options`)). NOTE: existing `_lttng` covers the lttng(1) client tool itself; this round adds the two daemons.
- **bpftune (Oracle eBPF kernel autotuner) + packetdrill (Google TCP/UDP stack test framework) + rabins (argus binner)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `bpftune` (oracle/bpftune from src/bpftune.c getopt_long — eBPF-based kernel sysctl auto-tuner. 14 flags: `-h/--help`, `-V/--version`, repeatable `*-a/--allow TUNER` allowlist, `-c/--cgroup DIR`, `-d/--debug`, `-D/--daemon` daemonize, `-l/--libdir DIR`, `-L/--legacy` force-legacy-BPF, `-r/--learning_rate RATE`, `-p/--port PORT`, `-q/--query QUERY` client-query-mode, `-R/--rollback`, `-s/--stderr` log-to-stderr, `-S/--support` print BPF support level), `packetdrill` (google/packetdrill from gtests/net/packetdrill/config.c long_options[] — DSL scripts test TCP/UDP/IP stack behavior with millisecond-precision timing. 3 modes: local (default), wire client (`--wire_server_at HOST:PORT`), wire server (`--wire_server`). 38 flags incl. `--ip_version ipv4|ipv4-mapped-ipv6|ipv6`, `--bind_port`/`--connect_port`, IP config (`--remote_ip`/`--local_ip`/`--gateway_ip`/`--netmask_ip`), link tuning (`--speed Mbps`/`--mss`/`--mtu`), code blocks (`--code_command`/`--code_format python`/`--code_sockopt`), init scripts (`--init_scripts CSV`), timing tolerance (`--tolerance_usecs`/`--tolerance_percent`), wire mode (3-way mutex `--wire_client`/`--wire_server`/`--wire_server_at` + `--wire_server_{ip,port,dev}`/`--wire_client_dev`), shared-object init (`--so_filename *.so`/`--so_flags`), TCP timestamp (`--tcp_ts_ecr_scaled`/`--tcp_ts_tick_usecs`), `--strict_segments`, `--non_fatal TYPES`, `--dry_run`, `--is_anyip`, `--send_omit_free`, `--psp_udp_port`, `--debug`, repeatable `*-D/--define symbol=value`, `-v/--verbose`; positional `*.pkt` scripts), `rabins` + `rabins2` (argus-client(1) Debian manpage — process argus records into time/count/size BINS. Multi-stem `#compdef rabins rabins2`. Subset of `_ra_common` (key ra(1) flags) plus rabins-specific: `-B SECS` bin holding-time, `-M time|count|size|hard|nomodify` combinable strategy modes, repeatable `*-m AGG_OBJ` aggregation object (28 choices same as ratop: srcid/smac/dmac/...sas/das/ias), `-P FIELD` output sort field (distinct from `-m`), `-w FILE` output filename supporting `$field` substitution + strftime). Note `_ra` separately covers ra/ratop/ralabel/rasort/racount/rasplit.
- **Argus network-flow client family (ra/ratop/ralabel/rasort/racount/rasplit)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `_ra` multi-stem `#compdef ra ratop ralabel rasort racount rasplit` covers the QoSient argus-clients suite for network flow record analysis. Single file with shared `_ra_common` array containing the 28 base ra(1) options (`-A` agg-stats, `-b` dump-compiled-match-code, `-c CHAR` delim, `-D 1-8` debug level, `-d` daemon toggle, `-e REGEX` user-data match, `-E FILE` rejected-records output, `-F CONF` config source, `-h` help, `-H` abbreviate numerics, `-L N` header label frequency, `-n` number-to-name toggle, `-N N` first-N-records, `-p DIGITS` float precision, `-q` quiet, `*-r FILE` read argus data (repeatable, `*.argus`/`*.arg`), `-R DIR` recurse dir, `*-s FIELD` print-fields (repeatable: stime/saddr/daddr/sport/dport/proto/bytes/...), `-S URI` remote source `[user[:pass]@]host[:port]`, `-t TIMERANGE` time filter, `-T SECS` remote-read duration, `-u` Unix-time format, `-w FILE` append output (argus format), `-X` reset to defaults, `-z` TCP-state status field, `-Z s|d|b` actual TCP flag values). Then `case $service` dispatches tool-specific flags: `ratop` adds `-m` aggregation object (28 choices: srcid/smac/dmac/saddr/daddr/matrix/proto/sport/dport/...) + `-M correct|nocorrect|preserve|nopreserve|norep|rmon|nocurses` curses mode; `ralabel` adds `-f LABEL_SPEC_FILE` + `-M ADDR` simple-list mode + `-m saddr|daddr|saddr,daddr` IANA label target; `rasort` adds repeatable `*-m FIELD` sort key (35 choices: stime/ltime/dur/bytes/pkts/saddr/daddr/sport/dport/scc/dcc/sas/das/...) + `-M replace` in-place overwrite; `racount` adds `-M proto|addr` count breakdown (IPv4/IPv6 unicast/multicast addr type, IP/ether/ARP/RARP protos); `rasplit` adds `-M MODE` for partitioning. All variants accept BPF-style filter expression as `*` positional. Verified via argus-client(1) Debian manpages (ratop.1/ralabel.1/rasort.1/racount.1/ra.1). NOTE: existing `_argus` covers the argus(8) collector daemon itself separately.
- **bgpdump (RIPE NCC MRT parser) + irrd (Internet Routing Registry Daemon)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `bgpdump` (RIPE-NCC/bgpdump from bgpdump.c usage block — MRT-format BGP routing-dump parser maintained by RIPE NCC since 2005, originally Dan Ardelean. 12 short flags only (no long forms): 3-way mutex output modes `-H` multi-line human (default) / `-m` one-line Unix timestamps / `-M` one-line human timestamps; `-O FILE` redirect output; 2-way mutex log destination `-s` syslog (default) / `-v` stderr; `-q` quiet; `-t dump|change` timestamp source for `-m`/`-M`; `-p` packet index at second position; `-l` large communities after regular; `-u` unassigned attributes in one-line mode; `-T` run unit tests + exit. Legacy flags `-i`/`-f`/`-o` recognized but ignored upstream — omitted from completion. MRT file positionals match `*.mrt`/`*.bz2`/`*.gz`/`*.bgp`), `irrd` (irrdnet/irrd from irrd/daemon/main.py argparse — Internet Routing Registry Daemon serving RPSL objects + RPKI integration. Daemon CLI is intentionally minimal — only 2 flags: `--config FILE` alternative YAML config path, `--foreground` do-not-detach. All operational config is YAML-driven). NOTE: existing `_bird` already covers `bird/bird2/birdc/bird6` multi-stem (BIRD routing daemon with all 12 verified flags from sysdep/unix/main.c getopt `"bc:dD:ps:P:u:g:flRh"` — no rewrite needed).
- **brimcap (Brim pcap analyzer) + cshark (CloudShark capture client)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `brimcap` (brimdata/brimcap from cmd/brimcap/*/command.go — search/analyze/inspect pcap files, drives Zeek + Suricata analyzers to emit ZNG/ZSON/NDJSON; NOT cobra — uses Brim's charm-spec CLI framework with single-dash long opts. 8 subcommands: `analyze` (-nostats, -z ZSON output, pcap positional), `config` (print config), `cut` (-r input, -w output), `index` (-r/-x/-n 10000 default size limit, -root common index dir for parallel search), `info` (no flags; pcap positional), `search` (-w + 7 search flags incl. REQUIRED `-ts` timestamp / `-proto tcp|udp|icmp` / `-src.ip` / `-dst.ip` + optional `-src.port` / `-dst.port` / `-duration 1ns` default), `slice` (-r/-w/-x index file/-from time/-to time/-p proto), `ts` (-r/-w timestamps). Root-only shared flags `-config` (env BRIMCAP_CONFIG) and `-root` (env BRIMCAP_ROOT) injected via SetRootOnlyFlags into analyze/config/search. Root persistent: `-json` (auto-true when stderr not a TTY)), `cshark` (cloudshark/cshark from src/cshark.c getopt `"i:w:s:T:P:S:p:kvh"` — OpenWrt-focused traffic capture + upload to cloudshark.org. 10 short flags only (no long forms): `-h` help, `-v` version, `-i IFACE` listen interface, `-w FILE` write raw packets, `-s BYTES` snarf snaplen, `-k` keep file after upload, `-T SEC` timeout (0=no), `-P N` packet limit (0=no), `-S BYTES` byte limit (0=no), `-p FILE` save PID).
- **ply (lightweight Linux BPF tracer, dtrace/awk-style) + tcpsnitch (LD_PRELOAD sockets API tracer)** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `ply` (wkz/ply from src/ply/ply.c getopt_long — Tobias Waldekranz's lightweight dynamic Linux tracer. Compiles dtrace/awk-style scripts to BPF via kprobes/tracepoints; only libc required at runtime. Multi-arch (x86_64/aarch64/arm/loongarch/mips/riscv32/riscv64/powerpc). 9 flags: `-h/--help`, `-v/--version`, `-c/--command CMD` (run shell command + exit when done), `-d/--debug`, `-e/--dry-run` (compile-only — no BPF load), `-k/--keep-going` (tolerate trace-buffer overruns), `-S/--dump` (show generated BPF), `-T/--self-test`, `-u/--unbuffer` (force stdout/stderr unbuffered); positional ply script e.g. `'kretprobe:vfs_read { @["size"] = quantize(retval); }'`), `tcpsnitch` (GregoryVds/tcpsnitch from bin/tcpsnitch bash getopts — LD_PRELOAD-based sockets API tracer that intercepts libc network calls. Records tcp_info socket option at user-defined intervals + .pcap per socket. Two modes: Linux (run app directly) or Android (API 23+ via `-a` launch + `-k` kill+pull). 14 flags: `-h` help, `--version`, `-a` Android launch, `-k PKG` Android kill+pull, `-b BYTES` tcp_info dump every N bytes (default 0=off), `-c` pcap capture (Linux only), `-d DIR` trace output dir (default random `/tmp` dir), `-f LEVEL` file log verbosity 0-5 (default 2), `-l LEVEL` stderr log verbosity 0-5 (default 2), `-n` no upload to tcpsnitch.org, `-p` pedantic confirmation mode, `-t MSEC` JSON dump interval (default 1000ms), `-u USEC` tcp_info dump every N µs (default 0=off), `-v` verbose; positional `<app> [<args>]` is the command/package to instrument). Disk-pressure note: `_tcpsnitch` was written via Bash heredoc as a workaround after the Write tool hit ENOSPC at 99.9% disk usage — the underlying volume had only 945Mi free of 926Gi.
- **traceectl (tracee control CLI) + pyrra (SLO management) — both NEW with deep subcommand maps** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `traceectl` (aquasecurity/tracee from cmd/traceectl/cmd/{root,event,stream,metrics,version}.go cobra — control CLI for a running tracee server. 4 top-level subcommands: `event` (with nested `enable EVENT`/`disable EVENT`/`describe [EVENT]`), `stream` (real-time gRPC consumer with `*POLICIES` filter), `metrics` (tracee performance + resource stats), `version`. Shared flags `--server` (unix socket path, default `client.DefaultSocket`), `--format table/json`, `--output` (dest, default stdout). The `event describe` form lists all events when called with no arg, describes a single event when given one), `pyrra` (pyrra-dev/pyrra from main.go kong — SLO management CLI: define Service Level Objectives in YAML, generates Prometheus rules + UI. 4 top-level subcommands: `api`/`filesystem`/`kubernetes`/`generate` with shared `_shared_prometheus` (`--prometheus-url` default `http://localhost:9090`, `--external-url` for alert annotations, `--enable-prometheus3-migration` default true) and shared `_shared_fs` (`--config-files` default `/etc/pyrra/*.yaml`, `--prometheus-folder` default `/etc/prometheus/pyrra/`, `--generic-rules` for Grafana). Per-subcommand maps: `api` adds `--prometheus-external-url`/`--grafana-external-url`/`--grafana-external-org-id` (default 1)/`--grafana-external-datasource-id`/`--api-url` (default `http://localhost:9444`)/`--route-prefix`/`--ui-route-prefix`/`--prometheus-bearer-token-path`/`--prometheus-basic-auth-{username,password}`/`--tls-{cert,private-key,client-ca}-file`/`--mimir-org-id`. `kubernetes` adds `--metrics-addr` (default `:8080`)/`--config-map-mode`/`--disable-webhooks` (default true)/`--mimir-url`/`--mimir-prometheus-prefix` (default `prometheus`)/`--mimir-basic-auth-*`/`--mimir-org-id`/`--mimir-write-alerting-rules`/`--mimir-deployment-mode standalone|distributed`/`--enable-leader-election`/`--leader-election-namespace`. `generate` adds `--operator-rule` (generate prometheus-operator `PrometheusRule` files). NOTE: existing `_tracee` already covers all 16 root flags (scope/events/policy/output/artifacts/config/enrichment/signatures-dir/no-signatures/detectors/buffers/stores/server/capabilities/logging/help) — no rewrite needed).
- **retsnoop (libbpf-based return-trace tool) + cilium `features` subcommand fix** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — NEW: `retsnoop` (anakryiko/retsnoop libbpf-based return-trace from src/env.c argp_option table — Andrii Nakryiko's eBPF tool capturing error-return stacks + args of kernel functions; 33 flags grouped via argp into 6 categories (TARGETING/RUNMODE/FILTERING/ADVANCED/CONFIG/HELP). TARGETING: repeatable `*-c/--case bpf|perf` (predefined use cases), repeatable `*-e/--entry GLOB` (error-trigger functions), repeatable `*-a/--allow GLOB`, repeatable `*-d/--deny GLOB`. RUNMODE (5-way mutex): `-E/--call-stack` (default), `-T/--trace`, `-A/--capture-args`, `-B/--lbr SPEC`, `--dry-run`; plus repeatable `*-J/--inject-probe PROBE`. PROBE TYPE (3-way mutex): `-M/--kprobes-multi`, `-K/--kprobes`, `-F/--fentries`. FILTERING: repeatable `*-p/-P/-n/-N` PID/PID-deny/comm/comm-deny, `-L/--longer ms` (duration filter), `-S/--success-stacks VALUE`, `-I/--interim-stacks` (incomplete stacks), repeatable `*-x/--allow-errors` and `*-X/--deny-errors` with 32-symbol errno completion (EPERM/EACCES/EINVAL/etc.), repeatable `*--allow-retval`/`*--deny-retval`. ADVANCED: `-k/--kernel PATH` (vmlinux+DWARF), `-s/--symbolize line|inlines|none`, repeatable `*--debug multi-kprobe|full-lbr|bpf`. CONFIG: repeatable `*-C/--config`, `--config-help` (full extra-config dump). HELP: `-h/--help`, `--usage`, repeatable `*-v/--verbose` (stackable — `-vv` debug, `-vvv` extra), `-V/--version`). FIX: `_cilium` — added missing `features` subcommand (cilium-cli/cli/cmd.go `newCmdFeatures()` — reports features Cilium has enabled). The earlier May-17 completion was written before the features command was added upstream. (grafana/beyla — eBPF auto-instrumentation agent for HTTP/gRPC traces; the binary itself takes only `-config FILE` (YAML) — Beyla is overwhelmingly env-var driven: `BEYLA_CONFIG_PATH`, `BEYLA_AUTO_TARGET_EXE` (glob), `BEYLA_OPEN_PORT` (CSV), `BEYLA_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT`, `BEYLA_LOG_LEVEL` DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR, `BEYLA_LOG_FORMAT` text/json, `BEYLA_TRACE_PRINTER`, `BEYLA_ENFORCE_SYS_CAPS`, `BEYLA_AUTO_TARGET_LANGUAGE`; env vars take precedence over YAML), `parca-agent` (parca-dev/parca-agent eBPF continuous profiler from flags/flags.go kong struct — 50+ flags incl. `--http-address` (default `127.0.0.1:7071`), `--config-path`, `--node`/`--environment-type`/`--machine-id`, repeatable `*--include-env-var`, `--tracers all/java/python/ruby/go/cuda`, `--memlock-rlimit`, mutex profile rates, Go runtime tuning, language-unwinding toggles (`--python-unwinding-disable`/`--ruby-unwinding-disable`/`--java-unwinding-disable` (default disabled)/`--instrument-cuda-launch`), embedded option groups via kong prefixes: `--profiling-*` (CPU sampling freq/duration), `--metadata-*` (container runtime socket, cache disable), `--local-store-directory`, `--remote-store-*` (address/bearer-token/bearer-token-file/insecure/insecure-skip-verify/batch-write-interval/rpc-logging-enable), `--debuginfo-*` (search dirs CSV/temp-dir/strip/upload-disable/upload-max-parallel), `--symbolizer-jit-symbols`, `--otlp-address`+`--otlp-exporter grpc/http`, `--object-file-pool-*` LRU/LFU, `--dwarf-unwinding-disable`/`--dwarf-unwinding-mixed`, `--bpf-verbose-logging`, `--offline-mode-*` (storage-path/rotation-interval), `--telemetry-disable`, `--off-cpu-threshold`, `--enable-oom-prof`/`--enable-oom-prof-allocs`, `--analytics-opt-out`, `--clock-sync-interval` (default 3m)). REWRITES: `parca` (parca-dev/parca server — replaced May-20 thin completion with full pkg/parca/parca.go Flags struct version; FIXED `--mode` choices: real upstream is `all/scraper-only/forwarder` not `all/scraper`; ADDED missing `--mutex-profile-fraction`, `--block-profile-rate`, `--profile-share-server` (default `api.pprof.me:443`), repeatable `*--external-label` (scraper-only mode), repeatable `*--grpc-headers`, repeatable `*--cors-allowed-origins`, `--enable-persistence`; documented `--port` as DEPRECATED in favor of `--http-address`), `bombardier` (codesenberg/bombardier HTTP load tester — replaced May-17 completion with full args_parser.go kingpin version; ADDED missing `-s/--stream` (chunked transfer body), `-a/--disableKeepAlives`, `--cert`/`--key` (TLS client cert), 3-way mutex `--fasthttp`/`--http1`/`--http2`; FIXED `--method` choices to include `CONNECT`/`TRACE`; documented required URL positional).
- **4 cargo-tool REWRITES verified against upstream clap/argh sources** (no new files — fixed bugs in existing completions; corpus unchanged at 28,207) — `cargo-machete` (bnjbvr/cargo-machete from src/main.rs argh MacheteArgs — REMOVED fake `--ignore` flag that doesn't exist upstream; ADDED missing `--no-ignore` (search ignored .gitignore/.ignore files); cleaned the wrapper-function pattern. Real flags: `--with-metadata`, `--skip-target-dir`, `--fix`, `--no-ignore`, `--version`, `--help` (argh has no short forms upstream); positional `paths`), `cargo-sweep` (holmgr/cargo-sweep from src/cli.rs Sweep clap — ADDED missing `-a/--all` (apply on all provided projects); FIXED `-s/--stamp` to take value `=path` instead of flag-only (upstream: stamp takes the timestamp-file path); FIXED `-f/--file` to take value `=path`; clarified criterion group constraint (exactly one of stamp/file/all/time/installed/toolchains/maxsize required). All 14 flags now match `[-d/--dry-run, -f/--file, --hidden, -i/--installed, -m/--maxsize, -r/--recursive, -s/--stamp, -t/--time, -a/--all, --toolchains, *-v/--verbose stackable]`), `cargo-rdme` (orium/cargo-rdme from src/options.rs clap — ADDED missing `-r` short form for `--readme-path`, missing `-f` short form for `--force`, missing `--entrypoint auto/lib/bin` choices, missing `--line-terminator auto/lf/crlf` choices, missing `--heading-base-level N` heading-offset; CLEAN: `--intralinks-strip-links`, `--intralinks-docs-rs-base-url URL`, `--intralinks-docs-rs-version VER` all confirmed real upstream. Documented exit codes 0/3/4 from spec), `cargo-modules` (regexident/cargo-modules from src/options.rs + src/command.rs — REMOVED non-existent `generate` subcommand (upstream Command enum has ONLY `structure`/`dependencies`/`orphans`). ADDED proper shared option groups `_project` (ProjectOptions: `--lib`/`--bin name` mutex via clap `group="target-group"`, `-p/--package`, `--no-default-features`, `--all-features`, repeatable `*--features`, `--target`, `--manifest-path` default `.`) and `_general` (GeneralOptions: `--verbose`); per-subcommand maps for structure (`--with-orphans`/`--with-tests`/`--with-types`/`--no-fns`/`--no-traits`/`--no-types`/`--sort-by name|visibility|kind`/`--max-depth`/`--focus-on`/`--cfg-test`), dependencies (`--no-externs`/`--no-sysroot`/`--no-uses`/`--acyclic`/`--layout dot|neato|twopi|circo|fdp|sfdp` Graphviz/`--focus-on`/`--max-depth`), orphans (`--cfg-test`/`--deny` exit-non-zero-if-found)).
- **bcc/eBPF NFS/ZFS distribution tracers + reset-trace + shell wrappers + cargo-msrv rewrite** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `nfsslower` + `nfsslower-bpfcc` (iovisor/bcc tools/nfsslower.py — trace slow NFS file ops (read/write/open/getattr) on the client; 3 flags `-j/--csv`, `-p/--pid`, `--ebpf`, positional `min_ms` default 10), `nfsdist` + `nfsdist-bpfcc` (tools/nfsdist.py — NFS operation latency histogram on the client; 5 flags `-T/--notimestamp`, `-m/--milliseconds` (default µs), `-p/--pid`, `--ebpf`, interval/count positionals), `zfsdist` + `zfsdist-bpfcc` (tools/zfsdist.py — ZFS operation latency histogram; same 5-flag shape as nfsdist), `reset-trace` + `reset-trace-bpfcc` (tools/reset-trace.sh — bash-only ftrace state cleanup; 4 flags `-F` force-reset-all, `-v` verbose, `-q` quiet, `-h` help. No Python, no eBPF — clears lingering ftrace config), `cobjnew` + `cobjnew-bpfcc` (tools/cobjnew.sh — 2-line bash wrapper that pins `uobjnew -l c` for C-only allocation tracing. Inherits uobjnew flags MINUS `-l/--language` (hardcoded to `c`): `-C/--top-count`, `-S/--top-size`, `-v/--verbose`, `--ebpf` + pid/interval positionals), `nodegc` + `nodegc-bpfcc` (tools/nodegc.sh — 2-line bash wrapper that pins `ugc -l node` for Node.js V8 GC event tracing. Inherits ugc flags MINUS `-l/--language` (hardcoded to `node`): `-v/--verbose`, `-m/--milliseconds`, `-M/--minimum ms` (default 0), `-F/--filter` text, `--ebpf` + pid positional). REWRITE: `cargo-msrv` (foresterre/cargo-msrv — replaced thin May-17 completion with full clap-derive-faithful version from src/cli/*.rs: 5 subcommands `find`/`list`/`set`/`show`/`verify`/`help` with shared option groups extracted as zsh arrays (`_shared` from SharedOpts+UserOutputOpts+DebugOutputOpts; `_rust_releases` for `--min`/`--minimum`/`--max`/`--maximum`/`--include-all-patch-releases`/`--release-source` rust-changelog|rust-dist; `_toolchain` for `--target`/repeatable `*--component` rustc|cargo|rustfmt|clippy|rust-src|rust-analyzer|llvm-tools-preview|miri|rust-docs; `_custom_check` for repeatable `*--features`/`--all-features`/`--no-default-features`). Deep per-subcommand maps: `find` adds mutex `--bisect`/`--linear`, alias pairs `--write-toolchain-file`/`--toolchain-file` and `--write-msrv`/`--set`, plus `--ignore-lockfile`/`--skip-unavailable-toolchains`/`--no-check-feedback` and the `--` passthrough custom check cmd. `list` adds `--variant direct-deps/direct-deps-extended/ordered-by-msrv`. `verify` adds `--rust-version` + `--ignore-lockfile`/`--no-check-feedback` + passthrough).
- **bcc/eBPF generic trace + error injection + tty/netq/critical/db/tplist** (dup-checked clean against `/usr/share/zsh` + `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh` + `/usr/local/share/zsh`) — `trace-bpfcc` (BCC variant only — macOS/BSD `trace` ktrace tool already covered by src/_trace) — iovisor/bcc tools/trace.py — generic event-tracing front-end attaching to functions and printing trace messages; 23 flags incl. `-b/--buffer-pages` (default 64), `-p/--pid` (dest=tgid), `-L/--tid` (dest=pid), `--uid`, `-v/--verbose` print-BPF, `-Z/--string-size` (default 80), `-S/--include-self`, `-M/--max-events`, `-t/--timestamp`, `-u/--unix-timestamp` (requires `-t`), `-T/--time`, `-C/--print_cpu`, `-c/--cgroup-path`, `-n/--name` proc-name substring, `-f/--msg-filter` event-msg substring, `-B/--bin_cmp` STRCMP with binary, `-s/--sym_file_list` CSV symbol files, `-K/--kernel-stack`, `-U/--user-stack`, `-a/--address` virtual addr in stacks, repeatable `*-I/--include` headers, `-A/--aggregate` aggregate amounts, `--ebpf`; probe spec `PROBE[(FILTER)]["FORMAT"[,ARGS]]` where `PROBE={p,r,t,u}:[LIB]:FUNC[+OFF]`), `inject` + `inject-bpfcc` (tools/inject.py — kernel error injection via `bpf_override_return` on selected call chains; 5 flags `*-I/--include` repeatable header, `-P/--probability` (default 1), `-v/--verbose`, `-c/--count` fails-before-bypass (default -1), positional `mode kmalloc/bio/alloc_page` + `spec` call-chain), `ttysnoop` + `ttysnoop-bpfcc` (tools/ttysnoop.py — observe a TTY in real time via tty driver writes; `-C/--noclear`, `-s/--datasize` (256), `-c/--datacount` (16), `--ebpf`, positional `device` (e.g. `/dev/tty0`, `/dev/pts/N`, or pts number)), `netqtop` + `netqtop-bpfcc` (tools/netqtop.py — top-like NIC TX/RX queue packet count + bytes; 4 flags `-n/--name` iface, `-i/--interval` (default 1.0 float), `-t/--throughput`, `--ebpf`), `criticalstat` + `criticalstat-bpfcc` (tools/criticalstat.py — trace long preempt-disabled/IRQ-disabled critical sections; mutex `-p/--preemptoff`/`-i/--irqoff`, `-d/--duration us` filter (default 100)), `dbslower` + `dbslower-bpfcc` (tools/dbslower.py — trace slow MySQL/PostgreSQL queries via USDT; 6 flags `-v/--verbose`, repeatable `*-p/--pid` (dest=pids), `-x/--exe` binary path (dest=path), `-m/--threshold ms` (default 1), `--ebpf`, positional `db` choice `mysql/postgres`), `dbstat` + `dbstat-bpfcc` (tools/dbstat.py — MySQL/PostgreSQL query latency histogram; 6 flags incl. `-u/--microseconds` (default ms), `-i/--interval`, `-m/--threshold ms` (default 0), repeatable `*-p/--pid`, positional `db` choice `mysql/postgres`), `tplist` + `tplist-bpfcc` (tools/tplist.py — display kernel tracepoints and USDT probes for trace-target discovery; 4 args `-p/--pid` process USDT scan, `-l/--lib` library/executable USDT scan, repeatable stackable `*-v` verbosity (action=count, dest=verbosity), positional `filter` regex).

7 flags `-l/--language java/node/perl/php/python/ruby/tcl` (default all), `-C/--noclear`, `-S/--sort thread/method/objnew/cload/excp/gc`, `-r/--maxrows` (20), `-d/--debug` print-BPF, `--ebpf`, interval (1)/count positionals. **NOTE**: this is BCC's userspace stats tool, NOT the POSIX `ustat()` utility), `uthreads` + `uthreads-bpfcc` (tools/lib/uthreads.py — trace pthread/Java thread creation events; minimal `-p/--pid` filter, `--ebpf`, interval (1)/count positionals), `vfscount` + `vfscount-bpfcc` (tools/vfscount.py — count VFS function calls; NO argparse — manual `sys.argv[1]` parsing — single `interval` positional only).

12 flags `-t/--timestamp`, `-w/--wide` IPv6 cols, `-p/--pid`, `-P/--port` CSV ports, `-E/--errors`, `-U/--print-uid`, `-u/--uid`, `--count` aggregate, `--cgroupmap`/`--mntnsmap`, `--ebpf`, `--debug-source`), `sslsniff-bpfcc` (tools/sslsniff.py — sniff OpenSSL/GnuTLS/NSS plaintext payloads via SSL_read/SSL_write uprobes; REQUIRED `-d/--root-directories` (comma-separated dirs to observe), same `-C/-r/-s/-p` shape as filetop but no `-a` / `--read-only` / `--write-only`).

`-p/--pid` (dest=tgid), `-a/--all-files` include non-regular (sockets, FIFOs), `--ebpf`, `min_ms` default 10), `argdist` + `argdist-bpfcc` (tools/argdist.py — display function-parameter values as histogram or freq count; 10 flags incl. `-O/--offcpu`, `-T/--timestamp`, `-m/--milliseconds`, `-P/--pids` per-PID histograms, `-L/--tids` per-TID, `-p/--pid` filter, `-I/--include-idle`, `-e/--extension` avg/total/count summary, interval/count positionals), `runqlen` + `runqlen-bpfcc` (tools/runqlen.py — CPU run queue LENGTH histogram (samples queue length at intervals — distinct from runqlat's wait-time); 9 flags `-v/--verbose` include non-audit checks, `-p/--pid`, `-K/--kernel-stack`, `-U/--user-stack`, `-x/--extra` extra TID+INSETID columns, `--cgroupmap`/`--mntnsmap` BPF map filters, `--unique` dedupe stacks per pid/cgroup), `statsnoop` + `statsnoop-bpfcc` (tools/statsnoop.py — trace stat() syscalls; 5 flags `-t/--timestamp`, `-s/--sysname` include syscall name, `-x/--failed` failures-only, `-p/--pid`, `--ebpf`).

deep per-subcommand maps: `builddir` (`-o/--offline` no-internet, `-f/--force` makepkg-force, target dir positional), `install` (`--asdeps` install-as-dep, `--offline`, repeatable AUR-package positional), `tarcheck` (positional `*.pkg.tar.{xz,gz,zst}` archive), `shellcheck` (PKGBUILD or directory, defaults to `./PKGBUILD`), `upgrade` (`-d/--devel` rebuild git/hg/svn/bzr/cvs/darcs devel packages, `--printonly` outdated-list-only, repeatable `*--ignore` package).

litecoin-node is the multiprocess variant, litecoin-qt is the Qt GUI), `litecoin-cli` (src/bitcoin-cli.cpp `SetupCliArgs` — 18 flags incl. `-generate` blocks-immediately with optional nblocks/maxtries, `-getinfo`, `-netinfo` 0-4 verbosity, `-named` named-args mode, `-rpcconnect`/`-rpcport` default 9332 mainnet / 19332 testnet / 38332 signet / 19443 regtest, `-rpccookiefile`/`-rpcuser`/`-rpcpassword`/`-rpcwallet` for non-default wallets, `-rpcwait`/`-rpcwaittimeout`, `-stdin`/`-stdinrpcpass`/`-stdinwalletpassphrase`), `litecoin-tx` (src/bitcoin-tx.cpp `SetupBitcoinTxArgs` — `-create`/`-json`/`-txid` + chain selection + positional commands `delin=N`/`delout=N`/`in=TXID:VOUT[:SEQUENCE]`/`outaddr=VALUE:ADDRESS`/`outpubkey=VALUE:PUBKEY[:FLAGS]`/`outmultisig=VALUE:REQUIRED:PUBKEYS:KEY1:KEY2...[:FLAGS]`/`outscript=VALUE:SCRIPT[:FLAGS]`/`outdata=[VALUE:]DATA`/`locktime=N`/`nversion=N`/`replaceable(=N)`/`sign=SIGHASH-FLAGS`/`load=NAME:FILENAME`/`set=NAME:JSON-STRING`), `litecoin-wallet` (src/bitcoin-wallet.cpp — `-datadir`/`-wallet`/`-debug`/`-printtoconsole` + chain selection + 3 subcommands `info`/`create`/`salvage`; note Litecoin Core does NOT yet have Bitcoin Core's newer `dump`/`createfromdump` subcommands). `squashfuse` + `squashfuse_ll` + `squashfuse_ls` + `squashfuse_extract` (vasi/squashfuse SquashFS FUSE driver — flags from fuseprivate.c `sqfs_usage()` + hl.c `fuse_opts[]` + ll_main.c + ls.c + extract.c; high-level driver `squashfuse` supports `-o offset=N`/`-o subdir=PATH`/`-o notify_pipe=PATH` plus FUSE pass-through (`-o ro`/`-o allow_other`/`-o allow_root`/`-o default_permissions`/`-o direct_io`/`-o kernel_cache`/`-o auto_cache`/`-o uid=N`/`-o gid=N`/`-o umask=M`); low-level `squashfuse_ll` adds `-o timeout=N` idle-auto-unmount + `-o uid=N`/`-o gid=N` file-owner mapping; `squashfuse_ls` takes a single ARCHIVE positional with no flags; `squashfuse_extract` takes `ARCHIVE PATH_TO_EXTRACT` or `ARCHIVE -a` for whole-archive extraction).

All three share `core.add_common_arguments` from nvchecker/core.py.

4 globals `--debug`/`--config`/`--version`/`--no-color`; deep per-subcommand maps for all build-* (`-r/--chroot` repeatable, `--memory` MiB, `--timeout`, `--background`/`--nowait`, `--isolation default/nspawn/simple`, `--exclude-chroot`, `--enable-net on/off`, `*--packit-forge-project`), package types each with distinct flag sets — `add-package-pypi`/`edit-package-pypi` (`--name`/`--pythonversions`/`--packagename`/`--packageversion`/`--spec-template`), `add-package-scm`/`edit-package-scm` (`--name`/`--clone-url`/`--commit`/`--subdir`/`--spec`/`--type git/svn`/`--method rpkg/tito/tito_test/make_srpm`), `add-package-distgit` (`--distgit`/`--namespace`/`--committish`), `add-package-rubygems` (`--gem`), `add-package-custom` (`--script`/`--script-builddeps`/`--script-resultdir`/`--script-chroot`)).

nested maps for `rules` (`check`/`list`) and `config` (`init`); 9 global flags `--config` yaml/yml, `--tls-mode default/insecure/tls-insecure`, `*-v/--verbose` (stackable), `--quiet`, `--user-agent-suffix`, `--no-update-check`, `--self-update`; per-subcommand maps for compile/flash (`-kb/--keyboard`, `-km/--keymap`, `-bl/--bootloader`, `-j/--parallel`, `-e/--env`, `-c/--clean`/`--no-clean`, `-n/--dry-run`, `--target`), new-keyboard (`-l/--layout`, `-t/--type` MCU, `-u/--username`, `-n/--realname`), info (`-l/--layouts`, `-m/--matrix`, `-f/--format` friendly/text/json, `--ascii`), lint (`--strict`), console (`-b/--bootloaders-only`, `-d/--device`, `-l/--list`, `-n/--numeric`, `-t/--timestamp`, `-w/--wait`), doctor (`-y/--yes`/`-n/--no`), mass-compile (`-c/--clean`, `-t/--no-temp`, `-j/--parallel`, `-f/--filter`), docs (`-b/--browser`, `-p/--port`)), `trakt-cli` (angristan/trakt-cli — 3 real subcommands `auth`/`history`/`search` from cmd/{auth,history,search}.go cobra with `--client-id`/`--client-secret` for auth device-code flow, `--type` movies/shows/seasons/episodes filter + `--limit`/`--start-at`/`--end-at` for history, `--type` movie/show/episode/person/list for search; config defaults to `~/.trakt.yaml`), `smithy` (smithy-lang/smithy AWS service-modeling IDL CLI — 12 subcommands `ast`/`build`/`clean`/`diff`/`format`/`init`/`lock`/`migrate`/`select`/`validate`/`version`/`warmup` from smithy-cli/src/main/java/.../commands/*.java with shared `ConfigOptions`/`DiscoveryOptions`/`ValidatorOptions` flag groups: `-c/--config` smithy-build.json + `--no-config`, `--discover`/`--discover-classpath`, `--severity` NOTE/WARNING/DANGER/ERROR + `--no-validate` + `--hide-validators`/`--show-validators` + `--quiet-validation-summary`, plus standard `-q/--quiet`, `--debug`, `--stacktrace`, `--logging` OFF/SEVERE/WARNING/INFO/CONFIG/FINE/FINER/FINEST/ALL, `--no-color`/`--force-color`; per-subcommand maps for build (`--projection`/`--plugin`/`--output`), diff (`--mode` arbitrary/project/git + `--old`/`--new` + `--old-format`/`--new-format`/`--format` text/csv/json + `--events-format`), init (`-l/--list`/`-o/--output`/`-t/--template`/`-u/--url`/`--include-archive`), migrate (`-d/--dry-run`), select (`-e/--expression` selector + `--show-traits` + `--vars`)).
